<sub>⏱️ 4m 53s</sub>

### 👤 User

I have recently started to refactor the parser into using a BlockParser struct, in order to modularize and improve code structure:

@src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs

Some elements have been migrated, but far from all.

The inspiration for this was to better resemble what pandoc is doing:

@pandoc/src/Text/Pandoc/Readers/Markdown.hs

Now I would like an objective assessment of this approach.

In particular, I am having trouble understanding the point of can_parse(). Is there any? It appears that it if often calling try_parse_* functions, which are then also called in parse(), which seems wasteful to me, potentially hampering performance too.

The previous implemention was in 0ba25b6dd38385f4374f49ee186494a123add433, which was much less structured, and needs a rehaul.

I am wondering if I am on the right track? If my refactoring is correct?

---

<sub>⏱️ 5m 5s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’ll inspect the new BlockParser design (especially `can_parse()`), and compare it to the pre-refactor implementation and Pandoc’s approach.

1. //! Block parser dispatcher for organizing block-level parsing.
2. //!
3. //! This module provides a trait-based abstraction for block parsers,
4. //! making it easier to add new block types and reducing duplication in parse_inner_content.
5. //!
6. //! Design principles:
7. //! - Single-pass parsing preserved (no backtracking)
8. //! - Each block parser operates independently
9. //! - Inline parsing still integrated (called from within block parsing)
10. //! - Maintains exact CST structure and losslessness
11. 
12. use crate::config::Config;
13. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
14. 
15. use super::blocks::code_blocks::{
16.     CodeBlockType, InfoString, parse_fenced_code_block, try_parse_fence_open,
17. };
18. use super::blocks::figures::{parse_figure, try_parse_figure};
19. use super::blocks::headings::{
20.     emit_atx_heading, emit_setext_heading, try_parse_atx_heading, try_parse_setext_heading,
21. };
22. use super::blocks::horizontal_rules::{emit_horizontal_rule, try_parse_horizontal_rule};
23. use super::blocks::metadata::try_parse_yaml_block;
24. use super::blocks::reference_links::try_parse_reference_definition;
25. use super::utils::container_stack::{ContainerStack, byte_index_at_column};
26. use super::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
27. 
28. /// Information about list indentation context.
29. ///
30. /// Used by block parsers that need to handle indentation stripping
31. /// when parsing inside list items (e.g., fenced code blocks).
32. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
33. pub(crate) struct ListIndentInfo {
34.     /// Number of columns to strip for list content
35.     pub content_col: usize,
36. }
37. 
38. /// Context passed to block parsers for decision-making.
39. ///
40. /// Contains immutable references to parser state that block parsers need
41. /// to check conditions (e.g., blank line before, blockquote depth, etc.).
42. pub(crate) struct BlockContext<'a> {
43.     /// Current line content (after blockquote markers stripped if any)
44.     pub content: &'a str,
45. 
46.     /// Whether there was a blank line before this line
47.     pub has_blank_before: bool,
48. 
49.     /// Whether we're at document start (pos == 0)
50.     pub at_document_start: bool,
51. 
52.     /// Current blockquote depth
53.     pub blockquote_depth: usize,
54. 
55.     /// Parser configuration
56.     pub config: &'a Config,
57. 
58.     /// Container stack for checking context (lists, blockquotes, etc.)
59.     #[allow(dead_code)] // Will be used as we migrate more blocks
60.     pub containers: &'a ContainerStack,
61. 
62.     /// Base indentation from container context (footnotes, definitions)
63.     pub content_indent: usize,
64. 
65.     /// List indentation info if inside a list
66.     pub list_indent_info: Option<ListIndentInfo>,
67. 
68.     /// Next line content for lookahead (used by setext headings)
69.     pub next_line: Option<&'a str>,
70. }
71. 
72. /// Result of detecting whether a block can be parsed.
73. pub(crate) enum BlockDetectionResult {
74.     /// Can parse this block, requires blank line before
75.     Yes,
76. 
77.     /// Can parse this block and can interrupt paragraphs (no blank line needed)
78.     #[allow(dead_code)] // Will be used when we migrate fenced code blocks
79.     YesCanInterrupt,
80. 
81.     /// Cannot parse this content
82.     No,
83. }
84. 
85. /// Trait for block-level parsers.
86. ///
87. /// Each block type implements this trait with a two-phase approach:
88. /// 1. Detection: Can this block type parse this content? (lightweight, no emission)
89. /// 2. Parsing: Actually parse and emit the block to the builder (called after preparation)
90. ///
91. /// This separation allows the caller to:
92. /// - Prepare for block elements (close paragraphs, flush buffers) BEFORE emission
93. /// - Handle blocks that can interrupt paragraphs vs those that need blank lines
94. /// - Maintain correct CST node ordering
95. ///
96. /// Note: This is purely organizational - the trait doesn't introduce
97. /// backtracking or multiple passes. Each parser operates during the
98. /// single forward pass through the document.
99. pub(crate) trait BlockParser {
100.     /// Detect if this parser can handle the content (lightweight check, no emission).
101.     ///
102.     /// Returns:
103.     /// - `Yes`: Can parse, requires blank line before
104.     /// - `YesCanInterrupt`: Can parse and can interrupt paragraphs
105.     /// - `No`: Cannot parse this content
106.     ///
107.     /// This method should be fast and do minimal work (peek at first few characters).
108.     /// It should NOT emit anything to the builder.
109.     ///
110.     /// # Parameters
111.     /// - `ctx`: Context with content and parser state
112.     /// - `lines`: All lines in the document (for look-ahead if needed)
113.     /// - `line_pos`: Current line position
114.     fn can_parse(
115.         &self,
116.         ctx: &BlockContext,
117.         lines: &[&str],
118.         line_pos: usize,
119.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult;
120. 
121.     /// Parse and emit this block type to the builder.
122.     ///
123.     /// Called only after `can_parse` returns `Yes` or `YesCanInterrupt`, and after
124.     /// the caller has prepared (closed paragraphs, flushed buffers).
125.     ///
126.     /// # Arguments
127.     /// - `ctx`: Context about the current parsing state
128.     /// - `builder`: Builder to emit syntax nodes to
129.     /// - `lines`: Full document lines (for multi-line blocks)
130.     /// - `line_pos`: Current line position in the document
131.     ///
132.     /// # Returns
133.     /// Number of lines consumed by this block
134.     ///
135.     /// # Single-pass guarantee
136.     /// This method is called during the single forward pass. It should:
137.     /// - Read ahead in `lines` if needed (tables, code blocks, etc.)
138.     /// - Emit inline elements immediately via inline_emission
139.     /// - Not modify any state outside of builder emission
140.     fn parse(
141.         &self,
142.         ctx: &BlockContext,
143.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
144.         lines: &[&str],
145.         line_pos: usize,
146.     ) -> usize;
147. 
148.     /// Name of this block parser (for debugging/logging)
149.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str;
150. }
151. 
152. // ============================================================================
153. // Concrete Block Parser Implementations
154. // ============================================================================
155.

159. impl BlockParser for HorizontalRuleParser {
160.     fn can_parse(
161.         &self,
162.         ctx: &BlockContext,
163.         _lines: &[&str],

165.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
166.         // Must have blank line before
167.         if !ctx.has_blank_before {
168.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
169.         }
170. 
171.         // Check if this looks like a horizontal rule
172.         if try_parse_horizontal_rule(ctx.content).is_some() {
173.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
174.         } else {

182.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
183.         lines: &[&str],
184.         line_pos: usize,
185.     ) -> usize {
186.         // Use ctx.content (blockquote markers already stripped)

188.         let (_, newline_str) = strip_newline(lines[line_pos]);
189.         let content_with_newline = if !newline_str.is_empty() {
190.             format!("{}{}", ctx.content.trim_end(), newline_str)
191.         } else {
192.             ctx.content.to_string()
193.         };
194. 
195.         emit_horizontal_rule(builder, &content_with_newline);
196.         1 // Consumed 1 line
197.     }
198. 
199.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
200.         "horizontal_rule"
201.     }
202. }

245. /// YAML metadata block parser (--- ... ---/...)
246. pub(crate) struct YamlMetadataParser;
247. 
248. impl BlockParser for YamlMetadataParser {
249.     fn can_parse(
250.         &self,
251.         ctx: &BlockContext,
252.         lines: &[&str],
253.         line_pos: usize,
254.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
255.         // Must be at top level (not inside blockquotes)
256.         if ctx.blockquote_depth > 0 {
257.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
258.         }
259. 
260.         // Must start with ---
261.         if ctx.content.trim() != "---" {
262.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
263.         }
264. 
265.         // YAML needs blank line before OR be at document start
266.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
267.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
268.         }
269. 
270.         // Look ahead: next line must NOT be blank (to distinguish from horizontal rule)
271.         if line_pos + 1 < lines.len() {
272.             let next_line = lines[line_pos + 1];
273.             if next_line.trim().is_empty() {
274.                 // This is a horizontal rule, not YAML
275.                 return BlockDetectionResult::No;
276.             }
277.         } else {
278.             // No content after ---, can't be YAML
279.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
280.         }
281. 
282.         BlockDetectionResult::Yes
283.     }
284. 
285.     fn parse(
286.         &self,
287.         ctx: &BlockContext,
288.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
289.         lines: &[&str],
290.         line_pos: usize,
291.     ) -> usize {
292.         // Pass at_document_start to try_parse_yaml_block
293.         if let Some(new_pos) = try_parse_yaml_block(lines, line_pos, builder, ctx.at_document_start)
294.         {
295.             new_pos - line_pos // Return lines consumed
296.         } else {
297.             // Should not happen since can_parse returned Yes
298.             1 // Consume at least the opening line
299.         }
300.     }
301. 
302.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
303.         "yaml_metadata"
304.     }
305. }
306.

347. /// Reference definition parser ([label]: url "title")
348. pub(crate) struct ReferenceDefinitionParser;
349. 
350. impl BlockParser for ReferenceDefinitionParser {
351.     fn can_parse(
352.         &self,
353.         ctx: &BlockContext,
354.         _lines: &[&str],
355.         _line_pos: usize,
356.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
357.         // Reference definitions don't need blank line before
358.         // Check if this looks like a reference definition
359.         if try_parse_reference_definition(ctx.content).is_some() {
360.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
361.         } else {
362.             BlockDetectionResult::No
363.         }
364.     }
365. 
366.     fn parse(
367.         &self,
368.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
369.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
370.         lines: &[&str],
371.         line_pos: usize,
372.     ) -> usize {
373.         use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
374. 
375.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::REFERENCE_DEFINITION.into());
376. 
377.         let full_line = lines[line_pos];
378.         let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(full_line);
379. 
380.         // Parse the reference definition with inline structure for the label
381.         emit_reference_definition_content(builder, content_without_newline);
382. 
383.         // Emit newline separately if present
384.         if !line_ending.is_empty() {
385.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
386.         }
387. 
388.         builder.finish_node();
389. 
390.         1 // Consumed 1 line
391.     }
392. 
393.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
394.         "reference_definition"
395.     }
396. }
397. 
398. /// Helper function to emit reference definition content with inline structure.
399. fn emit_reference_definition_content(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, text: &str) {
400.     use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
401. 
402.     if !text.starts_with('[') {
403.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
404.         return;
405.     }
406. 
407.     let rest = &text[1..];
408.     if let Some(close_pos) = rest.find(']') {
409.         let label = &rest[..close_pos];
410.         let after_bracket = &rest[close_pos + 1..];
411. 
412.         if after_bracket.starts_with(':') {
413.             // Emit LINK node with the label
414.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK.into());
415. 
416.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into());
417.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into(), "[");
418.             builder.finish_node();
419. 
420.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_TEXT.into());
421.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), label);
422.             builder.finish_node();
423. 
424.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "]");
425.             builder.finish_node(); // LINK
426. 
427.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), after_bracket);
428.             return;
429.         }
430.     }
431. 
432.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
433. }
434. 
435. /// Fenced code block parser (``` or ~~~)
436. pub(crate) struct FencedCodeBlockParser;
437. 
438. impl BlockParser for FencedCodeBlockParser {
439.     fn can_parse(
440.         &self,
441.         ctx: &BlockContext,
442.         _lines: &[&str],
443.         _line_pos: usize,
444.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
445.         // Calculate content to check - may need to strip list indentation
446.         let content_to_check = if let Some(list_info) = ctx.list_indent_info {
447.             // Strip list indentation before checking for fence
448.             if list_info.content_col > 0 && !ctx.content.is_empty() {
449.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(ctx.content, list_info.content_col);
450.                 &ctx.content[idx..]
451.             } else {
452.                 ctx.content
453.             }
454.         } else {
455.             ctx.content
456.         };
457. 
458.         // Try to detect fence opening
459.         let fence = match try_parse_fence_open(content_to_check) {
460.             Some(f) => f,
461.             None => return BlockDetectionResult::No,
462.         };
463. 
464.         // Parse info string to determine block type
465.         let info = InfoString::parse(&fence.info_string);
466. 
467.         // Check if this is an executable chunk in Pandoc-like flavor
468.         let is_executable = matches!(info.block_type, CodeBlockType::Executable { .. });
469.         let is_pandoc_like = matches!(
470.             ctx.config.flavor,
471.             crate::config::Flavor::Pandoc
472.                 | crate::config::Flavor::CommonMark
473.                 | crate::config::Flavor::Gfm
474.         );
475. 
476.         // In Pandoc-like flavors, executable chunks should NEVER be code blocks
477.         // They should be parsed as inline code (``` delimiters with executable syntax)
478.         if is_executable && is_pandoc_like {
479.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
480.         }
481. 
482.         // Fenced code blocks can interrupt paragraphs UNLESS:
483.         // It has no info string (might be inline code delimiter)
484.         let has_info = !fence.info_string.trim().is_empty();
485.         let can_interrupt = has_info;
486. 
487.         // Return based on whether we can parse this block
488.         if can_interrupt {
489.             // Can interrupt paragraphs - return YesCanInterrupt
490.             BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
491.         } else if ctx.has_blank_before {
492.             // Has blank line before, can parse normally
493.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
494.         } else {
495.             // Cannot parse (would need blank line but don't have one)
496.             BlockDetectionResult::No
497.         }
498.     }
499. 
500.     fn parse(
501.         &self,
502.         ctx: &BlockContext,
503.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
504.         lines: &[&str],
505.         line_pos: usize,
506.     ) -> usize {
507.         // Calculate content to check (with list indent stripped)
508.         let list_indent_stripped = if let Some(list_info) = ctx.list_indent_info {
509.             list_info.content_col
510.         } else {
511.             0
512.         };
513. 
514.         let content_to_check = if list_indent_stripped > 0 && !ctx.content.is_empty() {
515.             let idx = byte_index_at_column(ctx.content, list_indent_stripped);
516.             &ctx.content[idx..]
517.         } else {
518.             ctx.content
519.         };
520.
````

</details>

546. // ============================================================================
547. // Setext Heading Parser (position #3)
548. // ============================================================================
549. 
550. pub(crate) struct SetextHeadingParser;
551. 
552. impl BlockParser for SetextHeadingParser {
553.     fn can_parse(
554.         &self,
555.         ctx: &BlockContext,
556.         _lines: &[&str],
557.         _line_pos: usize,
558.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
559.         // Setext headings require blank line before (unless at document start)
560.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
561.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
562.         }
563. 
564.         // Need next line for lookahead
565.         let next_line = match ctx.next_line {
566.             Some(line) => line,
567.             None => return BlockDetectionResult::No,
568.         };
569. 
570.         // Create lines array for detection function
571.         let lines = vec![ctx.content, next_line];
572. 
573.         // Try to detect setext heading
574.         if try_parse_setext_heading(&lines, 0).is_some() {
575.             // Setext headings need blank line before (normal case)
576.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
577.         } else {
578.             BlockDetectionResult::No
579.         }
580.     }
581. 
582.     fn parse(
583.         &self,
584.         ctx: &BlockContext,
585.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
586.         lines: &[&str],
587.         pos: usize,
588.     ) -> usize {
589.         // Get text line and underline line
590.         let text_line = lines[pos];
591.         let underline_line = lines[pos + 1];
592. 
593.         // Determine level from underline character (no need to call try_parse again)
594.         // can_parse() already validated this is a valid setext heading
595.         let underline_char = underline_line.trim().chars().next().unwrap_or('=');
596.         let level = if underline_char == '=' { 1 } else { 2 };
597. 
598.         // Emit the setext heading
599.         emit_setext_heading(builder, text_line, underline_line, level, ctx.config);
600. 
601.         // Return lines consumed: text line + underline line
602.         2
603.     }
604. 
605.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
606.         "setext_heading"
607.     }
608. }
609. 
610. // ============================================================================
611. // Block Parser Registry
612. // ============================================================================
613. 
614. /// Registry of block parsers, ordered by priority.
615. ///
616. /// This dispatcher tries each parser in order until one succeeds.
617. /// The ordering follows Pandoc's approach - explicit list order rather
618. /// than numeric priorities.
619. pub(crate) struct BlockParserRegistry {
620.     parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>,
621. }
622. 
623. impl BlockParserRegistry {
624.     /// Create a new registry with all block parsers.
625.     ///
626.     /// Order matters! Parsers are tried in the order listed here.
627.     /// This follows Pandoc's design where ordering is explicit and documented.
628.     ///
629.     /// **Pandoc reference order** (from pandoc/src/Text/Pandoc/Readers/Markdown.hs:487-515):
630.     /// 1. blanklines (handled separately in our parser)
631.     /// 2. codeBlockFenced
632.     /// 3. yamlMetaBlock' ← YAML metadata comes early!
633.     /// 4. bulletList
634.     /// 5. divHtml
635.     /// 6. divFenced
636.     /// 7. header ← ATX headings
637.     /// 8. lhsCodeBlock
638.     /// 9. htmlBlock
639.     /// 10. table
640.     /// 11. codeBlockIndented
641.     /// 12. rawTeXBlock (LaTeX)
642.     /// 13. lineBlock
643.     /// 14. blockQuote
644.     /// 15. hrule ← Horizontal rules come AFTER headers!
645.     /// 16. orderedList
646.     /// 17. definitionList
647.     /// 18. noteBlock (footnotes)
648.     /// 19. referenceKey ← Reference definitions
649.     /// 20. abbrevKey
650.     /// 21. para
651.     /// 22. plain
652.     pub fn new() -> Self {
653.         let parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>> = vec![
654.             // Match Pandoc's ordering to ensure correct precedence:
655.             // (2) Fenced code blocks - can interrupt paragraphs!
656.             Box::new(FencedCodeBlockParser),
657.             // (3) YAML metadata - before headers and hrules!
658.             Box::new(YamlMetadataParser),
659.             // (7) Setext headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
660.             // Must come before ATX to properly handle `---` disambiguation
661.             Box::new(SetextHeadingParser),
662.             // (7) ATX headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
663.             Box::new(AtxHeadingParser),
664.             // (15) Horizontal rules - AFTER headings per Pandoc
665.             Box::new(HorizontalRuleParser),
666.             // Figures (standalone images) - Pandoc doesn't have these
667.             Box::new(FigureParser),
668.             // (19) Reference definitions
669.             Box::new(ReferenceDefinitionParser),
670.             // TODO: Migrate remaining blocks in Pandoc order:
671.             // - (4-6) Lists and divs (bulletList, divHtml, divFenced)
672.             // - (9) HTML blocks
673.             // - (10) Tables (grid, multiline, pipe, simple)
674.             // - (11) Indented code blocks (AFTER fenced!)
675.             // - (12) LaTeX blocks (rawTeXBlock)
676.             // - (13) Line blocks
677.             // - (16) Ordered lists
678.             // - (17) Definition lists
679.             // - (18) Footnote definitions (noteBlock)
680.         ];
681. 
682.         Self { parsers }
683.     }
684. 
685.     /// Try to parse a block using the registered parsers.
686.     ///
687.     /// This method implements the two-phase parsing:
688.     /// 1. Detection: Check if any parser can handle this content
689.     /// 2. Caller prepares (closes paragraphs, flushes buffers)
690.     /// 3. Parser emits the block
691.     ///
692.     /// Returns (parser_index, detection_result) if a parser can handle this,
693.     /// or None if no parser matched.
694.     pub fn detect(
695.         &self,
696.         ctx: &BlockContext,
697.         lines: &[&str],
698.         line_pos: usize,
699.     ) -> Option<(usize, BlockDetectionResult)> {
700.         for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
701.             let result = parser.can_parse(ctx, lines, line_pos);
702.             match result {
703.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes | BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
704.                     log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
705.                     return Some((i, result));
706.                 }
707.                 BlockDetectionResult::No => {
708.                     // Try next parser
709.                     continue;
710.                 }
711.             }
712.         }
713.         None
714.     }
715. 
716.     /// Parse a block using the specified parser (by index from detect()).
717.     ///
718.     /// Should only be called after detect() returns Some and after
719.     /// caller has prepared for the block element.
720.     pub fn parse(
721.         &self,
722.         parser_index: usize,
723.         ctx: &BlockContext,
724.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
725.         lines: &[&str],
726.         line_pos: usize,
727.     ) -> usize {
728.         let parser = &self.parsers[parser_index];
729.         log::debug!("Block parsed by: {}", parser.name());
730.         parser.parse(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos)
731.     }
732. }
```

</details>

use super::blocks::blockquotes;

use super::blocks::definition_lists;

use super::blocks::figures;
use super::blocks::headings;
use super::blocks::horizontal_rules;
use super::blocks::html_blocks;
use super::blocks::indented_code;
use super::blocks::latex_envs;
use super::blocks::line_blocks;
use super::blocks::lists;
use super::blocks::metadata;
use super::blocks::paragraphs;
use super::blocks::reference_links;
use super::blocks::tables;

use super::utils::helpers::{split_lines_inclusive, strip_newline};
use super::utils::inline_emission;
use super::utils::marker_utils;
use super::utils::text_buffer;

use code_blocks::{parse_fenced_code_block, try_parse_fence_open};
use container_stack::{Container, ContainerStack, byte_index_at_column, leading_indent};
use definition_lists::{emit_definition_marker, emit_term, try_parse_definition_marker};
use fenced_divs::{is_div_closing_fence, try_parse_div_fence_open};
use figures::{parse_figure, try_parse_figure};
use headings::{emit_atx_heading, try_parse_atx_heading};
use horizontal_rules::{emit_horizontal_rule, try_parse_horizontal_rule};
use html_blocks::{parse_html_block, try_parse_html_block_start};
use indented_code::{is_indented_code_line, parse_indented_code_block};
use latex_envs::{parse_latex_environment, try_parse_latex_env_begin};
use line_blocks::{parse_line_block, try_parse_line_block_start};
use lists::{is_content_nested_bullet_marker, markers_match, try_parse_list_marker};
use marker_utils::{count_blockquote_markers, parse_blockquote_marker_info};
use metadata::{try_parse_pandoc_title_block, try_parse_yaml_block};
use reference_links::{try_parse_footnote_marker, try_parse_reference_definition};
use tables::{
    is_caption_followed_by_table, try_parse_grid_table, try_parse_multiline_table,
    try_parse_pipe_table, try_parse_simple_table,
};
use text_buffer::TextBuffer;

fn init_logger() {
    let _ = env_logger::builder().is_test(true).try_init();
}

pub struct Parser<'a> {
    lines: Vec<&'a str>,
    pos: usize,
    builder: GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
    containers: ContainerStack,
    config: &'a Config,
}

impl<'a> Parser<'a> {
    pub fn new(input: &'a str, config: &'a Config) -> Self {
        // Use split_lines_inclusive to preserve line endings (both LF and CRLF)
        let lines = split_lines_inclusive(input);
        Self {
            lines,
            pos: 0,
            builder: GreenNodeBuilder::new(),
            containers: ContainerStack::new(),
            config,
        }
    }

    pub fn parse(mut self) -> SyntaxNode {
        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]
        {
            init_logger();
        }

        self.parse_document_stack();

        SyntaxNode::new_root(self.builder.finish())
    }

    /// Emit buffered PLAIN content if Definition container has open PLAIN.
    /// Close containers down to `keep`, emitting buffered content first.
    fn close_containers_to(&mut self, keep: usize) {
        // Emit buffered PARAGRAPH/PLAIN content before closing
        while self.containers.depth() > keep {
            match self.containers.stack.last() {
                // Handle ListItem with buffering
                Some(Container::ListItem { buffer, .. }) if !buffer.is_empty() => {
                    // Clone buffer to avoid borrow issues
                    let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();

                    log::debug!(
                        "Closing ListItem with buffer (is_empty={}, segment_count={})",
                        buffer_clone.is_empty(),
                        buffer_clone.segment_count()
                    );

                    // Determine if this should be Plain or PARAGRAPH:
                    // 1. Check if parent LIST has blank lines between items (list-level loose)
                    // 2. OR check if this item has blank lines within its content (item-level loose)
                    let parent_list_is_loose = self
                        .containers
                        .stack
                        .iter()
                        .rev()
                        .find_map(|c| match c {
                            Container::List {
                                has_blank_between_items,
                                ..
                            } => Some(*has_blank_between_items),
                            _ => None,
                        })
                        .unwrap_or(false);

                    let use_paragraph =
                        parent_list_is_loose || buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content();

                    log::debug!(
                        "Emitting ListItem buffer: use_paragraph={} (parent_list_is_loose={}, item_has_blanks={})",
                        use_paragraph,
                        parent_list_is_loose,
                        buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content()
                    );

                    // Pop container first
                    self.containers.stack.pop();
                    // Emit buffered content as Plain or PARAGRAPH
                    buffer_clone.emit_as_block(&mut self.builder, use_paragraph, self.config);
                    self.builder.finish_node(); // Close LIST_ITEM
                }
                // Handle ListItem without content
                Some(Container::ListItem { .. }) => {
                    log::debug!("Closing empty ListItem (no buffer content)");
                    // Just close normally (empty list item)
                    self.containers.stack.pop();
                    self.builder.finish_node();
                }
                // Handle Paragraph with buffering
                Some(Container::Paragraph { buffer }) if !buffer.is_empty() => {
                    // Clone buffer to avoid borrow issues
                    let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
                    // Pop container first
                    self.containers.stack.pop();
                    // Emit buffered content with inline parsing (handles markers)
                    buffer_clone.emit_with_inlines(&mut self.builder, self.config);
                    self.builder.finish_node();
                }
                // Handle Paragraph without content
                Some(Container::Paragraph { .. }) => {
                    // Just close normally
                    self.containers.stack.pop();
                    self.builder.finish_node();
                }
                // Handle Definition with buffered PLAIN
                Some(Container::Definition {
                    plain_open: true,
                    plain_buffer,
                    ..
                }) if !plain_buffer.is_empty() => {
                    // Emit PLAIN node with buffered inline-parsed content
                    self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::PLAIN.into());
                    let text = plain_buffer.get_accumulated_text();
                    inline_emission::emit_inlines(&mut self.builder, &text, self.config);
                    self.builder.finish_node();

                    // Mark PLAIN as closed and clear buffer
                    if let Some(Container::Definition {
                        plain_open,
                        plain_buffer,
                        ..
                    }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
                    {
                        plain_buffer.clear();
                        *plain_open = false;
                    }

                    // Pop container and finish node
                    self.containers.stack.pop();
                    self.builder.finish_node();
                }
                // Handle Definition with PLAIN open but empty buffer
                Some(Container::Definition {
                    plain_open: true, ..
                }) => {
                    // Mark PLAIN as closed
                    if let Some(Container::Definition {
                        plain_open,
                        plain_buffer,
                        ..
                    }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
                    {
                        plain_buffer.clear();
                        *plain_open = false;
                    }

                    // Pop container and finish node
                    self.containers.stack.pop();
                    self.builder.finish_node();
                }
                // All other containers
                _ => {
                    self.containers.stack.pop();
                    self.builder.finish_node();
                }
            }
        }
    }

    /// Emit buffered PLAIN content if there's an open PLAIN in a Definition.
    /// This is used when we need to close PLAIN but keep the Definition container open.
    fn emit_buffered_plain_if_needed(&mut self) {
        // Check if we have an open PLAIN with buffered content
        if let Some(Container::Definition {
            plain_open: true,
            plain_buffer,
            ..
        }) = self.containers.stack.last()
<exited with exit code 0>

53.                                  isInlineTag, isTextTag)

60. 
61. type MarkdownParser m = ParsecT Sources ParserState m
62. 
63. type F = Future ParserState
64. 
65. -- | Read markdown from an input string and return a Pandoc document.
66. readMarkdown :: (PandocMonad m, ToSources a)
67.              => ReaderOptions -- ^ Reader options
68.              -> a             -- ^ Input
69.              -> m Pandoc
70. readMarkdown opts s = do
71.   parsed <- readWithM parseMarkdown def{ stateOptions = opts }
72.                 (ensureFinalNewlines 3 (toSources s))
73.   case parsed of
74.     Right result -> return result
75.     Left e       -> throwError e
76. 
77. -- | Read a YAML string and convert it to pandoc metadata.
78. -- String scalars in the YAML are parsed as Markdown.
79. yamlToMeta :: PandocMonad m
80.            => ReaderOptions
81.            -> Maybe FilePath
82.            -> BS.ByteString
83.            -> m Meta
84. yamlToMeta opts mbfp bstr = do
85.   let parser = do
86.         oldPos <- getPosition
87.         setPosition $ initialPos (fromMaybe "" mbfp)
88.         meta <- yamlBsToMeta (fmap B.toMetaValue <$> parseBlocks) bstr
89.         checkNotes
90.         setPosition oldPos
91.         st <- getState
92.         let result = runF meta st
93.         reportLogMessages
94.         return result
95.   parsed <- readWithM parser def{ stateOptions = opts } ("" :: Text)
96.   case parsed of
97.     Right result -> return result
98.     Left e       -> throwError e
99. 
100. -- | Read a YAML string and extract references from the
101. -- 'references' field, filter using an id predicate and
102. -- parsing fields as Markdown.
103. yamlToRefs :: PandocMonad m
104.            => (Text -> Bool)
105.            -> ReaderOptions
106.            -> Maybe FilePath
107.            -> BS.ByteString
108.            -> m [MetaValue]
109. yamlToRefs idpred opts mbfp bstr = do
110.   let parser = do
111.         case mbfp of
112.           Nothing -> return ()
113.           Just fp -> setPosition $ initialPos fp
114.         refs <- yamlBsToRefs (fmap B.toMetaValue <$> parseBlocks) idpred bstr
115.         checkNotes
116.         st <- getState
117.         let result = runF refs st
118.         reportLogMessages
119.         return result
120.   parsed <- readWithM parser def{ stateOptions = opts } ("" :: Text)
121.   case parsed of
122.     Right result -> return result
123.     Left e       -> throwError e
124. 
125. 
126. 
127. 
128. --
129. -- Constants and data structure definitions
130. --
131. 
132. isBulletListMarker :: Char -> Bool
133. isBulletListMarker '*' = True
134. isBulletListMarker '+' = True
135. isBulletListMarker '-' = True
136. isBulletListMarker _   = False
137. 
138. isHruleChar :: Char -> Bool
139. isHruleChar '*' = True
140. isHruleChar '-' = True

<sub>⏱️ 5m 14s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’m going to spot-check how `can_parse()` is used in the dispatcher and how Pandoc does its block choice, then I’ll give a clear recommendation on whether to keep it and how to avoid double-work.

33. use html_blocks::{parse_html_block, try_parse_html_block_start};
34. use indented_code::{is_indented_code_line, parse_indented_code_block};
35. use latex_envs::{parse_latex_environment, try_parse_latex_env_begin};
36. use line_blocks::{parse_line_block, try_parse_line_block_start};
37. use lists::{is_content_nested_bullet_marker, markers_match, try_parse_list_marker};
38. use marker_utils::{count_blockquote_markers, parse_blockquote_marker_info};
39. use metadata::try_parse_pandoc_title_block;
40. use reference_links::try_parse_footnote_marker;

42.     is_caption_followed_by_table, try_parse_grid_table, try_parse_multiline_table,
43.     try_parse_pipe_table, try_parse_simple_table,
44. };
45. use text_buffer::TextBuffer;
46. 
47. fn init_logger() {
48.     let _ = env_logger::builder().is_test(true).try_init();
49. }
50. 
51. pub struct Parser<'a> {
52.     lines: Vec<&'a str>,
53.     pos: usize,
54.     builder: GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
55.     containers: ContainerStack,
56.     config: &'a Config,
57.     block_registry: BlockParserRegistry,
58. }
59. 
60. impl<'a> Parser<'a> {
61.     pub fn new(input: &'a str, config: &'a Config) -> Self {
62.         // Use split_lines_inclusive to preserve line endings (both LF and CRLF)
63.         let lines = split_lines_inclusive(input);
64.         Self {
65.             lines,
66.             pos: 0,
67.             builder: GreenNodeBuilder::new(),
68.             containers: ContainerStack::new(),
69.             config,
70.             block_registry: BlockParserRegistry::new(),
71.         }
72.     }
73. 
74.     pub fn parse(mut self) -> SyntaxNode {
75.         #[cfg(debug_assertions)]
76.         {
77.             init_logger();
78.         }
79. 
80.         self.parse_document_stack();
81. 
82.         SyntaxNode::new_root(self.builder.finish())
83.     }
84. 
85.     /// Emit buffered PLAIN content if Definition container has open PLAIN.
86.     /// Close containers down to `keep`, emitting buffered content first.
87.     fn close_containers_to(&mut self, keep: usize) {
88.         // Emit buffered PARAGRAPH/PLAIN content before closing
89.         while self.containers.depth() > keep {
90.             match self.containers.stack.last() {
91.                 // Handle ListItem with buffering
92.                 Some(Container::ListItem { buffer, .. }) if !buffer.is_empty() => {
93.                     // Clone buffer to avoid borrow issues
94.                     let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
95. 
96.                     log::debug!(
97.                         "Closing ListItem with buffer (is_empty={}, segment_count={})",
98.                         buffer_clone.is_empty(),
99.                         buffer_clone.segment_count()
100.                     );
101. 
102.                     // Determine if this should be Plain or PARAGRAPH:
103.                     // 1. Check if parent LIST has blank lines between items (list-level loose)
104.                     // 2. OR check if this item has blank lines within its content (item-level loose)
105.                     let parent_list_is_loose = self
106.                         .containers
107.                         .stack
108.                         .iter()
109.                         .rev()
110.                         .find_map(|c| match c {
111.                             Container::List {
112.                                 has_blank_between_items,
113.                                 ..
114.                             } => Some(*has_blank_between_items),
115.                             _ => None,
116.                         })
117.                         .unwrap_or(false);
118. 
119.                     let use_paragraph =
120.                         parent_list_is_loose || buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content();
121. 
122.                     log::debug!(
123.                         "Emitting ListItem buffer: use_paragraph={} (parent_list_is_loose={}, item_has_blanks={})",
124.                         use_paragraph,
125.                         parent_list_is_loose,
126.                         buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content()
127.                     );
128. 
129.                     // Pop container first
130.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
131.                     // Emit buffered content as Plain or PARAGRAPH
132.                     buffer_clone.emit_as_block(&mut self.builder, use_paragraph, self.config);
133.                     self.builder.finish_node(); // Close LIST_ITEM
134.                 }
135.                 // Handle ListItem without content
136.                 Some(Container::ListItem { .. }) => {
137.                     log::debug!("Closing empty ListItem (no buffer content)");
138.                     // Just close normally (empty list item)
139.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
140.                     self.builder.finish_node();
141.                 }
142.                 // Handle Paragraph with buffering
143.                 Some(Container::Paragraph { buffer }) if !buffer.is_empty() => {
144.                     // Clone buffer to avoid borrow issues
145.                     let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
146.                     // Pop container first
147.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
148.                     // Emit buffered content with inline parsing (handles markers)
149.                     buffer_clone.emit_with_inlines(&mut self.builder, self.config);
150.                     self.builder.finish_node();
151.                 }
152.                 // Handle Paragraph without content
153.                 Some(Container::Paragraph { .. }) => {
154.                     // Just close normally
155.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
156.                     self.builder.finish_node();
157.                 }
158.                 // Handle Definition with buffered PLAIN
159.                 Some(Container::Definition {
160.                     plain_open: true,
161.                     plain_buffer,
162.                     ..
163.                 }) if !plain_buffer.is_empty() => {
164.                     // Emit PLAIN node with buffered inline-parsed content
165.                     self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::PLAIN.into());
166.                     let text = plain_buffer.get_accumulated_text();
167.                     inline_emission::emit_inlines(&mut self.builder, &text, self.config);
168.                     self.builder.finish_node();
169. 
170.                     // Mark PLAIN as closed and clear buffer
171.                     if let Some(Container::Definition {
172.                         plain_open,
173.                         plain_buffer,
174.                         ..
175.                     }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
176.                     {
177.                         plain_buffer.clear();
178.                         *plain_open = false;
179.                     }
180. 
181.                     // Pop container and finish node
182.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
183.                     self.builder.finish_node();
184.                 }
185.                 // Handle Definition with PLAIN open but empty buffer
186.                 Some(Container::Definition {
187.                     plain_open: true, ..
188.                 }) => {
189.                     // Mark PLAIN as closed
190.                     if let Some(Container::Definition {
191.                         plain_open,
192.                         plain_buffer,
193.                         ..
194.                     }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
195.                     {
196.                         plain_buffer.clear();
197.                         *plain_open = false;
198.                     }
199. 
200.                     // Pop container and finish node
201.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
202.                     self.builder.finish_node();
203.                 }
204.                 // All other containers
205.                 _ => {
206.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
207.                     self.builder.finish_node();
208.                 }
209.             }
210.         }
211.     }
212. 
213.     /// Emit buffered PLAIN content if there's an open PLAIN in a Definition.
214.     /// This is used when we need to close PLAIN but keep the Definition container open.
215.     fn emit_buffered_plain_if_needed(&mut self) {
216.         // Check if we have an open PLAIN with buffered content
217.         if let Some(Container::Definition {
218.             plain_open: true,
219.             plain_buffer,
220.             ..
```

</details>

244.     /// Close blockquotes down to a target depth.
245.     ///
246.     /// Must use `Parser::close_containers_to` (not `ContainerStack::close_to`) so list/paragraph
247.     /// buffers are emitted for losslessness.
248.     fn close_blockquotes_to_depth(&mut self, target_depth: usize) {
249.         let mut current = self.current_blockquote_depth();
250.         while current > target_depth {
251.             while !matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::BlockQuote { .. })) {
252.                 if self.containers.depth() == 0 {
253.                     break;
254.                 }
255.                 self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
256.             }
257.             if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::BlockQuote { .. })) {
258.                 self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
259.                 current -= 1;
260.             } else {
261.                 break;
262.             }
263.         }
264.     }
265. 
266.     /// Emit buffered list item content if we're in a ListItem and it has content.
267.     /// This is used before starting block-level elements inside list items.
268.     fn emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed(&mut self) {
269.         if let Some(Container::ListItem { buffer, .. }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
270.             && !buffer.is_empty()
271.         {
272.             let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
273.             buffer.clear();
274.             let use_paragraph = buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content();
275.             buffer_clone.emit_as_block(&mut self.builder, use_paragraph, self.config);
276.         }
277.     }
278. 
279.     /// Check if a paragraph is currently open.
280.     fn is_paragraph_open(&self) -> bool {
281.         matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Paragraph { .. }))
282.     }
283. 
284.     /// Close paragraph if one is currently open.
285.     fn close_paragraph_if_open(&mut self) {
286.         if self.is_paragraph_open() {
287.             self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
288.         }
289.     }
290. 
291.     /// Prepare for a block-level element by flushing buffers and closing paragraphs.
292.     /// This is a common pattern before starting tables, code blocks, divs, etc.
293.     fn prepare_for_block_element(&mut self) {
294.         self.emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed();
295.         self.close_paragraph_if_open();
296.     }
297. 
298.     /// Get current blockquote depth from container stack.
299.     fn current_blockquote_depth(&self) -> usize {
300.         blockquotes::current_blockquote_depth(&self.containers)
301.     }
302. 
303.     /// Emit or buffer a blockquote marker depending on parser state.
304.     ///
305.     /// If a paragraph is open and we're using integrated parsing, buffer the marker.
306.     /// Otherwise emit it directly to the builder.
307.     fn emit_or_buffer_blockquote_marker(
308.         &mut self,
309.         leading_spaces: usize,
310.         has_trailing_space: bool,
311.     ) {
312.         // If paragraph is open, buffer the marker (it will be emitted at correct position)
313.         if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Paragraph { .. })) {
314.             // Buffer the marker in the paragraph
315.             paragraphs::append_paragraph_marker(
316.                 &mut self.containers,
317.                 leading_spaces,
318.                 has_trailing_space,
319.             );
320.         } else {
321.             // Emit directly
322.             blockquotes::emit_one_blockquote_marker(
323.                 &mut self.builder,
324.                 leading_spaces,
325.                 has_trailing_space,
326.             );
327.         }
328.     }
329. 
330.     fn parse_document_stack(&mut self) {
331.         self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DOCUMENT.into());
332. 
333.         log::debug!("Starting document parse");
334. 
335.         // Check for Pandoc title block at document start
336.         if self.pos == 0
337.             && !self.lines.is_empty()
338.             && let Some(new_pos) = try_parse_pandoc_title_block(&self.lines, 0, &mut self.builder)
339.         {
340.             self.pos = new_pos;
341.         }
342. 
343.         while self.pos < self.lines.len() {
344.             let line = self.lines[self.pos];
345. 
346.             log::debug!("Parsing line {}: {}", self.pos + 1, line);
347. 
348.             if self.parse_line(line) {
349.                 continue;
350.             }
351.             self.pos += 1;
352.         }
353. 
354.         self.close_containers_to(0);
355.         self.builder.finish_node(); // DOCUMENT
356.     }
357. 
358.     /// Returns true if the line was consumed.
359.     fn parse_line(&mut self, line: &str) -> bool {
360.         // Count blockquote markers on this line
361.         let (bq_depth, inner_content) = count_blockquote_markers(line);
362.         let current_bq_depth = self.current_blockquote_depth();
363. 
364.         log::debug!(
365.             "parse_line [{}]: bq_depth={}, current_bq={}, depth={}, line={:?}",
366.             self.pos,
367.             bq_depth,
368.             current_bq_depth,
369.             self.containers.depth(),
370.             line.trim_end()
371.         );
372. 
373.         // Handle blank lines specially (including blank lines inside blockquotes)
374.         // A line like ">" with nothing after is a blank line inside a blockquote
375.         // Note: lines may end with \n from split_inclusive
376.         let is_blank = line.trim_end_matches('\n').trim().is_empty()
377.             || (bq_depth > 0 && inner_content.trim_end_matches('\n').trim().is_empty());
378. 
379.         if is_blank {
380.             // Close paragraph if open
381.             self.close_paragraph_if_open();
382. 
383.             // Close Plain node in Definition if open
384.             // Blank lines should close Plain, allowing subsequent content to be siblings
385.             // Emit buffered PLAIN content before continuing
386.             self.emit_buffered_plain_if_needed();
387. 
388.             // Note: Blank lines between terms and definitions are now preserved
389.             // and emitted as part of the term parsing logic
390. 
391.             // For blank lines inside blockquotes, we need to handle them at the right depth
392.             // First, adjust blockquote depth if needed
393.             if bq_depth > current_bq_depth {
394.                 // Open blockquotes
395.                 for _ in current_bq_depth..bq_depth {
396.                     self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::BLOCKQUOTE.into());
397.                     self.containers.push(Container::BlockQuote {});
398.                 }
399.             } else if bq_depth < current_bq_depth {
400.                 // Close blockquotes down to bq_depth (must use Parser close to emit buffers)
401.                 self.close_blockquotes_to_depth(bq_depth);
402.             }
403. 
404.             // Peek ahead to determine what containers to keep open
405.             let mut peek = self.pos + 1;
406.             while peek < self.lines.len() && self.lines[peek].trim().is_empty() {
407.                 peek += 1;
408.             }
409. 
410.             // Determine what containers to keep open based on next line
411.             let levels_to_keep = if peek < self.lines.len() {
412.                 self.compute_levels_to_keep(self.lines[peek])
413.             } else {
414.                 0
415.             };
416.             log::trace!(
417.                 "Blank line: depth={}, levels_to_keep={}, next='{}'",
418.                 self.containers.depth(),
419.                 levels_to_keep,
420.                 if peek < self.lines.len() {
421.                     self.lines[peek]
422.                 } else {
423.                     "<EOF>"
424.                 }
425.             );
426. 
427.             // Check if blank line should be buffered in a ListItem BEFORE closing containers
428. 
429.             // Close containers down to the level we want to keep
430.             while self.containers.depth() > levels_to_keep {
431.                 match self.containers.last() {
432.                     Some(Container::ListItem { .. }) => {
433.                         // levels_to_keep wants to close the ListItem - blank line is between items
434.                         log::debug!(
435.                             "Closing ListItem at blank line (levels_to_keep={} < depth={})",
436.                             levels_to_keep,
437.                             self.containers.depth()
438.                         );
439.                         self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
440.                     }
441.                     Some(Container::List { .. })
442.                     | Some(Container::FootnoteDefinition { .. })
443.                     | Some(Container::Paragraph { .. })
444.                     | Some(Container::Definition { .. })
445.                     | Some(Container::DefinitionItem { .. })
446.                     | Some(Container::DefinitionList { .. }) => {
447.                         log::debug!(
448.                             "Closing {:?} at blank line (depth {} > levels_to_keep {})",
449.                             self.containers.last(),
450.                             self.containers.depth(),
451.                             levels_to_keep
452.                         );
453. 
454.                         self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
455.                     }
456.                     _ => break,
457.                 }
458.             }
459. 
460.             // If we kept a list item open, its first-line text may still be buffered.
461.             // Flush it *before* emitting the blank line node (and its blockquote markers)
462.             // so byte order matches the source.
463.             if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::ListItem { .. })) {
464.                 self.emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed();
465.             }
466. 
467.             // Emit blockquote markers for this blank line if inside blockquotes
468.             if bq_depth > 0 {
469.                 let marker_info = parse_blockquote_marker_info(line);
470.                 for i in 0..bq_depth {
471.                     if let Some(info) = marker_info.get(i) {
472.                         blockquotes::emit_one_blockquote_marker(
473.                             &mut self.builder,
474.                             info.leading_spaces,
475.                             info.has_trailing_space,
476.                         );
477.                     }
478.                 }
479.             }
480. 
481.             self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::BLANK_LINE.into());
482.             self.builder
483.                 .token(SyntaxKind::BLANK_LINE.into(), inner_content);
484.             self.builder.finish_node();
485. 
486.             self.pos += 1;
487.             return true;
488.         }
489. 
490.         // Handle blockquote depth changes
491.         if bq_depth > current_bq_depth {
492.             // Need to open new blockquote(s)
493.             // But first check blank_before_blockquote requirement
494.             if current_bq_depth == 0 && !blockquotes::can_start_blockquote(self.pos, &self.lines) {
495.                 // Can't start blockquote without blank line - treat as paragraph
496.                 paragraphs::start_paragraph_if_needed(&mut self.containers, &mut self.builder);
497.                 paragraphs::append_paragraph_line(
498.                     &mut self.containers,
499.                     &mut self.builder,
500.                     line,
501.                     self.config,
502.                 );
503.                 self.pos += 1;
504.                 return true;
505.             }
506. 
507.             // For nested blockquotes, also need blank line before (blank_before_blockquote)
508.             // Check if previous line inside the blockquote was blank
509.             let can_nest = if current_bq_depth > 0 {
510.                 // Check if we're right after a blank line or at start of blockquote
511.                 matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::BlockQuote { .. }))
512.                     || (self.pos > 0 && {
513.                         let prev_line = self.lines[self.pos - 1];
514.                         let (prev_bq_depth, prev_inner) = count_blockquote_markers(prev_line);
515.                         prev_bq_depth >= current_bq_depth && prev_inner.trim().is_empty()
516.                     })
517.             } else {
518.                 true
519.             };
520.
```

</details>

488. block = do
489.   res <- choice [ mempty <$ blanklines
490.                , codeBlockFenced
491.                , yamlMetaBlock'
492.                -- note: bulletList needs to be before header because of
493.                -- the possibility of empty list items: -
494.                , bulletList
495.                , divHtml
496.                , divFenced
497.                , header
498.                , lhsCodeBlock
499.                , htmlBlock
500.                , table
501.                , codeBlockIndented
502.                , rawTeXBlock
503.                , lineBlock
504.                , blockQuote
505.                , hrule
506.                , orderedList
507.                , definitionList
508.                , noteBlock
509.                , referenceKey
510.                , abbrevKey
511.                , para
512.                , plain
513.                ] <?> "block"
514.   trace (T.take 60 $ tshow $ B.toList $ runF res defaultParserState)
515.   return res
516. 
517. --
518. -- header blocks
519. --
520. 
521. header :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m (F Blocks)
522. header = setextHeader <|> atxHeader <?> "header"
523. 
524. atxChar :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m Char
525. atxChar = do
526.   exts <- getOption readerExtensions
527.   return $ if extensionEnabled Ext_literate_haskell exts
528.               then '='
529.               else '#'
530. 
531. atxHeader :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m (F Blocks)
532. atxHeader = try $ do
533.   level <- fmap length (atxChar >>= many1 . char)
534.   notFollowedBy $ guardEnabled Ext_fancy_lists >>
535.                   (char '.' <|> char ')') -- this would be a list
536.   guardDisabled Ext_space_in_atx_header <|> notFollowedBy nonspaceChar
537.   skipSpaces
538.   (text, raw) <- withRaw $ do
539.     oldAllowLineBreaks <- stateAllowLineBreaks <$> getState
540.     updateState $ \st -> st{ stateAllowLineBreaks = False }
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 5m 59s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

You’re on the right track: a registry/ordered list of block parsers is basically Pandoc’s `choice [...]` made explicit, and the *main* value of a separate detection phase is letting the caller decide “do I need to flush/close paragraph & list buffers before emitting this block, and can it interrupt?” while keeping precedence/ordering centralized.  
That said, in your current code `can_parse()` often isn’t “detection” but “parsing-lite” (calling `try_parse_*` that does real work, plus `SetextHeadingParser::can_parse` allocates a `Vec`, and `core.rs` ends up calling `detect()` multiple times per line in different branches), so you’re right that it’s wasted work and could become a perf footgun.  
If you keep the 2-phase API, make `can_parse()` strictly cheap (prefix/first-char checks + minimal lookahead) **or** have it return cached parse data (e.g. `Yes(ParsedFence{...})`, `Yes(level)`) that `parse()` consumes; alternatively, collapse to a single `try_parse(...) -\> Option\<ParsedBlock { can_interrupt, lines_consumed, cached_data }\>` so you still get the “prepare before emit” decision without double-invoking `try_parse_*`.

---

<sub>⏱️ 7m 55s</sub>

### 👤 User

What do you think is the best option? 

1. a single try_parse()
2. The current split, but make can_parse cheap.
3. The current split, but return data from can_parse() (maybe rename it)
4. Another option?

---

<sub>⏱️ 8m 14s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Option **3** is the best fit for your architecture: keep the “detect first, then prepare/flush, then emit” flow (which you genuinely need for CST ordering + container buffering), but make detection return a **typed match payload** so you don’t re-run `try_parse_*` in `parse()`.

Concretely: rename `can_parse` to something like `try_match`/`detect`, and have it return `Option\<BlockMatch\>` where `BlockMatch` includes (a) `can_interrupt`/`needs_blank_before` (or a small enum), (b) `lines_consumed` *or* enough data to compute it, and (c) cached parse data (`FenceOpen`, heading level, etc.) that `parse_with_match(...)` uses to emit without re-parsing.

Option 2 works but is fragile (people will “accidentally” call heavy helpers in `can_parse` again), and option 1 only really works if “try_parse” can produce a non-emitting prepared result anyway—in practice that becomes option 3 with a different name.

---

<sub>⏱️ 8m 49s</sub>

### 👤 User

Will this work with all the various block structs I have? Some are very complicated (not here yet, due to that)

---

<sub>⏱️ 9m 2s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Yes, it scales—even for “complicated” blocks—but you’ll likely need to adjust *how* you represent the match payload because you’re using `Vec\<Box\<dyn BlockParser\>\>`.  
With trait objects, the practical patterns are: (a) make the registry an `enum BlockParserKind` and return `BlockMatchKind` (also an enum) with parser-specific data, or (b) return `Box\<dyn Any\>`/`Box\<dyn BlockMatch\>` from `detect` and downcast in `parse_with_match` (more boilerplate, less type safety).  
For very complex blocks, you can keep the payload minimal (just the discriminating bits and indices) and still avoid the worst double work (e.g., don’t re-scan prefixes / re-parse fence openers / re-do expensive lookahead).

---

<sub>⏱️ 9m 26s</sub>

### 👤 User

Why do you prefer this over a single try_parse()?

---

<sub>⏱️ 9m 34s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Because you often need to decide “this line starts a block” **before** you emit anything, so the caller can flush list-item buffers / close paragraphs / adjust containers *first* to preserve CST byte order; a single `try_parse()` either (a) emits too early, or (b) must return a prepared result that you apply after preparation.  
Once you choose (b), you’ve essentially reinvented “detect returns data” (option 3), just with a different name.  
Option 3 makes that intent explicit and prevents the current “detect calls try_parse, parse calls try_parse again” duplication.

---

<sub>⏱️ 10m 8s</sub>

### 👤 User

But have you really looked at the code? This choice is extremely complicated, and it's not easy to detect up front what to return.

---

<sub>⏱️ 10m 20s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I did: in `block_dispatcher.rs` several `can_parse()` implementations already do real parsing work (`try_parse_*`, `InfoString::parse`, fence parsing; setext even allocates a `Vec`), and in `core.rs` you call `detect()` in multiple branches—so today you’re paying that cost repeatedly.  
For the complicated blocks, you don’t have to “predict” a rich payload up front: have `detect/try_match` return an **opaque prepared object** produced by the block (e.g. `PreparedBlock` with `kind/can_interrupt` + `emit(&mut builder)` + `lines_consumed()`), so each block decides what to cache internally without forcing a giant shared enum.  
That ends up being the best of “single try_parse” and option 3: one parse of the expensive bits, zero emission until the caller prepares, and no requirement that detection be trivially representable.

---

1. //! ATX heading parsing utilities.
2. 
3. use crate::config::Config;
4. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
5. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
6. 
7. use crate::parser::utils::attributes::{emit_attributes, try_parse_trailing_attributes};
8. use crate::parser::utils::inline_emission;
9. 
10. /// Try to parse an ATX heading from content, returns heading level (1-6) if found.
11. pub(crate) fn try_parse_atx_heading(content: &str) -> Option<usize> {
12.     let trimmed = content.trim_start();
13. 
14.     // Must start with 1-6 # characters
15.     let hash_count = trimmed.chars().take_while(|&c| c == '#').count();
16.     if hash_count == 0 || hash_count > 6 {
17.         return None;
18.     }
19. 
20.     // After hashes, must be end of line, space, or tab
21.     let after_hashes = &trimmed[hash_count..];
22.     if !after_hashes.is_empty() && !after_hashes.starts_with(' ') && !after_hashes.starts_with('\t')
23.     {
24.         return None;
25.     }
26. 
27.     // Check leading spaces (max 3)
28.     let leading_spaces = content.len() - trimmed.len();
29.     if leading_spaces > 3 {
30.         return None;
31.     }
32. 
33.     Some(hash_count)
34. }
35. 
36. /// Try to parse a setext heading from lines, returns (level, underline_char) if found.
37. ///
38. /// Setext headings consist of:
39. /// 1. A non-empty text line (heading content)
40. /// 2. An underline of `=` (level 1) or `-` (level 2) characters
41. ///
42. /// Rules:
43. /// - Underline must be at least 3 characters long
44. /// - Underline can have leading/trailing spaces (up to 3 leading spaces)
45. /// - All underline characters must be the same (`=` or `-`)
46. /// - Text line cannot be indented 4+ spaces (would be code block)
47. /// - Text line cannot be empty/blank
48. pub(crate) fn try_parse_setext_heading(lines: &[&str], pos: usize) -> Option<(usize, char)> {
49.     // Need current line (text) and next line (underline)
50.     if pos >= lines.len() {
51.         return None;
52.     }
53. 
54.     let text_line = lines[pos];
55.     let next_pos = pos + 1;
56.     if next_pos >= lines.len() {
57.         return None;
58.     }
59. 
60.     let underline = lines[next_pos];
61. 
62.     // Text line cannot be empty or blank
63.     if text_line.trim().is_empty() {
64.         return None;
65.     }
66. 
67.     // Text line cannot be indented 4+ spaces (would be code block)
68.     let leading_spaces = text_line.len() - text_line.trim_start().len();
69.     if leading_spaces >= 4 {
70.         return None;
71.     }
72. 
73.     // Check if underline is valid
74.     let underline_trimmed = underline.trim();
75. 
76.     // Must be at least 3 characters
77.     if underline_trimmed.len() < 3 {
78.         return None;
79.     }
80. 
81.     // Determine underline character and check consistency
82.     let first_char = underline_trimmed.chars().next()?;
83.     if first_char != '=' && first_char != '-' {
84.         return None;
85.     }
86. 
87.     // All characters must be the same
88.     if !underline_trimmed.chars().all(|c| c == first_char) {
89.         return None;
90.     }
91. 
92.     // Leading spaces in underline (max 3 for consistency with other block rules)
93.     let underline_leading_spaces = underline.len() - underline.trim_start().len();
94.     if underline_leading_spaces >= 4 {
95.         return None;
96.     }
97. 
98.     // Determine level: '=' is level 1, '-' is level 2
99.     let level = if first_char == '=' { 1 } else { 2 };
100. 
101.     Some((level, first_char))
102. }
103. 
104. /// Emit a setext heading node to the builder.
105. ///
106. /// Setext headings consist of a text line followed by an underline.
107. /// This function emits the complete HEADING node with both lines.
108. pub(crate) fn emit_setext_heading(
109.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
110.     text_line: &str,
111.     underline_line: &str,
112.     _level: usize,
113.     config: &Config,
114. ) {
115.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::HEADING.into());
116. 
117.     // Strip trailing newline from text line for processing
118.     let (text_without_newline, text_newline_str) =
119.         if let Some(stripped) = text_line.strip_suffix("\r\n") {
120.             (stripped, "\r\n")
121.         } else if let Some(stripped) = text_line.strip_suffix('\n') {
122.             (stripped, "\n")
123.         } else {
124.             (text_line, "")
125.         };
126. 
127.     // Handle leading spaces in text line
128.     let text_trimmed = text_without_newline.trim_start();
129.     let leading_spaces = text_without_newline.len() - text_trimmed.len();
130. 
131.     if leading_spaces > 0 {
132.         builder.token(
133.             SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(),
134.             &text_without_newline[..leading_spaces],
135.         );
136.     }
137. 
138.     // Try to parse trailing attributes from heading text
139.     let (text_content, attributes, space_before_attrs) =
140.         if let Some((attrs, text_before)) = try_parse_trailing_attributes(text_trimmed) {
141.             // Find where { starts in text_trimmed to get the space between text and attributes
142.             let start_brace_pos = text_trimmed.rfind('{').unwrap();
143.             let space = &text_trimmed[text_before.len()..start_brace_pos];
144.             (text_before, Some(attrs), space)
145.         } else {
146.             (text_trimmed, None, "")
147.         };
148. 
149.     // Emit heading content with inline parsing
150.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::HEADING_CONTENT.into());
151.     if !text_content.is_empty() {
152.         inline_emission::emit_inlines(builder, text_content, config);
153.     }
154.     builder.finish_node();
155. 
156.     // Emit space before attributes if present
157.     if !space_before_attrs.is_empty() {
158.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), space_before_attrs);
159.     }
160. 
161.     // Emit attributes if present
162.     if let Some(attrs) = attributes {
163.         emit_attributes(builder, &attrs);
164.     }
165. 
166.     // Emit newline after text line
167.     if !text_newline_str.is_empty() {
168.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), text_newline_str);
169.     }
170. 
171.     // Strip trailing newline from underline for processing
172.     let (underline_without_newline, underline_newline_str) =
173.         if let Some(stripped) = underline_line.strip_suffix("\r\n") {
174.             (stripped, "\r\n")
175.         } else if let Some(stripped) = underline_line.strip_suffix('\n') {
176.             (stripped, "\n")
177.         } else {
178.             (underline_line, "")
179.         };
180. 
181.     // Emit underline leading spaces if present
182.     let underline_trimmed = underline_without_newline.trim_start();
183.     let underline_leading_spaces = underline_without_newline.len() - underline_trimmed.len();
184. 
185.     if underline_leading_spaces > 0 {
186.         builder.token(
187.             SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(),
188.             &underline_without_newline[..underline_leading_spaces],
189.         );
190.     }
191. 
192.     // Emit the setext underline as a node containing a token
193.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::SETEXT_HEADING_UNDERLINE.into());
194.     builder.token(
195.         SyntaxKind::SETEXT_HEADING_UNDERLINE.into(),
196.         underline_trimmed,
197.     );
198.     builder.finish_node();
199. 
200.     // Emit trailing newline after underline
201.     if !underline_newline_str.is_empty() {
202.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), underline_newline_str);
203.     }
204. 
205.     builder.finish_node(); // HEADING
206. }
207. 
208. /// Emit an ATX heading node to the builder.
209. pub(crate) fn emit_atx_heading(
210.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
211.     content: &str,
212.     level: usize,
213.     config: &Config,
214. ) {
215.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::HEADING.into());
216. 
217.     // Strip trailing newline (LF or CRLF) for processing but remember to emit it later
218.     let (content_without_newline, newline_str) =
219.         if let Some(stripped) = content.strip_suffix("\r\n") {
220.             (stripped, "\r\n")
```

</details>
