1. //! Fenced code block parsing utilities.
2. 
3. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
4. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
5. 
6. use super::blockquotes::count_blockquote_markers;
7. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::{strip_leading_spaces, strip_newline};
8. 
9. /// Represents the type of code block based on its info string syntax.
10. #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
11. pub enum CodeBlockType {
12.     /// Display-only block with shortcut syntax: ```python
13.     DisplayShortcut { language: String },
14.     /// Display-only block with explicit Pandoc syntax: ```{.python}
15.     DisplayExplicit { classes: Vec<String> },
16.     /// Executable chunk (Quarto/RMarkdown): ```{python}
17.     Executable { language: String },
18.     /// Raw block for specific output format: ```{=html}
19.     Raw { format: String },
20.     /// No language specified: ```
21.     Plain,
22. }
23. 
24. /// Parsed attributes from a code block info string.
25. #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
26. pub struct InfoString {
27.     pub raw: String,
28.     pub block_type: CodeBlockType,
29.     pub attributes: Vec<(String, Option<String>)>, // key-value pairs
30. }
31. 
32. impl InfoString {
33.     /// Parse an info string into structured attributes.
34.     pub fn parse(raw: &str) -> Self {
35.         let trimmed = raw.trim();
36. 
37.         if trimmed.is_empty() {
38.             return InfoString {
39.                 raw: raw.to_string(),
40.                 block_type: CodeBlockType::Plain,
41.                 attributes: Vec::new(),
42.             };
43.         }
44. 
45.         // Check if it starts with '{' - explicit attribute block
46.         if let Some(stripped) = trimmed.strip_prefix('{')
47.             && let Some(content) = stripped.strip_suffix('}')
48.         {
49.             return Self::parse_explicit(raw, content);
50.         }
51. 
52.         // Check for mixed form: python {.numberLines}
53.         if let Some(brace_start) = trimmed.find('{') {
54.             let language = trimmed[..brace_start].trim();
55.             if !language.is_empty() && !language.contains(char::is_whitespace) {
56.                 let attr_part = &trimmed[brace_start..];
57.                 if let Some(stripped) = attr_part.strip_prefix('{')
58.                     && let Some(content) = stripped.strip_suffix('}')
59.                 {
60.                     let attrs = Self::parse_attributes(content);
61.                     return InfoString {
62.                         raw: raw.to_string(),
63.                         block_type: CodeBlockType::DisplayShortcut {
64.                             language: language.to_string(),
65.                         },
66.                         attributes: attrs,
67.                     };
68.                 }
69.             }
70.         }
71. 
72.         // Otherwise, it's a shortcut form (just the language name)
73.         // Only take the first word as language
74.         let language = trimmed.split_whitespace().next().unwrap_or(trimmed);
75.         InfoString {
76.             raw: raw.to_string(),
77.             block_type: CodeBlockType::DisplayShortcut {
78.                 language: language.to_string(),
79.             },
80.             attributes: Vec::new(),
81.         }
82.     }
83. 
84.     fn parse_explicit(raw: &str, content: &str) -> Self {
85.         // Check for raw attribute FIRST: {=format}
86.         // The content should start with '=' and have only alphanumeric chars after
87.         let trimmed_content = content.trim();
88.         if let Some(format_name) = trimmed_content.strip_prefix('=') {
89.             // Validate format name: alphanumeric only, no spaces
90.             if !format_name.is_empty()
91.                 && format_name.chars().all(|c| c.is_alphanumeric())
92.                 && !format_name.contains(char::is_whitespace)
93.             {
94.                 return InfoString {
95.                     raw: raw.to_string(),
96.                     block_type: CodeBlockType::Raw {
97.                         format: format_name.to_string(),
98.                     },
99.                     attributes: Vec::new(),
100.                 };
101.             }
102.         }
103. 
104.         // First, do a preliminary parse to determine block type
105.         // Use chunk options parser (comma-aware) for initial detection
106.         let prelim_attrs = Self::parse_chunk_options(content);
107. 
108.         // First non-ID, non-attribute token determines if it's executable or display
109.         let mut first_lang_token = None;
110.         for (key, val) in prelim_attrs.iter() {
111.             if val.is_none() && !key.starts_with('#') {
112.                 first_lang_token = Some(key.as_str());
113.                 break;
114.             }
115.         }
116. 
117.         let first_token = first_lang_token.unwrap_or("");
118. 
119.         if first_token.starts_with('.') {
120.             // Display block: {.python} or {.haskell .numberLines}
121.             // Re-parse with Pandoc-style parser (space-delimited)
122.             let attrs = Self::parse_pandoc_attributes(content);
123. 
124.             let classes: Vec<String> = attrs
125.                 .iter()
126.                 .filter(|(k, v)| k.starts_with('.') && v.is_none())
127.                 .map(|(k, _)| k[1..].to_string())
128.                 .collect();
129. 
130.             let non_class_attrs: Vec<(String, Option<String>)> = attrs
131.                 .into_iter()
132.                 .filter(|(k, _)| !k.starts_with('.') || k.contains('='))
133.                 .collect();
134. 
135.             InfoString {
136.                 raw: raw.to_string(),
137.                 block_type: CodeBlockType::DisplayExplicit { classes },
138.                 attributes: non_class_attrs,
139.             }
140.         } else if !first_token.is_empty() && !first_token.starts_with('#') {
141.             // Executable chunk: {python} or {r}
142.             // Use chunk options parser (comma-delimited)
143.             let attrs = Self::parse_chunk_options(content);
144.             let lang_index = attrs.iter().position(|(k, _)| k == first_token).unwrap();
145. 
146.             // Check if there's a second bareword (implicit label in R/Quarto chunks)
147.             // Pattern: {r mylabel} is equivalent to {r, label=mylabel}
148.             let mut has_implicit_label = false;
149.             let implicit_label_value = if lang_index + 1 < attrs.len() {
150.                 if let (label_key, None) = &attrs[lang_index + 1] {
151.                     // Second bareword after language
152.                     has_implicit_label = true;
153.                     Some(label_key.clone())
154.                 } else {
155.                     None
156.                 }
157.             } else {
158.                 None
159.             };
160. 
161.             let mut final_attrs: Vec<(String, Option<String>)> = attrs
162.                 .into_iter()
163.                 .enumerate()
164.                 .filter(|(i, _)| {
165.                     // Remove language token
166.                     if *i == lang_index {
167.                         return false;
168.                     }
169.                     // Remove implicit label token (will be added back explicitly)
170.                     if has_implicit_label && *i == lang_index + 1 {
171.                         return false;
172.                     }
173.                     true
174.                 })
175.                 .map(|(_, attr)| attr)
176.                 .collect();
177. 
178.             // Add explicit label if we found an implicit one
179.             if let Some(label_val) = implicit_label_value {
180.                 final_attrs.insert(0, ("label".to_string(), Some(label_val)));
181.             }
182. 
183.             InfoString {
184.                 raw: raw.to_string(),
185.                 block_type: CodeBlockType::Executable {
186.                     language: first_token.to_string(),
187.                 },
188.                 attributes: final_attrs,
189.             }
190.         } else {
191.             // Just attributes, no language - use Pandoc parser
192.             let attrs = Self::parse_pandoc_attributes(content);
193.             InfoString {
194.                 raw: raw.to_string(),
195.                 block_type: CodeBlockType::Plain,
196.                 attributes: attrs,
197.             }
198.         }
199.     }
200. 
201.     /// Parse Pandoc-style attributes for display blocks: {.class #id key="value"}
202.     /// Spaces are the primary delimiter. Pandoc spec prefers explicit quoting.
203.     fn parse_pandoc_attributes(content: &str) -> Vec<(String, Option<String>)> {
204.         let mut attrs = Vec::new();
205.         let mut chars = content.chars().peekable();
206. 
207.         while chars.peek().is_some() {
208.             // Skip whitespace
209.             while matches!(chars.peek(), Some(&' ') | Some(&'\t')) {
210.                 chars.next();
211.             }
212. 
213.             if chars.peek().is_none() {
214.                 break;
215.             }
216. 
217.             // Read key
218.             let mut key = String::new();
219.             while let Some(&ch) = chars.peek() {
220.                 if ch == '=' || ch == ' ' || ch == '\t' {
221.                     break;
222.                 }
223.                 key.push(ch);
224.                 chars.next();
225.             }
226. 
227.             if key.is_empty() {
228.                 break;
229.             }
230. 
231.             // Skip whitespace
232.             while matches!(chars.peek(), Some(&' ') | Some(&'\t')) {
233.                 chars.next();
234.             }
235. 
236.             // Check for value
237.             if chars.peek() == Some(&'=') {
238.                 chars.next(); // consume '='
239. 
240.                 // Skip whitespace after '='
241.                 while matches!(chars.peek(), Some(&' ') | Some(&'\t')) {
242.                     chars.next();
243.                 }
244. 
245.                 // Read value (might be quoted)
246.                 let value = if chars.peek() == Some(&'"') {
247.                     chars.next(); // consume opening quote
248.                     let mut val = String::new();
249.                     while let Some(&ch) = chars.peek() {
250.                         chars.next();
251.                         if ch == '"' {
252.                             break;
253.                         }
254.                         if ch == '\\' {
255.                             if let Some(&next_ch) = chars.peek() {
256.                                 chars.next();
257.                                 val.push(next_ch);
258.                             }
259.                         } else {
260.                             val.push(ch);
````

1. //! Horizontal rule parsing utilities.
2. 
3. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
4. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
5. 
6. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
7. 
8. /// Try to parse a horizontal rule from a line.
9. /// Returns true if this line is a valid horizontal rule.
10. ///
11. /// A horizontal rule is 3 or more `*`, `-`, or `_` characters,
12. /// optionally separated by spaces.
13. pub(crate) fn try_parse_horizontal_rule(line: &str) -> Option<char> {
14.     let trimmed = line.trim();
15. 
16.     // Must have at least 3 characters
17.     if trimmed.len() < 3 {
18.         return None;
19.     }
20. 
21.     // Determine which character is being used
22.     let rule_char = trimmed.chars().next()?;
23.     if !matches!(rule_char, '*' | '-' | '_') {
24.         return None;
25.     }
26. 
27.     // Check that the line only contains the rule character and spaces
28.     let mut count = 0;
29.     for ch in trimmed.chars() {
30.         match ch {
31.             c if c == rule_char => count += 1,
32.             ' ' | '\t' => continue,
33.             _ => return None,
34.         }
35.     }
36. 
37.     // Must have at least 3 of the rule character
38.     if count >= 3 { Some(rule_char) } else { None }
39. }
40. 
41. /// Emit a horizontal rule node to the builder.
42. pub(crate) fn emit_horizontal_rule(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, line: &str) {
43.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::HORIZONTAL_RULE.into());
44. 
45.     // Strip trailing newline and emit the rule content (trimmed)
46.     let (line_without_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(line);
47.     let content = line_without_newline.trim();
48.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::HORIZONTAL_RULE.into(), content);
49. 
50.     // Emit newline separately if present
51.     if !newline_str.is_empty() {
52.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
53.     }
54. 
55.     builder.finish_node();
56. }
57. 
58. #[cfg(test)]
59. mod tests {
60.     use super::*;
61. 
62.     #[test]
63.     fn test_asterisk_rule() {
64.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("***"), Some('*'));
65.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("* * *"), Some('*'));
66.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("*  *  *"), Some('*'));
67.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("****"), Some('*'));
68.     }
69. 
70.     #[test]
71.     fn test_dash_rule() {
72.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("---"), Some('-'));
73.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("- - -"), Some('-'));
74.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("---------------"), Some('-'));
75.     }
76. 
77.     #[test]
78.     fn test_underscore_rule() {
79.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("___"), Some('_'));
80.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("_ _ _"), Some('_'));
81.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("_____"), Some('_'));
82.     }
83. 
84.     #[test]
85.     fn test_with_leading_trailing_spaces() {
86.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("  ***  "), Some('*'));
87.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("\t---\t"), Some('-'));
88.     }
89. 
90.     #[test]
91.     fn test_too_few_characters() {
92.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("**"), None);
93.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("--"), None);
94.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("__"), None);
95.     }
96. 
97.     #[test]
98.     fn test_mixed_characters() {
99.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("*-*"), None);
100.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("*_*"), None);
101.     }
102. 
103.     #[test]
104.     fn test_with_other_content() {
105.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("*** hello"), None);
106.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("---a"), None);
107.     }
108. 
109.     #[test]
110.     fn test_empty_line() {
111.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule(""), None);
112.         assert_eq!(try_parse_horizontal_rule("   "), None);
113.     }
114. }
```

1. //! YAML metadata block parsing utilities.
2. 
3. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
4. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
5. 
6. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::{emit_line_tokens, strip_newline};
7. 
8. /// Try to parse a YAML metadata block starting at the given position.
9. /// Returns the new position after the block if successful, None otherwise.
10. ///
11. /// A YAML block:
12. /// - Starts with `---` (not followed by blank line)
13. /// - Ends with `---` or `...`
14. /// - At document start OR preceded by blank line
15. pub(crate) fn try_parse_yaml_block(
16.     lines: &[&str],
17.     pos: usize,
18.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
19.     at_document_start: bool,
20. ) -> Option<usize> {
21.     if pos >= lines.len() {
22.         return None;
23.     }
24. 
25.     let line = lines[pos];
26. 
27.     // Must start with ---
28.     if line.trim() != "---" {
29.         return None;
30.     }
31. 
32.     // If not at document start, previous line must be blank
33.     if !at_document_start && pos > 0 {
34.         let prev_line = lines[pos - 1];
35.         if !prev_line.trim().is_empty() {
36.             return None;
37.         }
38.     }
39. 
40.     // Check that next line (if exists) is NOT blank (this distinguishes from horizontal rule)
41.     if pos + 1 < lines.len() {
42.         let next_line = lines[pos + 1];
43.         if next_line.trim().is_empty() {
44.             // This is likely a horizontal rule, not YAML
45.             return None;
46.         }
47.     } else {
48.         // No content after ---, can't be a YAML block
49.         return None;
50.     }
51. 
52.     // Start metadata node
53.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::YAML_METADATA.into());
54. 
55.     // Opening delimiter - strip newline before emitting
56.     let (text, newline_str) = strip_newline(line);
57.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::YAML_METADATA_DELIM.into(), text.trim());
58.     if !newline_str.is_empty() {
59.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
60.     }
61. 
62.     let mut current_pos = pos + 1;
63.     let mut found_closing = false;
64. 
65.     // Collect content until we find closing delimiter
66.     while current_pos < lines.len() {
67.         let content_line = lines[current_pos];
68. 
69.         // Check for closing delimiter
70.         if content_line.trim() == "---" || content_line.trim() == "..." {
71.             found_closing = true;
72.             let (text, newline_str) = strip_newline(content_line);
73.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::YAML_METADATA_DELIM.into(), text.trim());
74.             if !newline_str.is_empty() {
75.                 builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
76.             }
77.             current_pos += 1;
78.             break;
79.         }
80. 
81.         // Add content line
82.         emit_line_tokens(builder, content_line);
83.         current_pos += 1;
84.     }
85. 
86.     builder.finish_node(); // YamlMetadata
87. 
88.     if found_closing {
89.         Some(current_pos)
90.     } else {
91.         // No closing delimiter found - this might be a horizontal rule after all
92.         // or malformed YAML. For now, accept it.
93.         Some(current_pos)
94.     }
95. }
96. 
97. /// Try to parse a Pandoc title block starting at the beginning of document.
98. /// Returns the new position after the block if successful, None otherwise.
99. ///
100. /// A Pandoc title block:
101. /// - Must be at document start (pos == 0)
102. /// - Has 1-3 lines starting with `%`
103. /// - Format: % title, % author(s), % date
104. /// - Continuation lines start with leading space
105. pub(crate) fn try_parse_pandoc_title_block(
106.     lines: &[&str],
107.     pos: usize,
108.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
109. ) -> Option<usize> {
110.     if pos != 0 || lines.is_empty() {
111.         return None;
112.     }
113. 
114.     let first_line = lines[0];
115.     if !first_line.trim_start().starts_with('%') {
116.         return None;
117.     }
118. 
119.     // Start title block node
120.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::PANDOC_TITLE_BLOCK.into());
121. 
122.     let mut current_pos = 0;
123.     let mut field_count = 0;
124. 
125.     // Parse up to 3 fields (title, author, date)
126.     while current_pos < lines.len() && field_count < 3 {
127.         let line = lines[current_pos];
128. 
129.         // Check if this line starts a field (begins with %)
130.         if line.trim_start().starts_with('%') {
131.             emit_line_tokens(builder, line);
132.             field_count += 1;
133.             current_pos += 1;
134. 
135.             // Collect continuation lines (start with leading space, not with %)
136.             while current_pos < lines.len() {
137.                 let cont_line = lines[current_pos];
138.                 if cont_line.is_empty() {
139.                     // Blank line ends title block
140.                     break;
141.                 }
142.                 if cont_line.trim_start().starts_with('%') {
143.                     // Next field
144.                     break;
145.                 }
146.                 if cont_line.starts_with(' ') || cont_line.starts_with('\t') {
147.                     // Continuation line
148.                     emit_line_tokens(builder, cont_line);
149.                     current_pos += 1;
150.                 } else {
151.                     // Non-continuation, non-% line ends title block
152.                     break;
153.                 }
154.             }
155.         } else {
156.             // Line doesn't start with %, title block ends
157.             break;
158.         }
159.     }
160. 
161.     builder.finish_node(); // PandocTitleBlock
162. 
163.     if field_count > 0 {
164.         Some(current_pos)
165.     } else {
166.         None
167.     }
168. }
169. 
170. #[cfg(test)]
171. mod tests {
172.     use super::*;
173. 
174.     #[test]
175.     fn test_yaml_block_at_start() {
176.         let lines = vec!["---", "title: Test", "---", "Content"];
177.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
178.         let result = try_parse_yaml_block(&lines, 0, &mut builder, true);
179.         assert_eq!(result, Some(3));
180.     }
181. 
182.     #[test]
183.     fn test_yaml_block_not_at_start() {
184.         let lines = vec!["Paragraph", "", "---", "title: Test", "---", "Content"];
185.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
186.         let result = try_parse_yaml_block(&lines, 2, &mut builder, false);
187.         assert_eq!(result, Some(5));
188.     }
189. 
190.     #[test]
191.     fn test_horizontal_rule_not_yaml() {
192.         let lines = vec!["---", "", "Content"];
193.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
194.         let result = try_parse_yaml_block(&lines, 0, &mut builder, true);
195.         assert_eq!(result, None); // Followed by blank line, so not YAML
196.     }
197. 
198.     #[test]
199.     fn test_yaml_with_dots_closer() {
200.         let lines = vec!["---", "title: Test", "...", "Content"];
201.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
202.         let result = try_parse_yaml_block(&lines, 0, &mut builder, true);
203.         assert_eq!(result, Some(3));
204.     }
205. 
206.     #[test]
207.     fn test_pandoc_title_simple() {
208.         let lines = vec!["% My Title", "% Author", "% Date", "", "Content"];
209.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
210.         let result = try_parse_pandoc_title_block(&lines, 0, &mut builder);
211.         assert_eq!(result, Some(3));
212.     }
213. 
214.     #[test]
215.     fn test_pandoc_title_with_continuation() {
216.         let lines = vec![
217.             "% My Title",
218.             "  on multiple lines",
219.             "% Author One",
220.             "  Author Two",
221.             "% June 15, 2006",
222.             "",
223.             "Content",
224.         ];
225.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
226.         let result = try_parse_pandoc_title_block(&lines, 0, &mut builder);
227.         assert_eq!(result, Some(5));
228.     }
229. 
230.     #[test]
231.     fn test_pandoc_title_partial() {
232.         let lines = vec!["% My Title", "%", "% June 15, 2006", "", "Content"];
233.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
234.         let result = try_parse_pandoc_title_block(&lines, 0, &mut builder);
235.         assert_eq!(result, Some(3));
236.     }
237. 
238.     #[test]
239.     fn test_pandoc_title_not_at_start() {
240.         let lines = vec!["Content", "% Title"];
241.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
242.         let result = try_parse_pandoc_title_block(&lines, 1, &mut builder);
243.         assert_eq!(result, None);
244.     }
245. }

1. //! Figure parsing for standalone images.
2. //!
3. //! In Pandoc, a paragraph containing only an image (and optional attributes)
4. //! is treated as a Figure block element rather than a paragraph with inline image.
5. 
6. use crate::config::Config;
7. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
8. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
9. 
10. use crate::parser::inlines::links::try_parse_inline_image;
11. use crate::parser::utils::helpers;
12. use crate::parser::utils::inline_emission::emit_inlines;
13. 
14. /// Try to parse a line as a standalone figure (image).
15. ///
16. /// Uses the existing inline image parser to validate the syntax properly.
17. /// Returns true if the line contains only a valid image (possibly with attributes).
18. pub(in crate::parser) fn try_parse_figure(line: &str) -> bool {
19.     let trimmed = line.trim();
20. 
21.     // Must start with ![
22.     if !trimmed.starts_with("![") {
23.         return false;
24.     }
25. 
26.     // Use the inline parser's image validation to check if this is a valid image
27.     // This handles all the bracket/paren matching, escapes, etc.
28.     if let Some((len, _alt, _dest, _attrs)) = try_parse_inline_image(trimmed) {
29.         // Check that the image spans the entire line (except trailing whitespace)
30.         // After the image, only whitespace should remain
31.         let after_image = &trimmed[len..];
32.         after_image.trim().is_empty()
33.     } else {
34.         false
35.     }
36. }
37. 
38. /// Parse a figure block (standalone image).
39. ///
40. /// Emits inline-parsed structure directly during block parsing.
41. pub(in crate::parser) fn parse_figure(
42.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
43.     line: &str,
44.     config: &Config,
45. ) {
46.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::FIGURE.into());
47. 
48.     // Split off trailing newline
49.     let (text_without_newline, newline_str) = helpers::strip_newline(line);
50. 
51.     // Parse inline content (IMAGE_LINK) directly
52.     if !text_without_newline.is_empty() {
53.         emit_inlines(builder, text_without_newline, config);
54.     }
55. 
56.     if !newline_str.is_empty() {
57.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
58.     }
59. 
60.     builder.finish_node(); // Close Figure
61. }
62. 
63. #[cfg(test)]
64. mod tests {
65.     use super::*;
66. 
67.     #[test]
68.     fn test_try_parse_figure_starts_with_image() {
69.         assert!(try_parse_figure("![alt text](image.png)"));
70.         assert!(try_parse_figure("  ![alt text](image.png)  "));
71.         assert!(try_parse_figure("![alt text](image.png)\n"));
72.         assert!(try_parse_figure("![](image.png)"));
73.         assert!(try_parse_figure("![alt text](image.png \"Title\")"));
74.     }
75. 
76.     #[test]
77.     fn test_try_parse_figure_not_a_figure() {
78.         // Has text before the image
79.         assert!(!try_parse_figure("Text before ![alt](img.png)"));
80. 
81.         // Not an image (regular link)
82.         assert!(!try_parse_figure("[text](url)"));
83. 
84.         // Empty or other content
85.         assert!(!try_parse_figure(""));
86.         assert!(!try_parse_figure("# Heading"));
87.     }
88. }
```

1. //! Reference definition and footnote parsing functions.
2. //!
3. //! Reference definitions have the form:
4. //! ```markdown
5. //! [label]: url "optional title"
6. //! [label]: url 'optional title'
7. //! [label]: url (optional title)
8. //! [label]: <url> "title"
9. //! ```
10. //!
11. //! Footnote definitions have the form:
12. //! ```markdown
13. //! [^id]: Footnote content here.
14. //!     Can continue on multiple lines
15. //!     as long as they're indented.
16. //! ```
17. 
18. /// Try to parse a reference definition starting at the current position.
19. /// Returns Some((length, label, url, title)) if successful.
20. ///
21. /// Syntax:
22. /// ```markdown
23. /// [label]: url "title"
24. /// [label]: <url> 'title'
25. /// [label]: url
26. ///          (title on next line)
27. /// ```
28. pub fn try_parse_reference_definition(
29.     text: &str,
30. ) -> Option<(usize, String, String, Option<String>)> {
31.     let bytes = text.as_bytes();
32. 
33.     // Must start at beginning of line with [
34.     if bytes.is_empty() || bytes[0] != b'[' {
35.         return None;
36.     }
37. 
38.     // Check if it's a footnote definition [^id]: - not a reference definition
39.     if bytes.len() >= 2 && bytes[1] == b'^' {
40.         return None;
41.     }
42. 
43.     // Find the closing ] for the label
44.     let mut pos = 1;
45.     let mut escape_next = false;
46. 
47.     while pos < bytes.len() {
48.         if escape_next {
49.             escape_next = false;
50.             pos += 1;
51.             continue;
52.         }
53. 
54.         match bytes[pos] {
55.             b'\\' => {
56.                 escape_next = true;
57.                 pos += 1;
58.             }
59.             b']' => {
60.                 break;
61.             }
62.             b'\n' => {
63.                 // Labels can't span lines
64.                 return None;
65.             }
66.             _ => {
67.                 pos += 1;
68.             }
69.         }
70.     }
71. 
72.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b']' {
73.         return None;
74.     }
75. 
76.     let label = &text[1..pos];
77.     if label.is_empty() {
78.         return None;
79.     }
80. 
81.     pos += 1; // Skip ]
82. 
83.     // Must be followed by :
84.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b':' {
85.         return None;
86.     }
87.     pos += 1;
88. 
89.     // Skip whitespace
90.     while pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[pos], b' ' | b'\t') {
91.         pos += 1;
92.     }
93. 
94.     // Parse URL
95.     let url_start = pos;
96.     let url_end;
97. 
98.     // Check for angle-bracketed URL <url>
99.     if pos < bytes.len() && bytes[pos] == b'<' {
100.         pos += 1;
101.         let url_content_start = pos;
102.         // Find closing >
103.         while pos < bytes.len() && bytes[pos] != b'>' && bytes[pos] != b'\n' && bytes[pos] != b'\r'
104.         {
105.             pos += 1;
106.         }
107.         if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b'>' {
108.             return None;
109.         }
110.         url_end = pos;
111.         let url = text[url_content_start..url_end].to_string();
112.         pos += 1; // Skip >
113. 
114.         // Parse optional title
115.         let title = parse_title(text, bytes, &mut pos)?;
116. 
117.         Some((pos, label.to_string(), url, title))
118.     } else {
119.         // Parse unbracketed URL (until whitespace or newline)
120.         while pos < bytes.len() && !matches!(bytes[pos], b' ' | b'\t' | b'\n' | b'\r') {
121.             pos += 1;
122.         }
123. 
124.         url_end = pos;
125.         if url_start == url_end {
126.             return None; // No URL found
127.         }
128. 
129.         let url = text[url_start..url_end].to_string();
130. 
131.         // Parse optional title
132.         let title = parse_title(text, bytes, &mut pos)?;
133. 
134.         Some((pos, label.to_string(), url, title))
135.     }
136. }
137. 
138. /// Parse an optional title after the URL.
139. /// Titles can be in double quotes, single quotes, or parentheses.
140. /// Returns Some(Some(title)) if title found, Some(None) if no title, None if malformed.
141. fn parse_title(text: &str, bytes: &[u8], pos: &mut usize) -> Option<Option<String>> {
142.     let base_pos = *pos;
143. 
144.     // Skip whitespace (including newlines for multi-line titles)
145.     while *pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[*pos], b' ' | b'\t' | b'\n' | b'\r') {
146.         *pos += 1;
147.     }
148. 
149.     // Check if there's a title
150.     if *pos >= bytes.len() {
151.         return Some(None);
152.     }
153. 
154.     let quote_char = bytes[*pos];
155.     if !matches!(quote_char, b'"' | b'\'' | b'(') {
156.         // No title, that's okay
157.         *pos = base_pos; // Reset position
158.         return Some(None);
159.     }
160. 
161.     let closing_char = if quote_char == b'(' { b')' } else { quote_char };
162. 
163.     *pos += 1; // Skip opening quote
164.     let title_start = *pos;
165. 
166.     // Find closing quote
167.     let mut escape_next = false;
168.     while *pos < bytes.len() {
169.         if escape_next {
170.             escape_next = false;
171.             *pos += 1;
172.             continue;
173.         }
174. 
175.         match bytes[*pos] {
176.             b'\\' => {
177.                 escape_next = true;
178.                 *pos += 1;
179.             }
180.             c if c == closing_char => {
181.                 let title_end = *pos;
182.                 *pos += 1; // Skip closing quote
183. 
184.                 // Skip trailing whitespace to end of line
185.                 while *pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[*pos], b' ' | b'\t') {
186.                     *pos += 1;
187.                 }
188. 
189.                 // Extract title from the original text using correct indices
190.                 let title = text[title_start..title_end].to_string();
191.                 return Some(Some(title));
192.             }
193.             b'\n' if quote_char == b'(' => {
194.                 // Parenthetical titles can span lines
195.                 *pos += 1;
196.             }
197.             _ => {
198.                 *pos += 1;
199.             }
200.         }
201.     }
202. 
203.     // No closing quote found
204.     None
205. }
206. 
207. /// Try to parse just the footnote marker [^id]: from a line.
208. /// Returns Some((id, content_start_col)) if the line starts with a footnote marker.
209. ///
210. /// Syntax:
211. /// ```markdown
212. /// [^id]: Footnote content.
213. /// ```
214. pub fn try_parse_footnote_marker(line: &str) -> Option<(String, usize)> {
215.     let bytes = line.as_bytes();
216. 
217.     // Must start with [^
218.     if bytes.len() < 4 || bytes[0] != b'[' || bytes[1] != b'^' {
219.         return None;
220.     }
221. 
222.     // Find the closing ] for the ID
223.     let mut pos = 2;
224.     while pos < bytes.len() && bytes[pos] != b']' && bytes[pos] != b'\n' && bytes[pos] != b'\r' {
225.         pos += 1;
226.     }
227. 
228.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b']' {
229.         return None;
230.     }
231. 
232.     let id = &line[2..pos];
233.     if id.is_empty() {
234.         return None;
235.     }
236. 
237.     pos += 1; // Skip ]
238. 
239.     // Must be followed by :
240.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b':' {
241.         return None;
242.     }
243.     pos += 1;
244. 
245.     // Skip spaces/tabs until content (or end of line)
246.     while pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[pos], b' ' | b'\t') {
247.         pos += 1;
248.     }
249. 
250.     Some((id.to_string(), pos))
251. }
````

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 11m 3s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

In your current migrated blocks, `can_parse()` is already *not* cheap: `FencedCodeBlockParser::can_parse` runs `try_parse_fence_open` **and** `InfoString::parse` (string/alloc-heavy), `SetextHeadingParser::can_parse` allocates a `Vec` and then calls `try_parse_setext_heading`, and `FigureParser::can_parse` calls `try_parse_inline_image` (a real inline parse) — and then several of those blocks re-run the same work again in `parse()` (ATX and fenced definitely do; setext avoids it).  
On top of that, `core.rs` calls `block_registry.detect(...)` in multiple branches (blank-before path, interrupt path, and again for refdefs), and it rebuilds `BlockContext` repeatedly; so the cost of “heavy detection” gets multiplied even before you count the duplicate parsing inside each block.  
Given *your* code shape, the cleanest fix is: keep the 2-step “detect then prepare then emit”, but make `detect` return a **prepared, parser-owned payload** (possibly opaque) that `parse` consumes (so fenced returns the parsed fence + pre-parsed info-string, setext returns level/underline info, figure returns the image span/validation result, etc.), instead of trying to force `can_parse` to be cheap everywhere.

987.     /// Get the total indentation to strip from content containers (footnotes + definitions).
988.     fn content_container_indent_to_strip(&self) -> usize {
989.         self.containers
990.             .stack
991.             .iter()
992.             .filter_map(|c| match c {
993.                 Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
994.                 Container::Definition { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
995.                 _ => None,
996.             })
997.             .sum()
998.     }
999. 
1000.     /// Parse content inside blockquotes (or at top level).
1001.     ///
1002.     /// `content` - The content to parse (may have indent/markers stripped)
1003.     /// `line_to_append` - Optional line to use when appending to paragraphs.
1004.     ///                    If None, uses self.lines[self.pos]
1005.     fn parse_inner_content(&mut self, content: &str, line_to_append: Option<&str>) -> bool {
1006.         log::debug!(
1007.             "parse_inner_content [{}]: depth={}, last={:?}, content={:?}",
1008.             self.pos,
1009.             self.containers.depth(),
1010.             self.containers.last(),
1011.             content.trim_end()
1012.         );
1013.         // Calculate how much indentation should be stripped for content containers
1014.         // (definitions, footnotes) FIRST, so we can check for block markers correctly
1015.         let content_indent = self.content_container_indent_to_strip();
1016.         let (stripped_content, indent_to_emit) = if content_indent > 0 {
1017.             let (indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(content);
1018.             if indent_cols >= content_indent {
1019.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(content, content_indent);
1020.                 (&content[idx..], Some(&content[..idx]))
1021.             } else {
1022.                 // Line has less indent than required - preserve leading whitespace
1023.                 let trimmed_start = content.trim_start();
1024.                 let ws_len = content.len() - trimmed_start.len();
1025.                 if ws_len > 0 {
1026.                     (trimmed_start, Some(&content[..ws_len]))
1027.                 } else {
1028.                     (content, None)
1029.                 }
1030.             }
1031.         } else {
1032.             (content, None)
1033.         };
1034. 
1035.         // Check if we're in a Definition container (with or without an open PLAIN)
1036.         // Continuation lines should be added to PLAIN, not treated as new blocks
1037.         // BUT: Don't treat lines with block element markers as continuations
1038.         if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Definition { .. })) {
1039.             // Check if this line starts with any block element marker
1040.             // Use stripped_content so we check AFTER removing footnote/definition indent
1041.             let is_block_element = try_parse_definition_marker(stripped_content).is_some()
1042.                 || try_parse_list_marker(stripped_content, self.config).is_some()
1043.                 || count_blockquote_markers(stripped_content).0 > 0
1044.                 || try_parse_fence_open(stripped_content).is_some()
1045.                 || try_parse_div_fence_open(stripped_content).is_some()
1046.                 || try_parse_horizontal_rule(stripped_content).is_some()
1047.                 || try_parse_atx_heading(stripped_content).is_some()
1048.                 || (self.config.extensions.raw_html
1049.                     && try_parse_html_block_start(stripped_content).is_some())
1050.                 || (self.config.extensions.raw_tex
1051.                     && try_parse_latex_env_begin(stripped_content).is_some());
1052. 
1053.             if is_block_element {
1054.                 // Close any open Plain block before processing the block element
1055.                 // Buffered PLAIN content will be emitted by emit_buffered_plain_if_needed()
1056.                 // Fall through to parse the block element
1057.             } else {
1058.                 // This is a continuation line - add to PLAIN (start one if needed)
1059.                 let full_line = self.lines[self.pos];
1060.                 let (text_without_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(full_line);
1061. 
1062.                 // Buffer the line for later inline parsing
1063.                 if let Some(Container::Definition {
1064.                     plain_open,
1065.                     plain_buffer,
1066.                     ..
1067.                 }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
1068.                 {
1069.                     // Include the newline in the buffered text for losslessness
1070.                     let line_with_newline = if !newline_str.is_empty() {
1071.                         format!("{}{}", text_without_newline, newline_str)
1072.                     } else {
1073.                         text_without_newline.to_string()
1074.                     };
1075.                     plain_buffer.push_line(line_with_newline);
1076.                     *plain_open = true; // Mark that we now have an open PLAIN
1077.                 }
1078. 
1079.                 self.pos += 1;
1080.                 return true;
1081.             }
1082.         }
1083. 
1084.         // Store the stripped content for later use
1085.         let content = stripped_content;
1086. 
1087.         // Check for heading (needs blank line before, or at start of container)
1088.         let has_blank_before = self.pos == 0
1089.             || self.lines[self.pos - 1].trim().is_empty()
1090.             || matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::BlockQuote { .. }))
1091.             || matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::List { .. }));
1092. 
1093.         // For indented code blocks, we need a stricter condition - only actual blank lines count
1094.         // Being at document start (pos == 0) is OK only if we're not inside a blockquote
1095.         let at_document_start = self.pos == 0 && self.current_blockquote_depth() == 0;
1096.         let prev_line_blank = if self.pos > 0 {
1097.             let prev_line = self.lines[self.pos - 1];
1098.             let (prev_bq_depth, prev_inner) = count_blockquote_markers(prev_line);
1099.             prev_line.trim().is_empty() || (prev_bq_depth > 0 && prev_inner.trim().is_empty())
1100.         } else {
1101.             false
1102.         };
1103.         let has_blank_before_strict = at_document_start || prev_line_blank;
1104. 
1105.         // Check for HTML block (if raw_html extension is enabled)
1106.         if self.config.extensions.raw_html
1107.             && let Some(block_type) = try_parse_html_block_start(content)
1108.         {
1109.             log::debug!("Parsed HTML block at line {}: {:?}", self.pos, block_type);
1110. 
1111.             // Prepare for HTML block
1112.             self.prepare_for_block_element();
1113. 
1114.             let bq_depth = self.current_blockquote_depth();
1115.             let new_pos = parse_html_block(
1116.                 &mut self.builder,
1117.                 &self.lines,
1118.                 self.pos,
1119.                 block_type,
1120.                 bq_depth,
```

1565.             list_indent_info: None, // Not needed for reference definitions
1566.             next_line,
1567.         };
1568. 
1569.         if let Some((parser_idx, _detection)) =
1570.             self.block_registry
1571.                 .detect(&block_ctx, &self.lines, self.pos)
1572.         {
1573.             // Reference definitions don't need preparation
1574.             let lines_consumed = self.block_registry.parse(
1575.                 parser_idx,
1576.                 &block_ctx,
1577.                 &mut self.builder,
1578.                 &self.lines,
1579.                 self.pos,
1580.             );
1581.             self.pos += lines_consumed;
1582.             return true;
1583.         }
1584. 
1585.         // Check for indented code block
1586.         // Inside a footnote, content needs 4 spaces for code (8 total in raw line)
1587.         // BUT: Don't treat as code if it's a list marker (list takes precedence)
1588.         if has_blank_before_strict
1589.             && is_indented_code_line(content)
1590.             && try_parse_list_marker(content, self.config).is_none()
1591.         {
1592.             // Prepare for indented code block
1593.             self.prepare_for_block_element();
1594. 
1595.             let bq_depth = self.current_blockquote_depth();
1596.             log::debug!("Parsed indented code block at line {}", self.pos);
1597.             let new_pos = parse_indented_code_block(
1598.                 &mut self.builder,
1599.                 &self.lines,
1600.                 self.pos,
1601.                 bq_depth,
1602.                 content_indent,
1603.             );
1604.             self.pos = new_pos;
1605.             return true;
1606.         }
1607. 
1608.         // Check for fenced div opening
1609.         if has_blank_before && let Some(div_fence) = try_parse_div_fence_open(content) {
1610.             log::debug!(
1611.                 "Parsed fenced div at line {}: {} colons",
1612.                 self.pos,
1613.                 div_fence.fence_count
1614.             );
1615. 
1616.             // Prepare for fenced div
1617.             self.prepare_for_block_element();
1618. 
1619.             // Start FencedDiv node
1620.             self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::FENCED_DIV.into());
1621. 
1622.             // Emit opening fence with attributes as child node to avoid duplication
1623.             self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_FENCE_OPEN.into());
1624. 
1625.             // Get original full line
1626.             let full_line = self.lines[self.pos];
1627.             let trimmed = full_line.trim_start();
1628. 
1629.             // Emit leading whitespace if present
1630.             let leading_ws_len = full_line.len() - trimmed.len();
1631.             if leading_ws_len > 0 {
1632.                 self.builder
1633.                     .token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &full_line[..leading_ws_len]);
1634.             }
1635. 
1636.             // Emit fence colons
1637.             let fence_str: String = ":".repeat(div_fence.fence_count);
1638.             self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), &fence_str);
1639. 
1640.             // Parse everything after colons
1641.             let after_colons = &trimmed[div_fence.fence_count..];
1642.             let (content_before_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(after_colons);
1643. 
1644.             // Emit optional space before attributes
1645.             let has_leading_space = content_before_newline.starts_with(' ');
1646.             if has_leading_space {
1647.                 self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
1648.             }
1649. 
1650.             // Get content after the leading space (if any)
1651.             let content_after_space = if has_leading_space {
1652.                 &content_before_newline[1..]
1653.             } else {
1654.                 content_before_newline
1655.             };
1656. 
1657.             // Emit attributes as DivInfo child node (avoids duplication)
1658.             self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_INFO.into());
1659.             self.builder
1660.                 .token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), &div_fence.attributes);
1661.             self.builder.finish_node(); // DivInfo
1662. 
1663.             // Check for trailing colons after attributes (symmetric fences)
1664.             let (trailing_space, trailing_colons) = if div_fence.attributes.starts_with('{') {
1665.                 // For bracketed attributes like {.class}, find what's after the closing brace
1666.                 if let Some(close_idx) = content_after_space.find('}') {
1667.                     let after_attrs = &content_after_space[close_idx + 1..];
1668.                     let trailing = after_attrs.trim_start();
1669.                     let space_count = after_attrs.len() - trailing.len();
1670.                     if !trailing.is_empty() && trailing.chars().all(|c| c == ':') {
1671.                         (space_count > 0, trailing)
1672.                     } else {
1673.                         (false, "")
1674.                     }
1675.                 } else {
1676.                     (false, "")
1677.                 }
1678.             } else {
1679.                 // For simple class names like "Warning", check after first word
1680.                 // content_after_space starts with the attribute (e.g., "Warning ::::::")
1681.                 let after_attrs = &content_after_space[div_fence.attributes.len()..];
1682.                 if let Some(after_space) = after_attrs.strip_prefix(' ') {
1683.                     if !after_space.is_empty() && after_space.chars().all(|c| c == ':') {
1684.                         (true, after_space)
1685.                     } else {
1686.                         (false, "")
1687.                     }
1688.                 } else {
1689.                     (false, "")
1690.                 }
1691.             };
1692. 
1693.             // Emit space before trailing colons if present
1694.             if trailing_space {
1695.                 self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
1696.             }
1697. 
1698.             // Emit trailing colons if present
1699.             if !trailing_colons.is_empty() {
1700.                 self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), trailing_colons);
1701.             }
1702. 
1703.             // Emit newline
1704.             if !newline_str.is_empty() {
1705.                 self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);

<sub>⏱️ 11m 34s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

In `core.rs`, the dispatcher currently can’t “own” block detection because `parse_inner_content` already does its own heavy “is this a block element?” scan (definition continuation guard at ~1040–1051) using `try_parse_fence_open`, `try_parse_horizontal_rule`, `try_parse_atx_heading`, etc., and then later you call `block_registry.detect(...)` up to **3 times** per line (blank-before path, interrupt path, refdef path) while rebuilding `BlockContext` each time—so right now you have *both* duplicated detection *and* duplicated parsing work inside some block parsers.  

Given that structure, the best refactor is: make the registry the single “Pandoc-style `choice`” for *all* block starts (including the “interrupt” case) and have it return a **prepared match** (opaque payload) so `core.rs` can (1) call detect exactly once, (2) decide/perform `prepare_for_block_element` based on `match.can_interrupt/needs_blank_before`, and (3) then emit via `parse_with_match` without re-running `try_parse_*`.  

Trying to “make `can_parse` cheap” will fight your current reality (your block detectors already *need* real parsing to disambiguate, e.g. fenced code vs inline/executable, figures using inline image parse), while “prepared match” lets each complicated block cache whatever it needs without forcing `core.rs` to predict a universal return shape.

---

<sub>⏱️ 13m 25s</sub>

### 👤 User

Wait, what are you concretely suggesting now?

---

<sub>⏱️ 13m 34s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Concretely:

1) **Stop using `can_parse()` as a boolean probe.** Replace the trait with a single entrypoint that produces a *prepared* result:
```rust
trait BlockParser {
  fn try_match(&self, ctx: &BlockContext, lines: &[&str], pos: usize) -\> Option\<BlockMatch\>;
  fn name(&self) -\> &'static str;
}

struct BlockMatch {
  can_interrupt: bool,
  requires_blank_before: bool,
  // opaque, parser-specific cached data:
  data: Box\<dyn std::any::Any\>,
}
```
and each parser’s `try_match` does the expensive work once and stores what it learned in `data` (e.g. `FenceOpen + InfoString`, heading level, etc.).

2) Add a second method to emit using that cached data (so no re-parsing):
```rust
trait BlockParser {
  fn emit(&self, m: &BlockMatch, ctx: &BlockContext, b: &mut GreenNodeBuilder\<'static\>, lines: &[&str], pos: usize) -\> usize;
}
```
Inside `emit`, downcast `m.data` to your parser’s internal type.

3) In `core.rs`, **call registry.detect exactly once per line** (not in 3 branches), then:
- if `m.requires_blank_before` and `!has_blank_before`: ignore match
- else: do `prepare_for_block_element()` iff needed (or `emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed + close_paragraph_if_open` if `can_interrupt`)
- then call `registry.emit(match, ...)`

That’s the concrete change: “detect” returns a prepared, cached parse result, and `core.rs` becomes “one detect → maybe prepare → emit”, eliminating the current double work and the repeated `detect()` calls.

---

<sub>⏱️ 14m 24s</sub>

### 👤 User

Should I revert and restart or continue from the current state?

---

<sub>⏱️ 14m 32s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Continue from the current state; you already have the registry ordering and several migrated blocks, so reverting would just throw away useful structure.  
Do it incrementally: introduce the “prepared match” API alongside the current `can_parse/parse`, convert one migrated parser first (fenced code is the biggest win), then adjust `core.rs` to call detect once and to use the cached match for emit, and only then migrate the remaining blocks.  
Only consider a revert if you feel `core.rs` has become unmaintainable mid-transition—but right now it’s still a straightforward evolution path.

---

/home/jola/projects/panache/src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs (lines 1-220)

<details>
<summary>220 lines</summary>

```
1. //! Block parser dispatcher for organizing block-level parsing.
2. //!
3. //! This module provides a trait-based abstraction for block parsers,
4. //! making it easier to add new block types and reducing duplication in parse_inner_content.
5. //!
6. //! Design principles:
7. //! - Single-pass parsing preserved (no backtracking)
8. //! - Each block parser operates independently
9. //! - Inline parsing still integrated (called from within block parsing)
10. //! - Maintains exact CST structure and losslessness
11. 
12. use crate::config::Config;
13. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
14. 
15. use super::blocks::code_blocks::{
16.     CodeBlockType, InfoString, parse_fenced_code_block, try_parse_fence_open,
17. };
18. use super::blocks::figures::{parse_figure, try_parse_figure};
19. use super::blocks::headings::{
20.     emit_atx_heading, emit_setext_heading, try_parse_atx_heading, try_parse_setext_heading,
21. };
22. use super::blocks::horizontal_rules::{emit_horizontal_rule, try_parse_horizontal_rule};
23. use super::blocks::metadata::try_parse_yaml_block;
24. use super::blocks::reference_links::try_parse_reference_definition;
25. use super::utils::container_stack::{ContainerStack, byte_index_at_column};
26. use super::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
27. 
28. /// Information about list indentation context.
29. ///
30. /// Used by block parsers that need to handle indentation stripping
31. /// when parsing inside list items (e.g., fenced code blocks).
32. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
33. pub(crate) struct ListIndentInfo {
34.     /// Number of columns to strip for list content
35.     pub content_col: usize,
36. }
37. 
38. /// Context passed to block parsers for decision-making.
39. ///
40. /// Contains immutable references to parser state that block parsers need
41. /// to check conditions (e.g., blank line before, blockquote depth, etc.).
42. pub(crate) struct BlockContext<'a> {
43.     /// Current line content (after blockquote markers stripped if any)
44.     pub content: &'a str,
45. 
46.     /// Whether there was a blank line before this line
47.     pub has_blank_before: bool,
48. 
49.     /// Whether we're at document start (pos == 0)
50.     pub at_document_start: bool,
51. 
52.     /// Current blockquote depth
53.     pub blockquote_depth: usize,
54. 
55.     /// Parser configuration
56.     pub config: &'a Config,
57. 
58.     /// Container stack for checking context (lists, blockquotes, etc.)
59.     #[allow(dead_code)] // Will be used as we migrate more blocks
60.     pub containers: &'a ContainerStack,
61. 
62.     /// Base indentation from container context (footnotes, definitions)
63.     pub content_indent: usize,
64. 
65.     /// List indentation info if inside a list
66.     pub list_indent_info: Option<ListIndentInfo>,
67. 
68.     /// Next line content for lookahead (used by setext headings)
69.     pub next_line: Option<&'a str>,
70. }
71. 
72. /// Result of detecting whether a block can be parsed.
73. pub(crate) enum BlockDetectionResult {
74.     /// Can parse this block, requires blank line before
75.     Yes,
76. 
77.     /// Can parse this block and can interrupt paragraphs (no blank line needed)
78.     #[allow(dead_code)] // Will be used when we migrate fenced code blocks
79.     YesCanInterrupt,
80. 
81.     /// Cannot parse this content
82.     No,
83. }
84. 
85. /// Trait for block-level parsers.
86. ///
87. /// Each block type implements this trait with a two-phase approach:
88. /// 1. Detection: Can this block type parse this content? (lightweight, no emission)
89. /// 2. Parsing: Actually parse and emit the block to the builder (called after preparation)
90. ///
91. /// This separation allows the caller to:
92. /// - Prepare for block elements (close paragraphs, flush buffers) BEFORE emission
93. /// - Handle blocks that can interrupt paragraphs vs those that need blank lines
94. /// - Maintain correct CST node ordering
95. ///
96. /// Note: This is purely organizational - the trait doesn't introduce
97. /// backtracking or multiple passes. Each parser operates during the
98. /// single forward pass through the document.
99. pub(crate) trait BlockParser {
100.     /// Detect if this parser can handle the content (lightweight check, no emission).
101.     ///
102.     /// Returns:
103.     /// - `Yes`: Can parse, requires blank line before
104.     /// - `YesCanInterrupt`: Can parse and can interrupt paragraphs
105.     /// - `No`: Cannot parse this content
106.     ///
107.     /// This method should be fast and do minimal work (peek at first few characters).
108.     /// It should NOT emit anything to the builder.
109.     ///
110.     /// # Parameters
111.     /// - `ctx`: Context with content and parser state
112.     /// - `lines`: All lines in the document (for look-ahead if needed)
113.     /// - `line_pos`: Current line position
114.     fn can_parse(
115.         &self,
116.         ctx: &BlockContext,
117.         lines: &[&str],
118.         line_pos: usize,
119.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult;
120. 
121.     /// Parse and emit this block type to the builder.
122.     ///
123.     /// Called only after `can_parse` returns `Yes` or `YesCanInterrupt`, and after
124.     /// the caller has prepared (closed paragraphs, flushed buffers).
125.     ///
126.     /// # Arguments
127.     /// - `ctx`: Context about the current parsing state
128.     /// - `builder`: Builder to emit syntax nodes to
129.     /// - `lines`: Full document lines (for multi-line blocks)
130.     /// - `line_pos`: Current line position in the document
131.     ///
132.     /// # Returns
133.     /// Number of lines consumed by this block
134.     ///
135.     /// # Single-pass guarantee
136.     /// This method is called during the single forward pass. It should:
137.     /// - Read ahead in `lines` if needed (tables, code blocks, etc.)
138.     /// - Emit inline elements immediately via inline_emission
139.     /// - Not modify any state outside of builder emission
140.     fn parse(
141.         &self,
142.         ctx: &BlockContext,
143.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
144.         lines: &[&str],
145.         line_pos: usize,
146.     ) -> usize;
147. 
148.     /// Name of this block parser (for debugging/logging)
149.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str;
150. }
151. 
152. // ============================================================================
153. // Concrete Block Parser Implementations
154. // ============================================================================
155. 
156. /// Horizontal rule parser
157. pub(crate) struct HorizontalRuleParser;
158. 
159. impl BlockParser for HorizontalRuleParser {
160.     fn can_parse(
161.         &self,
162.         ctx: &BlockContext,
163.         _lines: &[&str],
164.         _line_pos: usize,
165.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
166.         // Must have blank line before
167.         if !ctx.has_blank_before {
168.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
169.         }
170. 
171.         // Check if this looks like a horizontal rule
172.         if try_parse_horizontal_rule(ctx.content).is_some() {
173.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
174.         } else {
175.             BlockDetectionResult::No
176.         }
177.     }
178. 
179.     fn parse(
180.         &self,
181.         ctx: &BlockContext,
182.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
183.         lines: &[&str],
184.         line_pos: usize,
185.     ) -> usize {
186.         // Use ctx.content (blockquote markers already stripped)
187.         // But preserve newline from original line
188.         let (_, newline_str) = strip_newline(lines[line_pos]);
189.         let content_with_newline = if !newline_str.is_empty() {
190.             format!("{}{}", ctx.content.trim_end(), newline_str)
191.         } else {
192.             ctx.content.to_string()
193.         };
194. 
195.         emit_horizontal_rule(builder, &content_with_newline);
196.         1 // Consumed 1 line
197.     }
198. 
199.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
200.         "horizontal_rule"
201.     }
202. }
203. 
204. /// ATX heading parser (# Heading)
205. pub(crate) struct AtxHeadingParser;
206. 
207. impl BlockParser for AtxHeadingParser {
208.     fn can_parse(
209.         &self,
210.         ctx: &BlockContext,
211.         _lines: &[&str],
212.         _line_pos: usize,
213.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
214.         // Must have blank line before
215.         if !ctx.has_blank_before {
216.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
217.         }
218. 
219.         // Check if this looks like an ATX heading
220.         if try_parse_atx_heading(ctx.content).is_some() {
```

1000.     /// Parse content inside blockquotes (or at top level).
1001.     ///
1002.     /// `content` - The content to parse (may have indent/markers stripped)
1003.     /// `line_to_append` - Optional line to use when appending to paragraphs.
1004.     ///                    If None, uses self.lines[self.pos]
1005.     fn parse_inner_content(&mut self, content: &str, line_to_append: Option<&str>) -> bool {
1006.         log::debug!(
1007.             "parse_inner_content [{}]: depth={}, last={:?}, content={:?}",
1008.             self.pos,
1009.             self.containers.depth(),
1010.             self.containers.last(),
1011.             content.trim_end()
1012.         );
1013.         // Calculate how much indentation should be stripped for content containers
1014.         // (definitions, footnotes) FIRST, so we can check for block markers correctly
1015.         let content_indent = self.content_container_indent_to_strip();
1016.         let (stripped_content, indent_to_emit) = if content_indent > 0 {
1017.             let (indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(content);
1018.             if indent_cols >= content_indent {
1019.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(content, content_indent);
1020.                 (&content[idx..], Some(&content[..idx]))
1021.             } else {
1022.                 // Line has less indent than required - preserve leading whitespace
1023.                 let trimmed_start = content.trim_start();
1024.                 let ws_len = content.len() - trimmed_start.len();
1025.                 if ws_len > 0 {
1026.                     (trimmed_start, Some(&content[..ws_len]))
1027.                 } else {
1028.                     (content, None)
1029.                 }
1030.             }
1031.         } else {
1032.             (content, None)
1033.         };
1034. 
1035.         // Check if we're in a Definition container (with or without an open PLAIN)
1036.         // Continuation lines should be added to PLAIN, not treated as new blocks
1037.         // BUT: Don't treat lines with block element markers as continuations
1038.         if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Definition { .. })) {
1039.             // Check if this line starts with any block element marker
1040.             // Use stripped_content so we check AFTER removing footnote/definition indent
1041.             let is_block_element = try_parse_definition_marker(stripped_content).is_some()
1042.                 || try_parse_list_marker(stripped_content, self.config).is_some()
1043.                 || count_blockquote_markers(stripped_content).0 > 0
1044.                 || try_parse_fence_open(stripped_content).is_some()
1045.                 || try_parse_div_fence_open(stripped_content).is_some()
1046.                 || try_parse_horizontal_rule(stripped_content).is_some()
1047.                 || try_parse_atx_heading(stripped_content).is_some()
1048.                 || (self.config.extensions.raw_html
1049.                     && try_parse_html_block_start(stripped_content).is_some())
1050.                 || (self.config.extensions.raw_tex
1051.                     && try_parse_latex_env_begin(stripped_content).is_some());
1052. 
1053.             if is_block_element {
1054.                 // Close any open Plain block before processing the block element
1055.                 // Buffered PLAIN content will be emitted by emit_buffered_plain_if_needed()
1056.                 // Fall through to parse the block element
1057.             } else {
1058.                 // This is a continuation line - add to PLAIN (start one if needed)
1059.                 let full_line = self.lines[self.pos];
1060.                 let (text_without_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(full_line);
1061. 
1062.                 // Buffer the line for later inline parsing
1063.                 if let Some(Container::Definition {
1064.                     plain_open,
1065.                     plain_buffer,
1066.                     ..
1067.                 }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
1068.                 {
1069.                     // Include the newline in the buffered text for losslessness
1070.                     let line_with_newline = if !newline_str.is_empty() {
1071.                         format!("{}{}", text_without_newline, newline_str)
1072.                     } else {
1073.                         text_without_newline.to_string()
1074.                     };
1075.                     plain_buffer.push_line(line_with_newline);
1076.                     *plain_open = true; // Mark that we now have an open PLAIN
1077.                 }
1078. 
1079.                 self.pos += 1;
1080.                 return true;
1081.             }
1082.         }
1083. 
1084.         // Store the stripped content for later use

1087.         // Check for heading (needs blank line before, or at start of container)

1498.         // Check for footnote definition: [^id]: content

1506. 
1507.             // Close previous footnote if one is open
1508.             while matches!(
1509.                 self.containers.last(),
1510.                 Some(Container::FootnoteDefinition { .. })
1511.             ) {
1512.                 self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1513.             }
1514. 
1515.             // Start the footnote definition container
1516.             self.builder
1517.                 .start_node(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION.into());
1518. 
1519.             // Emit the marker
1520.             let marker_text = &content[..content_start];
1521.             self.builder
1522.                 .token(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_REFERENCE.into(), marker_text);
1523. 
1524.             // Calculate content column (minimum 4 spaces for continuation)
1525.             // The first line can start right after the marker, but subsequent lines
1526.             // need at least 4 spaces of indentation
1527.             let content_col = 4;
1528.             self.containers
1529.                 .push(Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col });
1530. 
1531.             // Parse the first line content (if any)
1532.             let first_line_content = &content[content_start..];
1533.             if !first_line_content.trim().is_empty() {
1534.                 paragraphs::start_paragraph_if_needed(&mut self.containers, &mut self.builder);
1535.                 paragraphs::append_paragraph_line(
1536.                     &mut self.containers,

1546. 
1547.         // Check for reference definition: [label]: url "title"

1549.         // Try dispatcher first
1550. 
1551.         let next_line = if self.pos + 1 < self.lines.len() {
1552.             Some(self.lines[self.pos + 1])
1553.         } else {
1554.             None
1555.         };

1557.         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
1558.             content,

427.     /// Legacy function - kept for backward compatibility in mixed-form parsing

434. 
435. /// Information about a detected code fence opening.
436. pub(crate) struct FenceInfo {
437.     pub fence_char: char,
438.     pub fence_count: usize,
439.     pub info_string: String,
440. }
441. 
442. /// Try to detect a fenced code block opening from content.
443. /// Returns fence info if this is a valid opening fence.
444. pub(crate) fn try_parse_fence_open(content: &str) -> Option<FenceInfo> {
445.     let trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(content);
446. 
447.     // Check for fence opening (``` or ~~~)
448.     let (fence_char, fence_count) = if trimmed.starts_with('`') {
449.         let count = trimmed.chars().take_while(|&c| c == '`').count();
450.         ('`', count)
451.     } else if trimmed.starts_with('~') {
452.         let count = trimmed.chars().take_while(|&c| c == '~').count();
453.         ('~', count)
454.     } else {
455.         return None;
456.     };
457. 
458.     if fence_count < 3 {
459.         return None;
460.     }
461. 
462.     let info_string_raw = &trimmed[fence_count..];
463.     // Strip trailing newline (LF or CRLF) and at most one leading space
464.     let (info_string_trimmed, _) = strip_newline(info_string_raw);
465.     let info_string = if let Some(stripped) = info_string_trimmed.strip_prefix(' ') {
466.         stripped.to_string()
467.     } else {
468.         info_string_trimmed.to_string()
469.     };
470. 
471.     Some(FenceInfo {
472.         fence_char,
473.         fence_count,
474.         info_string,
475.     })
476. }
477. 
478. /// Check if a line is a valid closing fence for the given fence info.
479. pub(crate) fn is_closing_fence(content: &str, fence: &FenceInfo) -> bool {
480.     let trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(content);
481. 
482.     if !trimmed.starts_with(fence.fence_char) {
483.         return false;
484.     }
485. 
486.     let closing_count = trimmed
487.         .chars()
488.         .take_while(|&c| c == fence.fence_char)
489.         .count();
490. 
491.     if closing_count < fence.fence_count {
492.         return false;
493.     }
494. 
495.     // Rest of line must be empty
496.     trimmed[closing_count..].trim().is_empty()
497. }
498. 
499. /// Emit chunk options as structured CST nodes while preserving all bytes.
500. /// This parses {r, echo=TRUE, fig.cap="text"} into CHUNK_OPTIONS with individual CHUNK_OPTION nodes.
501. fn emit_chunk_options(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, content: &str) {
502.     if content.trim().is_empty() {
503.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), content);
504.         return;
505.     }
506. 
507.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::CHUNK_OPTIONS.into());
508. 
509.     let mut pos = 0;
510.     let bytes = content.as_bytes();
511. 
512.     while pos < bytes.len() {
513.         // Emit leading whitespace/commas as TEXT
514.         let ws_start = pos;
515.         while pos < bytes.len() {
516.             let ch = bytes[pos] as char;
517.             if ch != ' ' && ch != '\t' && ch != ',' {
518.                 break;
519.             }
520.             pos += 1;
````

761. /// Parse a fenced code block, consuming lines from the parser.
762. /// Returns the new position after the code block.
763. /// Parse a fenced code block, consuming lines from the parser.
764. /// Returns the new position after the code block.
765. /// base_indent accounts for container indentation (e.g., footnotes) that should be stripped.
766. pub(crate) fn parse_fenced_code_block(
767.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
768.     lines: &[&str],
769.     start_pos: usize,
770.     fence: FenceInfo,
771.     bq_depth: usize,
772.     base_indent: usize,
773. ) -> usize {
774.     // Start code block
775.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::CODE_BLOCK.into());
776. 
777.     // Opening fence
778.     let first_line = lines[start_pos];
779.     let (_, first_inner) = count_blockquote_markers(first_line);
780. 
781.     // For lossless parsing: emit the base indent before stripping it
782.     let first_stripped = if base_indent > 0 && first_inner.len() >= base_indent {
783.         let indent_str = &first_inner[..base_indent];
784.         if !indent_str.is_empty() {
785.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), indent_str);
786.         }
787.         &first_inner[base_indent..]
788.     } else {
789.         first_inner
790.     };
791.     let first_trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(first_stripped);
792. 
793.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::CODE_FENCE_OPEN.into());
794.     builder.token(
795.         SyntaxKind::CODE_FENCE_MARKER.into(),
796.         &first_trimmed[..fence.fence_count],
797.     );
798. 
799.     // Emit any space between fence and info string (for losslessness)
800.     let after_fence = &first_trimmed[fence.fence_count..];
801.     if let Some(_space_stripped) = after_fence.strip_prefix(' ') {
802.         // There was a space - emit it as WHITESPACE
803.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
804.         // Parse and emit the info string as a structured node
805.         if !fence.info_string.is_empty() {
806.             emit_code_info_node(builder, &fence.info_string);
807.         }
808.     } else if !fence.info_string.is_empty() {
809.         // No space - parse and emit info_string as a structured node
810.         emit_code_info_node(builder, &fence.info_string);
811.     }
812. 
813.     // Extract and emit the actual newline from the opening fence line
814.     let (_, newline_str) = strip_newline(first_trimmed);
815.     if !newline_str.is_empty() {
816.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
817.     }
818.     builder.finish_node(); // CodeFenceOpen
819. 
820.     let mut current_pos = start_pos + 1;
821.     let mut content_lines: Vec<&str> = Vec::new(); // Store original lines for lossless parsing
822.     let mut found_closing = false;
823. 
824.     while current_pos < lines.len() {
825.         let line = lines[current_pos];
826. 
827.         // Strip blockquote markers to get inner content
828.         let (line_bq_depth, inner) = count_blockquote_markers(line);
829. 
830.         // If blockquote depth decreases, code block ends (we've left the blockquote)
831.         if line_bq_depth < bq_depth {
832.             break;
833.         }
834. 
835.         // Strip base indent (footnote context) from content lines for fence detection
```

72. /// Result of detecting whether a block can be parsed.
73. pub(crate) enum BlockDetectionResult {
74.     /// Can parse this block, requires blank line before
75.     Yes,
76. 
77.     /// Can parse this block and can interrupt paragraphs (no blank line needed)
78.     #[allow(dead_code)] // Will be used when we migrate fenced code blocks
79.     YesCanInterrupt,
80. 
81.     /// Cannot parse this content
82.     No,
83. }
84. 
85. /// Trait for block-level parsers.
86. ///
87. /// Each block type implements this trait with a two-phase approach:
88. /// 1. Detection: Can this block type parse this content? (lightweight, no emission)
89. /// 2. Parsing: Actually parse and emit the block to the builder (called after preparation)
90. ///
91. /// This separation allows the caller to:
92. /// - Prepare for block elements (close paragraphs, flush buffers) BEFORE emission
93. /// - Handle blocks that can interrupt paragraphs vs those that need blank lines
94. /// - Maintain correct CST node ordering
95. ///
96. /// Note: This is purely organizational - the trait doesn't introduce
97. /// backtracking or multiple passes. Each parser operates during the
98. /// single forward pass through the document.
99. pub(crate) trait BlockParser {
100.     /// Detect if this parser can handle the content (lightweight check, no emission).
101.     ///
102.     /// Returns:
103.     /// - `Yes`: Can parse, requires blank line before
104.     /// - `YesCanInterrupt`: Can parse and can interrupt paragraphs
105.     /// - `No`: Cannot parse this content
106.     ///
107.     /// This method should be fast and do minimal work (peek at first few characters).
108.     /// It should NOT emit anything to the builder.
109.     ///
110.     /// # Parameters
111.     /// - `ctx`: Context with content and parser state
112.     /// - `lines`: All lines in the document (for look-ahead if needed)
113.     /// - `line_pos`: Current line position
114.     fn can_parse(
115.         &self,
116.         ctx: &BlockContext,
117.         lines: &[&str],
118.         line_pos: usize,
119.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult;
120. 
121.     /// Parse and emit this block type to the builder.
122.     ///
123.     /// Called only after `can_parse` returns `Yes` or `YesCanInterrupt`, and after
124.     /// the caller has prepared (closed paragraphs, flushed buffers).
125.     ///
126.     /// # Arguments
127.     /// - `ctx`: Context about the current parsing state
128.     /// - `builder`: Builder to emit syntax nodes to
129.     /// - `lines`: Full document lines (for multi-line blocks)
130.     /// - `line_pos`: Current line position in the document
131.     ///
132.     /// # Returns
133.     /// Number of lines consumed by this block
134.     ///
135.     /// # Single-pass guarantee
136.     /// This method is called during the single forward pass. It should:
137.     /// - Read ahead in `lines` if needed (tables, code blocks, etc.)
138.     /// - Emit inline elements immediately via inline_emission
139.     /// - Not modify any state outside of builder emission
140.     fn parse(
141.         &self,
142.         ctx: &BlockContext,
143.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
144.         lines: &[&str],
145.         line_pos: usize,
146.     ) -> usize;
147. 
148.     /// Name of this block parser (for debugging/logging)
149.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str;
150. }
151. 
152. // ============================================================================
153. // Concrete Block Parser Implementations
154. // ============================================================================
155. 
156. /// Horizontal rule parser
157. pub(crate) struct HorizontalRuleParser;
158. 
159. impl BlockParser for HorizontalRuleParser {
160.     fn can_parse(
161.         &self,
162.         ctx: &BlockContext,
163.         _lines: &[&str],
164.         _line_pos: usize,
165.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
166.         // Must have blank line before
167.         if !ctx.has_blank_before {
168.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
169.         }
170.
