204. /// ATX heading parser (# Heading)
205. pub(crate) struct AtxHeadingParser;
206. 
207. impl BlockParser for AtxHeadingParser {
208.     fn can_parse(
209.         &self,
210.         ctx: &BlockContext,
211.         _lines: &[&str],
212.         _line_pos: usize,
213.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
214.         // Must have blank line before
215.         if !ctx.has_blank_before {
216.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
217.         }
218. 
219.         // Check if this looks like an ATX heading
220.         if try_parse_atx_heading(ctx.content).is_some() {
221.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
222.         } else {
223.             BlockDetectionResult::No
224.         }
225.     }
226. 
227.     fn parse(
228.         &self,
229.         ctx: &BlockContext,
230.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
231.         lines: &[&str],
232.         line_pos: usize,
233.     ) -> usize {
234.         let line = lines[line_pos];
235.         let heading_level = try_parse_atx_heading(ctx.content).unwrap();
236.         emit_atx_heading(builder, line, heading_level, ctx.config);
237.         1 // Consumed 1 line
238.     }
239. 
240.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
241.         "atx_heading"
242.     }
243. }
244. 
245. /// YAML metadata block parser (--- ... ---/...)
246. pub(crate) struct YamlMetadataParser;
247. 
248. impl BlockParser for YamlMetadataParser {
249.     fn can_parse(
250.         &self,
251.         ctx: &BlockContext,
252.         lines: &[&str],
253.         line_pos: usize,
254.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
255.         // Must be at top level (not inside blockquotes)
256.         if ctx.blockquote_depth > 0 {
257.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
258.         }
259. 
260.         // Must start with ---
261.         if ctx.content.trim() != "---" {
262.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
263.         }
264. 
265.         // YAML needs blank line before OR be at document start
266.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
267.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
268.         }
269. 
270.         // Look ahead: next line must NOT be blank (to distinguish from horizontal rule)
```

</details>

435. /// Fenced code block parser (``` or ~~~)
436. pub(crate) struct FencedCodeBlockParser;
437. 
438. impl BlockParser for FencedCodeBlockParser {
439.     fn can_parse(
440.         &self,
441.         ctx: &BlockContext,
442.         _lines: &[&str],
443.         _line_pos: usize,
444.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
445.         // Calculate content to check - may need to strip list indentation
446.         let content_to_check = if let Some(list_info) = ctx.list_indent_info {
447.             // Strip list indentation before checking for fence
448.             if list_info.content_col > 0 && !ctx.content.is_empty() {
449.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(ctx.content, list_info.content_col);
450.                 &ctx.content[idx..]
451.             } else {
452.                 ctx.content
453.             }
454.         } else {
455.             ctx.content
456.         };
457. 
458.         // Try to detect fence opening
459.         let fence = match try_parse_fence_open(content_to_check) {
460.             Some(f) => f,
461.             None => return BlockDetectionResult::No,
462.         };
463. 
464.         // Parse info string to determine block type
465.         let info = InfoString::parse(&fence.info_string);
466. 
467.         // Check if this is an executable chunk in Pandoc-like flavor
468.         let is_executable = matches!(info.block_type, CodeBlockType::Executable { .. });
469.         let is_pandoc_like = matches!(
470.             ctx.config.flavor,
471.             crate::config::Flavor::Pandoc
472.                 | crate::config::Flavor::CommonMark
473.                 | crate::config::Flavor::Gfm
474.         );
475. 
476.         // In Pandoc-like flavors, executable chunks should NEVER be code blocks
477.         // They should be parsed as inline code (``` delimiters with executable syntax)
478.         if is_executable && is_pandoc_like {
479.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
480.         }
481. 
482.         // Fenced code blocks can interrupt paragraphs UNLESS:
483.         // It has no info string (might be inline code delimiter)
484.         let has_info = !fence.info_string.trim().is_empty();
485.         let can_interrupt = has_info;
486. 
487.         // Return based on whether we can parse this block
488.         if can_interrupt {
489.             // Can interrupt paragraphs - return YesCanInterrupt
490.             BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
491.         } else if ctx.has_blank_before {
492.             // Has blank line before, can parse normally
493.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
494.         } else {
495.             // Cannot parse (would need blank line but don't have one)
496.             BlockDetectionResult::No
497.         }
498.     }
499. 
500.     fn parse(
501.         &self,
502.         ctx: &BlockContext,
503.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
504.         lines: &[&str],
505.         line_pos: usize,
506.     ) -> usize {
507.         // Calculate content to check (with list indent stripped)
508.         let list_indent_stripped = if let Some(list_info) = ctx.list_indent_info {
509.             list_info.content_col
510.         } else {
511.             0
512.         };
513. 
514.         let content_to_check = if list_indent_stripped > 0 && !ctx.content.is_empty() {
515.             let idx = byte_index_at_column(ctx.content, list_indent_stripped);
516.             &ctx.content[idx..]
517.         } else {
518.             ctx.content
519.         };
520. 
521.         // Get fence info (we know it exists from can_parse)
522.         let fence = try_parse_fence_open(content_to_check).expect("Fence should exist");
523. 
524.         // Calculate total indent: base content indent + list indent
525.         let total_indent = ctx.content_indent + list_indent_stripped;
526. 
527.         // Parse the fenced code block
528.         let new_pos = parse_fenced_code_block(
529.             builder,
530.             lines,
531.             line_pos,
532.             fence,
533.             ctx.blockquote_depth,
534.             total_indent,
535.         );
536. 
537.         // Return lines consumed
538.         new_pos - line_pos
539.     }
540. 
541.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
542.         "fenced_code_block"
543.     }
544. }
545.

558.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
559.         // Setext headings require blank line before (unless at document start)
560.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {

```
610. // ============================================================================
611. // Block Parser Registry
612. // ============================================================================
613. 
614. /// Registry of block parsers, ordered by priority.
615. ///
616. /// This dispatcher tries each parser in order until one succeeds.
617. /// The ordering follows Pandoc's approach - explicit list order rather
618. /// than numeric priorities.
619. pub(crate) struct BlockParserRegistry {
620.     parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>,
621. }
622. 
623. impl BlockParserRegistry {
624.     /// Create a new registry with all block parsers.
625.     ///
626.     /// Order matters! Parsers are tried in the order listed here.
627.     /// This follows Pandoc's design where ordering is explicit and documented.
628.     ///
629.     /// **Pandoc reference order** (from pandoc/src/Text/Pandoc/Readers/Markdown.hs:487-515):
630.     /// 1. blanklines (handled separately in our parser)
631.     /// 2. codeBlockFenced
632.     /// 3. yamlMetaBlock' ← YAML metadata comes early!
633.     /// 4. bulletList
634.     /// 5. divHtml
635.     /// 6. divFenced
636.     /// 7. header ← ATX headings
637.     /// 8. lhsCodeBlock
638.     /// 9. htmlBlock
639.     /// 10. table
640.     /// 11. codeBlockIndented
641.     /// 12. rawTeXBlock (LaTeX)
642.     /// 13. lineBlock
643.     /// 14. blockQuote
644.     /// 15. hrule ← Horizontal rules come AFTER headers!
645.     /// 16. orderedList
646.     /// 17. definitionList
647.     /// 18. noteBlock (footnotes)
648.     /// 19. referenceKey ← Reference definitions
649.     /// 20. abbrevKey
650.     /// 21. para
651.     /// 22. plain
652.     pub fn new() -> Self {
653.         let parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>> = vec![
654.             // Match Pandoc's ordering to ensure correct precedence:
655.             // (2) Fenced code blocks - can interrupt paragraphs!
656.             Box::new(FencedCodeBlockParser),
657.             // (3) YAML metadata - before headers and hrules!
658.             Box::new(YamlMetadataParser),
659.             // (7) Setext headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
660.             // Must come before ATX to properly handle `---` disambiguation
661.             Box::new(SetextHeadingParser),
662.             // (7) ATX headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
663.             Box::new(AtxHeadingParser),
664.             // (15) Horizontal rules - AFTER headings per Pandoc
665.             Box::new(HorizontalRuleParser),
666.             // Figures (standalone images) - Pandoc doesn't have these
667.             Box::new(FigureParser),
668.             // (19) Reference definitions
669.             Box::new(ReferenceDefinitionParser),
670.             // TODO: Migrate remaining blocks in Pandoc order:
671.             // - (4-6) Lists and divs (bulletList, divHtml, divFenced)
672.             // - (9) HTML blocks
673.             // - (10) Tables (grid, multiline, pipe, simple)
674.             // - (11) Indented code blocks (AFTER fenced!)
675.             // - (12) LaTeX blocks (rawTeXBlock)
676.             // - (13) Line blocks
677.             // - (16) Ordered lists
678.             // - (17) Definition lists
679.             // - (18) Footnote definitions (noteBlock)
680.         ];
681. 
682.         Self { parsers }
683.     }
684. 
685.     /// Try to parse a block using the registered parsers.
686.     ///
687.     /// This method implements the two-phase parsing:
688.     /// 1. Detection: Check if any parser can handle this content
689.     /// 2. Caller prepares (closes paragraphs, flushes buffers)
690.     /// 3. Parser emits the block
691.     ///
692.     /// Returns (parser_index, detection_result) if a parser can handle this,
693.     /// or None if no parser matched.
694.     pub fn detect(
695.         &self,
696.         ctx: &BlockContext,
697.         lines: &[&str],
698.         line_pos: usize,
699.     ) -> Option<(usize, BlockDetectionResult)> {
700.         for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
701.             let result = parser.can_parse(ctx, lines, line_pos);
702.             match result {
703.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes | BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
704.                     log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
705.                     return Some((i, result));
706.                 }
707.                 BlockDetectionResult::No => {
708.                     // Try next parser
709.                     continue;
710.                 }
711.             }
712.         }
713.         None
714.     }
715. 
716.     /// Parse a block using the specified parser (by index from detect()).
717.     ///
718.     /// Should only be called after detect() returns Some and after
719.     /// caller has prepared for the block element.
720.     pub fn parse(
721.         &self,
722.         parser_index: usize,
723.         ctx: &BlockContext,
724.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
725.         lines: &[&str],
726.         line_pos: usize,
727.     ) -> usize {
728.         let parser = &self.parsers[parser_index];
729.         log::debug!("Block parsed by: {}", parser.name());
730.         parser.parse(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos)
731.     }
732. }
```

</details>

1427.             None
1428.         };
1429. 
1430.         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
1431.             content,
1432.             has_blank_before,
1433.             at_document_start,
1434.             blockquote_depth: self.current_blockquote_depth(),
1435.             config: self.config,
1436.             containers: &self.containers,
1437.             content_indent,
1438.             list_indent_info,
1439.             next_line,
1440.         };
1441. 
1442.         if let Some(block_match) = self
1443.             .block_registry
1444.             .detect_prepared(&block_ctx, &self.lines, self.pos)
1445.         {
1446.             let detection = block_match.detection;
1447.             // Check if this is a block that can interrupt paragraphs
1448.             if matches!(detection, BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt) {
1449.                 // Block can interrupt paragraphs
1450.                 // Emit list item buffer if needed
1451.                 self.emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed();
1452. 
1453.                 // Close paragraph if one is open
1454.                 if self.is_paragraph_open() {
1455.                     self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1456.                 }
1457. 
1458.                 // Recreate context for parsing
1459.                 let list_indent_info = if lists::in_list(&self.containers) {
1460.                     let content_col = paragraphs::current_content_col(&self.containers);
1461.                     if content_col > 0 {
1462.                         Some(super::block_dispatcher::ListIndentInfo { content_col })
1463.                     } else {
1464.                         None
1465.                     }
1466.                 } else {
1467.                     None
1468.                 };
1469. 
1470.                 let next_line = if self.pos + 1 < self.lines.len() {
1471.                     Some(self.lines[self.pos + 1])
1472.                 } else {
1473.                     None
1474.                 };
1475. 
1476.                 let block_ctx = BlockContext {
1477.                     content,
1478.                     has_blank_before,
1479.                     at_document_start,
1480.                     blockquote_depth: self.current_blockquote_depth(),
1481.                     config: self.config,
1482.                     containers: &self.containers,
1483.                     content_indent,
1484.                     list_indent_info,
1485.                     next_line,
1486.                 };
1487. 
1488.                 let lines_consumed = self.block_registry.parse_prepared(
1489.                     &block_match,
1490.                     &block_ctx,
1491.                     &mut self.builder,
1492.                     &self.lines,
1493.                     self.pos,
1494.                 );
1495.                 self.pos += lines_consumed;
1496.                 return true;
1497.             }
1498.         }
1499. 
1500.         // Check for footnote definition: [^id]: content
1501.         // Similar to list items - marker followed by content that can span multiple lines
1502.         // Must check BEFORE reference definitions since both start with [
1503.         if let Some((id, content_start)) = try_parse_footnote_marker(content) {
1504.             log::debug!("Parsed footnote definition at line {}: [^{}]", self.pos, id);
1505. 
1506.             // Close paragraph if one is open
1507.             self.close_paragraph_if_open();
1508. 
1509.             // Close previous footnote if one is open
1510.             while matches!(
1511.                 self.containers.last(),
1512.                 Some(Container::FootnoteDefinition { .. })
1513.             ) {
1514.                 self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1515.             }
1516. 
1517.             // Start the footnote definition container
1518.             self.builder
1519.                 .start_node(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION.into());
1520. 
1521.             // Emit the marker
1522.             let marker_text = &content[..content_start];
1523.             self.builder
1524.                 .token(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_REFERENCE.into(), marker_text);
1525. 
1526.             // Calculate content column (minimum 4 spaces for continuation)
1527.             // The first line can start right after the marker, but subsequent lines
1528.             // need at least 4 spaces of indentation
1529.             let content_col = 4;
1530.             self.containers
1531.                 .push(Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col });
1532. 
1533.             // Parse the first line content (if any)
1534.             let first_line_content = &content[content_start..];
1535.             if !first_line_content.trim().is_empty() {
1536.                 paragraphs::start_paragraph_if_needed(&mut self.containers, &mut self.builder);
1537.                 paragraphs::append_paragraph_line(
1538.                     &mut self.containers,
1539.                     &mut self.builder,
1540.                     first_line_content,
1541.                     self.config,
1542.                 );
1543.             }
1544. 
1545.             self.pos += 1;
1546.             return true;
1547.         }
1548. 
1549.         // Check for reference definition: [label]: url "title"
1550.         // These can appear anywhere in the document (no blank line needed)
1551.         // Try dispatcher first
1552. 
1553.         let next_line = if self.pos + 1 < self.lines.len() {
1554.             Some(self.lines[self.pos + 1])
1555.         } else {
1556.             None
1557.         };
1558. 
1559.         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
1560.             content,
1561.             has_blank_before,
1562.             at_document_start,
1563.             blockquote_depth: self.current_blockquote_depth(),
1564.             config: self.config,
1565.             containers: &self.containers,
1566.             content_indent,
1567.             list_indent_info: None, // Not needed for reference definitions
1568.             next_line,
1569.         };
1570. 
1571.         if let Some(block_match) = self
1572.             .block_registry
1573.             .detect_prepared(&block_ctx, &self.lines, self.pos)
1574.         {
1575.             // Reference definitions don't need preparation
1576.             let lines_consumed = self.block_registry.parse_prepared(
1577.                 &block_match,
1578.                 &block_ctx,
1579.                 &mut self.builder,
1580.                 &self.lines,
1581.                 self.pos,
1582.             );
1583.             self.pos += lines_consumed;
1584.             return true;
1585.         }
1586. 
1587.         // Check for indented code block
1588.         // Inside a footnote, content needs 4 spaces for code (8 total in raw line)
1589.         // BUT: Don't treat as code if it's a list marker (list takes precedence)
1590.         if has_blank_before_strict
1591.             && is_indented_code_line(content)
1592.             && try_parse_list_marker(content, self.config).is_none()
1593.         {
1594.             // Prepare for indented code block
1595.             self.prepare_for_block_element();
1596. 
1597.             let bq_depth = self.current_blockquote_depth();
1598.             log::debug!("Parsed indented code block at line {}", self.pos);
1599.             let new_pos = parse_indented_code_block(
1600.                 &mut self.builder,
```

</details>

+    /// Like `detect()`, but allows parsers to return cached payload for emission.
+    pub fn detect_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        lines: &[&str],

+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> usize {
+        let parser = &self.parsers[block_match.parser_index];
+        log::debug!("Block parsed by: {}", parser.name());

+            ctx,
+            builder,
+            lines,
+            line_pos,
+            block_match.payload.as_deref(),
+        )

<sub>⏱️ 23m 2s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Implemented the first step of the “prepared match” refactor (low-churn path):

- Added `PreparedBlockMatch` + `detect_prepared()` + `parse_prepared()` to `BlockParserRegistry`.
- Extended `BlockParser` with default `detect_prepared()` / `parse_prepared()` hooks (backwards compatible).
- Converted **FencedCodeBlockParser** to cache `FenceInfo` (and avoid re-parsing the fence in `parse_prepared`), with a safe fallback for legacy `parse()` calls.
- Updated `core.rs` callsites that used the dispatcher to use `detect_prepared`/`parse_prepared`.
- All tests pass (`cargo test -q`).

Next step is converting ATX heading (cache level), then reference definitions (cache parsed tuple), then setext (cache level/underline and remove Vec alloc), and finally removing the duplicated `is_block_element` probe by delegating to the registry.

<sub>⏱️ 46m 39s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

/home/jola/.local/state/.copilot/session-state/8111c140-3b00-44e8-81bb-2bc5c0b6ac76/plan.md

<details>
<summary>65 lines</summary>

```
1. # Plan: Block dispatcher “prepared match” refactor
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Current `BlockParser::can_parse()` often does non-trivial work (e.g. fenced code parses fence + `InfoString::parse`, setext allocates, figure uses inline image parsing). This work is frequently repeated because:
5. - several parsers call `try_parse_*` again inside `parse()`
6. - `core.rs` calls `block_registry.detect(...)` multiple times per line in different branches
7. - `core.rs` separately re-implements “is this a block element marker?” checks (e.g. Definition continuation guard), further duplicating detection.
8. 
9. ## Goal
10. Keep the good part of the refactor (Pandoc-like ordered registry + ability to “prepare/flush buffers before emitting”), while eliminating duplicate detection/parsing and reducing `core.rs` complexity.
11. 
12. ## Approach (high level)
13. Introduce a **prepared match** concept: detection returns a match object that caches any expensive parsing results, and emission consumes that cached data (no re-parsing). Then rework `core.rs` so each line does **one** detection pass, decides whether the match is allowed (blank-line rules / interrupt rules), performs preparation, and emits.
14. 
15. ## Key design decision (to confirm)
16. Two viable representations:
17. 1) **Trait objects + opaque payload** (recommended for minimal churn): registry remains `Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>`. Detection returns a `BlockMatch` containing a parser index + interrupt/blank requirements + `Box<dyn Any>` payload; emission downcasts payload.
18. 2) **Enum registry + typed payload** (slightly faster/cleaner types, but larger refactor): registry becomes `Vec<BlockParserKind>` and match payload becomes an enum.
19. 
20. ## Workplan
21. 
22. ### A. Baseline & safety
23. - [ ] Run baseline tests/benchmarks to capture current behavior (`cargo test` at minimum).
24. - [ ] Identify a small set of “high-signal” golden cases to watch while refactoring (fenced code, setext, yaml/hrule ambiguity, refdefs).
25. 
26. ### B. Introduce prepared-match API (non-invasive)
27. - [ ] Add new types in `src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs`:
28.   - `BlockMatch` (contains: `kind`/parser index, `can_interrupt`, `requires_blank_before`, and cached payload)
29.   - `DetectOutcome` if needed (e.g. `No | Match(BlockMatch)`)
30. - [ ] Extend `BlockParserRegistry` with new entrypoints (keep old API temporarily):
31.   - `detect_prepared(...) -> Option<BlockMatch>`
32.   - `emit_prepared(match, ...) -> usize`
33. 
34. ### C. Convert migrated parsers (start with biggest perf win)
35. - [ ] Fenced code: `try_match` parses fence + `InfoString` once; `emit` reuses cached fence/info.
36. - [ ] ATX heading: `try_match` returns cached level; `emit` uses it (no second `try_parse_atx_heading`).
37. - [ ] YAML: `try_match` validates opening and maybe caches “is yaml not hrule”; `emit` calls existing `try_parse_yaml_block` or reuses cached facts.
38. - [ ] Figure: `try_match` caches validation span from inline image parse so it isn’t repeated.
39. - [ ] Setext: remove allocation in detection (no `Vec`); cache `(level, underline_char)`.
40. - [ ] Reference definition: cache parse tuple from `try_parse_reference_definition`.
41. 
42. ### D. Refactor `core.rs` to “single detect per line”
43. - [ ] Create one `BlockContext` per line and reuse it (avoid repeated rebuilds).
44. - [ ] Replace the multiple `block_registry.detect(...)` calls (~3 sites) with one flow:
45.   - detect once
46.   - check `requires_blank_before` vs computed blank condition
47.   - if `can_interrupt`: flush list item buffer + close paragraph
48.   - else: `prepare_for_block_element()`
49.   - emit using prepared match
50. - [ ] Rework the Definition continuation guard (`is_block_element` at ~1041): instead of calling many `try_parse_*` helpers, use the registry’s prepared detection (or a dedicated “block start probe” function that calls into the registry) so you don’t duplicate marker logic.
51. 
52. ### E. Consolidate ordering/precedence in the registry
53. - [ ] Move remaining “block start” checks from `core.rs` into the registry progressively (tables/divs/html/latex/etc.), keeping Pandoc ordering notes.
54. - [ ] Ensure special-case precedence rules stay correct (e.g., YAML vs hrule, setext vs hrule, list vs header edge cases).
55. 
56. ### F. Cleanup
57. - [ ] Remove old `can_parse/parse` API once all migrated blocks use prepared matches.
58. - [ ] Remove redundant detection logic left in `core.rs`.
59. - [ ] Run full test suite + clippy + fmt check.
60. 
61. ## Notes / Constraints
62. - Maintain single-pass, lossless CST emission; prepared matches must not emit to the builder.
63. - Prepared payload should avoid borrowing `ctx.content` directly unless lifetimes are carefully managed; prefer storing indices / owned small strings only when needed.
64. - Performance expectation: dynamic dispatch/downcast overhead is negligible vs string scanning/allocs; eliminating repeated `InfoString::parse` etc. is the main win.
65.
```

</details>

187.     fn parse(

212. /// ATX heading parser (# Heading)
213. pub(crate) struct AtxHeadingParser;
214. 
215. impl BlockParser for AtxHeadingParser {
216.     fn can_parse(
217.         &self,
218.         ctx: &BlockContext,
219.         _lines: &[&str],
220.         _line_pos: usize,
221.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
222.         // Must have blank line before
223.         if !ctx.has_blank_before {
224.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
225.         }
226. 
227.         // Check if this looks like an ATX heading
228.         if try_parse_atx_heading(ctx.content).is_some() {
229.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
230.         } else {
231.             BlockDetectionResult::No
232.         }
233.     }
234. 
235.     fn parse(
236.         &self,
237.         ctx: &BlockContext,
238.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
239.         lines: &[&str],
240.         line_pos: usize,
241.     ) -> usize {
242.         let line = lines[line_pos];
243.         let heading_level = try_parse_atx_heading(ctx.content).unwrap();
244.         emit_atx_heading(builder, line, heading_level, ctx.config);
245.         1 // Consumed 1 line
246.     }
247. 
248.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
249.         "atx_heading"
250.     }
251. }

253. /// YAML metadata block parser (--- ... ---/...)
254. pub(crate) struct YamlMetadataParser;
255. 
256. impl BlockParser for YamlMetadataParser {
257.     fn can_parse(
258.         &self,
259.         ctx: &BlockContext,
260.         lines: &[&str],
261.         line_pos: usize,
262.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
263.         // Must be at top level (not inside blockquotes)
264.         if ctx.blockquote_depth > 0 {
265.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
266.         }
267. 
268.         // Must start with ---
269.         if ctx.content.trim() != "---" {
270.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
271.         }
272. 
273.         // YAML needs blank line before OR be at document start
274.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
275.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
276.         }
277. 
278.         // Look ahead: next line must NOT be blank (to distinguish from horizontal rule)
279.         if line_pos + 1 < lines.len() {
280.             let next_line = lines[line_pos + 1];
281.             if next_line.trim().is_empty() {
282.                 // This is a horizontal rule, not YAML
283.                 return BlockDetectionResult::No;
284.             }
285.         } else {
286.             // No content after ---, can't be YAML
287.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
288.         }
289. 
290.         BlockDetectionResult::Yes
291.     }
292. 
293.     fn parse(
294.         &self,
295.         ctx: &BlockContext,
296.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
297.         lines: &[&str],
298.         line_pos: usize,
299.     ) -> usize {
300.         // Pass at_document_start to try_parse_yaml_block
301.         if let Some(new_pos) = try_parse_yaml_block(lines, line_pos, builder, ctx.at_document_start)
302.         {
303.             new_pos - line_pos // Return lines consumed
304.         } else {
305.             // Should not happen since can_parse returned Yes
306.             1 // Consume at least the opening line
307.         }
308.     }
309. 
310.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
311.         "yaml_metadata"
312.     }
313. }
314.

319.     fn can_parse(
320.         &self,

355. /// Reference definition parser ([label]: url "title")
356. pub(crate) struct ReferenceDefinitionParser;
357. 
358. impl BlockParser for ReferenceDefinitionParser {
359.     fn can_parse(
360.         &self,
361.         ctx: &BlockContext,
362.         _lines: &[&str],
363.         _line_pos: usize,
364.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
365.         // Reference definitions don't need blank line before
366.         // Check if this looks like a reference definition
367.         if try_parse_reference_definition(ctx.content).is_some() {
368.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
369.         } else {
370.             BlockDetectionResult::No
371.         }
372.     }
373. 
374.     fn parse(
375.         &self,
376.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
377.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
378.         lines: &[&str],
379.         line_pos: usize,
380.     ) -> usize {
381.         use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
382. 
383.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::REFERENCE_DEFINITION.into());
384. 
385.         let full_line = lines[line_pos];
386.         let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(full_line);
387. 
388.         // Parse the reference definition with inline structure for the label
389.         emit_reference_definition_content(builder, content_without_newline);
390. 
391.         // Emit newline separately if present
392.         if !line_ending.is_empty() {
393.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
394.         }
395. 
396.         builder.finish_node();
397. 
398.         1 // Consumed 1 line
399.     }
400. 
401.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
402.         "reference_definition"
403.     }
404. }
405. 
406. /// Helper function to emit reference definition content with inline structure.
407. fn emit_reference_definition_content(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, text: &str) {
408.     use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
409. 
410.     if !text.starts_with('[') {
411.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
412.         return;
413.     }
414. 
415.     let rest = &text[1..];
416.     if let Some(close_pos) = rest.find(']') {
417.         let label = &rest[..close_pos];
418.         let after_bracket = &rest[close_pos + 1..];
419. 
420.         if after_bracket.starts_with(':') {
421.             // Emit LINK node with the label
422.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK.into());
423. 
424.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into());
425.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into(), "[");
426.             builder.finish_node();
427. 
428.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_TEXT.into());
429.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), label);
430.             builder.finish_node();
431. 
432.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "]");
433.             builder.finish_node(); // LINK
434. 
435.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), after_bracket);
436.             return;
437.         }
438.     }
439. 
440.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
441. }
442. 
443. /// Fenced code block parser (``` or ~~~)
444. pub(crate) struct FencedCodeBlockParser;
445. 
446. impl BlockParser for FencedCodeBlockParser {
447.     fn can_parse(
448.         &self,
449.         ctx: &BlockContext,
450.         _lines: &[&str],
451.         _line_pos: usize,
452.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
453.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, _lines, _line_pos)
454.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
455.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
456.     }
457. 
458.     fn detect_prepared(
459.         &self,
460.         ctx: &BlockContext,
461.         _lines: &[&str],
462.         _line_pos: usize,
463.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
464.         // Calculate content to check - may need to strip list indentation
465.         let content_to_check = if let Some(list_info) = ctx.list_indent_info {
466.             if list_info.content_col > 0 && !ctx.content.is_empty() {
467.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(ctx.content, list_info.content_col);
468.                 &ctx.content[idx..]
469.             } else {
470.                 ctx.content
471.             }
472.         } else {
473.             ctx.content
474.         };
475. 
476.         let fence = try_parse_fence_open(content_to_check)?;
477. 
478.         // Parse info string to determine block type (expensive, but now cached via fence)
479.         let info = InfoString::parse(&fence.info_string);
480. 
481.         let is_executable = matches!(info.block_type, CodeBlockType::Executable { .. });
482.         let is_pandoc_like = matches!(
483.             ctx.config.flavor,
484.             crate::config::Flavor::Pandoc
485.                 | crate::config::Flavor::CommonMark
486.                 | crate::config::Flavor::Gfm
487.         );
488.         if is_executable && is_pandoc_like {
489.             return None;
490.         }
491. 
492.         // Fenced code blocks can interrupt paragraphs only if they have an info string.
493.         let has_info = !fence.info_string.trim().is_empty();
494.         let detection = if has_info {
495.             BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
496.         } else if ctx.has_blank_before {
497.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
498.         } else {
499.             BlockDetectionResult::No
500.         };
501. 
502.         match detection {
503.             BlockDetectionResult::No => None,
504.             _ => Some((detection, Some(Box::new(fence)))),
505.         }
506.     }
507. 
508.     fn parse(
509.         &self,
510.         ctx: &BlockContext,
511.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
512.         lines: &[&str],
513.         line_pos: usize,
514.     ) -> usize {
515.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
516.     }
517. 
518.     fn parse_prepared(
519.         &self,
520.         ctx: &BlockContext,
````

</details>

561. // ============================================================================
562. // Setext Heading Parser (position #3)
563. // ============================================================================
564. 
565. pub(crate) struct SetextHeadingParser;
566. 
567. impl BlockParser for SetextHeadingParser {
568.     fn can_parse(
569.         &self,
570.         ctx: &BlockContext,
571.         _lines: &[&str],
572.         _line_pos: usize,
573.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
574.         // Setext headings require blank line before (unless at document start)
575.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
576.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
577.         }
578. 
579.         // Need next line for lookahead
580.         let next_line = match ctx.next_line {
581.             Some(line) => line,
582.             None => return BlockDetectionResult::No,
583.         };
584. 
585.         // Create lines array for detection function
586.         let lines = vec![ctx.content, next_line];
587. 
588.         // Try to detect setext heading
589.         if try_parse_setext_heading(&lines, 0).is_some() {
590.             // Setext headings need blank line before (normal case)
591.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
592.         } else {
593.             BlockDetectionResult::No
594.         }
595.     }
596. 
597.     fn parse(
598.         &self,
599.         ctx: &BlockContext,
600.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
601.         lines: &[&str],
602.         pos: usize,
603.     ) -> usize {
604.         // Get text line and underline line
605.         let text_line = lines[pos];
606.         let underline_line = lines[pos + 1];
607. 
608.         // Determine level from underline character (no need to call try_parse again)
609.         // can_parse() already validated this is a valid setext heading
610.         let underline_char = underline_line.trim().chars().next().unwrap_or('=');
611.         let level = if underline_char == '=' { 1 } else { 2 };
612. 
613.         // Emit the setext heading
614.         emit_setext_heading(builder, text_line, underline_line, level, ctx.config);
615. 
616.         // Return lines consumed: text line + underline line
617.         2
618.     }
619. 
620.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
621.         "setext_heading"
622.     }
623. }
624. 
625. // ============================================================================
626. // Block Parser Registry
627. // ============================================================================
628. 
629. /// Registry of block parsers, ordered by priority.
630. ///
631. /// This dispatcher tries each parser in order until one succeeds.
632. /// The ordering follows Pandoc's approach - explicit list order rather
633. /// than numeric priorities.
634. pub(crate) struct BlockParserRegistry {
635.     parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>,
636. }
637. 
638. impl BlockParserRegistry {
639.     /// Create a new registry with all block parsers.
640.     ///
641.     /// Order matters! Parsers are tried in the order listed here.
642.     /// This follows Pandoc's design where ordering is explicit and documented.
643.     ///
644.     /// **Pandoc reference order** (from pandoc/src/Text/Pandoc/Readers/Markdown.hs:487-515):
645.     /// 1. blanklines (handled separately in our parser)
646.     /// 2. codeBlockFenced
647.     /// 3. yamlMetaBlock' ← YAML metadata comes early!
648.     /// 4. bulletList
649.     /// 5. divHtml
650.     /// 6. divFenced
651.     /// 7. header ← ATX headings
652.     /// 8. lhsCodeBlock
653.     /// 9. htmlBlock
654.     /// 10. table
655.     /// 11. codeBlockIndented
656.     /// 12. rawTeXBlock (LaTeX)
657.     /// 13. lineBlock
658.     /// 14. blockQuote
659.     /// 15. hrule ← Horizontal rules come AFTER headers!
660.     /// 16. orderedList
```

1. //! Block parser dispatcher for organizing block-level parsing.
2. //!
3. //! This module provides a trait-based abstraction for block parsers,
4. //! making it easier to add new block types and reducing duplication in parse_inner_content.
5. //!
6. //! Design principles:
7. //! - Single-pass parsing preserved (no backtracking)
8. //! - Each block parser operates independently
9. //! - Inline parsing still integrated (called from within block parsing)
10. //! - Maintains exact CST structure and losslessness
11. 
12. use crate::config::Config;
13. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
14. use std::any::Any;
15. 
16. use super::blocks::code_blocks::{
17.     CodeBlockType, FenceInfo, InfoString, parse_fenced_code_block, try_parse_fence_open,
18. };
19. use super::blocks::figures::{parse_figure, try_parse_figure};
20. use super::blocks::headings::{
21.     emit_atx_heading, emit_setext_heading, try_parse_atx_heading, try_parse_setext_heading,
22. };
23. use super::blocks::horizontal_rules::{emit_horizontal_rule, try_parse_horizontal_rule};
24. use super::blocks::metadata::try_parse_yaml_block;
25. use super::blocks::reference_links::try_parse_reference_definition;
26. use super::utils::container_stack::{ContainerStack, byte_index_at_column};
27. use super::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
28. 
29. /// Information about list indentation context.
30. ///
31. /// Used by block parsers that need to handle indentation stripping
32. /// when parsing inside list items (e.g., fenced code blocks).
33. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
34. pub(crate) struct ListIndentInfo {
35.     /// Number of columns to strip for list content
36.     pub content_col: usize,
37. }
38. 
39. /// Context passed to block parsers for decision-making.
40. ///
41. /// Contains immutable references to parser state that block parsers need
42. /// to check conditions (e.g., blank line before, blockquote depth, etc.).
43. pub(crate) struct BlockContext<'a> {
44.     /// Current line content (after blockquote markers stripped if any)
45.     pub content: &'a str,
46. 
47.     /// Whether there was a blank line before this line
48.     pub has_blank_before: bool,
49. 
50.     /// Whether we're at document start (pos == 0)
51.     pub at_document_start: bool,
52. 
53.     /// Current blockquote depth
54.     pub blockquote_depth: usize,
55. 
56.     /// Parser configuration
57.     pub config: &'a Config,
58. 
59.     /// Container stack for checking context (lists, blockquotes, etc.)
60.     #[allow(dead_code)] // Will be used as we migrate more blocks
61.     pub containers: &'a ContainerStack,
62. 
63.     /// Base indentation from container context (footnotes, definitions)
64.     pub content_indent: usize,
65. 
66.     /// List indentation info if inside a list
67.     pub list_indent_info: Option<ListIndentInfo>,
68. 
69.     /// Next line content for lookahead (used by setext headings)
70.     pub next_line: Option<&'a str>,
71. }
72. 
73. /// Result of detecting whether a block can be parsed.
74. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
75. pub(crate) enum BlockDetectionResult {
76.     /// Can parse this block, requires blank line before
77.     Yes,
78. 
79.     /// Can parse this block and can interrupt paragraphs (no blank line needed)
80.     #[allow(dead_code)]
81.     YesCanInterrupt,
82. 
83.     /// Cannot parse this content
84.     No,
85. }
86. 
87. /// A prepared (cached) detection result.
88. ///
89. /// This allows expensive detection logic (e.g., fence parsing) to be performed once,
90. /// while emission happens only after the caller prepares (flushes buffers/closes paragraphs).
91. pub(crate) struct PreparedBlockMatch {
92.     pub parser_index: usize,
93.     pub detection: BlockDetectionResult,
94.     pub payload: Option<Box<dyn Any>>,
95. }
96. 
97. /// Trait for block-level parsers.
98. ///
99. /// Each block type implements this trait with a two-phase approach:
100. /// 1. Detection: Can this block type parse this content? (lightweight, no emission)
101. /// 2. Parsing: Actually parse and emit the block to the builder (called after preparation)
102. ///
103. /// This separation allows the caller to:
104. /// - Prepare for block elements (close paragraphs, flush buffers) BEFORE emission
105. /// - Handle blocks that can interrupt paragraphs vs those that need blank lines
106. /// - Maintain correct CST node ordering
107. ///
108. /// Note: This is purely organizational - the trait doesn't introduce
109. /// backtracking or multiple passes. Each parser operates during the
110. /// single forward pass through the document.
111. pub(crate) trait BlockParser {
112.     /// Detect if this parser can handle the content (no emission).
113.     fn can_parse(&self, ctx: &BlockContext, lines: &[&str], line_pos: usize) -> BlockDetectionResult;
114. 
115.     /// Prepared detection hook.
116.     ///
117.     /// Default implementation just calls `can_parse()` and returns no payload.
118.     fn detect_prepared(
119.         &self,
120.         ctx: &BlockContext,
```

</details>

155. 
156.     /// Name of this block parser (for debugging/logging)

+    /// Like `detect()`, but allows parsers to return cached payload for emission.
+    pub fn detect_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        lines: &[&str],

+        block_match: &PreparedBlockMatch,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> usize {
+        let parser = &self.parsers[block_match.parser_index];
+        log::debug!("Block parsed by: {}", parser.name());
+        parser.parse_prepared(
+            ctx,
+            builder,
+            lines,
+            line_pos,
+            block_match.payload.as_deref(),
+        )
+    }

---

<sub>⏱️ 50m 45s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Kept going: converted **ATX headings** to use `detect_prepared()` with cached level payload (and `parse_prepared()` uses it), removed the `Vec` allocation in **setext detection** (now uses `[&str; 2]`), and updated the remaining `core.rs` dispatcher callsites to use `detect_prepared()` + `parse_prepared()` (no more `parser_idx` plumbing). `cargo test -q` is green; plan.md is updated to reflect progress and next steps. Next up is doing the same payload-caching for **reference definitions** (and optionally YAML/figure), then tackling the bigger `core.rs` “single detect per line” consolidation.

1. //! Reference definition and footnote parsing functions.
2. //!
3. //! Reference definitions have the form:
4. //! ```markdown
5. //! [label]: url "optional title"
6. //! [label]: url 'optional title'
7. //! [label]: url (optional title)
8. //! [label]: <url> "title"
9. //! ```
10. //!
11. //! Footnote definitions have the form:
12. //! ```markdown
13. //! [^id]: Footnote content here.
14. //!     Can continue on multiple lines
15. //!     as long as they're indented.
16. //! ```
17. 
18. /// Try to parse a reference definition starting at the current position.
19. /// Returns Some((length, label, url, title)) if successful.
20. ///
21. /// Syntax:
22. /// ```markdown
23. /// [label]: url "title"
24. /// [label]: <url> 'title'
25. /// [label]: url
26. ///          (title on next line)
27. /// ```
28. pub fn try_parse_reference_definition(
29.     text: &str,
30. ) -> Option<(usize, String, String, Option<String>)> {
31.     let bytes = text.as_bytes();
32. 
33.     // Must start at beginning of line with [
34.     if bytes.is_empty() || bytes[0] != b'[' {
35.         return None;
36.     }
37. 
38.     // Check if it's a footnote definition [^id]: - not a reference definition
39.     if bytes.len() >= 2 && bytes[1] == b'^' {
40.         return None;
41.     }
42. 
43.     // Find the closing ] for the label
44.     let mut pos = 1;
45.     let mut escape_next = false;
46. 
47.     while pos < bytes.len() {
48.         if escape_next {
49.             escape_next = false;
50.             pos += 1;
51.             continue;
52.         }
53. 
54.         match bytes[pos] {
55.             b'\\' => {
56.                 escape_next = true;
57.                 pos += 1;
58.             }
59.             b']' => {
60.                 break;
61.             }
62.             b'\n' => {
63.                 // Labels can't span lines
64.                 return None;
65.             }
66.             _ => {
67.                 pos += 1;
68.             }
69.         }
70.     }
71. 
72.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b']' {
73.         return None;
74.     }
75. 
76.     let label = &text[1..pos];
77.     if label.is_empty() {
78.         return None;
79.     }
80. 
81.     pos += 1; // Skip ]
82. 
83.     // Must be followed by :
84.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b':' {
85.         return None;
86.     }
87.     pos += 1;
88. 
89.     // Skip whitespace
90.     while pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[pos], b' ' | b'\t') {
91.         pos += 1;
92.     }
93. 
94.     // Parse URL
95.     let url_start = pos;
96.     let url_end;
97. 
98.     // Check for angle-bracketed URL <url>
99.     if pos < bytes.len() && bytes[pos] == b'<' {
100.         pos += 1;
101.         let url_content_start = pos;
102.         // Find closing >
103.         while pos < bytes.len() && bytes[pos] != b'>' && bytes[pos] != b'\n' && bytes[pos] != b'\r'
104.         {
105.             pos += 1;
106.         }
107.         if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b'>' {
108.             return None;
109.         }
110.         url_end = pos;
111.         let url = text[url_content_start..url_end].to_string();
112.         pos += 1; // Skip >
113. 
114.         // Parse optional title
115.         let title = parse_title(text, bytes, &mut pos)?;
116. 
117.         Some((pos, label.to_string(), url, title))
118.     } else {
119.         // Parse unbracketed URL (until whitespace or newline)
120.         while pos < bytes.len() && !matches!(bytes[pos], b' ' | b'\t' | b'\n' | b'\r') {
121.             pos += 1;
122.         }
123. 
124.         url_end = pos;
125.         if url_start == url_end {
126.             return None; // No URL found
127.         }
128. 
129.         let url = text[url_start..url_end].to_string();
130. 
131.         // Parse optional title
132.         let title = parse_title(text, bytes, &mut pos)?;
133. 
134.         Some((pos, label.to_string(), url, title))
135.     }
136. }
137. 
138. /// Parse an optional title after the URL.
139. /// Titles can be in double quotes, single quotes, or parentheses.
140. /// Returns Some(Some(title)) if title found, Some(None) if no title, None if malformed.
141. fn parse_title(text: &str, bytes: &[u8], pos: &mut usize) -> Option<Option<String>> {
142.     let base_pos = *pos;
143. 
144.     // Skip whitespace (including newlines for multi-line titles)
145.     while *pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[*pos], b' ' | b'\t' | b'\n' | b'\r') {
146.         *pos += 1;
147.     }
148. 
149.     // Check if there's a title
150.     if *pos >= bytes.len() {
151.         return Some(None);
152.     }
153. 
154.     let quote_char = bytes[*pos];
155.     if !matches!(quote_char, b'"' | b'\'' | b'(') {
156.         // No title, that's okay
157.         *pos = base_pos; // Reset position
158.         return Some(None);
159.     }
160. 
161.     let closing_char = if quote_char == b'(' { b')' } else { quote_char };
162. 
163.     *pos += 1; // Skip opening quote
164.     let title_start = *pos;
165. 
166.     // Find closing quote
167.     let mut escape_next = false;
168.     while *pos < bytes.len() {
169.         if escape_next {
170.             escape_next = false;
171.             *pos += 1;
172.             continue;
173.         }
174. 
175.         match bytes[*pos] {
176.             b'\\' => {
177.                 escape_next = true;
178.                 *pos += 1;
179.             }
180.             c if c == closing_char => {
181.                 let title_end = *pos;
182.                 *pos += 1; // Skip closing quote
183. 
184.                 // Skip trailing whitespace to end of line
185.                 while *pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[*pos], b' ' | b'\t') {
186.                     *pos += 1;
187.                 }
188. 
189.                 // Extract title from the original text using correct indices
190.                 let title = text[title_start..title_end].to_string();
191.                 return Some(Some(title));
192.             }
193.             b'\n' if quote_char == b'(' => {
194.                 // Parenthetical titles can span lines
195.                 *pos += 1;
196.             }
197.             _ => {
198.                 *pos += 1;
199.             }
200.         }
201.     }
202. 
203.     // No closing quote found
204.     None
205. }
206. 
207. /// Try to parse just the footnote marker [^id]: from a line.
208. /// Returns Some((id, content_start_col)) if the line starts with a footnote marker.
209. ///
210. /// Syntax:
211. /// ```markdown
212. /// [^id]: Footnote content.
213. /// ```
214. pub fn try_parse_footnote_marker(line: &str) -> Option<(String, usize)> {
215.     let bytes = line.as_bytes();
216. 
217.     // Must start with [^
218.     if bytes.len() < 4 || bytes[0] != b'[' || bytes[1] != b'^' {
219.         return None;
220.     }
````

</details>

340.         &self,

344.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
345.         // Must have blank line before

347.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
348.         }
349.

358.     fn parse(

374. 
375. /// Reference definition parser ([label]: url "title")
376. pub(crate) struct ReferenceDefinitionParser;
377. 
378. impl BlockParser for ReferenceDefinitionParser {
379.     fn can_parse(
380.         &self,
381.         ctx: &BlockContext,
382.         _lines: &[&str],
383.         _line_pos: usize,
384.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
385.         // Reference definitions don't need blank line before
386.         // Check if this looks like a reference definition
387.         if try_parse_reference_definition(ctx.content).is_some() {
388.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
389.         } else {
390.             BlockDetectionResult::No
391.         }
392.     }
393. 
394.     fn parse(
395.         &self,
396.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
397.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
398.         lines: &[&str],
399.         line_pos: usize,
400.     ) -> usize {
401.         use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
402. 
403.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::REFERENCE_DEFINITION.into());
404. 
405.         let full_line = lines[line_pos];
406.         let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(full_line);
407. 
408.         // Parse the reference definition with inline structure for the label
409.         emit_reference_definition_content(builder, content_without_newline);
410. 
411.         // Emit newline separately if present
412.         if !line_ending.is_empty() {
413.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
414.         }
415. 
416.         builder.finish_node();
417. 
418.         1 // Consumed 1 line
419.     }
420. 
421.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
422.         "reference_definition"
423.     }
424. }
425. 
426. /// Helper function to emit reference definition content with inline structure.
427. fn emit_reference_definition_content(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, text: &str) {
428.     use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
429. 
430.     if !text.starts_with('[') {
431.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
432.         return;
433.     }
434. 
435.     let rest = &text[1..];
436.     if let Some(close_pos) = rest.find(']') {
437.         let label = &rest[..close_pos];
438.         let after_bracket = &rest[close_pos + 1..];
439. 
440.         if after_bracket.starts_with(':') {
441.             // Emit LINK node with the label
442.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK.into());
443. 
444.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into());
445.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into(), "[");
446.             builder.finish_node();
447. 
448.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_TEXT.into());
449.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), label);
450.             builder.finish_node();
451. 
452.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "]");
453.             builder.finish_node(); // LINK
454. 
455.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), after_bracket);
456.             return;
457.         }
458.     }
459. 
460.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>39 lines</summary>

```
20. ## Workplan
21. 
22. ### A. Baseline & safety
23. - [x] Run baseline tests/benchmarks to capture current behavior (`cargo test` at minimum).
24. - [ ] Identify a small set of “high-signal” golden cases to watch while refactoring (fenced code, setext, yaml/hrule ambiguity, refdefs).
25. 
26. ### B. Introduce prepared-match API (non-invasive)
27. - [x] Add new types in `src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs`:
28.   - `PreparedBlockMatch` (parser index + detection + cached payload)
29. - [x] Extend `BlockParserRegistry` with new entrypoints (keep old API temporarily):
30.   - `detect_prepared(...) -> Option<PreparedBlockMatch>`
31.   - `parse_prepared(match, ...) -> usize`
32. 
33. ### C. Convert migrated parsers (start with biggest perf win)
34. - [x] Fenced code: detection parses fence + `InfoString` once; parse reuses cached `FenceInfo`.
35. - [x] ATX heading: detection returns cached level; parse uses it (no second `try_parse_atx_heading`).
36. - [ ] YAML: optionally cache “is yaml not hrule” disambiguation.
37. - [ ] Figure: cache validation span from inline image parse so it isn’t repeated.
38. - [x] Setext: remove allocation in detection (no `Vec`).
39. - [ ] Reference definition: optionally cache parse tuple from `try_parse_reference_definition`.
40. 
41. ### D. Refactor `core.rs` to “single detect per line”
42. - [ ] Create one `BlockContext` per line and reuse it (avoid repeated rebuilds).
43. - [ ] Replace the multiple dispatcher detections with one flow per line (detect once, then prepare/emit).
44. - [ ] Rework the Definition continuation guard (`is_block_element` at ~1041) to delegate to the registry instead of duplicating `try_parse_*` checks.
45. 
46. ### E. Consolidate ordering/precedence in the registry
47. - [ ] Move remaining “block start” checks from `core.rs` into the registry progressively (tables/divs/html/latex/etc.), keeping Pandoc ordering notes.
48. - [ ] Ensure special-case precedence rules stay correct (e.g., YAML vs hrule, setext vs hrule, list vs header edge cases).
49. 
50. ### F. Cleanup
51. - [ ] Remove old `can_parse/parse` API once all migrated blocks use prepared matches.
52. - [ ] Remove redundant detection logic left in `core.rs`.
53. - [ ] Run full test suite + clippy + fmt check.
54. 
55. ## Notes / Constraints
56. - Maintain single-pass, lossless CST emission; prepared matches must not emit to the builder.
57. - Prepared payload should avoid borrowing `ctx.content` directly unless lifetimes are carefully managed; prefer storing indices / owned small strings only when needed.
58. - Performance expectation: dynamic dispatch/downcast overhead is negligible vs string scanning/allocs; eliminating repeated `InfoString::parse` etc. is the main win.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 52m 36s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

No second thoughts yet—the **prepared match** architecture still looks like the right “Pandoc-like, single-pass, no backtracking” compromise: it keeps ordering explicit, lets `core.rs` flush/close containers before emission, and eliminates the big duplication/perf cliff from `can_parse()` doing real work twice.

Kept going:
- Migrated **ReferenceDefinitionParser** to `detect_prepared()` and cached the full `try_parse_reference_definition(...)` tuple in the payload (emitter still only uses the original line today, but now we won’t re-run the parser later when we do richer emission).
- Updated **plan.md** to mark this step done.
- `cargo test -q` passes.

Next concrete step is the bigger win: refactor `core.rs` to do **one dispatcher detect per line** (instead of multiple detect/contexts), and then route the Definition continuation “is_block_element” probe through the registry.

1423.

1448.             if matches!(detection, BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt) {

1456.                 }
1457. 
1458.                 // Recreate context for parsing

1462.                         Some(super::block_dispatcher::ListIndentInfo { content_col })

1473.                     None
1474.                 };

1476.                 let block_ctx = BlockContext {
1477.                     content,

1483.                     content_indent,
1484.                     list_indent_info,

1488.                 let lines_consumed = self.block_registry.parse_prepared(

1498.         }
1499. 
1500.         // Check for footnote definition: [^id]: content
1501.         // Similar to list items - marker followed by content that can span multiple lines
1502.         // Must check BEFORE reference definitions since both start with [
1503.         if let Some((id, content_start)) = try_parse_footnote_marker(content) {
1504.             log::debug!("Parsed footnote definition at line {}: [^{}]", self.pos, id);
1505. 
1506.             // Close paragraph if one is open
1507.             self.close_paragraph_if_open();
1508. 
1509.             // Close previous footnote if one is open
1510.             while matches!(
1511.                 self.containers.last(),
1512.                 Some(Container::FootnoteDefinition { .. })
1513.             ) {
1514.                 self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1515.             }
1516. 
1517.             // Start the footnote definition container
1518.             self.builder
1519.                 .start_node(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION.into());
1520. 
1521.             // Emit the marker
1522.             let marker_text = &content[..content_start];
1523.             self.builder
1524.                 .token(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_REFERENCE.into(), marker_text);
1525. 
1526.             // Calculate content column (minimum 4 spaces for continuation)
1527.             // The first line can start right after the marker, but subsequent lines
1528.             // need at least 4 spaces of indentation
1529.             let content_col = 4;
1530.             self.containers
1531.                 .push(Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col });
1532. 
1533.             // Parse the first line content (if any)
1534.             let first_line_content = &content[content_start..];
1535.             if !first_line_content.trim().is_empty() {
1536.                 paragraphs::start_paragraph_if_needed(&mut self.containers, &mut self.builder);
1537.                 paragraphs::append_paragraph_line(
1538.                     &mut self.containers,
1539.                     &mut self.builder,
1540.                     first_line_content,
1541.                     self.config,
1542.                 );

1544. 
1545.             self.pos += 1;
1546.             return true;
1547.         }
1548. 
1549.         // Check for reference definition: [label]: url "title"
1550.         // These can appear anywhere in the document (no blank line needed)
1551.         // Try dispatcher first
1552. 
1553.         let next_line = if self.pos + 1 < self.lines.len() {
1554.             Some(self.lines[self.pos + 1])
1555.         } else {
1556.             None
1557.         };
1558. 
1559.         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
1560.             content,
1561.             has_blank_before,
1562.             at_document_start,
1563.             blockquote_depth: self.current_blockquote_depth(),
1564.             config: self.config,
1565.             containers: &self.containers,
1566.             content_indent,
1567.             list_indent_info: None, // Not needed for reference definitions

1571.         if let Some(block_match) = self
1572.             .block_registry
1573.             .detect_prepared(&block_ctx, &self.lines, self.pos)
1574.         {
1575.             // Reference definitions don't need preparation
1576.             let lines_consumed = self.block_registry.parse_prepared(

1578.                 &block_ctx,
1579.                 &mut self.builder,
1580.                 &self.lines,
1581.                 self.pos,

1583.             self.pos += lines_consumed;

1586. 
1587.         // Check for indented code block

1590.         if has_blank_before_strict
1591.             && is_indented_code_line(content)

1594.             // Prepare for indented code block

1599.             let new_pos = parse_indented_code_block(
1600.                 &mut self.builder,

1603.                 bq_depth,
1604.                 content_indent,

987.     /// Get the total indentation to strip from content containers (footnotes + definitions).
988.     fn content_container_indent_to_strip(&self) -> usize {
989.         self.containers
990.             .stack
991.             .iter()
992.             .filter_map(|c| match c {
993.                 Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
994.                 Container::Definition { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
995.                 _ => None,
996.             })
997.             .sum()
998.     }
999. 
1000.     /// Parse content inside blockquotes (or at top level).
1001.     ///
1002.     /// `content` - The content to parse (may have indent/markers stripped)
1003.     /// `line_to_append` - Optional line to use when appending to paragraphs.
1004.     ///                    If None, uses self.lines[self.pos]
1005.     fn parse_inner_content(&mut self, content: &str, line_to_append: Option<&str>) -> bool {
1006.         log::debug!(
1007.             "parse_inner_content [{}]: depth={}, last={:?}, content={:?}",
1008.             self.pos,
1009.             self.containers.depth(),
1010.             self.containers.last(),
1011.             content.trim_end()
1012.         );
1013.         // Calculate how much indentation should be stripped for content containers
1014.         // (definitions, footnotes) FIRST, so we can check for block markers correctly
1015.         let content_indent = self.content_container_indent_to_strip();
1016.         let (stripped_content, indent_to_emit) = if content_indent > 0 {
1017.             let (indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(content);
1018.             if indent_cols >= content_indent {
1019.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(content, content_indent);
1020.                 (&content[idx..], Some(&content[..idx]))
1021.             } else {
1022.                 // Line has less indent than required - preserve leading whitespace
1023.                 let trimmed_start = content.trim_start();
1024.                 let ws_len = content.len() - trimmed_start.len();
1025.                 if ws_len > 0 {
1026.                     (trimmed_start, Some(&content[..ws_len]))
1027.                 } else {
1028.                     (content, None)
1029.                 }
1030.             }
1031.         } else {
1032.             (content, None)
1033.         };
1034. 
1035.         // Check if we're in a Definition container (with or without an open PLAIN)
1036.         // Continuation lines should be added to PLAIN, not treated as new blocks
1037.         // BUT: Don't treat lines with block element markers as continuations

1045.                 || try_parse_div_fence_open(stripped_content).is_some()
1046.                 || try_parse_horizontal_rule(stripped_content).is_some()
1047.                 || try_parse_atx_heading(stripped_content).is_some()
1048.                 || (self.config.extensions.raw_html
1049.                     && try_parse_html_block_start(stripped_content).is_some())
1050.                 || (self.config.extensions.raw_tex
1051.                     && try_parse_latex_env_begin(stripped_content).is_some());
1052. 
1053.             if is_block_element {
1054.                 // Close any open Plain block before processing the block element
1055.                 // Buffered PLAIN content will be emitted by emit_buffered_plain_if_needed()
1056.                 // Fall through to parse the block element
1057.             } else {
1058.                 // This is a continuation line - add to PLAIN (start one if needed)
1059.                 let full_line = self.lines[self.pos];
1060.                 let (text_without_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(full_line);
1061. 
1062.                 // Buffer the line for later inline parsing
1063.                 if let Some(Container::Definition {
1064.                     plain_open,
1065.                     plain_buffer,
1066.                     ..
1067.                 }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()

1069.                     // Include the newline in the buffered text for losslessness

1073.                         text_without_newline.to_string()
1074.                     };

266.     /// Emit buffered list item content if we're in a ListItem and it has content.
267.     /// This is used before starting block-level elements inside list items.
268.     fn emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed(&mut self) {
269.         if let Some(Container::ListItem { buffer, .. }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
270.             && !buffer.is_empty()
271.         {
272.             let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
273.             buffer.clear();
274.             let use_paragraph = buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content();
275.             buffer_clone.emit_as_block(&mut self.builder, use_paragraph, self.config);
276.         }
277.     }
278. 
279.     /// Check if a paragraph is currently open.
280.     fn is_paragraph_open(&self) -> bool {
281.         matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Paragraph { .. }))
282.     }
283. 
284.     /// Close paragraph if one is currently open.
285.     fn close_paragraph_if_open(&mut self) {
286.         if self.is_paragraph_open() {
287.             self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
288.         }
289.     }
290. 
291.     /// Prepare for a block-level element by flushing buffers and closing paragraphs.
292.     /// This is a common pattern before starting tables, code blocks, divs, etc.
293.     fn prepare_for_block_element(&mut self) {
294.         self.emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed();
295.         self.close_paragraph_if_open();
296.     }
297. 
298.     /// Get current blockquote depth from container stack.
299.     fn current_blockquote_depth(&self) -> usize {
300.         blockquotes::current_blockquote_depth(&self.containers)
301.     }
302. 
303.     /// Emit or buffer a blockquote marker depending on parser state.
304.     ///
305.     /// If a paragraph is open and we're using integrated parsing, buffer the marker.
306.     /// Otherwise emit it directly to the builder.
307.     fn emit_or_buffer_blockquote_marker(
308.         &mut self,
309.         leading_spaces: usize,
310.         has_trailing_space: bool,
311.     ) {
312.         // If paragraph is open, buffer the marker (it will be emitted at correct position)
313.         if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Paragraph { .. })) {
314.             // Buffer the marker in the paragraph
315.             paragraphs::append_paragraph_marker(
316.                 &mut self.containers,
317.                 leading_spaces,
318.                 has_trailing_space,
319.             );
320.         } else {
321.             // Emit directly
322.             blockquotes::emit_one_blockquote_marker(
323.                 &mut self.builder,
324.                 leading_spaces,
325.                 has_trailing_space,
326.             );
327.         }
328.     }

1. use crate::config::Config;

4.

19. use super::blocks::reference_links;
20. use super::blocks::tables;

22. use super::utils::helpers::{split_lines_inclusive, strip_newline};
23. use super::utils::inline_emission;
24. use super::utils::marker_utils;
25. use super::utils::text_buffer;
26. 
27. use code_blocks::try_parse_fence_open;
28. use container_stack::{Container, ContainerStack, byte_index_at_column, leading_indent};
29. use definition_lists::{emit_definition_marker, emit_term, try_parse_definition_marker};
30. use fenced_divs::{is_div_closing_fence, try_parse_div_fence_open};
31. use headings::try_parse_atx_heading;
32. use horizontal_rules::try_parse_horizontal_rule;
33. use html_blocks::{parse_html_block, try_parse_html_block_start};
34. use indented_code::{is_indented_code_line, parse_indented_code_block};
35. use latex_envs::{parse_latex_environment, try_parse_latex_env_begin};
36. use line_blocks::{parse_line_block, try_parse_line_block_start};
37. use lists::{is_content_nested_bullet_marker, markers_match, try_parse_list_marker};
38. use marker_utils::{count_blockquote_markers, parse_blockquote_marker_info};
39. use metadata::try_parse_pandoc_title_block;
40. use reference_links::try_parse_footnote_marker;
41. use tables::{
42.     is_caption_followed_by_table, try_parse_grid_table, try_parse_multiline_table,
43.     try_parse_pipe_table, try_parse_simple_table,
44. };
45. use text_buffer::TextBuffer;
46. 
47. fn init_logger() {
48.     let _ = env_logger::builder().is_test(true).try_init();
49. }
50. 
51. pub struct Parser<'a> {
52.     lines: Vec<&'a str>,
53.     pos: usize,
54.     builder: GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
55.     containers: ContainerStack,
56.     config: &'a Config,
57.     block_registry: BlockParserRegistry,
58. }
59. 
60. impl<'a> Parser<'a> {
61.     pub fn new(input: &'a str, config: &'a Config) -> Self {
62.         // Use split_lines_inclusive to preserve line endings (both LF and CRLF)
63.         let lines = split_lines_inclusive(input);
64.         Self {
65.             lines,
66.             pos: 0,
67.             builder: GreenNodeBuilder::new(),
68.             containers: ContainerStack::new(),
69.             config,
70.             block_registry: BlockParserRegistry::new(),
71.         }
72.     }
73. 
74.     pub fn parse(mut self) -> SyntaxNode {
75.         #[cfg(debug_assertions)]
76.         {
77.             init_logger();
78.         }
79. 
80.         self.parse_document_stack();
81. 
82.         SyntaxNode::new_root(self.builder.finish())
83.     }
84. 
85.     /// Emit buffered PLAIN content if Definition container has open PLAIN.
86.     /// Close containers down to `keep`, emitting buffered content first.
87.     fn close_containers_to(&mut self, keep: usize) {
88.         // Emit buffered PARAGRAPH/PLAIN content before closing
89.         while self.containers.depth() > keep {
90.             match self.containers.stack.last() {
91.                 // Handle ListItem with buffering
92.                 Some(Container::ListItem { buffer, .. }) if !buffer.is_empty() => {
93.                     // Clone buffer to avoid borrow issues
94.                     let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
95. 
96.                     log::debug!(
97.                         "Closing ListItem with buffer (is_empty={}, segment_count={})",
98.                         buffer_clone.is_empty(),
99.                         buffer_clone.segment_count()
100.                     );
101. 
102.                     // Determine if this should be Plain or PARAGRAPH:
103.                     // 1. Check if parent LIST has blank lines between items (list-level loose)
104.                     // 2. OR check if this item has blank lines within its content (item-level loose)
105.                     let parent_list_is_loose = self
106.                         .containers
107.                         .stack
108.                         .iter()
109.                         .rev()
110.                         .find_map(|c| match c {
111.                             Container::List {
112.                                 has_blank_between_items,
113.                                 ..
114.                             } => Some(*has_blank_between_items),
115.                             _ => None,
116.                         })
117.                         .unwrap_or(false);
118. 
119.                     let use_paragraph =
120.                         parent_list_is_loose || buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content();
```

1462.             // Parse the first line content (if any)
1463.             let first_line_content = &content[content_start..];
1464.             if !first_line_content.trim().is_empty() {
1465.                 paragraphs::start_paragraph_if_needed(&mut self.containers, &mut self.builder);
1466.                 paragraphs::append_paragraph_line(
1467.                     &mut self.containers,
1468.                     &mut self.builder,
1469.                     first_line_content,
1470.                     self.config,
1471.                 );
1472.             }
1473. 
1474.             self.pos += 1;
1475.             return true;
1476.         }
1477. 
1478.         // Check for reference definition: [label]: url "title"
1479.         // These can appear anywhere in the document (no blank line needed)
1480.         // Try dispatcher first
1481. 
1482.         let next_line = if self.pos + 1 < self.lines.len() {
1483.             Some(self.lines[self.pos + 1])
1484.         } else {
1485.             None
1486.         };
1487. 
1488.         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
1489.             content,
1490.             has_blank_before,
1491.             at_document_start,
1492.             blockquote_depth: self.current_blockquote_depth(),
1493.             config: self.config,
1494.             containers: &self.containers,
1495.             content_indent,
1496.             list_indent_info: None, // Not needed for reference definitions
1497.             next_line,
1498.         };
1499. 
1500.         if let Some(block_match) = self
1501.             .block_registry
1502.             .detect_prepared(&block_ctx, &self.lines, self.pos)
1503.         {
1504.             // Reference definitions don't need preparation
1505.             let lines_consumed = self.block_registry.parse_prepared(
1506.                 &block_match,
1507.                 &block_ctx,
1508.                 &mut self.builder,
1509.                 &self.lines,
1510.                 self.pos,
1511.             );
1512.             self.pos += lines_consumed;
1513.             return true;
1514.         }
1515. 
1516.         // Check for indented code block
1517.         // Inside a footnote, content needs 4 spaces for code (8 total in raw line)
1518.         // BUT: Don't treat as code if it's a list marker (list takes precedence)
1519.         if has_blank_before_strict
1520.             && is_indented_code_line(content)
```

</details>

1082.                     plain_open,
1083.                     plain_buffer,
1084.                     ..
1085.                 }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
1086.                 {
1087.                     // Include the newline in the buffered text for losslessness
1088.                     let line_with_newline = if !newline_str.is_empty() {
1089.                         format!("{}{}", text_without_newline, newline_str)
1090.                     } else {
1091.                         text_without_newline.to_string()
1092.                     };
1093.                     plain_buffer.push_line(line_with_newline);
1094.                     *plain_open = true; // Mark that we now have an open PLAIN
1095.                 }
1096. 
1097.                 self.pos += 1;
1098.                 return true;
1099.             }
1100.         }
1101. 
1102.         // Store the stripped content for later use

1104. 
1105.         // Check for heading (needs blank line before, or at start of container)
1106.         let has_blank_before = self.pos == 0
1107.             || self.lines[self.pos - 1].trim().is_empty()
1108.             || matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::BlockQuote { .. }))
1109.             || matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::List { .. }));
1110. 
1111.         // For indented code blocks, we need a stricter condition - only actual blank lines count
