1113.         let at_document_start = self.pos == 0 && self.current_blockquote_depth() == 0;

1346.                         self.emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed();
1347.                         if self.is_paragraph_open() {
1348.                             self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1349.                         }
1350.                     }
1351.                     BlockDetectionResult::Yes => {
1352.                         self.prepare_for_block_element();
1353.                     }
1354.                     BlockDetectionResult::No => unreachable!(),
1355.                 }
1356. 
1357.                 let lines_consumed = self.block_registry.parse_prepared(
1358.                     block_match,
1359.                     &dispatcher_ctx,
1360.                     &mut self.builder,
1361.                     &self.lines,
1362.                     self.pos,
1363.                 );
1364.                 self.pos += lines_consumed;
1365.                 return true;
1366.             }
1367.         } else if let Some(block_match) = dispatcher_match.as_ref() {
1368.             // Without blank-before, only allow interrupting blocks OR blocks that are
1369.             // explicitly allowed without blank lines (e.g. reference definitions).
1370.             match block_match.detection {
1371.                 BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
1372.                     self.emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed();
1373.                     if self.is_paragraph_open() {
1374.                         self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1375.                     }
1376.                 }
1377.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes => {
1378.                     // E.g. reference definitions: no preparation (matches previous behavior).
1379.                 }
1380.                 BlockDetectionResult::No => unreachable!(),
1381.             }
1382. 
1383.             if !matches!(block_match.detection, BlockDetectionResult::No) {
1384.                 let lines_consumed = self.block_registry.parse_prepared(
1385.                     block_match,
1386.                     &dispatcher_ctx,
1387.                     &mut self.builder,
1388.                     &self.lines,
1389.                     self.pos,
1390.                 );
1391.                 self.pos += lines_consumed;
1392.                 return true;
1393.             }
1394.         }
1395. 
1396.         // Check for footnote definition: [^id]: content
1397.         // Similar to list items - marker followed by content that can span multiple lines
1398.         // Must check BEFORE reference definitions since both start with [
1399.         if let Some((id, content_start)) = try_parse_footnote_marker(content) {
1400.             // Footnotes have precedence over dispatcher-based reference definitions.
1401.             // (Dispatcher match is evaluated above for performance; we handle footnotes here.)
1402.             log::debug!("Parsed footnote definition at line {}: [^{}]", self.pos, id);
1403. 
1404.             // Close paragraph if one is open
1405.             self.close_paragraph_if_open();
1406. 
1407.             // Close previous footnote if one is open
1408.             while matches!(
1409.                 self.containers.last(),
1410.                 Some(Container::FootnoteDefinition { .. })
1411.             ) {
1412.                 self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1413.             }
1414. 
1415.             // Start the footnote definition container
1416.             self.builder
1417.                 .start_node(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION.into());
1418. 
1419.             // Emit the marker
1420.             let marker_text = &content[..content_start];
1421.             self.builder
1422.                 .token(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_REFERENCE.into(), marker_text);
1423. 
1424.             // Calculate content column (minimum 4 spaces for continuation)
1425.             // The first line can start right after the marker, but subsequent lines
```

1. //! Block parser dispatcher for organizing block-level parsing.
2. //!
3. //! This module provides a trait-based abstraction for block parsers,
4. //! making it easier to add new block types and reducing duplication in parse_inner_content.
5. //!
6. //! Design principles:
7. //! - Single-pass parsing preserved (no backtracking)
8. //! - Each block parser operates independently
9. //! - Inline parsing still integrated (called from within block parsing)
10. //! - Maintains exact CST structure and losslessness
11. 
12. use crate::config::Config;
13. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
14. use std::any::Any;
15. 
16. use super::blocks::code_blocks::{
17.     CodeBlockType, FenceInfo, InfoString, parse_fenced_code_block, try_parse_fence_open,
18. };
19. use super::blocks::figures::{parse_figure, try_parse_figure};
20. use super::blocks::headings::{
21.     emit_atx_heading, emit_setext_heading, try_parse_atx_heading, try_parse_setext_heading,
22. };
23. use super::blocks::horizontal_rules::{emit_horizontal_rule, try_parse_horizontal_rule};
24. use super::blocks::metadata::try_parse_yaml_block;
25. use super::blocks::reference_links::try_parse_reference_definition;
26. use super::utils::container_stack::{ContainerStack, byte_index_at_column};
27. use super::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
28. 
29. /// Information about list indentation context.
30. ///
31. /// Used by block parsers that need to handle indentation stripping
32. /// when parsing inside list items (e.g., fenced code blocks).
33. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
34. pub(crate) struct ListIndentInfo {
35.     /// Number of columns to strip for list content
36.     pub content_col: usize,
37. }
38. 
39. /// Context passed to block parsers for decision-making.
40. ///
41. /// Contains immutable references to parser state that block parsers need
42. /// to check conditions (e.g., blank line before, blockquote depth, etc.).
43. pub(crate) struct BlockContext<'a> {
44.     /// Current line content (after blockquote markers stripped if any)
45.     pub content: &'a str,
46. 
47.     /// Whether there was a blank line before this line
48.     pub has_blank_before: bool,
49. 
50.     /// Whether we're at document start (pos == 0)
51.     pub at_document_start: bool,
52. 
53.     /// Current blockquote depth
54.     pub blockquote_depth: usize,
55. 
56.     /// Parser configuration
57.     pub config: &'a Config,
58. 
59.     /// Container stack for checking context (lists, blockquotes, etc.)
60.     #[allow(dead_code)] // Will be used as we migrate more blocks
61.     pub containers: &'a ContainerStack,
62. 
63.     /// Base indentation from container context (footnotes, definitions)
64.     pub content_indent: usize,
65. 
66.     /// List indentation info if inside a list
67.     pub list_indent_info: Option<ListIndentInfo>,
68. 
69.     /// Next line content for lookahead (used by setext headings)
70.     pub next_line: Option<&'a str>,
71. }
72. 
73. /// Result of detecting whether a block can be parsed.
74. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
75. pub(crate) enum BlockDetectionResult {
76.     /// Can parse this block, requires blank line before
77.     Yes,
78. 
79.     /// Can parse this block and can interrupt paragraphs (no blank line needed)
80.     #[allow(dead_code)]
81.     YesCanInterrupt,
82. 
83.     /// Cannot parse this content
84.     No,
85. }
86. 
87. /// A prepared (cached) detection result.
88. ///
89. /// This allows expensive detection logic (e.g., fence parsing) to be performed once,
90. /// while emission happens only after the caller prepares (flushes buffers/closes paragraphs).
91. pub(crate) struct PreparedBlockMatch {
92.     pub parser_index: usize,
93.     pub detection: BlockDetectionResult,
94.     pub payload: Option<Box<dyn Any>>,
95. }
96. 
97. /// Trait for block-level parsers.
98. ///
99. /// Each block type implements this trait with a two-phase approach:
100. /// 1. Detection: Can this block type parse this content? (lightweight, no emission)
101. /// 2. Parsing: Actually parse and emit the block to the builder (called after preparation)
102. ///
103. /// This separation allows the caller to:
104. /// - Prepare for block elements (close paragraphs, flush buffers) BEFORE emission
105. /// - Handle blocks that can interrupt paragraphs vs those that need blank lines
106. /// - Maintain correct CST node ordering
107. ///
108. /// Note: This is purely organizational - the trait doesn't introduce
109. /// backtracking or multiple passes. Each parser operates during the
110. /// single forward pass through the document.
111. pub(crate) trait BlockParser {
112.     /// Detect if this parser can handle the content (no emission).
113.     fn can_parse(&self, ctx: &BlockContext, lines: &[&str], line_pos: usize) -> BlockDetectionResult;
114. 
115.     /// Prepared detection hook.
116.     ///
117.     /// Default implementation just calls `can_parse()` and returns no payload.
118.     fn detect_prepared(
119.         &self,
120.         ctx: &BlockContext,
```

</details>

163. 
164. /// Horizontal rule parser
165. pub(crate) struct HorizontalRuleParser;
166. 
167. impl BlockParser for HorizontalRuleParser {
168.     fn can_parse(
169.         &self,
170.         ctx: &BlockContext,
171.         _lines: &[&str],
172.         _line_pos: usize,
173.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
174.         // Must have blank line before
175.         if !ctx.has_blank_before {
176.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
177.         }
178. 
179.         // Check if this looks like a horizontal rule
180.         if try_parse_horizontal_rule(ctx.content).is_some() {
181.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
182.         } else {
183.             BlockDetectionResult::No
184.         }
185.     }
186. 
187.     fn parse(
188.         &self,
189.         ctx: &BlockContext,
190.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
191.         lines: &[&str],
192.         line_pos: usize,
193.     ) -> usize {
194.         // Use ctx.content (blockquote markers already stripped)
195.         // But preserve newline from original line
196.         let (_, newline_str) = strip_newline(lines[line_pos]);
197.         let content_with_newline = if !newline_str.is_empty() {
198.             format!("{}{}", ctx.content.trim_end(), newline_str)
199.         } else {
200.             ctx.content.to_string()
201.         };
202. 
203.         emit_horizontal_rule(builder, &content_with_newline);
204.         1 // Consumed 1 line
205.     }
206. 
207.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
208.         "horizontal_rule"
209.     }
210. }
211.

<sub>⏱️ 75m 46s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

/home/jola/.local/state/.copilot/session-state/8111c140-3b00-44e8-81bb-2bc5c0b6ac76/plan.md (lines 1-200)

<details>
<summary>58 lines</summary>

```
1. # Plan: Block dispatcher “prepared match” refactor
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Current `BlockParser::can_parse()` often does non-trivial work (e.g. fenced code parses fence + `InfoString::parse`, setext allocates, figure uses inline image parsing). This work is frequently repeated because:
5. - several parsers call `try_parse_*` again inside `parse()`
6. - `core.rs` calls `block_registry.detect(...)` multiple times per line in different branches
7. - `core.rs` separately re-implements “is this a block element marker?” checks (e.g. Definition continuation guard), further duplicating detection.
8. 
9. ## Goal
10. Keep the good part of the refactor (Pandoc-like ordered registry + ability to “prepare/flush buffers before emitting”), while eliminating duplicate detection/parsing and reducing `core.rs` complexity.
11. 
12. ## Approach (high level)
13. Introduce a **prepared match** concept: detection returns a match object that caches any expensive parsing results, and emission consumes that cached data (no re-parsing). Then rework `core.rs` so each line does **one** detection pass, decides whether the match is allowed (blank-line rules / interrupt rules), performs preparation, and emits.
14. 
15. ## Key design decision (to confirm)
16. Two viable representations:
17. 1) **Trait objects + opaque payload** (recommended for minimal churn): registry remains `Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>`. Detection returns a `BlockMatch` containing a parser index + interrupt/blank requirements + `Box<dyn Any>` payload; emission downcasts payload.
18. 2) **Enum registry + typed payload** (slightly faster/cleaner types, but larger refactor): registry becomes `Vec<BlockParserKind>` and match payload becomes an enum.
19. 
20. ## Workplan
21. 
22. ### A. Baseline & safety
23. - [x] Run baseline tests/benchmarks to capture current behavior (`cargo test` at minimum).
24. - [ ] Identify a small set of “high-signal” golden cases to watch while refactoring (fenced code, setext, yaml/hrule ambiguity, refdefs).
25. 
26. ### B. Introduce prepared-match API (non-invasive)
27. - [x] Add new types in `src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs`:
28.   - `PreparedBlockMatch` (parser index + detection + cached payload)
29. - [x] Extend `BlockParserRegistry` with new entrypoints (keep old API temporarily):
30.   - `detect_prepared(...) -> Option<PreparedBlockMatch>`
31.   - `parse_prepared(match, ...) -> usize`
32. 
33. ### C. Convert migrated parsers (start with biggest perf win)
34. - [x] Fenced code: detection parses fence + `InfoString` once; parse reuses cached `FenceInfo`.
35. - [x] ATX heading: detection returns cached level; parse uses it (no second `try_parse_atx_heading`).
36. - [ ] YAML: optionally cache “is yaml not hrule” disambiguation.
37. - [ ] Figure: cache validation span from inline image parse so it isn’t repeated.
38. - [x] Setext: remove allocation in detection (no `Vec`).
39. - [x] Reference definition: cache parse tuple from `try_parse_reference_definition` (payload currently unused by emitter).
40. 
41. ### D. Refactor `core.rs` to “single detect per line”
42. - [ ] Create one `BlockContext` per line and reuse it (avoid repeated rebuilds).
43. - [x] Replace the multiple dispatcher detections with one flow per line (detect once, then prepare/emit).
44. - [x] Rework the Definition continuation guard (`is_block_element` at ~1041) to delegate to the registry for migrated blocks (keeps special-cases for def/list/bq/html/tex).
45. 
46. ### E. Consolidate ordering/precedence in the registry
47. - [ ] Move remaining “block start” checks from `core.rs` into the registry progressively (tables/divs/html/latex/etc.), keeping Pandoc ordering notes.
48. - [ ] Ensure special-case precedence rules stay correct (e.g., YAML vs hrule, setext vs hrule, list vs header edge cases).
49. 
50. ### F. Cleanup
51. - [ ] Remove old `can_parse/parse` API once all migrated blocks use prepared matches.
52. - [ ] Remove redundant detection logic left in `core.rs`.
53. - [ ] Run full test suite + clippy + fmt check.
54. 
55. ## Notes / Constraints
56. - Maintain single-pass, lossless CST emission; prepared matches must not emit to the builder.
57. - Prepared payload should avoid borrowing `ctx.content` directly unless lifetimes are carefully managed; prefer storing indices / owned small strings only when needed.
58. - Performance expectation: dynamic dispatch/downcast overhead is negligible vs string scanning/allocs; eliminating repeated `InfoString::parse` etc. is the main win.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 75m 56s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Got it working again and kept the “single detect per line” architecture intact.

- Fixed the `parse_prepared` call to pass the `PreparedBlockMatch` by value (it was accidentally being passed by `&` in one branch).
- Restored correct precedence for footnotes over dispatcher-based reference definitions by explicitly nulling `dispatcher_match` when `try_parse_footnote_marker(content)` matches.
- Resolved the borrow-checker failures by removing `&self.containers` from `BlockContext` (it created a long-lived immutable borrow that prevented `prepare_for_block_element()` / container mutations); tests now pass.

Validation: `cargo test -q` is green (all suites passing).

---

276. /// YAML metadata block parser (--- ... ---/...)
277. pub(crate) struct YamlMetadataParser;
278. 
279. impl BlockParser for YamlMetadataParser {
280.     fn can_parse(
281.         &self,
282.         ctx: &BlockContext,
283.         lines: &[&str],
284.         line_pos: usize,
285.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
286.         // Must be at top level (not inside blockquotes)
287.         if ctx.blockquote_depth > 0 {
288.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
289.         }
290. 
291.         // Must start with ---
292.         if ctx.content.trim() != "---" {
293.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
294.         }
295. 
296.         // YAML needs blank line before OR be at document start
297.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
298.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
299.         }
300. 
301.         // Look ahead: next line must NOT be blank (to distinguish from horizontal rule)
302.         if line_pos + 1 < lines.len() {
303.             let next_line = lines[line_pos + 1];
304.             if next_line.trim().is_empty() {
305.                 // This is a horizontal rule, not YAML
306.                 return BlockDetectionResult::No;
307.             }
308.         } else {
309.             // No content after ---, can't be YAML
310.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
311.         }
312. 
313.         BlockDetectionResult::Yes
314.     }
315. 
316.     fn parse(
317.         &self,
318.         ctx: &BlockContext,
319.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
320.         lines: &[&str],
321.         line_pos: usize,
322.     ) -> usize {
323.         // Pass at_document_start to try_parse_yaml_block
324.         if let Some(new_pos) = try_parse_yaml_block(lines, line_pos, builder, ctx.at_document_start)
325.         {
326.             new_pos - line_pos // Return lines consumed
327.         } else {
328.             // Should not happen since can_parse returned Yes
329.             1 // Consume at least the opening line
330.         }
331.     }
332. 
333.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
334.         "yaml_metadata"
335.     }
336. }
337.

1. //! Figure parsing for standalone images.
2. //!
3. //! In Pandoc, a paragraph containing only an image (and optional attributes)
4. //! is treated as a Figure block element rather than a paragraph with inline image.
5. 
6. use crate::config::Config;
7. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
8. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
9. 
10. use crate::parser::inlines::links::try_parse_inline_image;
11. use crate::parser::utils::helpers;
12. use crate::parser::utils::inline_emission::emit_inlines;
13. 
14. /// Try to parse a line as a standalone figure (image).
15. ///
16. /// Uses the existing inline image parser to validate the syntax properly.
17. /// Returns true if the line contains only a valid image (possibly with attributes).
18. pub(in crate::parser) fn try_parse_figure(line: &str) -> bool {
19.     let trimmed = line.trim();
20. 
21.     // Must start with ![
22.     if !trimmed.starts_with("![") {
23.         return false;
24.     }
25. 
26.     // Use the inline parser's image validation to check if this is a valid image
27.     // This handles all the bracket/paren matching, escapes, etc.
28.     if let Some((len, _alt, _dest, _attrs)) = try_parse_inline_image(trimmed) {
29.         // Check that the image spans the entire line (except trailing whitespace)
30.         // After the image, only whitespace should remain
31.         let after_image = &trimmed[len..];
32.         after_image.trim().is_empty()
33.     } else {
34.         false
35.     }
36. }
37. 
38. /// Parse a figure block (standalone image).
39. ///
40. /// Emits inline-parsed structure directly during block parsing.
41. pub(in crate::parser) fn parse_figure(
42.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
43.     line: &str,
44.     config: &Config,
45. ) {
46.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::FIGURE.into());
47. 
48.     // Split off trailing newline
49.     let (text_without_newline, newline_str) = helpers::strip_newline(line);
50. 
51.     // Parse inline content (IMAGE_LINK) directly
52.     if !text_without_newline.is_empty() {
53.         emit_inlines(builder, text_without_newline, config);
54.     }
55. 
56.     if !newline_str.is_empty() {
57.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
58.     }
59. 
60.     builder.finish_node(); // Close Figure
61. }
62. 
63. #[cfg(test)]
64. mod tests {
65.     use super::*;
66. 
67.     #[test]
68.     fn test_try_parse_figure_starts_with_image() {
69.         assert!(try_parse_figure("![alt text](image.png)"));
70.         assert!(try_parse_figure("  ![alt text](image.png)  "));
71.         assert!(try_parse_figure("![alt text](image.png)\n"));
72.         assert!(try_parse_figure("![](image.png)"));
73.         assert!(try_parse_figure("![alt text](image.png \"Title\")"));
74.     }
75. 
76.     #[test]
77.     fn test_try_parse_figure_not_a_figure() {
78.         // Has text before the image
79.         assert!(!try_parse_figure("Text before ![alt](img.png)"));
80. 
81.         // Not an image (regular link)
82.         assert!(!try_parse_figure("[text](url)"));
83. 
84.         // Empty or other content
85.         assert!(!try_parse_figure(""));
86.         assert!(!try_parse_figure("# Heading"));
87.     }
88. }
```

</details>

1. //! ATX heading parsing utilities.
2. 
3. use crate::config::Config;
4. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
5. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
6. 
7. use crate::parser::utils::attributes::{emit_attributes, try_parse_trailing_attributes};
8. use crate::parser::utils::inline_emission;
9. 
10. /// Try to parse an ATX heading from content, returns heading level (1-6) if found.
11. pub(crate) fn try_parse_atx_heading(content: &str) -> Option<usize> {
12.     let trimmed = content.trim_start();
13. 
14.     // Must start with 1-6 # characters
15.     let hash_count = trimmed.chars().take_while(|&c| c == '#').count();
16.     if hash_count == 0 || hash_count > 6 {
17.         return None;
18.     }
19. 
20.     // After hashes, must be end of line, space, or tab
21.     let after_hashes = &trimmed[hash_count..];
22.     if !after_hashes.is_empty() && !after_hashes.starts_with(' ') && !after_hashes.starts_with('\t')
23.     {
24.         return None;
25.     }
26. 
27.     // Check leading spaces (max 3)
28.     let leading_spaces = content.len() - trimmed.len();
29.     if leading_spaces > 3 {
30.         return None;
31.     }
32. 
33.     Some(hash_count)
34. }
35. 
36. /// Try to parse a setext heading from lines, returns (level, underline_char) if found.
37. ///
38. /// Setext headings consist of:
39. /// 1. A non-empty text line (heading content)
40. /// 2. An underline of `=` (level 1) or `-` (level 2) characters
41. ///
42. /// Rules:
43. /// - Underline must be at least 3 characters long
44. /// - Underline can have leading/trailing spaces (up to 3 leading spaces)
45. /// - All underline characters must be the same (`=` or `-`)
46. /// - Text line cannot be indented 4+ spaces (would be code block)
47. /// - Text line cannot be empty/blank
48. pub(crate) fn try_parse_setext_heading(lines: &[&str], pos: usize) -> Option<(usize, char)> {
49.     // Need current line (text) and next line (underline)
50.     if pos >= lines.len() {
51.         return None;
52.     }
53. 
54.     let text_line = lines[pos];
55.     let next_pos = pos + 1;
56.     if next_pos >= lines.len() {
57.         return None;
58.     }
59. 
60.     let underline = lines[next_pos];
61. 
62.     // Text line cannot be empty or blank
63.     if text_line.trim().is_empty() {
64.         return None;
65.     }
66. 
67.     // Text line cannot be indented 4+ spaces (would be code block)
68.     let leading_spaces = text_line.len() - text_line.trim_start().len();
69.     if leading_spaces >= 4 {
70.         return None;
71.     }
72. 
73.     // Check if underline is valid
74.     let underline_trimmed = underline.trim();
75. 
76.     // Must be at least 3 characters
77.     if underline_trimmed.len() < 3 {
78.         return None;
79.     }
80. 
81.     // Determine underline character and check consistency
82.     let first_char = underline_trimmed.chars().next()?;
83.     if first_char != '=' && first_char != '-' {
84.         return None;
85.     }
86. 
87.     // All characters must be the same
88.     if !underline_trimmed.chars().all(|c| c == first_char) {
89.         return None;
90.     }
91. 
92.     // Leading spaces in underline (max 3 for consistency with other block rules)
93.     let underline_leading_spaces = underline.len() - underline.trim_start().len();
94.     if underline_leading_spaces >= 4 {
95.         return None;
96.     }
97. 
98.     // Determine level: '=' is level 1, '-' is level 2
99.     let level = if first_char == '=' { 1 } else { 2 };
100. 
101.     Some((level, first_char))
102. }
103. 
104. /// Emit a setext heading node to the builder.
105. ///
106. /// Setext headings consist of a text line followed by an underline.
107. /// This function emits the complete HEADING node with both lines.
108. pub(crate) fn emit_setext_heading(
109.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
110.     text_line: &str,
111.     underline_line: &str,
112.     _level: usize,
113.     config: &Config,
114. ) {
115.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::HEADING.into());
116. 
117.     // Strip trailing newline from text line for processing
118.     let (text_without_newline, text_newline_str) =
119.         if let Some(stripped) = text_line.strip_suffix("\r\n") {
120.             (stripped, "\r\n")
```

823.     fn compute_levels_to_keep(&self, next_line: &str) -> usize {

<sub>⏱️ 81m 18s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Next chunk done: I pushed more “is this nested block structure?” logic through the registry so `core.rs` keeps shrinking.

- In `compute_levels_to_keep()` (Definition continuation after blank lines), I removed the hardcoded `try_parse_fence_open` / `try_parse_div_fence_open` / `try_parse_horizontal_rule` checks and replaced them with a registry `detect_prepared(...)` probe.
- Cleaned up now-unused imports in `core.rs`.
- `cargo test -q` still fully green.

Want me to do the next obvious win: switch that probe to a small dedicated `BlockContext` helper (so we don’t rebuild it inline), or start migrating another complicated block into prepared payloads (YAML disambiguation or Figure caching)?

818.     /// Compute how many container levels to keep open based on next line content.
819.     fn compute_levels_to_keep(&self, next_line: &str) -> usize {
820.         let (next_bq_depth, next_inner) = count_blockquote_markers(next_line);
821.         let (raw_indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(next_inner);
822.         let next_marker = try_parse_list_marker(next_inner, self.config);
823. 
824.         // Calculate current blockquote depth for proper indent calculation
825.         let current_bq_depth = self.current_blockquote_depth();
826. 
827.         log::debug!(
828.             "compute_levels_to_keep: next_line indent={}, has_marker={}, stack_depth={}, current_bq={}, next_bq={}",
829.             raw_indent_cols,
830.             next_marker.is_some(),
831.             self.containers.depth(),
832.             current_bq_depth,
833.             next_bq_depth
834.         );
835. 
836.         let mut keep_level = 0;
837.         let mut content_indent_so_far = 0usize;
838. 
839.         // First, account for blockquotes
840.         for (i, c) in self.containers.stack.iter().enumerate() {
841.             match c {
842.                 Container::BlockQuote { .. } => {
843.                     // Count blockquotes up to this point
844.                     let bq_count = self.containers.stack[..=i]
845.                         .iter()
846.                         .filter(|x| matches!(x, Container::BlockQuote { .. }))
847.                         .count();
848.                     if bq_count <= next_bq_depth {
849.                         keep_level = i + 1;
850.                     }
851.                 }
852.                 Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col, .. } => {
853.                     // Track footnote indent for nested containers
854.                     content_indent_so_far += *content_col;
855.                     // Footnote continuation: line must be indented at least 4 spaces
856.                     // (or at the content column if content started after marker)
857.                     let min_indent = (*content_col).max(4);
858.                     if raw_indent_cols >= min_indent {
859.                         keep_level = i + 1;
860.                     }
861.                 }
862.                 Container::Definition { content_col, .. } => {
863.                     // Definition continuation: line must be indented at least 4 spaces
864.                     // After a blank line, only keep if there's nested block content (lists, code, etc)
865.                     // Plain text after blank line should close the definition
866.                     let min_indent = (*content_col).max(4);
867.                     if raw_indent_cols >= min_indent {
868.                         // Check what kind of content this is
869.                         let after_content_indent = if raw_indent_cols >= content_indent_so_far {
870.                             let idx = byte_index_at_column(next_line, content_indent_so_far);
871.                             &next_line[idx..]
872.                         } else {
873.                             next_line
874.                         };
875. 
876.                         // Keep Definition if there's a definition marker or nested block structure
877.                         let has_definition_marker =
878.                             try_parse_definition_marker(after_content_indent).is_some();
879.                         let has_list_marker =
880.                             try_parse_list_marker(after_content_indent, self.config).is_some();
881.                         let has_block_structure = has_list_marker
882.                             || count_blockquote_markers(after_content_indent).0 > 0
883.                             || {
884.                                 let block_ctx = BlockContext {
885.                                     content: after_content_indent,
886.                                     has_blank_before: true,
887.                                     at_document_start: false,
888.                                     blockquote_depth: self.current_blockquote_depth(),
889.                                     config: self.config,
890.                                     content_indent: 0,
891.                                     list_indent_info: None,
892.                                     // Avoid setext heading detection here; we only care about
893.                                     // marker-like nested block structure after blank lines.
894.                                     next_line: None,
895.                                 };
896.                                 self.block_registry
897.                                     .detect_prepared(&block_ctx, &[], 0)
898.                                     .is_some()
899.                             };
900. 
901.                         if !has_definition_marker && has_block_structure {
902.                             // Keep Definition for nested block content
903.                             keep_level = i + 1;
904.                         }
905.                         // Otherwise let Definition close (either new definition or plain text)
906.                     }
907.                 }
908.                 Container::List {
909.                     marker,
910.                     base_indent_cols,
911.                     ..
912.                 } => {
913.                     // Adjust indent for footnote context
914.                     let effective_indent = raw_indent_cols.saturating_sub(content_indent_so_far);
915.                     let continues_list = if let Some((ref nm, _, _)) = next_marker {
916.                         markers_match(marker, nm) && effective_indent <= base_indent_cols + 3
917.                     } else {
918.                         // For non-list-marker lines, must be indented past list content
919.                         let item_content_col = self
920.                             .containers
921.                             .stack
922.                             .get(i + 1)

924.                                 Container::ListItem { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
925.                                 _ => None,
926.                             })
927.                             // If no list item, require at least 1 space indent to continue list
928.                             .unwrap_or(1);
929.                         effective_indent >= item_content_col
930.                     };
931.                     if continues_list {
932.                         keep_level = i + 1;
933.                     }
934.                 }
935.                 Container::ListItem { content_col, .. } => {
936.                     // Keep list item if next line is indented to content column
937.                     // BUT NOT if it's a new list item marker at an outer level
938.

/home/jola/projects/panache/src/parser/blocks/reference_links.rs (lines 1-220)

<details>
<summary>220 lines</summary>

````
1. //! Reference definition and footnote parsing functions.
2. //!
3. //! Reference definitions have the form:
4. //! ```markdown
5. //! [label]: url "optional title"
6. //! [label]: url 'optional title'
7. //! [label]: url (optional title)
8. //! [label]: <url> "title"
9. //! ```
10. //!
11. //! Footnote definitions have the form:
12. //! ```markdown
13. //! [^id]: Footnote content here.
14. //!     Can continue on multiple lines
15. //!     as long as they're indented.
16. //! ```
17. 
18. /// Try to parse a reference definition starting at the current position.
19. /// Returns Some((length, label, url, title)) if successful.
20. ///
21. /// Syntax:
22. /// ```markdown
23. /// [label]: url "title"
24. /// [label]: <url> 'title'
25. /// [label]: url
26. ///          (title on next line)
27. /// ```
28. pub fn try_parse_reference_definition(
29.     text: &str,
30. ) -> Option<(usize, String, String, Option<String>)> {
31.     let bytes = text.as_bytes();
32. 
33.     // Must start at beginning of line with [
34.     if bytes.is_empty() || bytes[0] != b'[' {
35.         return None;
36.     }
37. 
38.     // Check if it's a footnote definition [^id]: - not a reference definition
39.     if bytes.len() >= 2 && bytes[1] == b'^' {
40.         return None;
41.     }
42. 
43.     // Find the closing ] for the label
44.     let mut pos = 1;
45.     let mut escape_next = false;
46. 
47.     while pos < bytes.len() {
48.         if escape_next {
49.             escape_next = false;
50.             pos += 1;
51.             continue;
52.         }
53. 
54.         match bytes[pos] {
55.             b'\\' => {
56.                 escape_next = true;
57.                 pos += 1;
58.             }
59.             b']' => {
60.                 break;
61.             }
62.             b'\n' => {
63.                 // Labels can't span lines
64.                 return None;
65.             }
66.             _ => {
67.                 pos += 1;
68.             }
69.         }
70.     }
71. 
72.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b']' {
73.         return None;
74.     }
75. 
76.     let label = &text[1..pos];
77.     if label.is_empty() {
78.         return None;
79.     }
80. 
81.     pos += 1; // Skip ]
82. 
83.     // Must be followed by :
84.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b':' {
85.         return None;
86.     }
87.     pos += 1;
88. 
89.     // Skip whitespace
90.     while pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[pos], b' ' | b'\t') {
91.         pos += 1;
92.     }
93. 
94.     // Parse URL
95.     let url_start = pos;
96.     let url_end;
97. 
98.     // Check for angle-bracketed URL <url>
99.     if pos < bytes.len() && bytes[pos] == b'<' {
100.         pos += 1;
101.         let url_content_start = pos;
102.         // Find closing >
103.         while pos < bytes.len() && bytes[pos] != b'>' && bytes[pos] != b'\n' && bytes[pos] != b'\r'
104.         {
105.             pos += 1;
106.         }
107.         if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b'>' {
108.             return None;
109.         }
110.         url_end = pos;
111.         let url = text[url_content_start..url_end].to_string();
112.         pos += 1; // Skip >
113. 
114.         // Parse optional title
115.         let title = parse_title(text, bytes, &mut pos)?;
116. 
117.         Some((pos, label.to_string(), url, title))
118.     } else {
119.         // Parse unbracketed URL (until whitespace or newline)
120.         while pos < bytes.len() && !matches!(bytes[pos], b' ' | b'\t' | b'\n' | b'\r') {
121.             pos += 1;
122.         }
123. 
124.         url_end = pos;
125.         if url_start == url_end {
126.             return None; // No URL found
127.         }
128. 
129.         let url = text[url_start..url_end].to_string();
130. 
131.         // Parse optional title
132.         let title = parse_title(text, bytes, &mut pos)?;
133. 
134.         Some((pos, label.to_string(), url, title))
135.     }
136. }
137. 
138. /// Parse an optional title after the URL.
139. /// Titles can be in double quotes, single quotes, or parentheses.
140. /// Returns Some(Some(title)) if title found, Some(None) if no title, None if malformed.
141. fn parse_title(text: &str, bytes: &[u8], pos: &mut usize) -> Option<Option<String>> {
142.     let base_pos = *pos;
143. 
144.     // Skip whitespace (including newlines for multi-line titles)
145.     while *pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[*pos], b' ' | b'\t' | b'\n' | b'\r') {
146.         *pos += 1;
147.     }
148. 
149.     // Check if there's a title
150.     if *pos >= bytes.len() {
151.         return Some(None);
152.     }
153. 
154.     let quote_char = bytes[*pos];
155.     if !matches!(quote_char, b'"' | b'\'' | b'(') {
156.         // No title, that's okay
157.         *pos = base_pos; // Reset position
158.         return Some(None);
159.     }
160. 
161.     let closing_char = if quote_char == b'(' { b')' } else { quote_char };
162. 
163.     *pos += 1; // Skip opening quote
164.     let title_start = *pos;
165. 
166.     // Find closing quote
167.     let mut escape_next = false;
168.     while *pos < bytes.len() {
169.         if escape_next {
170.             escape_next = false;
171.             *pos += 1;
172.             continue;
173.         }
174. 
175.         match bytes[*pos] {
176.             b'\\' => {
177.                 escape_next = true;
178.                 *pos += 1;
179.             }
180.             c if c == closing_char => {
181.                 let title_end = *pos;
182.                 *pos += 1; // Skip closing quote
183. 
184.                 // Skip trailing whitespace to end of line
185.                 while *pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[*pos], b' ' | b'\t') {
186.                     *pos += 1;
187.                 }
188. 
189.                 // Extract title from the original text using correct indices
190.                 let title = text[title_start..title_end].to_string();
191.                 return Some(Some(title));
192.             }
193.             b'\n' if quote_char == b'(' => {
194.                 // Parenthetical titles can span lines
195.                 *pos += 1;
196.             }
197.             _ => {
198.                 *pos += 1;
199.             }
200.         }
201.     }
202. 
203.     // No closing quote found
204.     None
205. }
206. 
207. /// Try to parse just the footnote marker [^id]: from a line.
208. /// Returns Some((id, content_start_col)) if the line starts with a footnote marker.
209. ///
210. /// Syntax:
211. /// ```markdown
212. /// [^id]: Footnote content.
213. /// ```
214. pub fn try_parse_footnote_marker(line: &str) -> Option<(String, usize)> {
215.     let bytes = line.as_bytes();
216. 
217.     // Must start with [^
218.     if bytes.len() < 4 || bytes[0] != b'[' || bytes[1] != b'^' {
219.         return None;
220.     }
````

</details>

1. //! Parsing for links, images, and automatic links.
2. //!
3. //! Implements:
4. //! - Automatic links: `<http://example.com>` and `<user@example.com>`
5. //! - Inline links: `[text](url)` and `[text](url "title")`
6. //! - Link attributes: `[text](url){#id .class key=value}`
7. //! - Inline images: `![alt](url)` and `![alt](url "title")`
8. //! - Image attributes: `![alt](url){#id .class key=value}`
9. //! - Reference links: `[text][ref]`, `[text][]`, `[text]`
10. //! - Reference images: `![alt][ref]`, `![alt][]`, `![alt]`
11. 
12. use super::core::parse_inline_text;
13. use crate::config::Config;
14. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
15. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
16. 
17. // Import attribute parsing
18. use crate::parser::utils::attributes::try_parse_trailing_attributes;
19. 
20. /// Try to parse an inline image starting at the current position.
21. ///
22. /// Inline images have the form `![alt](url)` or `![alt](url "title")`.
23. /// Can also have trailing attributes: `![alt](url){#id .class}`.
24. /// Returns Some((length, alt_text, dest_content, raw_attributes)) if a valid image is found.
25. pub fn try_parse_inline_image(text: &str) -> Option<(usize, &str, &str, Option<&str>)> {
26.     if !text.starts_with("![") {
27.         return None;
28.     }
29. 
30.     // Find the closing ]
31.     let mut bracket_depth = 0;
32.     let mut escape_next = false;
33.     let mut close_bracket_pos = None;
34. 
35.     for (i, ch) in text[2..].char_indices() {
36.         if escape_next {
37.             escape_next = false;
38.             continue;
39.         }
40. 
41.         match ch {
42.             '\\' => escape_next = true,
43.             '[' => bracket_depth += 1,
44.             ']' => {
45.                 if bracket_depth == 0 {
46.                     close_bracket_pos = Some(i + 2);
47.                     break;
48.                 }
49.                 bracket_depth -= 1;
50.             }
51.             _ => {}
52.         }
53.     }
54. 
55.     let close_bracket = close_bracket_pos?;
56.     let alt_text = &text[2..close_bracket];
57. 
58.     // Check for immediate ( after ]
59.     let after_bracket = close_bracket + 1;
60.     if text.len() <= after_bracket || !text[after_bracket..].starts_with('(') {
61.         return None;
62.     }
63. 
64.     // Find closing ) for destination (reuse same logic as links)
65.     let dest_start = after_bracket + 1;
66.     let remaining = &text[dest_start..];
67. 
68.     let mut paren_depth = 0;
69.     let mut escape_next = false;
70.     let mut in_quotes = false;
71.     let mut close_paren_pos = None;
72. 
73.     for (i, ch) in remaining.char_indices() {
74.         if escape_next {
75.             escape_next = false;
76.             continue;
77.         }
78. 
79.         match ch {
80.             '\\' => escape_next = true,
```

1. //! Figure parsing for standalone images.
2. //!
3. //! In Pandoc, a paragraph containing only an image (and optional attributes)
4. //! is treated as a Figure block element rather than a paragraph with inline image.
5. 
6. use crate::config::Config;
7. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
8. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
9. 
10. use crate::parser::inlines::links::try_parse_inline_image;
11. use crate::parser::utils::helpers;
12. use crate::parser::utils::inline_emission::emit_inlines;
13. 
14. /// Try to parse a line as a standalone figure (image).
15. ///
16. /// Uses the existing inline image parser to validate the syntax properly.
17. /// Returns true if the line contains only a valid image (possibly with attributes).
18. pub(in crate::parser) fn try_parse_figure(line: &str) -> bool {
19.     let trimmed = line.trim();
20. 
21.     // Must start with ![
22.     if !trimmed.starts_with("![") {
23.         return false;
24.     }
25. 
26.     // Use the inline parser's image validation to check if this is a valid image
27.     // This handles all the bracket/paren matching, escapes, etc.
28.     if let Some((len, _alt, _dest, _attrs)) = try_parse_inline_image(trimmed) {
29.         // Check that the image spans the entire line (except trailing whitespace)
30.         // After the image, only whitespace should remain
31.         let after_image = &trimmed[len..];
32.         after_image.trim().is_empty()
33.     } else {
34.         false
35.     }
36. }
37. 
38. /// Parse a figure block (standalone image).
39. ///
40. /// Emits inline-parsed structure directly during block parsing.
41. pub(in crate::parser) fn parse_figure(
42.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
43.     line: &str,
44.     config: &Config,
45. ) {
46.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::FIGURE.into());
47. 
48.     // Split off trailing newline
49.     let (text_without_newline, newline_str) = helpers::strip_newline(line);
50. 
51.     // Parse inline content (IMAGE_LINK) directly
52.     if !text_without_newline.is_empty() {
53.         emit_inlines(builder, text_without_newline, config);
54.     }
55. 
56.     if !newline_str.is_empty() {
57.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
58.     }
59. 
60.     builder.finish_node(); // Close Figure
61. }
62. 
63. #[cfg(test)]
64. mod tests {
65.     use super::*;
66. 
67.     #[test]
68.     fn test_try_parse_figure_starts_with_image() {
69.         assert!(try_parse_figure("![alt text](image.png)"));
70.         assert!(try_parse_figure("  ![alt text](image.png)  "));
```

</details>

1. //! Figure parsing for standalone images.
2. //!
3. //! In Pandoc, a paragraph containing only an image (and optional attributes)
4. //! is treated as a Figure block element rather than a paragraph with inline image.
5. 
6. use crate::config::Config;
7. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
8. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
9. 
10. use crate::parser::utils::helpers;
11. use crate::parser::utils::inline_emission::emit_inlines;
12. 
13. 
14. /// Parse a figure block (standalone image).
15. ///
16. /// Emits inline-parsed structure directly during block parsing.
17. pub(in crate::parser) fn parse_figure(
18.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
19.     line: &str,
20.     config: &Config,
21. ) {
22.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::FIGURE.into());
23. 
24.     // Split off trailing newline
25.     let (text_without_newline, newline_str) = helpers::strip_newline(line);
26. 
27.     // Parse inline content (IMAGE_LINK) directly
28.     if !text_without_newline.is_empty() {
29.         emit_inlines(builder, text_without_newline, config);
30.     }
31. 
32.     if !newline_str.is_empty() {
33.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
34.     }
35. 
36.     builder.finish_node(); // Close Figure
37. }
38. 
39. #[cfg(test)]
40. mod tests {
41.     use super::*;
42. 
43. }
```

<sub>⏱️ 87m 31s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Kept going and took the next high-impact win: **Figure detection is now “prepared” and caches the expensive inline-image validation**.

- `FigureParser` now implements `detect_prepared()` using `try_parse_inline_image(...)` once, and returns a payload (currently the parsed length) so we don’t re-validate later.
- Removed the now-unused `try_parse_figure()` helper (and its tests) from `src/parser/blocks/figures.rs` to avoid dead code.
- `cargo test -q` is green (now 837 tests in that first suite, because those removed unit tests were part of it).

Next obvious win after this: do the same prepared caching for **YAML metadata disambiguation** (avoid re-checking and make the YAML-vs-hrule decision a cached payload).

1. //! YAML metadata block parsing utilities.
2. 
3. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
4. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
5. 
6. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::{emit_line_tokens, strip_newline};
7. 
8. /// Try to parse a YAML metadata block starting at the given position.
9. /// Returns the new position after the block if successful, None otherwise.
10. ///
11. /// A YAML block:
12. /// - Starts with `---` (not followed by blank line)
13. /// - Ends with `---` or `...`
14. /// - At document start OR preceded by blank line
15. pub(crate) fn try_parse_yaml_block(
16.     lines: &[&str],
17.     pos: usize,
18.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
19.     at_document_start: bool,
20. ) -> Option<usize> {
21.     if pos >= lines.len() {
22.         return None;
23.     }
24. 
25.     let line = lines[pos];
26. 
27.     // Must start with ---
28.     if line.trim() != "---" {
29.         return None;
30.     }
31. 
32.     // If not at document start, previous line must be blank
33.     if !at_document_start && pos > 0 {
34.         let prev_line = lines[pos - 1];
35.         if !prev_line.trim().is_empty() {
36.             return None;
37.         }
38.     }
39. 
40.     // Check that next line (if exists) is NOT blank (this distinguishes from horizontal rule)
41.     if pos + 1 < lines.len() {
42.         let next_line = lines[pos + 1];
43.         if next_line.trim().is_empty() {
44.             // This is likely a horizontal rule, not YAML
45.             return None;
46.         }
47.     } else {
48.         // No content after ---, can't be a YAML block
49.         return None;
50.     }
51. 
52.     // Start metadata node
53.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::YAML_METADATA.into());
54. 
55.     // Opening delimiter - strip newline before emitting
56.     let (text, newline_str) = strip_newline(line);
57.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::YAML_METADATA_DELIM.into(), text.trim());
58.     if !newline_str.is_empty() {
59.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
60.     }
61. 
62.     let mut current_pos = pos + 1;
63.     let mut found_closing = false;
64. 
65.     // Collect content until we find closing delimiter
66.     while current_pos < lines.len() {
67.         let content_line = lines[current_pos];
68. 
69.         // Check for closing delimiter
70.         if content_line.trim() == "---" || content_line.trim() == "..." {
71.             found_closing = true;
72.             let (text, newline_str) = strip_newline(content_line);
73.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::YAML_METADATA_DELIM.into(), text.trim());
74.             if !newline_str.is_empty() {
75.                 builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
76.             }
77.             current_pos += 1;
78.             break;
79.         }
80. 
81.         // Add content line
82.         emit_line_tokens(builder, content_line);
83.         current_pos += 1;
84.     }
85. 
86.     builder.finish_node(); // YamlMetadata
87. 
88.     if found_closing {
89.         Some(current_pos)
90.     } else {
91.         // No closing delimiter found - this might be a horizontal rule after all
92.         // or malformed YAML. For now, accept it.
93.         Some(current_pos)
94.     }
95. }
96. 
97. /// Try to parse a Pandoc title block starting at the beginning of document.
98. /// Returns the new position after the block if successful, None otherwise.
99. ///
100. /// A Pandoc title block:
101. /// - Must be at document start (pos == 0)
102. /// - Has 1-3 lines starting with `%`
103. /// - Format: % title, % author(s), % date
104. /// - Continuation lines start with leading space
105. pub(crate) fn try_parse_pandoc_title_block(
106.     lines: &[&str],
107.     pos: usize,
108.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
109. ) -> Option<usize> {
110.     if pos != 0 || lines.is_empty() {
111.         return None;
112.     }
113. 
114.     let first_line = lines[0];
115.     if !first_line.trim_start().starts_with('%') {
116.         return None;
117.     }
118. 
119.     // Start title block node
120.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::PANDOC_TITLE_BLOCK.into());
121. 
122.     let mut current_pos = 0;
123.     let mut field_count = 0;
124. 
125.     // Parse up to 3 fields (title, author, date)
126.     while current_pos < lines.len() && field_count < 3 {
127.         let line = lines[current_pos];
128. 
129.         // Check if this line starts a field (begins with %)
130.         if line.trim_start().starts_with('%') {
131.             emit_line_tokens(builder, line);
132.             field_count += 1;
133.             current_pos += 1;
134. 
135.             // Collect continuation lines (start with leading space, not with %)
136.             while current_pos < lines.len() {
137.                 let cont_line = lines[current_pos];
138.                 if cont_line.is_empty() {
139.                     // Blank line ends title block
140.                     break;
141.                 }
142.                 if cont_line.trim_start().starts_with('%') {
143.                     // Next field
144.                     break;
145.                 }
146.                 if cont_line.starts_with(' ') || cont_line.starts_with('\t') {
147.                     // Continuation line
148.                     emit_line_tokens(builder, cont_line);
149.                     current_pos += 1;
150.                 } else {
151.                     // Non-continuation, non-% line ends title block
152.                     break;
153.                 }
154.             }
155.         } else {
156.             // Line doesn't start with %, title block ends
157.             break;
158.         }
159.     }
160. 
161.     builder.finish_node(); // PandocTitleBlock
162. 
163.     if field_count > 0 {
164.         Some(current_pos)
165.     } else {
166.         None
167.     }
168. }
169. 
170. #[cfg(test)]
171. mod tests {
172.     use super::*;
173. 
174.     #[test]
175.     fn test_yaml_block_at_start() {
176.         let lines = vec!["---", "title: Test", "---", "Content"];
177.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
178.         let result = try_parse_yaml_block(&lines, 0, &mut builder, true);
179.         assert_eq!(result, Some(3));
180.     }
181. 
182.     #[test]
183.     fn test_yaml_block_not_at_start() {
184.         let lines = vec!["Paragraph", "", "---", "title: Test", "---", "Content"];
185.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
186.         let result = try_parse_yaml_block(&lines, 2, &mut builder, false);
187.         assert_eq!(result, Some(5));
188.     }
189. 
190.     #[test]
191.     fn test_horizontal_rule_not_yaml() {
192.         let lines = vec!["---", "", "Content"];
193.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
194.         let result = try_parse_yaml_block(&lines, 0, &mut builder, true);
195.         assert_eq!(result, None); // Followed by blank line, so not YAML
196.     }
197. 
198.     #[test]
199.     fn test_yaml_with_dots_closer() {
200.         let lines = vec!["---", "title: Test", "...", "Content"];
201.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
202.         let result = try_parse_yaml_block(&lines, 0, &mut builder, true);
203.         assert_eq!(result, Some(3));
204.     }
205. 
206.     #[test]
207.     fn test_pandoc_title_simple() {
208.         let lines = vec!["% My Title", "% Author", "% Date", "", "Content"];
209.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
210.         let result = try_parse_pandoc_title_block(&lines, 0, &mut builder);
211.         assert_eq!(result, Some(3));
212.     }
213. 
214.     #[test]
215.     fn test_pandoc_title_with_continuation() {
216.         let lines = vec![
217.             "% My Title",
218.             "  on multiple lines",
219.             "% Author One",
220.             "  Author Two",
```

277. /// YAML metadata block parser (--- ... ---/...)
278. pub(crate) struct YamlMetadataParser;
279. 
280. impl BlockParser for YamlMetadataParser {
281.     fn can_parse(
282.         &self,
283.         ctx: &BlockContext,
284.         lines: &[&str],
285.         line_pos: usize,
286.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
287.         // Must be at top level (not inside blockquotes)
288.         if ctx.blockquote_depth > 0 {
289.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
290.         }
291. 
292.         // Must start with ---
293.         if ctx.content.trim() != "---" {
294.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
295.         }
296. 
297.         // YAML needs blank line before OR be at document start
298.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
299.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
300.         }
301. 
302.         // Look ahead: next line must NOT be blank (to distinguish from horizontal rule)
303.         if line_pos + 1 < lines.len() {
304.             let next_line = lines[line_pos + 1];
305.             if next_line.trim().is_empty() {
306.                 // This is a horizontal rule, not YAML
307.                 return BlockDetectionResult::No;
308.             }
309.         } else {
310.             // No content after ---, can't be YAML
311.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
312.         }
313. 
314.         BlockDetectionResult::Yes
315.     }
316. 
317.     fn parse(
318.         &self,
319.         ctx: &BlockContext,
320.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
321.         lines: &[&str],
322.         line_pos: usize,
323.     ) -> usize {
324.         // Pass at_document_start to try_parse_yaml_block
325.         if let Some(new_pos) = try_parse_yaml_block(lines, line_pos, builder, ctx.at_document_start)
326.         {
327.             new_pos - line_pos // Return lines consumed
328.         } else {
329.             // Should not happen since can_parse returned Yes
330.             1 // Consume at least the opening line
331.         }
332.     }
333. 
334.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
335.         "yaml_metadata"
336.     }
337. }
338. 
339. /// Figure parser (standalone image on its own line)
340. pub(crate) struct FigureParser;
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 88m 55s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Done: `YamlMetadataParser` now uses the prepared API so YAML-vs-hrule disambiguation (the “next line must not be blank” lookahead) only happens once.

- Implemented `detect_prepared()` for YAML and cached `at_document_start` as payload.
- Switched `parse()` to delegate to `parse_prepared()` and read the cached flag.
- `cargo test -q` is green.

Next best target is similar: **reference definitions** (we’re already caching the tuple, but emission still ignores it), or **div fences** (they tend to do non-trivial scanning).

413.     fn parse_prepared(
414.         &self,
415.         ctx: &BlockContext,
416.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
417.         lines: &[&str],
418.         line_pos: usize,
419.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,

421.         // If detection succeeded, we already validated that this is a standalone image.
422.         // Payload currently only caches the parsed length (future-proofing).
423.         let _len = payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<usize>().copied());
424. 
425.         let line = lines[line_pos];
426.         parse_figure(builder, line, ctx.config);
427.         1
428.     }
429. 
430.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
431.         "figure"
432.     }
433. }
434. 
435. /// Reference definition parser ([label]: url "title")
436. pub(crate) struct ReferenceDefinitionParser;
437. 
438. impl BlockParser for ReferenceDefinitionParser {
439.     fn can_parse(
440.         &self,
441.         ctx: &BlockContext,
442.         lines: &[&str],
443.         line_pos: usize,
444.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
445.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
446.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
447.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
448.     }
449. 
450.     fn detect_prepared(
451.         &self,
452.         ctx: &BlockContext,
453.         _lines: &[&str],
454.         _line_pos: usize,
455.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
456.         let parsed = try_parse_reference_definition(ctx.content)?;
457.         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, Some(Box::new(parsed))))
458.     }
459. 
460.     fn parse(
461.         &self,
462.         ctx: &BlockContext,
463.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
464.         lines: &[&str],
465.         line_pos: usize,
466.     ) -> usize {
467.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
468.     }
469. 
470.     fn parse_prepared(
471.         &self,
472.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
473.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
474.         lines: &[&str],
475.         line_pos: usize,
476.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
477.     ) -> usize {
478.         use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
479. 
480.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::REFERENCE_DEFINITION.into());
481. 
482.         let full_line = lines[line_pos];
483.         let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(full_line);
484. 
485.         // Currently we only cache that this *is* a refdef.
486.         // When we migrate refdefs fully, we can reuse `parsed` to emit URL/title structure too.
487.         let _parsed =
488.             payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<(usize, String, String, Option<String>)>());
489. 
490.         emit_reference_definition_content(builder, content_without_newline);
491. 
492.         if !line_ending.is_empty() {
493.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
494.         }
495. 
496.         builder.finish_node();
497. 
498.         1
499.     }
500. 
501.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
502.         "reference_definition"
503.     }
504. }
505.

506. /// Helper function to emit reference definition content with inline structure.
507. fn emit_reference_definition_content(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, text: &str) {
508.     use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
509. 
510.     if !text.starts_with('[') {
511.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
512.         return;
513.     }
514. 
515.     let rest = &text[1..];
516.     if let Some(close_pos) = rest.find(']') {
517.         let label = &rest[..close_pos];
518.         let after_bracket = &rest[close_pos + 1..];
519. 
520.         if after_bracket.starts_with(':') {
521.             // Emit LINK node with the label
522.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK.into());
523. 
524.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into());
525.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into(), "[");
526.             builder.finish_node();
527. 
528.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_TEXT.into());
529.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), label);
530.             builder.finish_node();
531. 
532.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "]");
533.             builder.finish_node(); // LINK
534. 
535.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), after_bracket);
536.             return;
537.         }
538.     }
539. 
540.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
541. }
542. 
543. /// Fenced code block parser (``` or ~~~)
544. pub(crate) struct FencedCodeBlockParser;
545. 
546. impl BlockParser for FencedCodeBlockParser {
547.     fn can_parse(
548.         &self,
549.         ctx: &BlockContext,
550.         _lines: &[&str],
551.         _line_pos: usize,
552.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
553.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, _lines, _line_pos)
554.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
555.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
556.     }
557. 
558.     fn detect_prepared(
559.         &self,
560.         ctx: &BlockContext,
561.         _lines: &[&str],
562.         _line_pos: usize,
563.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
564.         // Calculate content to check - may need to strip list indentation
565.         let content_to_check = if let Some(list_info) = ctx.list_indent_info {
566.             if list_info.content_col > 0 && !ctx.content.is_empty() {
567.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(ctx.content, list_info.content_col);
568.                 &ctx.content[idx..]
569.             } else {
570.                 ctx.content
571.             }
572.         } else {
573.             ctx.content
574.         };
575. 
576.         let fence = try_parse_fence_open(content_to_check)?;
577. 
578.         // Parse info string to determine block type (expensive, but now cached via fence)
579.         let info = InfoString::parse(&fence.info_string);
580. 
581.         let is_executable = matches!(info.block_type, CodeBlockType::Executable { .. });
582.         let is_pandoc_like = matches!(
583.             ctx.config.flavor,
584.             crate::config::Flavor::Pandoc
585.                 | crate::config::Flavor::CommonMark
586.                 | crate::config::Flavor::Gfm
587.         );
588.         if is_executable && is_pandoc_like {
589.             return None;
590.         }
591. 
592.         // Fenced code blocks can interrupt paragraphs only if they have an info string.
593.         let has_info = !fence.info_string.trim().is_empty();
594.         let detection = if has_info {
595.             BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
596.         } else if ctx.has_blank_before {
597.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
598.         } else {
599.             BlockDetectionResult::No
600.         };
601. 
602.         match detection {
603.             BlockDetectionResult::No => None,
604.             _ => Some((detection, Some(Box::new(fence)))),
605.         }
606.     }
607. 
608.     fn parse(
609.         &self,
610.         ctx: &BlockContext,
611.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
612.         lines: &[&str],
613.         line_pos: usize,
614.     ) -> usize {
615.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
616.     }
617. 
618.     fn parse_prepared(
619.         &self,
620.         ctx: &BlockContext,
````

1. //! Syntax kinds and language definition for the Quarto/Pandoc CST.
2. 
3. use rowan::Language;
4. 
5. #[allow(non_camel_case_types)]
6. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash)]
7. #[repr(u16)]
8. pub enum SyntaxKind {
9.     // Tokens
10.     WHITESPACE = 0,
11.     NEWLINE,
12.     TEXT,
13.     BACKSLASH,           // \ (for escaping)
14.     ESCAPED_CHAR,        // Any escaped character
15.     NONBREAKING_SPACE,   // \<space>
16.     HARD_LINE_BREAK,     // \<newline>
17.     DIV_MARKER,          // :::
18.     YAML_METADATA_DELIM, // --- or ... (for YAML blocks)
19.     BLOCKQUOTE_MARKER,   // >
20.     IMAGE_LINK_START,    // ![
21.     LIST_MARKER,         // - + *
22.     TASK_CHECKBOX,       // [ ] or [x] or [X]
23.     COMMENT_START,       // <!--
24.     COMMENT_END,         // -->
25.     ATTRIBUTE,           // {#label} for headings, math, etc.
26.     HORIZONTAL_RULE,     // --- or *** or ___
27.     BLANK_LINE,
28. 
29.     // Links and images
30.     LINK_START,           // [
31.     LINK,                 // [text](url)
32.     LINK_TEXT,            // text part of link
33.     LINK_TEXT_END,        // ] closing link text
34.     LINK_DEST_START,      // ( opening link destination
35.     LINK_DEST,            // (url) or (url "title")
36.     LINK_DEST_END,        // ) closing link destination
37.     LINK_REF,             // [ref] in reference links
38.     IMAGE_LINK,           // ![alt](url)
39.     IMAGE_ALT,            // alt text in image
40.     IMAGE_ALT_END,        // ] closing image alt
41.     IMAGE_DEST_START,     // ( opening image destination
42.     IMAGE_DEST_END,       // ) closing image destination
43.     AUTO_LINK,            // <http://example.com>
44.     AUTO_LINK_MARKER,     // < and >
45.     REFERENCE_DEFINITION, // [label]: url "title"
46.     FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION,  // [^id]: content
47.     FOOTNOTE_REFERENCE,   // [^id]
48.     REFERENCE_LABEL,      // [label] part
49.     REFERENCE_URL,        // url part
50.     REFERENCE_TITLE,      // "title" part
51. 
52.     // Math
53.     INLINE_MATH_MARKER,  // $
54.     DISPLAY_MATH_MARKER, // $$
55.     INLINE_MATH,
56.     DISPLAY_MATH,
57.     MATH_CONTENT,
58. 
59.     // Footnotes
60.     INLINE_FOOTNOTE_START, // ^[
61.     INLINE_FOOTNOTE_END,   // ]
62.     INLINE_FOOTNOTE,       // ^[text]
63. 
64.     // Citations
65.     CITATION,             // [@key] or @key
66.     CITATION_MARKER,      // @ or -@
67.     CITATION_KEY,         // The citation key identifier
68.     CITATION_BRACE_OPEN,  // { for complex keys
69.     CITATION_BRACE_CLOSE, // } for complex keys
70.     CITATION_CONTENT,     // Text content in bracketed citations
71.     CITATION_SEPARATOR,   // ; between multiple citations
72. 
73.     // Spans
74.     BRACKETED_SPAN,     // [text]{.class}
75.     SPAN_CONTENT,       // text inside span
76.     SPAN_ATTRIBUTES,    // {.class key="val"}
77.     SPAN_BRACKET_OPEN,  // [
78.     SPAN_BRACKET_CLOSE, // ]
79. 
80.     // Shortcodes (Quarto)
81.     SHORTCODE,              // {{< name args >}} or {{{< name args >}}}
82.     SHORTCODE_MARKER_OPEN,  // {{< or {{{<
83.     SHORTCODE_MARKER_CLOSE, // >}} or >}}}
84.     SHORTCODE_CONTENT,      // content between markers
85. 
86.     // Code
87.     CODE_SPAN,
88.     CODE_SPAN_MARKER,  // ` or `` or ```
89.     CODE_FENCE_MARKER, // ``` or ~~~
90.     CODE_BLOCK,
91. 
92.     // Raw inline spans
93.     RAW_INLINE,         // `content`{=format}
94.     RAW_INLINE_MARKER,  // ` markers
95.     RAW_INLINE_FORMAT,  // format name (html, latex, etc.)
96.     RAW_INLINE_CONTENT, // raw content
97. 
98.     // Inline emphasis and formatting
99.     EMPHASIS,           // *text* or _text_
100.     STRONG,             // **text** or __text__
101.     STRIKEOUT,          // ~~text~~
102.     SUPERSCRIPT,        // ^text^
103.     SUBSCRIPT,          // ~text~
104.     EMPHASIS_MARKER,    // * or _ (for emphasis)
105.     STRONG_MARKER,      // ** or __ (for strong)
106.     STRIKEOUT_MARKER,   // ~~ (for strikeout)
107.     SUPERSCRIPT_MARKER, // ^ (for superscript)
108.     SUBSCRIPT_MARKER,   // ~ (for subscript)
109. 
110.     // Composite nodes
111.     DOCUMENT,
112.     YAML_METADATA,
113.     PANDOC_TITLE_BLOCK,
114.     FENCED_DIV,
115.     PARAGRAPH,
116.     PLAIN, // Inline content without paragraph break (tight lists, definition lists, table cells)
117.     BLOCKQUOTE,
118.     LIST,
119.     LIST_ITEM,
120.     DEFINITION_LIST,
````

452.         ctx: &BlockContext,

460.     fn parse(
461.         &self,
462.         ctx: &BlockContext,
463.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
464.         lines: &[&str],
465.         line_pos: usize,
466.     ) -> usize {
467.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
468.     }
469. 
470.     fn parse_prepared(
471.         &self,
472.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
473.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
474.         lines: &[&str],
475.         line_pos: usize,
476.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
477.     ) -> usize {
478.         use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
479. 
480.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::REFERENCE_DEFINITION.into());
481. 
482.         let full_line = lines[line_pos];
483.         let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(full_line);
484. 
485.         let parsed = payload
486.             .and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<(usize, String, String, Option<String>)>())
487.             .or_else(|| try_parse_reference_definition(content_without_newline));
488. 
489.         if let Some((_len, label, url, title)) = parsed {
490.             emit_reference_definition_content_preparsed(
491.                 builder,
492.                 content_without_newline,
493.                 label,
494.                 url,
495.                 title.as_deref(),
496.             );
497.         } else {
498.             emit_reference_definition_content(builder, content_without_newline);
499.         }
500. 
501.         if !line_ending.is_empty() {
502.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
503.         }
504. 
505.         builder.finish_node();
506. 
507.         1
508.     }
509. 
510.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
511.         "reference_definition"
512.     }
513. }
514. 
515. /// Helper function to emit reference definition content with inline structure.
516. fn emit_reference_definition_content(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, text: &str) {
517.     use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
518. 
519.     if !text.starts_with('[') {
520.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
521.         return;
522.     }
523. 
524.     let rest = &text[1..];
525.     if let Some(close_pos) = rest.find(']') {
526.         let label = &rest[..close_pos];
527.         let after_bracket = &rest[close_pos + 1..];
528. 
529.         if after_bracket.starts_with(':') {
530.             // Emit LINK node with the label
531.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK.into());
532. 
533.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into());
534.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into(), "[");
535.             builder.finish_node();
536. 
537.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_TEXT.into());
538.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), label);
539.             builder.finish_node();
540. 
541.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "]");
542.             builder.finish_node(); // LINK
543. 
544.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), after_bracket);
545.             return;
546.         }
547.     }
548. 
549.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
550. }
551. 
552. fn emit_reference_definition_content_preparsed(
553.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
554.     full_text: &str,
555.     label: &str,
556.     url: &str,
557.     title: Option<&str>,
558. ) {
559.     use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
560. 
561.     // Emit the exact prefix up to the label.
562.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::REFERENCE_LABEL.into());
563.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into(), "[");
564.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), label);
565.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "]: ");
566.     builder.finish_node();
567. 
568.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::REFERENCE_URL.into());
569.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), url);
570.     builder.finish_node();
571. 
572.     if let Some(title) = title {
573.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::REFERENCE_TITLE.into());
574.         // Preserve original spacing/quoting by falling back to raw emission if we can't find it.
575.         if let Some(idx) = full_text.find(url).map(|i| i + url.len()) {
576.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), &full_text[idx..]);
577.         } else {
578.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), title);
579.         }
580.         builder.finish_node();
581.     }
582. 
583. /// Fenced code block parser (``` or ~~~)
584. pub(crate) struct FencedCodeBlockParser;
585. 
586. impl BlockParser for FencedCodeBlockParser {
587.     fn can_parse(
588.         &self,
589.         ctx: &BlockContext,
590.         _lines: &[&str],
591.         _line_pos: usize,
592.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
593.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, _lines, _line_pos)
594.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
595.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
596.     }
597. 
598.     fn detect_prepared(
599.         &self,
600.         ctx: &BlockContext,
601.         _lines: &[&str],
602.         _line_pos: usize,
603.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
604.         // Calculate content to check - may need to strip list indentation
605.         let content_to_check = if let Some(list_info) = ctx.list_indent_info {
606.             if list_info.content_col > 0 && !ctx.content.is_empty() {
607.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(ctx.content, list_info.content_col);
608.                 &ctx.content[idx..]
609.             } else {
610.                 ctx.content
611.             }
612.         } else {
613.             ctx.content
614.         };
615. 
616.         let fence = try_parse_fence_open(content_to_check)?;
617. 
618.         // Parse info string to determine block type (expensive, but now cached via fence)
619.         let info = InfoString::parse(&fence.info_string);
620.
````

<sub>⏱️ 92m 40s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’m going to keep reference definitions using the existing CST shape (so golden CST snapshots don’t churn), but ensure emission never re-parses when we already have the cached tuple.

         _lines: &[&str],

         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, Some(Box::new(parsed))))

+        debug_assert!(payload
+            .and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<(usize, String, String, Option<String>)>())
+            .is_some());
 
         emit_reference_definition_content(builder, content_without_newline);
 
@@ -540,6 +542,7 @@ fn emit_reference_definition_content(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, te

 pub(crate) struct FencedCodeBlockParser;

<sub>⏱️ 93m 19s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Done for reference definitions (without changing CST shape).

- `ReferenceDefinitionParser::detect_prepared()` already caches the parsed tuple from `try_parse_reference_definition(...)`; I made `parse_prepared()` *actually rely on that* by adding a `debug_assert!` that the payload is present (so we don’t silently regress to re-parsing later).
- Emission stays via the existing `emit_reference_definition_content(...)`, which matches your current golden CST (LINK node + trailing TEXT), so we don’t break `tests/cases/reference_links/*` snapshots.
- `cargo test -q` is green.

If you want the *next* step for refdefs, we can add an internal “fully-structured refdef emission” behind a flag and update the golden CSTs intentionally—but that’s a semantic tree change, not just a perf/duplication win.
