```
1. //! Fenced div parsing utilities.
2. 
3. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::strip_leading_spaces;
4. 
5. /// Information about a detected div fence opening.
6. pub(crate) struct DivFenceInfo {
7.     pub attributes: String,
8.     pub fence_count: usize,
9. }
10. 
11. /// Try to detect a fenced div opening from content.
12. /// Returns div fence info if this is a valid opening fence.
13. ///
14. /// Opening fences MUST have attributes (or the fences are treated as closing).
15. /// Format: `::: {.class #id}` or `::: classname` or `::::: {#id} :::::`
16. pub(crate) fn try_parse_div_fence_open(content: &str) -> Option<DivFenceInfo> {
17.     let trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(content);
18. 
19.     // Check for fence opening (:::)
20.     if !trimmed.starts_with(':') {
21.         return None;
22.     }
23. 
24.     let colon_count = trimmed.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ':').count();
25. 
26.     if colon_count < 3 {
27.         return None;
28.     }
29. 
30.     // Get the part after the colons
31.     let after_colons = trimmed[colon_count..].trim_start();
32. 
33.     // Check if there are attributes
34.     // Attributes can be:
35.     // 1. Curly braces: {.class #id key="value"}
36.     // 2. Single word (treated as class): classname
37.     // 3. Attributes followed by more colons (optional): {.class} :::
38. 
39.     let attributes = if after_colons.starts_with('{') {
40.         // Find the closing brace
41.         if let Some(close_idx) = after_colons.find('}') {
42.             after_colons[..=close_idx].to_string()
43.         } else {
44.             // Unclosed brace, not valid
45.             return None;
46.         }
47.     } else if after_colons.is_empty() {
48.         // No attributes, this is a closing fence
49.         return None;
50.     } else {
51.         // Single word or words until optional trailing colons
52.         let content_before_colons = after_colons.trim_end_matches(':').trim_end();
53. 
54.         if content_before_colons.is_empty() {
55.             // Only colons, no attributes
56.             return None;
57.         }
58. 
59.         // Take the first word as the class name
60.         content_before_colons.split_whitespace().next()?.to_string()
61.     };
62. 
63.     Some(DivFenceInfo {
64.         attributes,
65.         fence_count: colon_count,
66.     })
67. }
68. 
69. /// Check if a line is a valid closing fence for a div.
70. /// Closing fences have NO attributes and at least 3 colons.
71. pub(crate) fn is_div_closing_fence(content: &str) -> bool {
72.     let trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(content);
73. 
74.     if !trimmed.starts_with(':') {
75.         return false;
76.     }
77. 
78.     let colon_count = trimmed.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ':').count();
79. 
80.     if colon_count < 3 {
81.         return false;
82.     }
83. 
84.     // Rest of line must be empty (only colons are allowed)
85.     trimmed[colon_count..].trim().is_empty()
86. }
87. 
88. #[cfg(test)]
89. mod tests {
90.     use super::*;
91. 
92.     #[test]
93.     fn test_parse_div_fence_open_with_curly_braces() {
94.         let line = "::: {.callout-note}";
95.         let fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(line).unwrap();
96.         assert_eq!(fence.attributes, "{.callout-note}");
97.     }
98. 
99.     #[test]
100.     fn test_parse_div_fence_open_with_class_name() {
101.         let line = "::: Warning";
102.         let fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(line).unwrap();
103.         assert_eq!(fence.attributes, "Warning");
104.     }
105. 
106.     #[test]
107.     fn test_parse_div_fence_open_with_trailing_colons() {
108.         let line = "::::: {#special .sidebar} :::::";
109.         let fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(line).unwrap();
110.         assert_eq!(fence.attributes, "{#special .sidebar}");
111.     }
112. 
113.     #[test]
114.     fn test_closing_fence_no_attributes() {
115.         let line = ":::";
116.         assert!(try_parse_div_fence_open(line).is_none());
117.         assert!(is_div_closing_fence(line));
118.     }
119. 
120.     #[test]
121.     fn test_closing_fence_many_colons() {
122.         let line = "::::::::::::::";
123.         assert!(try_parse_div_fence_open(line).is_none());
124.         assert!(is_div_closing_fence(line));
125.     }
126. 
127.     #[test]
128.     fn test_not_a_fence_too_few_colons() {
129.         let line = ":: something";
130.         assert!(try_parse_div_fence_open(line).is_none());
131.         assert!(!is_div_closing_fence(line));
132.     }
133. }
```

</details>

1619.                 self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
1620.             }
1621.             self.builder.finish_node(); // DivFenceClose
1622. 
1623.             // Pop the FencedDiv container (this will finish the FencedDiv node)
1624.             self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1625.
```

</details>

300.     /// Otherwise emit it directly to the builder.
301.     fn emit_or_buffer_blockquote_marker(
302.         &mut self,
303.         leading_spaces: usize,
304.         has_trailing_space: bool,
305.     ) {
306.         // If paragraph is open, buffer the marker (it will be emitted at correct position)
307.         if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Paragraph { .. })) {
308.             // Buffer the marker in the paragraph
309.             paragraphs::append_paragraph_marker(
310.                 &mut self.containers,
311.                 leading_spaces,
312.                 has_trailing_space,
313.             );
314.         } else {
315.             // Emit directly
316.             blockquotes::emit_one_blockquote_marker(
317.                 &mut self.builder,
318.                 leading_spaces,
319.                 has_trailing_space,
320.             );
321.         }
322.     }
323. 
324.     fn parse_document_stack(&mut self) {
325.         self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DOCUMENT.into());
326. 
327.         log::debug!("Starting document parse");
328. 
329.         // Check for Pandoc title block at document start
330.         if self.pos == 0
331.             && !self.lines.is_empty()
332.             && let Some(new_pos) = try_parse_pandoc_title_block(&self.lines, 0, &mut self.builder)
333.         {
334.             self.pos = new_pos;
335.         }
336. 
337.         while self.pos < self.lines.len() {
338.             let line = self.lines[self.pos];
339. 
340.             log::debug!("Parsing line {}: {}", self.pos + 1, line);
341. 
342.             if self.parse_line(line) {
343.                 continue;
344.             }
345.             self.pos += 1;
346.         }
347. 
348.         self.close_containers_to(0);
349.         self.builder.finish_node(); // DOCUMENT
350.     }
351. 
352.     /// Returns true if the line was consumed.
353.     fn parse_line(&mut self, line: &str) -> bool {
354.         // Count blockquote markers on this line
355.         let (bq_depth, inner_content) = count_blockquote_markers(line);
356.         let current_bq_depth = self.current_blockquote_depth();
357. 
358.         log::debug!(
359.             "parse_line [{}]: bq_depth={}, current_bq={}, depth={}, line={:?}",
360.             self.pos,

682. 
683.     fn parse_prepared(
684.         &self,
685.         ctx: &BlockContext,
686.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
687.         lines: &[&str],
688.         line_pos: usize,
689.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
690.     ) -> usize {
691.         let list_indent_stripped = ctx.list_indent_info.map(|i| i.content_col).unwrap_or(0);
692. 
693.         let fence = if let Some(fence) = payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<FenceInfo>()) {
694.             fence.clone()
695.         } else {
696.             // Backward-compat: if called via legacy `parse()`, recompute.
697.             let content_to_check = if list_indent_stripped > 0 && !ctx.content.is_empty() {
698.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(ctx.content, list_indent_stripped);
699.                 &ctx.content[idx..]
700.             } else {
701.                 ctx.content
702.             };
703.             try_parse_fence_open(content_to_check).expect("Fence should exist")
704.         };
705. 
706.         // Calculate total indent: base content indent + list indent
707.         let total_indent = ctx.content_indent + list_indent_stripped;
708. 
709.         let new_pos = parse_fenced_code_block(
710.             builder,
711.             lines,
712.             line_pos,
713.             fence,
714.             ctx.blockquote_depth,
715.             total_indent,
716.         );
717. 
718.         new_pos - line_pos
719.     }
720. 
721.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
722.         "fenced_code_block"
723.     }
724. }
725. 
726. // ============================================================================
727. // Setext Heading Parser (position #3)
728. // ============================================================================
729. 
730. pub(crate) struct SetextHeadingParser;
731. 
732. impl BlockParser for SetextHeadingParser {
733.     fn can_parse(
734.         &self,
735.         ctx: &BlockContext,
736.         _lines: &[&str],
737.         _line_pos: usize,
738.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
739.         // Setext headings require blank line before (unless at document start)
740.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
741.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
742.         }
743. 
744.         // Need next line for lookahead
745.         let next_line = match ctx.next_line {
746.             Some(line) => line,
747.             None => return BlockDetectionResult::No,
748.         };
749. 
750.         // Create lines array for detection function (avoid allocation)
751.         let lines = [ctx.content, next_line];
752. 
753.         // Try to detect setext heading
754.         if try_parse_setext_heading(&lines, 0).is_some() {
755.             // Setext headings need blank line before (normal case)
756.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
757.         } else {
758.             BlockDetectionResult::No
759.         }
760.     }
761. 
762.     fn parse(
763.         &self,
764.         ctx: &BlockContext,
765.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
766.         lines: &[&str],
767.         pos: usize,
768.     ) -> usize {
769.         // Get text line and underline line
770.         let text_line = lines[pos];
771.         let underline_line = lines[pos + 1];
772. 
773.         // Determine level from underline character (no need to call try_parse again)
774.         // can_parse() already validated this is a valid setext heading
775.         let underline_char = underline_line.trim().chars().next().unwrap_or('=');
776.         let level = if underline_char == '=' { 1 } else { 2 };
777. 
778.         // Emit the setext heading
779.         emit_setext_heading(builder, text_line, underline_line, level, ctx.config);
780. 
781.         // Return lines consumed: text line + underline line
782.         2
783.     }
784. 
785.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
786.         "setext_heading"
787.     }
788. }
789. 
790. // ============================================================================
791. // Block Parser Registry
792. // ============================================================================
793. 
794. /// Registry of block parsers, ordered by priority.
795. ///
796. /// This dispatcher tries each parser in order until one succeeds.
797. /// The ordering follows Pandoc's approach - explicit list order rather
798. /// than numeric priorities.
799. pub(crate) struct BlockParserRegistry {
800.     parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>,
801. }
802. 
803. impl BlockParserRegistry {
804.     /// Create a new registry with all block parsers.
805.     ///
806.     /// Order matters! Parsers are tried in the order listed here.
807.     /// This follows Pandoc's design where ordering is explicit and documented.
808.     ///
809.     /// **Pandoc reference order** (from pandoc/src/Text/Pandoc/Readers/Markdown.hs:487-515):
810.     /// 1. blanklines (handled separately in our parser)
811.     /// 2. codeBlockFenced
812.     /// 3. yamlMetaBlock' ← YAML metadata comes early!
813.     /// 4. bulletList
814.     /// 5. divHtml
815.     /// 6. divFenced
816.     /// 7. header ← ATX headings
817.     /// 8. lhsCodeBlock
818.     /// 9. htmlBlock
819.     /// 10. table
820.     /// 11. codeBlockIndented
```

</details>

820.     /// 11. codeBlockIndented
821.     /// 12. rawTeXBlock (LaTeX)
822.     /// 13. lineBlock
823.     /// 14. blockQuote
824.     /// 15. hrule ← Horizontal rules come AFTER headers!
825.     /// 16. orderedList
826.     /// 17. definitionList
827.     /// 18. noteBlock (footnotes)
828.     /// 19. referenceKey ← Reference definitions
829.     /// 20. abbrevKey
830.     /// 21. para
831.     /// 22. plain
832.     pub fn new() -> Self {
833.         let parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>> = vec![
834.             // Match Pandoc's ordering to ensure correct precedence:
835.             // (2) Fenced code blocks - can interrupt paragraphs!
836.             Box::new(FencedCodeBlockParser),
837.             // (3) YAML metadata - before headers and hrules!
838.             Box::new(YamlMetadataParser),
839.             // (7) Setext headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
840.             // Must come before ATX to properly handle `---` disambiguation
841.             Box::new(SetextHeadingParser),
842.             // (7) ATX headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
843.             Box::new(AtxHeadingParser),
844.             // (15) Horizontal rules - AFTER headings per Pandoc
845.             Box::new(HorizontalRuleParser),
846.             // Figures (standalone images) - Pandoc doesn't have these
847.             Box::new(FigureParser),
848.             // (19) Reference definitions
849.             Box::new(ReferenceDefinitionParser),
850.             // TODO: Migrate remaining blocks in Pandoc order:
851.             // - (4-6) Lists and divs (bulletList, divHtml, divFenced)
852.             // - (9) HTML blocks
853.             // - (10) Tables (grid, multiline, pipe, simple)
854.             // - (11) Indented code blocks (AFTER fenced!)
855.             // - (12) LaTeX blocks (rawTeXBlock)
856.             // - (13) Line blocks
857.             // - (16) Ordered lists
858.             // - (17) Definition lists
859.             // - (18) Footnote definitions (noteBlock)
860.         ];
861. 
862.         Self { parsers }
863.     }
864. 
865.     /// Try to parse a block using the registered parsers.
866.     ///
867.     /// This method implements the two-phase parsing:
868.     /// 1. Detection: Check if any parser can handle this content
869.     /// 2. Caller prepares (closes paragraphs, flushes buffers)
870.     /// 3. Parser emits the block
871.     ///
872.     /// Returns (parser_index, detection_result) if a parser can handle this,
873.     /// or None if no parser matched.
874.     #[allow(dead_code)]
875.     pub fn detect(
876.         &self,
877.         ctx: &BlockContext,
878.         lines: &[&str],
879.         line_pos: usize,
880.     ) -> Option<(usize, BlockDetectionResult)> {
881.         for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
882.             let result = parser.can_parse(ctx, lines, line_pos);
883.             match result {
884.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes | BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
885.                     log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
886.                     return Some((i, result));
887.                 }
888.                 BlockDetectionResult::No => continue,
889.             }
890.         }
891.         None
892.     }
893. 
894.     /// Like `detect()`, but allows parsers to return cached payload for emission.
895.     pub fn detect_prepared(
896.         &self,
897.         ctx: &BlockContext,
898.         lines: &[&str],
899.         line_pos: usize,
900.     ) -> Option<PreparedBlockMatch> {

1. //! Block parser dispatcher for organizing block-level parsing.
2. //!
3. //! This module provides a trait-based abstraction for block parsers,
4. //! making it easier to add new block types and reducing duplication in parse_inner_content.
5. //!
6. //! Design principles:
7. //! - Single-pass parsing preserved (no backtracking)
8. //! - Each block parser operates independently
9. //! - Inline parsing still integrated (called from within block parsing)
10. //! - Maintains exact CST structure and losslessness
11. 
12. use crate::config::Config;
13. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
14. use std::any::Any;
15. 
16. use super::blocks::code_blocks::{
17.     CodeBlockType, FenceInfo, InfoString, parse_fenced_code_block, try_parse_fence_open,
18. };
19. use super::blocks::figures::parse_figure;
20. use super::blocks::headings::{
21.     emit_atx_heading, emit_setext_heading, try_parse_atx_heading, try_parse_setext_heading,
22. };
23. use super::blocks::horizontal_rules::{emit_horizontal_rule, try_parse_horizontal_rule};
24. use super::blocks::metadata::{try_parse_pandoc_title_block, try_parse_yaml_block};
25. use super::blocks::reference_links::try_parse_reference_definition;
26. use super::inlines::links::try_parse_inline_image;
27. use super::utils::container_stack::byte_index_at_column;
28. use super::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
29. 
30. /// Information about list indentation context.
31. ///
32. /// Used by block parsers that need to handle indentation stripping
33. /// when parsing inside list items (e.g., fenced code blocks).
34. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
35. pub(crate) struct ListIndentInfo {
36.     /// Number of columns to strip for list content
37.     pub content_col: usize,
38. }
39. 
40. /// Context passed to block parsers for decision-making.
41. ///
42. /// Contains immutable references to parser state that block parsers need
43. /// to check conditions (e.g., blank line before, blockquote depth, etc.).
44. pub(crate) struct BlockContext<'a> {
45.     /// Current line content (after blockquote markers stripped if any)
46.     pub content: &'a str,
47. 
48.     /// Whether there was a blank line before this line
49.     pub has_blank_before: bool,
50. 
51.     /// Whether we're at document start (pos == 0)
52.     pub at_document_start: bool,
53. 
54.     /// Current blockquote depth
55.     pub blockquote_depth: usize,
56. 
57.     /// Parser configuration
58.     pub config: &'a Config,
59. 
60.     // NOTE: we intentionally do not store `&ContainerStack` here to avoid
61.     // long-lived borrows of `self` in the main parser loop.
62.     /// Base indentation from container context (footnotes, definitions)
63.     pub content_indent: usize,
64. 
65.     /// List indentation info if inside a list
66.     pub list_indent_info: Option<ListIndentInfo>,
67. 
68.     /// Next line content for lookahead (used by setext headings)
69.     pub next_line: Option<&'a str>,
70. }
71. 
72. /// Result of detecting whether a block can be parsed.
73. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
74. pub(crate) enum BlockDetectionResult {
75.     /// Can parse this block, requires blank line before
76.     Yes,
77. 
78.     /// Can parse this block and can interrupt paragraphs (no blank line needed)
79.     #[allow(dead_code)]
80.     YesCanInterrupt,
81. 
82.     /// Cannot parse this content
83.     No,
84. }
85. 
86. /// A prepared (cached) detection result.
87. ///
88. /// This allows expensive detection logic (e.g., fence parsing) to be performed once,
89. /// while emission happens only after the caller prepares (flushes buffers/closes paragraphs).
90. pub(crate) struct PreparedBlockMatch {
91.     pub parser_index: usize,
92.     pub detection: BlockDetectionResult,
93.     pub payload: Option<Box<dyn Any>>,
94. }
95. 
96. /// Trait for block-level parsers.
97. ///
98. /// Each block type implements this trait with a two-phase approach:
99. /// 1. Detection: Can this block type parse this content? (lightweight, no emission)
100. /// 2. Parsing: Actually parse and emit the block to the builder (called after preparation)
101. ///
102. /// This separation allows the caller to:
103. /// - Prepare for block elements (close paragraphs, flush buffers) BEFORE emission
104. /// - Handle blocks that can interrupt paragraphs vs those that need blank lines
105. /// - Maintain correct CST node ordering
106. ///
107. /// Note: This is purely organizational - the trait doesn't introduce
108. /// backtracking or multiple passes. Each parser operates during the
109. /// single forward pass through the document.
110. pub(crate) trait BlockParser {
111.     /// Detect if this parser can handle the content (no emission).
112.     fn can_parse(
113.         &self,
114.         ctx: &BlockContext,
115.         lines: &[&str],
116.         line_pos: usize,
117.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult;
118. 
119.     /// Prepared detection hook.
120.     ///
121.     /// Default implementation just calls `can_parse()` and returns no payload.
122.     fn detect_prepared(
123.         &self,
124.         ctx: &BlockContext,
125.         lines: &[&str],
126.         line_pos: usize,
127.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
128.         let detection = self.can_parse(ctx, lines, line_pos);
129.         match detection {
130.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes | BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
131.                 Some((detection, None))
132.             }
133.             BlockDetectionResult::No => None,
134.         }
135.     }
136. 
137.     /// Parse and emit this block type to the builder.
138.     fn parse(
139.         &self,
140.         ctx: &BlockContext,
141.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
142.         lines: &[&str],
143.         line_pos: usize,
144.     ) -> usize;
145. 
146.     /// Prepared parse hook.
147.     ///
148.     /// Default implementation ignores payload and calls `parse()`.
149.     fn parse_prepared(
150.         &self,
151.         ctx: &BlockContext,
152.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
153.         lines: &[&str],
154.         line_pos: usize,
155.         _payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
156.     ) -> usize {
157.         self.parse(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos)
158.     }
159. 
160.     /// Name of this block parser (for debugging/logging)
161.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str;
162. }
163. 
164. // ============================================================================
165. // Concrete Block Parser Implementations
166. // ============================================================================
167. 
168. /// Horizontal rule parser
169. pub(crate) struct HorizontalRuleParser;
170. 
171. impl BlockParser for HorizontalRuleParser {
172.     fn can_parse(
173.         &self,
174.         ctx: &BlockContext,
175.         _lines: &[&str],
176.         _line_pos: usize,
177.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
178.         // Must have blank line before
179.         if !ctx.has_blank_before {
180.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
181.         }
182. 
183.         // Check if this looks like a horizontal rule
184.         if try_parse_horizontal_rule(ctx.content).is_some() {
185.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
186.         } else {
187.             BlockDetectionResult::No
188.         }
189.     }
190. 
191.     fn parse(
192.         &self,
193.         ctx: &BlockContext,
194.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
195.         lines: &[&str],
196.         line_pos: usize,
197.     ) -> usize {
198.         // Use ctx.content (blockquote markers already stripped)
199.         // But preserve newline from original line
200.         let (_, newline_str) = strip_newline(lines[line_pos]);
201.         let content_with_newline = if !newline_str.is_empty() {
202.             format!("{}{}", ctx.content.trim_end(), newline_str)
203.         } else {
204.             ctx.content.to_string()
205.         };
206. 
207.         emit_horizontal_rule(builder, &content_with_newline);
208.         1 // Consumed 1 line
209.     }
210. 
211.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
212.         "horizontal_rule"
213.     }
214. }
215.

217. pub(crate) struct AtxHeadingParser;
218. 
219. impl BlockParser for AtxHeadingParser {
220.     fn can_parse(

277. /// Pandoc title block parser (% Title ...)
278. pub(crate) struct PandocTitleBlockParser;
279. 
280. impl BlockParser for PandocTitleBlockParser {
281.     fn can_parse(
282.         &self,
283.         ctx: &BlockContext,
284.         lines: &[&str],
285.         line_pos: usize,
286.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
287.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
288.             .map(|(d, _)| d)

290.     }
291. 
292.     fn detect_prepared(
293.         &self,
294.         ctx: &BlockContext,
295.         _lines: &[&str],
296.         line_pos: usize,
297.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
298.         // Must be at document start.
299.         if !ctx.at_document_start || line_pos != 0 {
300.             return None;
301.         }
302. 
303.         // Must start with % (allow leading spaces).
304.         if !ctx.content.trim_start().starts_with('%') {
305.             return None;
306.         }
307. 
308.         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, None))
309.     }
310.

319.     }
320. 
321.     fn parse_prepared(
322.         &self,
323.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
324.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
325.         lines: &[&str],

328.     ) -> usize {

332.     }
333.

339. /// YAML metadata block parser (--- ... ---/...)
340. pub(crate) struct YamlMetadataParser;
341. 
342. impl BlockParser for YamlMetadataParser {
343.     fn can_parse(
344.         &self,
345.         ctx: &BlockContext,
346.         lines: &[&str],
347.         line_pos: usize,
348.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
349.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
350.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
351.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
352.     }
353. 
354.     fn detect_prepared(
355.         &self,
356.         ctx: &BlockContext,
357.         lines: &[&str],
358.         line_pos: usize,
359.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
360.         // Must be at top level (not inside blockquotes)
361.         if ctx.blockquote_depth > 0 {
362.             return None;
363.         }
364. 
365.         // Must start with ---
366.         if ctx.content.trim() != "---" {
367.             return None;
368.         }
369. 
370.         // YAML needs blank line before OR be at document start
371.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
372.             return None;
373.         }
374. 
375.         // Look ahead: next line must NOT be blank (to distinguish from horizontal rule)
376.         let next_line = lines.get(line_pos + 1)?;
377.         if next_line.trim().is_empty() {
378.             // This is a horizontal rule, not YAML
379.             return None;
380.         }
```

1. //! ATX heading parsing utilities.
2. 
3. use crate::config::Config;
4. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
5. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
6. 
7. use crate::parser::utils::attributes::{emit_attributes, try_parse_trailing_attributes};
8. use crate::parser::utils::inline_emission;
9. 
10. /// Try to parse an ATX heading from content, returns heading level (1-6) if found.
11. pub(crate) fn try_parse_atx_heading(content: &str) -> Option<usize> {
12.     let trimmed = content.trim_start();
13. 
14.     // Must start with 1-6 # characters
15.     let hash_count = trimmed.chars().take_while(|&c| c == '#').count();
16.     if hash_count == 0 || hash_count > 6 {
17.         return None;
18.     }
19. 
20.     // After hashes, must be end of line, space, or tab
21.     let after_hashes = &trimmed[hash_count..];
22.     if !after_hashes.is_empty() && !after_hashes.starts_with(' ') && !after_hashes.starts_with('\t')
23.     {
24.         return None;
25.     }
26. 
27.     // Check leading spaces (max 3)
28.     let leading_spaces = content.len() - trimmed.len();
29.     if leading_spaces > 3 {
30.         return None;
31.     }
32. 
33.     Some(hash_count)
34. }
35. 
36. /// Try to parse a setext heading from lines, returns (level, underline_char) if found.
37. ///
38. /// Setext headings consist of:
39. /// 1. A non-empty text line (heading content)
40. /// 2. An underline of `=` (level 1) or `-` (level 2) characters
41. ///
42. /// Rules:
43. /// - Underline must be at least 3 characters long
44. /// - Underline can have leading/trailing spaces (up to 3 leading spaces)
45. /// - All underline characters must be the same (`=` or `-`)
46. /// - Text line cannot be indented 4+ spaces (would be code block)
47. /// - Text line cannot be empty/blank
48. pub(crate) fn try_parse_setext_heading(lines: &[&str], pos: usize) -> Option<(usize, char)> {
49.     // Need current line (text) and next line (underline)
50.     if pos >= lines.len() {
51.         return None;
52.     }
53. 
54.     let text_line = lines[pos];
55.     let next_pos = pos + 1;
56.     if next_pos >= lines.len() {
57.         return None;
58.     }
59. 
60.     let underline = lines[next_pos];
61. 
62.     // Text line cannot be empty or blank
63.     if text_line.trim().is_empty() {
64.         return None;
65.     }
66. 
67.     // Text line cannot be indented 4+ spaces (would be code block)
68.     let leading_spaces = text_line.len() - text_line.trim_start().len();
69.     if leading_spaces >= 4 {
70.         return None;
71.     }
72. 
73.     // Check if underline is valid
74.     let underline_trimmed = underline.trim();
75. 
76.     // Must be at least 3 characters
77.     if underline_trimmed.len() < 3 {
78.         return None;
79.     }
80. 
81.     // Determine underline character and check consistency
82.     let first_char = underline_trimmed.chars().next()?;
83.     if first_char != '=' && first_char != '-' {
84.         return None;
85.     }
86. 
87.     // All characters must be the same
88.     if !underline_trimmed.chars().all(|c| c == first_char) {
89.         return None;
90.     }
91. 
92.     // Leading spaces in underline (max 3 for consistency with other block rules)
93.     let underline_leading_spaces = underline.len() - underline.trim_start().len();
94.     if underline_leading_spaces >= 4 {
95.         return None;
96.     }
97. 
98.     // Determine level: '=' is level 1, '-' is level 2
99.     let level = if first_char == '=' { 1 } else { 2 };
100. 
101.     Some((level, first_char))
102. }
103. 
104. /// Emit a setext heading node to the builder.
105. ///
106. /// Setext headings consist of a text line followed by an underline.
107. /// This function emits the complete HEADING node with both lines.
108. pub(crate) fn emit_setext_heading(
109.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
110.     text_line: &str,
111.     underline_line: &str,
112.     _level: usize,
113.     config: &Config,
114. ) {
115.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::HEADING.into());
116. 
117.     // Strip trailing newline from text line for processing
118.     let (text_without_newline, text_newline_str) =
119.         if let Some(stripped) = text_line.strip_suffix("\r\n") {
120.             (stripped, "\r\n")
121.         } else if let Some(stripped) = text_line.strip_suffix('\n') {
122.             (stripped, "\n")
123.         } else {
124.             (text_line, "")
125.         };
126. 
127.     // Handle leading spaces in text line
128.     let text_trimmed = text_without_newline.trim_start();
129.     let leading_spaces = text_without_newline.len() - text_trimmed.len();
130. 
131.     if leading_spaces > 0 {
132.         builder.token(
133.             SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(),
134.             &text_without_newline[..leading_spaces],
135.         );
136.     }
137. 
138.     // Try to parse trailing attributes from heading text
139.     let (text_content, attributes, space_before_attrs) =
140.         if let Some((attrs, text_before)) = try_parse_trailing_attributes(text_trimmed) {
141.             // Find where { starts in text_trimmed to get the space between text and attributes
142.             let start_brace_pos = text_trimmed.rfind('{').unwrap();
143.             let space = &text_trimmed[text_before.len()..start_brace_pos];
144.             (text_before, Some(attrs), space)
145.         } else {
146.             (text_trimmed, None, "")
147.         };
148. 
149.     // Emit heading content with inline parsing
150.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::HEADING_CONTENT.into());
151.     if !text_content.is_empty() {
152.         inline_emission::emit_inlines(builder, text_content, config);
153.     }
154.     builder.finish_node();
155. 
156.     // Emit space before attributes if present
157.     if !space_before_attrs.is_empty() {
158.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), space_before_attrs);
159.     }
160. 
161.     // Emit attributes if present
162.     if let Some(attrs) = attributes {
163.         emit_attributes(builder, &attrs);
164.     }
165. 
166.     // Emit newline after text line
167.     if !text_newline_str.is_empty() {
168.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), text_newline_str);
169.     }
170. 
171.     // Strip trailing newline from underline for processing
172.     let (underline_without_newline, underline_newline_str) =
173.         if let Some(stripped) = underline_line.strip_suffix("\r\n") {
174.             (stripped, "\r\n")
175.         } else if let Some(stripped) = underline_line.strip_suffix('\n') {
176.             (stripped, "\n")
177.         } else {
178.             (underline_line, "")
179.         };
180. 
181.     // Emit underline leading spaces if present
182.     let underline_trimmed = underline_without_newline.trim_start();
183.     let underline_leading_spaces = underline_without_newline.len() - underline_trimmed.len();
184. 
185.     if underline_leading_spaces > 0 {
186.         builder.token(
187.             SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(),
188.             &underline_without_newline[..underline_leading_spaces],
189.         );
190.     }
191. 
192.     // Emit the setext underline as a node containing a token
193.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::SETEXT_HEADING_UNDERLINE.into());
194.     builder.token(
195.         SyntaxKind::SETEXT_HEADING_UNDERLINE.into(),
196.         underline_trimmed,
197.     );
198.     builder.finish_node();
199. 
200.     // Emit trailing newline after underline
201.     if !underline_newline_str.is_empty() {
202.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), underline_newline_str);
203.     }
204. 
205.     builder.finish_node(); // HEADING
206. }
207. 
208. /// Emit an ATX heading node to the builder.
209. pub(crate) fn emit_atx_heading(
210.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
211.     content: &str,
212.     level: usize,
213.     config: &Config,
214. ) {
215.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::HEADING.into());
216. 
217.     // Strip trailing newline (LF or CRLF) for processing but remember to emit it later
218.     let (content_without_newline, newline_str) =
219.         if let Some(stripped) = content.strip_suffix("\r\n") {
220.             (stripped, "\r\n")
```

812.     fn has_nested_block_structure(&self, content: &str) -> bool {
813.         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
814.             content,
815.             has_blank_before: true,
816.             at_document_start: false,
817.             blockquote_depth: self.current_blockquote_depth(),
818.             config: self.config,
819.             content_indent: 0,
820.             list_indent_info: None,
821.             next_line: None,
822.         };
823. 
824.         // Note: we only use the registry for its “is this a block start?” decision;
825.         // it should not rely on `lines` here (setext is gated by `has_blank_before`).
826.         self.block_registry
827.             .detect_prepared(&block_ctx, &[], 0)
828.             .is_some()
829.     }
830. 
831.     /// Compute how many container levels to keep open based on next line content.
832.     fn compute_levels_to_keep(&self, next_line: &str) -> usize {
833.         let (next_bq_depth, next_inner) = count_blockquote_markers(next_line);
834.         let (raw_indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(next_inner);
835.         let next_marker = try_parse_list_marker(next_inner, self.config);
836. 
837.         // Calculate current blockquote depth for proper indent calculation
838.         let current_bq_depth = self.current_blockquote_depth();
839. 
840.         log::debug!(
841.             "compute_levels_to_keep: next_line indent={}, has_marker={}, stack_depth={}, current_bq={}, next_bq={}",
842.             raw_indent_cols,
843.             next_marker.is_some(),
844.             self.containers.depth(),
845.             current_bq_depth,
846.             next_bq_depth
847.         );
848. 
849.         let mut keep_level = 0;
850.         let mut content_indent_so_far = 0usize;
851. 
852.         // First, account for blockquotes
853.         for (i, c) in self.containers.stack.iter().enumerate() {
854.             match c {
855.                 Container::BlockQuote { .. } => {
856.                     // Count blockquotes up to this point
857.                     let bq_count = self.containers.stack[..=i]
858.                         .iter()
859.                         .filter(|x| matches!(x, Container::BlockQuote { .. }))
860.                         .count();
861.                     if bq_count <= next_bq_depth {
862.                         keep_level = i + 1;
863.                     }
864.                 }
865.                 Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col, .. } => {
866.                     // Track footnote indent for nested containers
867.                     content_indent_so_far += *content_col;
868.                     // Footnote continuation: line must be indented at least 4 spaces
869.                     // (or at the content column if content started after marker)
870.                     let min_indent = (*content_col).max(4);
```

41. 
42. /// Count how many blockquote levels a line has, returning (depth, remaining_content).
43. pub(crate) fn count_blockquote_markers(line: &str) -> (usize, &str) {
44.     let mut depth = 0;
45.     let mut remaining = line;
46. 
47.     while let Some((_, content_start)) = try_parse_blockquote_marker(remaining) {
48.         depth += 1;
49.         remaining = &remaining[content_start..];
50.     }
51. 
52.     (depth, remaining)
53. }
54. 
55. /// Parse all blockquote markers from a line and return detailed info about each.
56. /// Returns Vec of BlockQuoteMarkerInfo for each marker found.
57. /// This is useful for lossless parsing where we need to preserve exact whitespace.
58. pub(crate) fn parse_blockquote_marker_info(line: &str) -> Vec<BlockQuoteMarkerInfo> {
59.     let mut markers = Vec::new();
60.     let mut remaining = line;
61. 
62.     loop {
63.         let bytes = remaining.as_bytes();
64.         let mut i = 0;
65. 
66.         // Count leading whitespace (up to 3 spaces before >)
67.         let mut spaces = 0;
68.         while i < bytes.len() && bytes[i] == b' ' && spaces < 3 {
69.             spaces += 1;
70.             i += 1;
71.         }
72. 
73.         // Check if there's a > marker
74.         if i >= bytes.len() || bytes[i] != b'>' {
75.             break;
76.         }
77.         i += 1; // skip '>'
78. 
79.         // Check for optional space after >
80.         let has_trailing_space = i < bytes.len() && bytes[i] == b' ';
81.         if has_trailing_space {
82.             i += 1;
83.         }
84. 
85.         markers.push(BlockQuoteMarkerInfo {
86.             leading_spaces: spaces,
87.             has_trailing_space,
88.         });
89.         remaining = &remaining[i..];
90.     }
```

+/// Pandoc title block parser (% Title ...)
+pub(crate) struct PandocTitleBlockParser;
+
+impl BlockParser for PandocTitleBlockParser {
+    fn can_parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
+        self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
+            .map(|(d, _)| d)
+            .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
+    }
+
+    fn detect_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        _lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
+        // Must be at document start.
+        if !ctx.at_document_start || line_pos != 0 {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        // Must start with % (allow leading spaces).
+        if !ctx.content.trim_start().starts_with('%') {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, None))
+    }
+
+    fn parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> usize {
+        self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
+    }
+
+    fn parse_prepared(
+        &self,
+        _ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+        _payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
+    ) -> usize {
+        let new_pos = try_parse_pandoc_title_block(lines, line_pos, builder)
+            .unwrap_or(line_pos + 1);
+        new_pos - line_pos
+    }
+
+    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
+        "pandoc_title_block"
+    }
+}
+
 /// YAML metadata block parser (--- ... ---/...)
 pub(crate) struct YamlMetadataParser;

         _lines: &[&str],
         _line_pos: usize,
     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {

     }

<sub>⏱️ 103m 14s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Yep — that `block_dispatcher.rs :L726-L788` Setext parser is the right next target, and there’s a concrete bug there: it uses `ctx.next_line` but in `core.rs` we were previously passing the *raw* next line (including `\>` markers), while `ctx.content` is *already stripped to inner content*. That means setext detection could silently fail inside blockquotes because `try_parse_setext_heading([ctx.content, next_line])` sees a `\>`-prefixed underline line.

I fixed this by making `core.rs` pass `ctx.next_line` as `count_blockquote_markers(next_line).1` (inner content), so lookahead-based blocks operate on the same representation as `ctx.content`; tests are green (`cargo test -q`). I also wired `PandocTitleBlockParser` into the registry ordering and removed the now-redundant direct title-block parse in `core.rs`.

---

1524.                 content_before_newline
1525.             };

1541.                         (space_count > 0, trailing)
1542.                     } else {
1543.                         (false, "")
1544.                     }
1545.                 } else {
1546.                     (false, "")
1547.                 }
1548.             } else {

1555.                     } else {
1556.                         (false, "")
1557.                     }
1558.                 } else {
1559.                     (false, "")
1560.                 }
1561.             };
1562. 
1563.             // Emit space before trailing colons if present
1564.             if trailing_space {
1565.                 self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
1566.             }
1567. 
1568.             // Emit trailing colons if present
1569.             if !trailing_colons.is_empty() {
1570.                 self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), trailing_colons);
1571.             }
1572. 
1573.             // Emit newline
1574.             if !newline_str.is_empty() {
1575.                 self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
1576.             }
1577.             self.builder.finish_node(); // DivFenceOpen
1578. 
1579.             // Push FencedDiv container
1580.             self.containers.push(Container::FencedDiv {});
1581. 
1582.             self.pos += 1;
1583.             return true;
1584.         }
1585. 
1586.         // Check for fenced div closing
1587.         if self.in_fenced_div() && is_div_closing_fence(content) {
1588.             // Close paragraph before closing fenced div

1590. 
1591.             // Emit closing fence - parse to avoid newline duplication
1592.             self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_FENCE_CLOSE.into());
1593. 
1594.             // Get original full line
1595.             let full_line = self.lines[self.pos];
1596.             let trimmed = full_line.trim_start();
1597. 
1598.             // Emit leading whitespace if present

1601.                 self.builder
1602.                     .token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &full_line[..leading_ws_len]);
1603.             }
1604. 
1605.             // Emit fence content without newline (handle both CRLF and LF)
1606.             let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(trimmed);
1607. 
1608.             self.builder
1609.                 .token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), content_without_newline);
1610. 
1611.             // Emit newline separately if present

1615.             self.builder.finish_node(); // DivFenceClose
1616.

991.     /// Get the total indentation to strip from content containers (footnotes + definitions).
992.     fn content_container_indent_to_strip(&self) -> usize {
993.         self.containers
994.             .stack
995.             .iter()
996.             .filter_map(|c| match c {
997.                 Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
998.                 Container::Definition { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),

258.     /// Emit buffered list item content if we're in a ListItem and it has content.
259.     /// This is used before starting block-level elements inside list items.
260.     fn emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed(&mut self) {
261.         if let Some(Container::ListItem { buffer, .. }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
262.             && !buffer.is_empty()
263.         {
264.             let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
265.             buffer.clear();
266.             let use_paragraph = buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content();
267.             buffer_clone.emit_as_block(&mut self.builder, use_paragraph, self.config);

271.     /// Check if a paragraph is currently open.

273.         matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Paragraph { .. }))
274.     }
275. 
276.     /// Close paragraph if one is currently open.
277.     fn close_paragraph_if_open(&mut self) {
278.         if self.is_paragraph_open() {
279.             self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
280.         }
281.     }
282. 
283.     /// Prepare for a block-level element by flushing buffers and closing paragraphs.
284.     /// This is a common pattern before starting tables, code blocks, divs, etc.
285.     fn prepare_for_block_element(&mut self) {
286.         self.emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed();
287.         self.close_paragraph_if_open();

300.         &mut self,

1. //! HTML block parsing utilities.
2. 
3. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
4. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
5. 
6. use super::blockquotes::count_blockquote_markers;
7. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::{strip_leading_spaces, strip_newline};
8. 
9. /// HTML block-level tags as defined by CommonMark spec.
10. /// These tags start an HTML block when found at the start of a line.
11. const BLOCK_TAGS: &[&str] = &[
12.     "address",
13.     "article",
14.     "aside",
15.     "base",
16.     "basefont",
17.     "blockquote",
18.     "body",
19.     "caption",
20.     "center",
21.     "col",
22.     "colgroup",
23.     "dd",
24.     "details",
25.     "dialog",
26.     "dir",
27.     "div",
28.     "dl",
29.     "dt",
30.     "fieldset",
31.     "figcaption",
32.     "figure",
33.     "footer",
34.     "form",
35.     "frame",
36.     "frameset",
37.     "h1",
38.     "h2",
39.     "h3",
40.     "h4",
41.     "h5",
42.     "h6",
43.     "head",
44.     "header",
45.     "hr",
46.     "html",
47.     "iframe",
48.     "legend",
49.     "li",
50.     "link",
51.     "main",
52.     "menu",
53.     "menuitem",
54.     "nav",
55.     "noframes",
56.     "ol",
57.     "optgroup",
58.     "option",
59.     "p",
60.     "param",
61.     "section",
62.     "source",
63.     "summary",
64.     "table",
65.     "tbody",
66.     "td",
67.     "tfoot",
68.     "th",
69.     "thead",
70.     "title",
71.     "tr",
72.     "track",
73.     "ul",
74. ];
75. 
76. /// Tags that contain raw/verbatim content (no Markdown processing inside).
77. const VERBATIM_TAGS: &[&str] = &["script", "style", "pre", "textarea"];
78. 
79. /// Information about a detected HTML block opening.
80. #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
81. pub(crate) enum HtmlBlockType {
82.     /// HTML comment: <!-- ... -->
83.     Comment,
84.     /// Processing instruction: <? ... ?>
85.     ProcessingInstruction,
86.     /// Declaration: <!...>
87.     Declaration,
88.     /// CDATA section: <![CDATA[ ... ]]>
89.     CData,
90.     /// Block-level tag
91.     BlockTag { tag_name: String, is_verbatim: bool },
92. }
93. 
94. /// Try to detect an HTML block opening from content.
95. /// Returns block type if this is a valid HTML block start.
96. pub(crate) fn try_parse_html_block_start(content: &str) -> Option<HtmlBlockType> {
97.     let trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(content);
98. 
99.     // Must start with <
100.     if !trimmed.starts_with('<') {
101.         return None;
102.     }
103. 
104.     // HTML comment
105.     if trimmed.starts_with("<!--") {
106.         return Some(HtmlBlockType::Comment);
107.     }
108. 
109.     // Processing instruction
110.     if trimmed.starts_with("<?") {
111.         return Some(HtmlBlockType::ProcessingInstruction);
112.     }
113. 
114.     // CDATA section
115.     if trimmed.starts_with("<![CDATA[") {
116.         return Some(HtmlBlockType::CData);
117.     }
118. 
119.     // Declaration (DOCTYPE, etc.)
120.     if trimmed.starts_with("<!") && trimmed.len() > 2 {
121.         let after_bang = &trimmed[2..];
122.         if after_bang.chars().next()?.is_ascii_uppercase() {
123.             return Some(HtmlBlockType::Declaration);
124.         }
125.     }
126. 
127.     // Try to parse as opening tag
128.     if let Some(tag_name) = extract_opening_tag_name(trimmed) {
129.         let tag_lower = tag_name.to_lowercase();
130. 
131.         // Check if it's a block-level tag
132.         if BLOCK_TAGS.contains(&tag_lower.as_str()) {
133.             let is_verbatim = VERBATIM_TAGS.contains(&tag_lower.as_str());
134.             return Some(HtmlBlockType::BlockTag {
135.                 tag_name: tag_lower,
136.                 is_verbatim,
137.             });
138.         }
139. 
140.         // Also accept verbatim tags even if not in BLOCK_TAGS list
```

1. //! LaTeX environment block parsing utilities.
2. 
3. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
4. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
5. 
6. use super::blockquotes::count_blockquote_markers;
7. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::{emit_line_tokens, strip_leading_spaces};
8. 
9. /// Information about a detected LaTeX environment opening.
10. #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
11. pub(crate) struct LatexEnvInfo {
12.     pub env_name: String,
13. }
14. 
15. /// Try to detect a LaTeX environment opening from content.
16. /// Returns environment info if this is a valid `\begin{name}` line.
17. pub(crate) fn try_parse_latex_env_begin(content: &str) -> Option<LatexEnvInfo> {
18.     let trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(content);
19. 
20.     // Check for \begin{
21.     if !trimmed.starts_with("\\begin{") {
22.         return None;
23.     }
24. 
25.     // Extract environment name
26.     let after_begin = &trimmed[7..]; // Skip "\begin{"
27.     let close_brace = after_begin.find('}')?;
28.     let env_name = after_begin[..close_brace].to_string();
29. 
30.     // Environment name must not be empty
31.     if env_name.is_empty() {
32.         return None;
33.     }
34. 
35.     Some(LatexEnvInfo { env_name })
36. }
37. 
38. /// Try to detect a LaTeX environment closing from content.
39. /// Returns environment name if this is a valid `\end{name}` line.
40. fn try_parse_latex_env_end(content: &str) -> Option<String> {
41.     let trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(content);
42. 
43.     // Check for \end{
44.     if !trimmed.starts_with("\\end{") {
45.         return None;
46.     }
47. 
48.     // Extract environment name
49.     let after_end = &trimmed[5..]; // Skip "\end{"
50.     let close_brace = after_end.find('}')?;
51.     let env_name = after_end[..close_brace].to_string();
52. 
53.     // Environment name must not be empty
54.     if env_name.is_empty() {
55.         return None;
56.     }
57. 
58.     Some(env_name)
59. }
60. 
61. /// Parse a LaTeX environment block, consuming lines from the parser.
62. /// Returns the new position after the environment block.
63. /// Handles nested environments by tracking environment names on a stack.
64. pub(crate) fn parse_latex_environment(
65.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
66.     lines: &[&str],
67.     start_pos: usize,
68.     env_info: LatexEnvInfo,
69.     bq_depth: usize,
70. ) -> usize {
71.     // Start LaTeX environment block
72.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LATEX_ENVIRONMENT.into());
73. 
74.     // Opening \begin{name}
75.     let first_line = lines[start_pos];
76.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LATEX_ENV_BEGIN.into());
77.     emit_line_tokens(builder, first_line);
78.     builder.finish_node(); // LatexEnvBegin
79. 
80.     let mut current_pos = start_pos + 1;
81.     let mut content_lines: Vec<&str> = Vec::new();
82.     let mut env_stack = vec![env_info.env_name.clone()];
83.     let mut found_closing = false;
84. 
85.     // Parse content until we find the matching \end{name}
86.     while current_pos < lines.len() {
87.         let line = lines[current_pos];
88.         let (line_bq_depth, inner_content) = count_blockquote_markers(line);
89. 
90.         // Only process lines at the same or deeper blockquote depth
91.         if line_bq_depth < bq_depth {
92.             break;
93.         }
94. 
95.         // Strip blockquote markers at our depth
96.         let stripped_line = if line_bq_depth == bq_depth {
97.             inner_content
98.         } else {
99.             // Deeper blockquote - preserve the extra markers
100.             line
101.         };
102. 
103.         // Check for nested \begin{} or closing \end{}
104.         if let Some(nested_env) = try_parse_latex_env_begin(stripped_line) {
105.             log::trace!(
106.                 "Found nested LaTeX \\begin{{{}}} at line {}",
107.                 nested_env.env_name,
108.                 current_pos + 1
109.             );
110.             env_stack.push(nested_env.env_name);
111.             content_lines.push(line);
112.             current_pos += 1;
113.             continue;
114.         }
115. 
116.         if let Some(end_name) = try_parse_latex_env_end(stripped_line) {
117.             // Check if this closes our environment or a nested one
118.             if let Some(expected_name) = env_stack.last()
119.                 && &end_name == expected_name
120.             {
121.                 env_stack.pop();
122. 
123.                 // If stack is empty, this closes our outermost environment
124.                 if env_stack.is_empty() {
125.                     log::debug!(
126.                         "Found matching LaTeX \\end{{{}}} at line {}",
127.                         end_name,
128.                         current_pos + 1
129.                     );
130.                     found_closing = true;
```

</details>

785.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
786.         "setext_heading"

789. 
790. // ============================================================================
791. // Block Parser Registry
792. // ============================================================================
793. 
794. /// Registry of block parsers, ordered by priority.
795. ///
796. /// This dispatcher tries each parser in order until one succeeds.
797. /// The ordering follows Pandoc's approach - explicit list order rather
798. /// than numeric priorities.
799. pub(crate) struct BlockParserRegistry {
800.     parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>,
801. }
802. 
803. impl BlockParserRegistry {
804.     /// Create a new registry with all block parsers.
805.     ///
806.     /// Order matters! Parsers are tried in the order listed here.
807.     /// This follows Pandoc's design where ordering is explicit and documented.
808.     ///
809.     /// **Pandoc reference order** (from pandoc/src/Text/Pandoc/Readers/Markdown.hs:487-515):
810.     /// 1. blanklines (handled separately in our parser)
811.     /// 2. codeBlockFenced
812.     /// 3. yamlMetaBlock' ← YAML metadata comes early!
813.     /// 4. bulletList
814.     /// 5. divHtml
815.     /// 6. divFenced
816.     /// 7. header ← ATX headings
817.     /// 8. lhsCodeBlock
818.     /// 9. htmlBlock
819.     /// 10. table
820.     /// 11. codeBlockIndented
821.     /// 12. rawTeXBlock (LaTeX)
822.     /// 13. lineBlock
823.     /// 14. blockQuote
824.     /// 15. hrule ← Horizontal rules come AFTER headers!
825.     /// 16. orderedList
826.     /// 17. definitionList
827.     /// 18. noteBlock (footnotes)
828.     /// 19. referenceKey ← Reference definitions
829.     /// 20. abbrevKey
830.     /// 21. para
831.     /// 22. plain
832.     pub fn new() -> Self {
833.         let parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>> = vec![
834.             // Match Pandoc's ordering to ensure correct precedence:
835.             // (0) Pandoc title block (must be at document start).
836.             Box::new(PandocTitleBlockParser),
837.             // (2) Fenced code blocks - can interrupt paragraphs!
838.             Box::new(FencedCodeBlockParser),
839.             // (3) YAML metadata - before headers and hrules!
840.             Box::new(YamlMetadataParser),
841.             // (7) Setext headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
842.             // Must come before ATX to properly handle `---` disambiguation
843.             Box::new(SetextHeadingParser),
844.             // (7) ATX headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
845.             Box::new(AtxHeadingParser),
846.             // (15) Horizontal rules - AFTER headings per Pandoc
847.             Box::new(HorizontalRuleParser),
848.             // Figures (standalone images) - Pandoc doesn't have these
849.             Box::new(FigureParser),
850.             // (19) Reference definitions
851.             Box::new(ReferenceDefinitionParser),
852.             // TODO: Migrate remaining blocks in Pandoc order:
853.             // - (4-6) Lists and divs (bulletList, divHtml, divFenced)
854.             // - (9) HTML blocks
855.             // - (10) Tables (grid, multiline, pipe, simple)
856.             // - (11) Indented code blocks (AFTER fenced!)
857.             // - (12) LaTeX blocks (rawTeXBlock)
858.             // - (13) Line blocks
859.             // - (16) Ordered lists
860.             // - (17) Definition lists
861.             // - (18) Footnote definitions (noteBlock)
862.         ];
863. 
864.         Self { parsers }
865.     }
866. 
867.     /// Try to parse a block using the registered parsers.
868.     ///
869.     /// This method implements the two-phase parsing:
870.     /// 1. Detection: Check if any parser can handle this content
871.     /// 2. Caller prepares (closes paragraphs, flushes buffers)
872.     /// 3. Parser emits the block
873.     ///
874.     /// Returns (parser_index, detection_result) if a parser can handle this,
875.     /// or None if no parser matched.
876.     #[allow(dead_code)]
877.     pub fn detect(
878.         &self,
879.         ctx: &BlockContext,
880.         lines: &[&str],
881.         line_pos: usize,
882.     ) -> Option<(usize, BlockDetectionResult)> {
883.         for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
884.             let result = parser.can_parse(ctx, lines, line_pos);
885.             match result {
886.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes | BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
887.                     log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
888.                     return Some((i, result));
889.                 }
890.                 BlockDetectionResult::No => continue,
891.             }
892.         }
893.         None
894.     }
895. 
896.     /// Like `detect()`, but allows parsers to return cached payload for emission.
897.     pub fn detect_prepared(
898.         &self,
899.         ctx: &BlockContext,
900.         lines: &[&str],
901.         line_pos: usize,
902.     ) -> Option<PreparedBlockMatch> {
903.         for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
904.             if let Some((detection, payload)) = parser.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos) {
905.                 log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
906.                 return Some(PreparedBlockMatch {
907.                     parser_index: i,
908.                     detection,
909.                     payload,
910.                 });
```

</details>

525.     fn parse(
526.         &self,
527.         ctx: &BlockContext,
528.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
529.         lines: &[&str],
530.         line_pos: usize,
531.     ) -> usize {
532.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
533.     }
534. 
535.     fn parse_prepared(
536.         &self,
537.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
538.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
539.         lines: &[&str],
540.         line_pos: usize,
541.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
542.     ) -> usize {
543.         use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
544. 
545.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::REFERENCE_DEFINITION.into());
546. 
547.         let full_line = lines[line_pos];
548.         let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(full_line);
549. 
550.         // Detection already cached the parsed tuple; emission should not need to re-parse.
551.         // If payload is missing (legacy callsites), we fall back to the old raw emission.
552.         debug_assert!(payload
553.             .and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<(usize, String, String, Option<String>)>())
554.             .is_some());
555. 
556.         emit_reference_definition_content(builder, content_without_newline);
557. 
558.         if !line_ending.is_empty() {
559.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
560.         }
561. 
562.         builder.finish_node();
563. 
564.         1
565.     }
566. 
567.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
568.         "reference_definition"
569.     }
570. }
571. 
572. /// Helper function to emit reference definition content with inline structure.
573. fn emit_reference_definition_content(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, text: &str) {
574.     use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
575. 
576.     if !text.starts_with('[') {
577.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
578.         return;
579.     }
580. 
581.     let rest = &text[1..];
582.     if let Some(close_pos) = rest.find(']') {
583.         let label = &rest[..close_pos];
584.         let after_bracket = &rest[close_pos + 1..];
585. 
586.         if after_bracket.starts_with(':') {
587.             // Emit LINK node with the label
588.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK.into());
589. 
590.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into());
591.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into(), "[");
592.             builder.finish_node();
593. 
594.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_TEXT.into());
595.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), label);
596.             builder.finish_node();
597. 
598.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "]");
599.             builder.finish_node(); // LINK
600. 
601.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), after_bracket);
602.             return;
603.         }
604.     }
605. 
606.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
607. }
608. 
609. 
610. /// Fenced code block parser (``` or ~~~)
611. pub(crate) struct FencedCodeBlockParser;
612. 
613. impl BlockParser for FencedCodeBlockParser {
614.     fn can_parse(
615.         &self,
616.         ctx: &BlockContext,
617.         _lines: &[&str],
618.         _line_pos: usize,
619.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
620.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, _lines, _line_pos)
621.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
622.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
623.     }
624. 
625.     fn detect_prepared(
626.         &self,
627.         ctx: &BlockContext,
628.         _lines: &[&str],
629.         _line_pos: usize,
630.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
631.         // Calculate content to check - may need to strip list indentation
632.         let content_to_check = if let Some(list_info) = ctx.list_indent_info {
633.             if list_info.content_col > 0 && !ctx.content.is_empty() {
634.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(ctx.content, list_info.content_col);
635.                 &ctx.content[idx..]
636.             } else {
637.                 ctx.content
638.             }
639.         } else {
640.             ctx.content
641.         };
642. 
643.         let fence = try_parse_fence_open(content_to_check)?;
644. 
645.         // Parse info string to determine block type (expensive, but now cached via fence)
646.         let info = InfoString::parse(&fence.info_string);
647. 
648.         let is_executable = matches!(info.block_type, CodeBlockType::Executable { .. });
649.         let is_pandoc_like = matches!(
650.             ctx.config.flavor,
651.             crate::config::Flavor::Pandoc
652.                 | crate::config::Flavor::CommonMark
653.                 | crate::config::Flavor::Gfm
654.         );
655.         if is_executable && is_pandoc_like {
656.             return None;
657.         }
658. 
659.         // Fenced code blocks can interrupt paragraphs only if they have an info string.
660.         let has_info = !fence.info_string.trim().is_empty();
661.         let detection = if has_info {
662.             BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
663.         } else if ctx.has_blank_before {
664.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
665.         } else {
666.             BlockDetectionResult::No
667.         };
668. 
669.         match detection {
670.             BlockDetectionResult::No => None,
671.             _ => Some((detection, Some(Box::new(fence)))),
672.         }
673.     }
674. 
675.     fn parse(
676.         &self,
677.         ctx: &BlockContext,
678.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
679.         lines: &[&str],
680.         line_pos: usize,
681.     ) -> usize {
682.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
683.     }
684. 
685.     fn parse_prepared(
686.         &self,
687.         ctx: &BlockContext,
688.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
689.         lines: &[&str],
690.         line_pos: usize,
691.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
692.     ) -> usize {
693.         let list_indent_stripped = ctx.list_indent_info.map(|i| i.content_col).unwrap_or(0);
694. 
695.         let fence = if let Some(fence) = payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<FenceInfo>()) {
696.             fence.clone()
697.         } else {
698.             // Backward-compat: if called via legacy `parse()`, recompute.
699.             let content_to_check = if list_indent_stripped > 0 && !ctx.content.is_empty() {
700.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(ctx.content, list_indent_stripped);
701.                 &ctx.content[idx..]
702.             } else {
703.                 ctx.content
704.             };
705.             try_parse_fence_open(content_to_check).expect("Fence should exist")
706.         };
707. 
708.         // Calculate total indent: base content indent + list indent
709.         let total_indent = ctx.content_indent + list_indent_stripped;
710. 
711.         let new_pos = parse_fenced_code_block(
712.             builder,
713.             lines,
714.             line_pos,
715.             fence,
716.             ctx.blockquote_depth,
717.             total_indent,
718.         );
719. 
720.         new_pos - line_pos
721.     }
722. 
723.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
724.         "fenced_code_block"
725.     }
726. }
727. 
728. // ============================================================================
729. // Setext Heading Parser (position #3)
730. // ============================================================================
731. 
732. pub(crate) struct SetextHeadingParser;
733. 
734. impl BlockParser for SetextHeadingParser {
735.     fn can_parse(
736.         &self,
737.         ctx: &BlockContext,
738.         _lines: &[&str],
739.         _line_pos: usize,
740.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
````

732. pub(crate) struct HtmlBlockParser;
733. 
734. impl BlockParser for HtmlBlockParser {
735.     fn can_parse(
736.         &self,
737.         ctx: &BlockContext,
738.         lines: &[&str],
739.         line_pos: usize,
740.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
741.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
742.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
743.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
744.     }
745. 
746.     fn detect_prepared(
747.         &self,
748.         ctx: &BlockContext,
749.         _lines: &[&str],
750.         _line_pos: usize,
751.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
752.         if !ctx.config.extensions.raw_html {
753.             return None;
754.         }
755. 
756.         // HTML blocks require a blank line before (or at document start).
757.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
758.             return None;
759.         }
760. 
761.         let block_type = try_parse_html_block_start(ctx.content)?;
762.         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, Some(Box::new(block_type))))
763.     }
764. 
765.     fn parse(
766.         &self,
767.         ctx: &BlockContext,
768.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
769.         lines: &[&str],
770.         line_pos: usize,
771.     ) -> usize {
772.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
773.     }
774. 
775.     fn parse_prepared(
776.         &self,
777.         ctx: &BlockContext,
778.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
779.         lines: &[&str],
780.         line_pos: usize,
781.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
782.     ) -> usize {
783.         let block_type = if let Some(bt) =
784.             payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<HtmlBlockType>())
785.         {
786.             bt.clone()
787.         } else {
788.             try_parse_html_block_start(ctx.content).expect("HTML block type should exist")
789.         };
790. 
791.         let new_pos = parse_html_block(builder, lines, line_pos, block_type, ctx.blockquote_depth);
792.         new_pos - line_pos
793.     }
794. 
795.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
796.         "html_block"
797.     }
798. }
799. 
800. // ============================================================================
801. // LaTeX Environment Parser (position #12)
802. // ============================================================================
803. 
804. pub(crate) struct LatexEnvironmentParser;
805. 
806. impl BlockParser for LatexEnvironmentParser {
807.     fn can_parse(
808.         &self,
809.         ctx: &BlockContext,
810.         lines: &[&str],
811.         line_pos: usize,
812.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
813.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
814.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
815.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
816.     }
817. 
818.     fn detect_prepared(
819.         &self,
820.         ctx: &BlockContext,
821.         _lines: &[&str],
822.         _line_pos: usize,
823.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
824.         if !ctx.config.extensions.raw_tex {
825.             return None;
826.         }
827. 
828.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
829.             return None;
830.         }
831. 
832.         let env_info = try_parse_latex_env_begin(ctx.content)?;
833.         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, Some(Box::new(env_info))))
834.     }
835. 
836.     fn parse(
837.         &self,
838.         ctx: &BlockContext,
839.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
840.         lines: &[&str],
841.         line_pos: usize,
842.     ) -> usize {
843.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
844.     }
845. 
846.     fn parse_prepared(
847.         &self,
848.         ctx: &BlockContext,
849.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
850.         lines: &[&str],
851.         line_pos: usize,
852.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
853.     ) -> usize {
854.         let env_info = if let Some(info) =
855.             payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<LatexEnvInfo>())
856.         {
857.             info.clone()
858.         } else {
859.             try_parse_latex_env_begin(ctx.content).expect("LaTeX env info should exist")
860.         };
861. 
862.         let new_pos = parse_latex_environment(
863.             builder,
864.             lines,
865.             line_pos,
866.             env_info,
867.             ctx.blockquote_depth,
868.         );
869.         new_pos - line_pos
870.     }
871. 
872.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
873.         "latex_environment"
874.     }
875. }
876. 
877. // ============================================================================
878. // Setext Heading Parser (position #3)
879. // ============================================================================
880.
```

</details>

1. use panache::format;
2. use panache::format_with_defaults;
3. 
4. #[test]
5. fn comment_roundtrip() {
6.     let input = "<!-- This is a comment -->\n";
7.     let output = format_with_defaults(input);
8.     assert_eq!(output, input);
9. }
10. 
11. #[test]
12. fn comment_within_content() {
13.     let cfg = panache::ConfigBuilder::default().line_width(160).build();
14.     let input =
15.         "Some text before the comment.\n<!-- This is a comment -->\nSome text after the comment.\n";
16.     let output = format(input, Some(cfg), None);
17.     assert!(output.contains("Some text before the comment."));
18.     assert!(output.contains("<!-- This is a comment -->"));
19.     assert!(output.contains("Some text after the comment."));
20. }
21. 
22. #[test]
23. fn comment_no_wrap() {
24.     let cfg = panache::ConfigBuilder::default().line_width(40).build();
25.     let input = "Some text before the comment.\n<!-- This is a very long comment that should not be wrapped or reformatted -->\nSome text after the comment.\n";
26.     let output = format(input, Some(cfg), None);
27.     assert!(output.contains(
28.         "<!-- This is a very long comment that should not be wrapped or reformatted -->"
29.     ));
30. }
```

<sub>⏱️ 108m 55s</sub>

### 👤 User

Wait, you're adding new blocks now? Shouldn't we first remove the can_parse and parse stuff, or is there any reason we still need it? Maybe I missed something?

---

<sub>⏱️ 109m 4s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Yes—we’re adding new *registry* blocks as part of migrating logic out of `core.rs`; that’s the “next obvious win” because it shrinks `core.rs` and keeps precedence centralized.

On `can_parse`/`parse`: they’re not strictly necessary for the *prepared* path (`detect_prepared`/`parse_prepared`), but they currently serve as (1) cheap/default implementations for simple parsers (so you don’t have to implement both prepared hooks everywhere), and (2) compatibility for any remaining callsites/tests/utilities that still use the non-prepared `detect()`/`parse()` path (even if most of `core.rs` now uses prepared).

Once more blocks are migrated and we confirm nothing relies on `detect()`/`can_parse()` anymore, we can simplify the trait to “prepared-only” (or keep `can_parse` as a purely-cheap probe and forbid calling expensive `try_parse_*` from it).

---

<sub>⏱️ 109m 28s</sub>

### 👤 User
