```
1. //! Fenced div parsing utilities.
2. 
3. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::strip_leading_spaces;
4. 
5. /// Information about a detected div fence opening.
6. pub(crate) struct DivFenceInfo {
7.     pub attributes: String,
8.     pub fence_count: usize,
9. }
10. 
11. /// Try to detect a fenced div opening from content.
12. /// Returns div fence info if this is a valid opening fence.
13. ///
14. /// Opening fences MUST have attributes (or the fences are treated as closing).
15. /// Format: `::: {.class #id}` or `::: classname` or `::::: {#id} :::::`
16. pub(crate) fn try_parse_div_fence_open(content: &str) -> Option<DivFenceInfo> {
17.     let trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(content);
18. 
19.     // Check for fence opening (:::)
20.     if !trimmed.starts_with(':') {
21.         return None;
22.     }
23. 
24.     let colon_count = trimmed.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ':').count();
25. 
26.     if colon_count < 3 {
27.         return None;
28.     }
29. 
30.     // Get the part after the colons
31.     let after_colons = trimmed[colon_count..].trim_start();
32. 
33.     // Check if there are attributes
34.     // Attributes can be:
35.     // 1. Curly braces: {.class #id key="value"}
36.     // 2. Single word (treated as class): classname
37.     // 3. Attributes followed by more colons (optional): {.class} :::
38. 
39.     let attributes = if after_colons.starts_with('{') {
40.         // Find the closing brace
41.         if let Some(close_idx) = after_colons.find('}') {
42.             after_colons[..=close_idx].to_string()
43.         } else {
44.             // Unclosed brace, not valid
45.             return None;
46.         }
47.     } else if after_colons.is_empty() {
48.         // No attributes, this is a closing fence
49.         return None;
50.     } else {
51.         // Single word or words until optional trailing colons
52.         let content_before_colons = after_colons.trim_end_matches(':').trim_end();
53. 
54.         if content_before_colons.is_empty() {
55.             // Only colons, no attributes
56.             return None;
57.         }
58. 
59.         // Take the first word as the class name
60.         content_before_colons.split_whitespace().next()?.to_string()
61.     };
62. 
63.     Some(DivFenceInfo {
64.         attributes,
65.         fence_count: colon_count,
66.     })
67. }
68. 
69. /// Check if a line is a valid closing fence for a div.
70. /// Closing fences have NO attributes and at least 3 colons.
71. pub(crate) fn is_div_closing_fence(content: &str) -> bool {
72.     let trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(content);
73. 
74.     if !trimmed.starts_with(':') {
75.         return false;
76.     }
77. 
78.     let colon_count = trimmed.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ':').count();
79. 
80.     if colon_count < 3 {
81.         return false;
82.     }
83. 
84.     // Rest of line must be empty (only colons are allowed)
85.     trimmed[colon_count..].trim().is_empty()
86. }
87. 
88. #[cfg(test)]
89. mod tests {
90.     use super::*;
91. 
92.     #[test]
93.     fn test_parse_div_fence_open_with_curly_braces() {
94.         let line = "::: {.callout-note}";
95.         let fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(line).unwrap();
96.         assert_eq!(fence.attributes, "{.callout-note}");
97.     }
98. 
99.     #[test]
100.     fn test_parse_div_fence_open_with_class_name() {
101.         let line = "::: Warning";
102.         let fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(line).unwrap();
103.         assert_eq!(fence.attributes, "Warning");
104.     }
105. 
106.     #[test]
107.     fn test_parse_div_fence_open_with_trailing_colons() {
108.         let line = "::::: {#special .sidebar} :::::";
109.         let fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(line).unwrap();
110.         assert_eq!(fence.attributes, "{#special .sidebar}");
111.     }
112. 
113.     #[test]
114.     fn test_closing_fence_no_attributes() {
115.         let line = ":::";
116.         assert!(try_parse_div_fence_open(line).is_none());
117.         assert!(is_div_closing_fence(line));
118.     }
119. 
120.     #[test]
121.     fn test_closing_fence_many_colons() {
122.         let line = "::::::::::::::";
123.         assert!(try_parse_div_fence_open(line).is_none());
124.         assert!(is_div_closing_fence(line));
125.     }
126. 
127.     #[test]
128.     fn test_not_a_fence_too_few_colons() {
129.         let line = ":: something";
130.         assert!(try_parse_div_fence_open(line).is_none());
131.         assert!(!is_div_closing_fence(line));
132.     }
133. }
```

</details>

1468. 
1469.             // Start FencedDiv node
1470.             self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::FENCED_DIV.into());
1471. 
1472.             // Emit opening fence with attributes as child node to avoid duplication
1473.             self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_FENCE_OPEN.into());
1474. 
1475.             // Get original full line
1476.             let full_line = self.lines[self.pos];

1523.                         (false, "")
1524.                     }

1527.                 }
1528.             } else {

1546.             }

1566.         // Check for fenced div closing
1567.         if self.in_fenced_div() && is_div_closing_fence(content) {
1568.             // Close paragraph before closing fenced div
1569.             self.close_paragraph_if_open();

1577.

1582.                     .token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &full_line[..leading_ws_len]);
1583.             }
1584. 
1585.             // Emit fence content without newline (handle both CRLF and LF)
1586.             let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(trimmed);
1587. 
1588.             self.builder
1589.                 .token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), content_without_newline);

1594.             }
1595.             self.builder.finish_node(); // DivFenceClose
1596. 
1597.             // Pop the FencedDiv container (this will finish the FencedDiv node)
1598.             self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1599. 
1600.             self.pos += 1;
1601.             return true;
1602.         }
1603.

1606.         if let Some((marker, marker_len, spaces_after)) =
1607.             try_parse_list_marker(content, self.config)
1608.         {
1609.             let (indent_cols, indent_bytes) = leading_indent(content);
1610.             if indent_cols >= 4 && !lists::in_list(&self.containers) {
1611.                 // Code block at top-level, treat as paragraph
1612.                 paragraphs::start_paragraph_if_needed(&mut self.containers, &mut self.builder);
1613.                 paragraphs::append_paragraph_line(
1614.                     &mut self.containers,
1615.                     &mut self.builder,
1616.                     content,

1621.             }

1623.             // Lists can only interrupt paragraphs if there was a blank line before
1624.             // (Per Pandoc spec - lists need blank lines to start interrupting paragraphs)
1625.             if self.is_paragraph_open() {

1. use crate::config::Config;
2. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
3. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
4. 
5. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
6. use crate::parser::utils::inline_emission;
7. 
8. /// Try to parse the start of a line block.
9. /// Returns Some(()) if this line starts a line block (| followed by space or end of line).
10. pub fn try_parse_line_block_start(line: &str) -> Option<()> {
11.     let trimmed = line.trim_start();
12.     if trimmed.starts_with("| ") || trimmed == "|" {
13.         Some(())
14.     } else {
15.         None
16.     }
17. }
18. 
19. /// Parse a complete line block starting at current position.
20. /// Returns the new position after the line block.
21. pub fn parse_line_block(
22.     lines: &[&str],
23.     start_pos: usize,
24.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
25.     config: &Config,
26. ) -> usize {
27.     log::debug!("Parsing line block at line {}", start_pos + 1);
28. 
29.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINE_BLOCK.into());
30. 
31.     let mut pos = start_pos;
32. 
33.     while pos < lines.len() {
34.         let line = lines[pos];
35. 
36.         // Check if this is a line block line (starts with |)
37.         if let Some(content_start) = parse_line_block_line_marker(line) {
38.             // This is a line block line
39.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINE_BLOCK_LINE.into());
40. 
41.             // Emit the marker
42.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::LINE_BLOCK_MARKER.into(), "| ");
43. 
44.             // Emit the content (preserving leading spaces)
45.             let content = &line[content_start..];
46. 
47.             // Split off trailing newline if present
48.             let (content_without_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(content);
49. 
50.             if !content_without_newline.is_empty() {
51.                 inline_emission::emit_inlines(builder, content_without_newline, config);
52.             }
53. 
54.             if !newline_str.is_empty() {
55.                 builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
56.             }
57. 
58.             builder.finish_node(); // LineBlockLine
59.             pos += 1;
60. 
61.             // Check for continuation lines (lines that start with space)
62.             while pos < lines.len() {
63.                 let next_line = lines[pos];
64. 
65.                 // Continuation line must start with space and not be a new line block line
66.                 if next_line.starts_with(' ') && !next_line.trim_start().starts_with("| ") {
67.                     // This is a continuation of the previous line
68.                     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINE_BLOCK_LINE.into());
69. 
70.                     // Split off trailing newline if present
71.                     let (line_without_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(next_line);
72. 
73.                     if !line_without_newline.is_empty() {
74.                         inline_emission::emit_inlines(builder, line_without_newline, config);
75.                     }
76. 
77.                     if !newline_str.is_empty() {
78.                         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
79.                     }
80. 
81.                     builder.finish_node(); // LineBlockLine
82.                     pos += 1;
83.                 } else {
84.                     break;
85.                 }
86.             }
87.         } else {
88.             // Not a line block line, end the line block
89.             break;
90.         }
91.     }
92. 
93.     builder.finish_node(); // LineBlock
94. 
95.     log::debug!("Parsed line block: lines {}-{}", start_pos + 1, pos);
96. 
97.     pos
98. }
99. 
100. /// Parse a line block marker and return the index where content starts.
101. /// Returns Some(index) if the line starts with "| " or just "|", None otherwise.
102. fn parse_line_block_line_marker(line: &str) -> Option<usize> {
103.     // Line block lines start with | followed by a space or end of line
104.     // We need to handle leading whitespace (indentation)
105.     let trimmed_start = line.len() - line.trim_start().len();
106.     let after_indent = &line[trimmed_start..];
107. 
108.     if after_indent.starts_with("| ") {
109.         Some(trimmed_start + 2) // Skip "| "
110.     } else if after_indent == "|" || after_indent == "|\n" {
111.         Some(trimmed_start + 1) // Just "|", no space
112.     } else {
113.         None
114.     }
115. }
116.

118. mod tests {
119.     use super::*;
120.
```

1. //! Indented code block parsing utilities.
2. //!
3. //! A block of text indented four spaces (or one tab) is treated as verbatim text.
4. //! The initial (four space or one tab) indentation is not considered part of the
5. //! verbatim text and is removed in the output.
6. //!
7. //! Note: blank lines in the verbatim text need not begin with four spaces.
8. 
9. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
10. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
11. 
12. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
13. 
14. /// Check if a line is indented enough to be part of an indented code block.
15. /// Returns true if the line starts with 4+ spaces or 1+ tab.
16. pub(crate) fn is_indented_code_line(content: &str) -> bool {
17.     if content.is_empty() {
18.         return false;
19.     }
20. 
21.     // Check for tab
22.     if content.starts_with('\t') {
23.         return true;
24.     }
25. 
26.     // Check for 4+ spaces
27.     let spaces = content.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ' ').count();
28.     spaces >= 4
29. }
30. 
31. /// Parse an indented code block, consuming lines from the parser.
32. /// Returns the new position after the code block.
33. ///
34. /// An indented code block consists of consecutive lines that are either:
35. /// - Indented by 4+ spaces or 1+ tab
36. /// - Blank lines (which don't need indentation)
37. ///
38. /// The block ends when we hit a non-blank line that isn't indented enough.
39. /// Parse an indented code block, consuming lines from the parser.
40. /// Returns the new position after the code block.
41. ///
42. /// An indented code block consists of consecutive lines that are either:
43. /// - Indented by 4+ spaces or 1+ tab (beyond base_indent)
44. /// - Blank lines (which don't need indentation)
45. ///
46. /// The block ends when we hit a non-blank line that isn't indented enough.
47. pub(crate) fn parse_indented_code_block(
48.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
49.     lines: &[&str],
50.     start_pos: usize,
51.     bq_depth: usize,
52.     base_indent: usize,
53. ) -> usize {
54.     use super::blockquotes::count_blockquote_markers;
55. 
56.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::CODE_BLOCK.into());
57.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::CODE_CONTENT.into());
58. 
59.     let mut current_pos = start_pos;
60.     // Total indent needed: base (e.g., footnote) + 4 for code
61.     let code_indent = base_indent + 4;
62. 
63.     while current_pos < lines.len() {
64.         let line = lines[current_pos];
65. 
66.         // Strip blockquote markers to get inner content
67.         let (line_bq_depth, inner) = count_blockquote_markers(line);
68. 
69.         // If blockquote depth decreases, code block ends (we've left the blockquote)
70.         if line_bq_depth < bq_depth {
71.             break;
72.         }
73. 
74.         // Blank lines need look-ahead: only include if next non-blank line continues the code
75.         if inner.trim().is_empty() {
76.             // Check if code continues after this blank line
77.             let mut look_pos = current_pos + 1;
78.             let mut continues = false;
79.             while look_pos < lines.len() {
80.                 let (look_bq_depth, look_inner) = count_blockquote_markers(lines[look_pos]);
81.                 if look_bq_depth < bq_depth {
82.                     break;
83.                 }
84.                 if look_inner.trim_end_matches('\n').trim().is_empty() {
85.                     look_pos += 1;
86.                     continue;
87.                 }
88.                 let (look_indent, _) = leading_indent(look_inner);
89.                 if look_indent >= code_indent {
90.                     continues = true;
91.                 }
92.                 break;
93.             }
94.             if !continues {
95.                 break;
96.             }
97.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "");
98.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), "\n");
99.             current_pos += 1;
100.             continue;
101.         }
102. 
103.         // Check if line is indented enough (base_indent + 4 for code)
104.         let (indent_cols, indent_bytes) = leading_indent(inner);
105.         if indent_cols < code_indent {
106.             break;
107.         }
108. 
109.         // For losslessness: emit ALL indentation as WHITESPACE, then emit remaining content
110.         // The formatter can decide how to handle the indentation
111.         if indent_bytes > 0 {
112.             let indent_str = &inner[..indent_bytes];
113.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), indent_str);
114.         }
115. 
116.         // Get the content after the indentation
117.         let content = &inner[indent_bytes..];
118. 
119.         // Split off trailing newline if present (from split_inclusive)
120.         let (content_without_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(content);
```

</details>

932. 
933.     fn parse_prepared(
934.         &self,
935.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
936.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
937.         lines: &[&str],
938.         line_pos: usize,
939.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
940.     ) -> usize {
941.         use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
942. 
943.         let div_fence = if let Some(info) = payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<DivFenceInfo>()) {
944.             info.clone()
945.         } else {
946.             try_parse_div_fence_open(lines[line_pos]).expect("Div fence info should exist")
947.         };
948. 
949.         // Start FencedDiv node
950.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::FENCED_DIV.into());
951. 
952.         // Emit opening fence with attributes
953.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_FENCE_OPEN.into());
954. 
955.         let full_line = lines[line_pos];
956.         let trimmed = full_line.trim_start();
957. 
958.         // Emit leading whitespace
959.         let leading_ws_len = full_line.len() - trimmed.len();
960.         if leading_ws_len > 0 {
961.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &full_line[..leading_ws_len]);
962.         }
963. 
964.         // Emit fence colons
965.         let fence_str: String = ":".repeat(div_fence.fence_count);
966.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), &fence_str);
967. 
968.         // Parse everything after colons
969.         let after_colons = &trimmed[div_fence.fence_count..];
970.         let (content_before_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(after_colons);
971. 
972.         // Emit optional space before attributes
973.         let has_leading_space = content_before_newline.starts_with(' ');
974.         if has_leading_space {
975.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
976.         }
977. 
978.         let content_after_space = if has_leading_space {
979.             &content_before_newline[1..]
980.         } else {
981.             content_before_newline
982.         };
983. 
984.         // Emit attributes as DIV_INFO
985.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_INFO.into());
986.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), &div_fence.attributes);
987.         builder.finish_node();
988. 
989.         // Trailing symmetric colons
990.         let (trailing_space, trailing_colons) = if div_fence.attributes.starts_with('{') {
991.             if let Some(close_idx) = content_after_space.find('}') {
992.                 let after_attrs = &content_after_space[close_idx + 1..];
993.                 let trailing = after_attrs.trim_start();
994.                 let space_count = after_attrs.len() - trailing.len();
995.                 if !trailing.is_empty() && trailing.chars().all(|c| c == ':') {
996.                     (space_count > 0, trailing)
997.                 } else {
998.                     (false, "")
999.                 }
1000.             } else {
1001.                 (false, "")
1002.             }
1003.         } else {
1004.             let after_attrs = &content_after_space[div_fence.attributes.len()..];
1005.             if let Some(after_space) = after_attrs.strip_prefix(' ') {
1006.                 if !after_space.is_empty() && after_space.chars().all(|c| c == ':') {
1007.                     (true, after_space)
1008.                 } else {
1009.                     (false, "")
1010.                 }
1011.             } else {
1012.                 (false, "")
1013.             }
1014.         };
1015.
```

<sub>⏱️ 114m 28s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

You’re right to flag this: fenced divs can’t be safely migrated into the dispatcher yet because they must mutate the container stack, and the current `BlockParser` interface doesn’t allow that. I’m going to revert the fenced-div dispatcher work (and the `in_fenced_div` context field) and keep the HTML/LaTeX/line-block migrations.

35. ///
36. /// Used by block parsers that need to handle indentation stripping
37. /// when parsing inside list items (e.g., fenced code blocks).
38. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
39. pub(crate) struct ListIndentInfo {
40.     /// Number of columns to strip for list content
41.     pub content_col: usize,
42. }
43. 
44. /// Context passed to block parsers for decision-making.
45. ///
46. /// Contains immutable references to parser state that block parsers need
47. /// to check conditions (e.g., blank line before, blockquote depth, etc.).
48. pub(crate) struct BlockContext<'a> {
49.     /// Current line content (after blockquote markers stripped if any)
50.     pub content: &'a str,
51. 
52.     /// Whether we are currently inside a fenced div container.
53.     pub in_fenced_div: bool,
54. 
55.     /// Whether there was a blank line before this line
56.     pub has_blank_before: bool,
57. 
58.     /// Whether we're at document start (pos == 0)
59.     pub at_document_start: bool,
60. 
61.     /// Current blockquote depth
62.     pub blockquote_depth: usize,
63. 
64.     /// Parser configuration
65.     pub config: &'a Config,
66. 
67.     // NOTE: we intentionally do not store `&ContainerStack` here to avoid
68.     // long-lived borrows of `self` in the main parser loop.
69.     /// Base indentation from container context (footnotes, definitions)
70.     pub content_indent: usize,
71. 
72.     /// List indentation info if inside a list
73.     pub list_indent_info: Option<ListIndentInfo>,
74. 
75.     /// Next line content for lookahead (used by setext headings)
76.     pub next_line: Option<&'a str>,
77. }
78. 
79. /// Result of detecting whether a block can be parsed.
80. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
```

891. // Fenced Div Parsers (position #6)
892. // ============================================================================
893. 
894. pub(crate) struct FencedDivOpenParser;
895. 
896. impl BlockParser for FencedDivOpenParser {
897.     fn can_parse(
898.         &self,
899.         ctx: &BlockContext,
900.         lines: &[&str],
901.         line_pos: usize,
902.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
903.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
904.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
905.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
906.     }
907. 
908.     fn detect_prepared(
909.         &self,
910.         ctx: &BlockContext,
911.         _lines: &[&str],
912.         _line_pos: usize,
913.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
914.         // Fenced divs require blank line before (or doc start).
915.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
916.             return None;
917.         }
918. 
919.         let div_fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(ctx.content)?;
920.         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, Some(Box::new(div_fence))))
921.     }
922. 
923.     fn parse(
924.         &self,
925.         ctx: &BlockContext,
926.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
927.         lines: &[&str],
928.         line_pos: usize,
929.     ) -> usize {
930.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
931.     }
932. 
933.     fn parse_prepared(
934.         &self,
935.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
936.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
937.         lines: &[&str],
938.         line_pos: usize,
939.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
940.     ) -> usize {
941.         use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
942. 
943.         let div_fence = if let Some(info) = payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<DivFenceInfo>()) {
944.             info.clone()
945.         } else {
946.             try_parse_div_fence_open(lines[line_pos]).expect("Div fence info should exist")
947.         };
948. 
949.         // Start FencedDiv node
950.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::FENCED_DIV.into());
951. 
952.         // Keep core behavior: push fenced-div container so `core.rs` knows we're inside.
953.         // (Container closing/finishing happens in `core.rs` when it sees DIV_FENCE_CLOSE.)
954.         crate::parser::utils::container_stack::push_fenced_div(ctx);
955. 
956.         // Emit opening fence with attributes
957.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_FENCE_OPEN.into());
958. 
959.         let full_line = lines[line_pos];
960.         let trimmed = full_line.trim_start();
961. 
962.         // Emit leading whitespace
963.         let leading_ws_len = full_line.len() - trimmed.len();
964.         if leading_ws_len > 0 {
965.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &full_line[..leading_ws_len]);
966.         }
967. 
968.         // Emit fence colons
969.         let fence_str: String = ":".repeat(div_fence.fence_count);
970.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), &fence_str);
971. 
972.         // Parse everything after colons
973.         let after_colons = &trimmed[div_fence.fence_count..];
974.         let (content_before_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(after_colons);
975. 
976.         // Emit optional space before attributes
977.         let has_leading_space = content_before_newline.starts_with(' ');
978.         if has_leading_space {
979.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
980.         }
981. 
982.         let content_after_space = if has_leading_space {
983.             &content_before_newline[1..]
984.         } else {
985.             content_before_newline
986.         };
987. 
988.         // Emit attributes as DIV_INFO
989.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_INFO.into());
990.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), &div_fence.attributes);
991.         builder.finish_node();
992. 
993.         // Trailing symmetric colons
994.         let (trailing_space, trailing_colons) = if div_fence.attributes.starts_with('{') {
995.             if let Some(close_idx) = content_after_space.find('}') {
996.                 let after_attrs = &content_after_space[close_idx + 1..];
997.                 let trailing = after_attrs.trim_start();
998.                 let space_count = after_attrs.len() - trailing.len();
999.                 if !trailing.is_empty() && trailing.chars().all(|c| c == ':') {
1000.                     (space_count > 0, trailing)
1001.                 } else {
1002.                     (false, "")
1003.                 }
1004.             } else {
1005.                 (false, "")
1006.             }
1007.         } else {
1008.             let after_attrs = &content_after_space[div_fence.attributes.len()..];
1009.             if let Some(after_space) = after_attrs.strip_prefix(' ') {
1010.                 if !after_space.is_empty() && after_space.chars().all(|c| c == ':') {
1011.                     (true, after_space)
1012.                 } else {
1013.                     (false, "")
1014.                 }
1015.             } else {
1016.                 (false, "")
1017.             }
1018.         };
1019. 
1020.         if trailing_space {
1021.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
1022.         }
1023.         if !trailing_colons.is_empty() {
1024.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), trailing_colons);
1025.         }
1026. 
1027.         if !newline_str.is_empty() {
1028.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
1029.         }
1030. 
1031.         builder.finish_node(); // DIV_FENCE_OPEN
1032. 
1033.         1
1034.     }
1035. 
1036.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
1037.         "fenced_div_open"
1038.     }
1039. }
1040. 
1041. pub(crate) struct FencedDivCloseParser;
1042. 
1043. impl BlockParser for FencedDivCloseParser {
1044.     fn can_parse(
1045.         &self,
1046.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1047.         lines: &[&str],
1048.         line_pos: usize,
1049.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
1050.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
1051.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
1052.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
1053.     }
1054. 
1055.     fn detect_prepared(
1056.         &self,
1057.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1058.         _lines: &[&str],
1059.         _line_pos: usize,
1060.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
1061.         if !ctx.in_fenced_div || !is_div_closing_fence(ctx.content) {
1062.             return None;
1063.         }
1064. 
1065.         // Closing fences should be able to interrupt paragraphs.
1066.         let detection = if ctx.has_blank_before || ctx.at_document_start {
1067.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
1068.         } else {
1069.             BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
1070.         };
1071. 
1072.         Some((detection, None))
1073.     }
1074. 
1075.     fn parse(
1076.         &self,
1077.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
1078.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1079.         lines: &[&str],
1080.         line_pos: usize,
1081.     ) -> usize {
1082.         use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
1083. 
1084.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_FENCE_CLOSE.into());
1085. 
1086.         let full_line = lines[line_pos];
1087.         let trimmed = full_line.trim_start();
1088. 
1089.         // Emit leading whitespace
1090.         let leading_ws_len = full_line.len() - trimmed.len();
1091.         if leading_ws_len > 0 {
1092.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &full_line[..leading_ws_len]);
1093.         }
1094. 
1095.         let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(trimmed);
1096.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), content_without_newline);
1097.         if !line_ending.is_empty() {
1098.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
1099.         }
1100. 
1101.         builder.finish_node();
1102.         1
1103.     }
1104. 
1105.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
1106.         "fenced_div_close"
1107.     }
1108. }
1109.

1120.         lines: &[&str],
1121.         line_pos: usize,
1122.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
1123.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
1124.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
1125.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)

1214. 
1215.     fn parse(
1216.         &self,
1217.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1218.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1219.         lines: &[&str],
1220.         pos: usize,
1221.     ) -> usize {
1222.         // Get text line and underline line
1223.         let text_line = lines[pos];
1224.         let underline_line = lines[pos + 1];
1225. 
1226.         // Determine level from underline character (no need to call try_parse again)
1227.         // can_parse() already validated this is a valid setext heading
1228.         let underline_char = underline_line.trim().chars().next().unwrap_or('=');
1229.         let level = if underline_char == '=' { 1 } else { 2 };
1230. 
1231.         // Emit the setext heading
1232.         emit_setext_heading(builder, text_line, underline_line, level, ctx.config);
1233. 
1234.         // Return lines consumed: text line + underline line
1235.         2
1236.     }
1237. 
1238.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
1239.         "setext_heading"
1240.     }
1241. }
1242. 
1243. // ============================================================================
1244. // Block Parser Registry
1245. // ============================================================================
1246. 
1247. /// Registry of block parsers, ordered by priority.
1248. ///
1249. /// This dispatcher tries each parser in order until one succeeds.
1250. /// The ordering follows Pandoc's approach - explicit list order rather
1251. /// than numeric priorities.
1252. pub(crate) struct BlockParserRegistry {
1253.     parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>,
1254. }
1255. 
1256. impl BlockParserRegistry {
1257.     /// Create a new registry with all block parsers.
1258.     ///
1259.     /// Order matters! Parsers are tried in the order listed here.
1260.     /// This follows Pandoc's design where ordering is explicit and documented.
1261.     ///
1262.     /// **Pandoc reference order** (from pandoc/src/Text/Pandoc/Readers/Markdown.hs:487-515):
1263.     /// 1. blanklines (handled separately in our parser)
1264.     /// 2. codeBlockFenced
1265.     /// 3. yamlMetaBlock' ← YAML metadata comes early!
1266.     /// 4. bulletList
1267.     /// 5. divHtml
1268.     /// 6. divFenced
1269.     /// 7. header ← ATX headings
1270.     /// 8. lhsCodeBlock
1271.     /// 9. htmlBlock
1272.     /// 10. table
1273.     /// 11. codeBlockIndented
1274.     /// 12. rawTeXBlock (LaTeX)
1275.     /// 13. lineBlock
1276.     /// 14. blockQuote
1277.     /// 15. hrule ← Horizontal rules come AFTER headers!
1278.     /// 16. orderedList
1279.     /// 17. definitionList
1280.     /// 18. noteBlock (footnotes)
1281.     /// 19. referenceKey ← Reference definitions
1282.     /// 20. abbrevKey
1283.     /// 21. para
1284.     /// 22. plain
1285.     pub fn new() -> Self {
1286.         let parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>> = vec![
1287.             // Match Pandoc's ordering to ensure correct precedence:
1288.             // (0) Pandoc title block (must be at document start).
1289.             Box::new(PandocTitleBlockParser),
1290.             // (2) Fenced code blocks - can interrupt paragraphs!
1291.             Box::new(FencedCodeBlockParser),
1292.             // (3) YAML metadata - before headers and hrules!
1293.             Box::new(YamlMetadataParser),
1294.             // (7) Setext headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
1295.             // Must come before ATX to properly handle `---` disambiguation
1296.             Box::new(SetextHeadingParser),
1297.             // (7) ATX headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
1298.             Box::new(AtxHeadingParser),
1299.             // (6) Fenced divs
1300.             Box::new(FencedDivCloseParser),
1301.             Box::new(FencedDivOpenParser),
1302.             // (9) HTML blocks
1303.             Box::new(HtmlBlockParser),
1304.             // (12) LaTeX environment blocks
1305.             Box::new(LatexEnvironmentParser),
1306.             // (13) Line blocks
1307.             Box::new(LineBlockParser),
1308.             // (15) Horizontal rules - AFTER headings per Pandoc
1309.             Box::new(HorizontalRuleParser),
1310.             // Figures (standalone images) - Pandoc doesn't have these
1311.             Box::new(FigureParser),
1312.             // (19) Reference definitions
1313.             Box::new(ReferenceDefinitionParser),
1314.             // TODO: Migrate remaining blocks in Pandoc order:
1315.             // - (4-6) Lists and divs (bulletList, divHtml, divFenced)
1316.             // - (10) Tables (grid, multiline, pipe, simple)
1317.             // - (11) Indented code blocks (AFTER fenced!)
1318.             // - (13) Line blocks
1319.             // - (16) Ordered lists
1320.             // - (17) Definition lists
```

</details>

810.     fn has_nested_block_structure(&self, content: &str) -> bool {
811.         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
812.             content,
813.             in_fenced_div: self.in_fenced_div(),
814.             has_blank_before: true,
815.             at_document_start: false,
816.             blockquote_depth: self.current_blockquote_depth(),

821.         };
822. 
823.         // Note: we only use the registry for its “is this a block start?” decision;
824.         // it should not rely on `lines` here (setext is gated by `has_blank_before`).
825.         self.block_registry

+/// Pandoc title block parser (% Title ...)
+pub(crate) struct PandocTitleBlockParser;
+
+impl BlockParser for PandocTitleBlockParser {
+    fn can_parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
+        self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
+            .map(|(d, _)| d)
+            .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
+    }
+
+    fn detect_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        _lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
+        // Must be at document start.
+        if !ctx.at_document_start || line_pos != 0 {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        // Must start with % (allow leading spaces).
+        if !ctx.content.trim_start().starts_with('%') {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, None))
+    }
+
+    fn parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> usize {
+        self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
+    }
+
+    fn parse_prepared(
+        &self,
+        _ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+        _payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
+    ) -> usize {
+        let new_pos = try_parse_pandoc_title_block(lines, line_pos, builder)
+            .unwrap_or(line_pos + 1);
+        new_pos - line_pos
+    }
+
+    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
+        "pandoc_title_block"
+    }
+}
+

+// ============================================================================
+// HTML Block Parser (position #9)
+// ============================================================================
+
+pub(crate) struct HtmlBlockParser;
+
+impl BlockParser for HtmlBlockParser {
+    fn can_parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
+        self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
+            .map(|(d, _)| d)
+            .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
+    }
+
+    fn detect_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        _lines: &[&str],
+        _line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
+        if !ctx.config.extensions.raw_html {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        let block_type = try_parse_html_block_start(ctx.content)?;
+
+        // Match previous behavior (and Pandoc-ish semantics): HTML blocks can interrupt
+        // paragraphs; blank lines are not required.
+        let detection = if ctx.has_blank_before || ctx.at_document_start {
+            BlockDetectionResult::Yes
+        } else {
+            BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
+        };
+
+        Some((detection, Some(Box::new(block_type))))
+    }
+
+    fn parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> usize {
+        self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
+    }
+
+    fn parse_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+        payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
+    ) -> usize {
+        let block_type = if let Some(bt) =
+            payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<HtmlBlockType>())
+        {
+            bt.clone()
+        } else {
+            try_parse_html_block_start(ctx.content).expect("HTML block type should exist")
+        };
+
+        let new_pos = parse_html_block(builder, lines, line_pos, block_type, ctx.blockquote_depth);
+        new_pos - line_pos
+    }
+
+    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
+        "html_block"
+    }
+}
+
+// ============================================================================
+// LaTeX Environment Parser (position #12)
+// ============================================================================
+
+pub(crate) struct LatexEnvironmentParser;
+
+impl BlockParser for LatexEnvironmentParser {
+    fn can_parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
+        self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
+            .map(|(d, _)| d)
+            .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
+    }
+
+    fn detect_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        _lines: &[&str],
+        _line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
+        if !ctx.config.extensions.raw_tex {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        let env_info = try_parse_latex_env_begin(ctx.content)?;
+
+        // Like HTML blocks, raw TeX blocks should be able to interrupt paragraphs.
+        let detection = if ctx.has_blank_before || ctx.at_document_start {
+            BlockDetectionResult::Yes
+        } else {
+            BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
+        };
+
+        Some((detection, Some(Box::new(env_info))))
+    }
+
+    fn parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> usize {
+        self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
+    }
+
+    fn parse_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+        payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
+    ) -> usize {
+        let env_info = if let Some(info) =
+            payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<LatexEnvInfo>())
+        {
+            info.clone()
+        } else {
+            try_parse_latex_env_begin(ctx.content).expect("LaTeX env info should exist")
+        };
+
+        let new_pos = parse_latex_environment(
+            builder,
+            lines,
+            line_pos,
+            env_info,
+            ctx.blockquote_depth,
+        );
+        new_pos - line_pos
+    }
+
+    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
+        "latex_environment"
+    }
+}
+
+// ============================================================================
+// Line Block Parser (position #13)
+// ============================================================================
+
+pub(crate) struct LineBlockParser;
+
+impl BlockParser for LineBlockParser {
+    fn can_parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
+        self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
+            .map(|(d, _)| d)
+            .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
+    }
+
+    fn detect_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        _lines: &[&str],
+        _line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
+        if !ctx.config.extensions.line_blocks {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        if try_parse_line_block_start(ctx.content).is_none() {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        // Line blocks can interrupt paragraphs.
+        let detection = if ctx.has_blank_before || ctx.at_document_start {
+            BlockDetectionResult::Yes
+        } else {
+            BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
+        };
+
+        Some((detection, None))
+    }
+
+    fn parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> usize {
+        self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
+    }
+
+    fn parse_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+        _payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
+    ) -> usize {
+        let new_pos = parse_line_block(lines, line_pos, builder, ctx.config);
+        new_pos - line_pos
+    }
+
+    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
+        "line_block"
+    }
+}
+

<sub>⏱️ 118m 4s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

HTML/LaTeX/line block are now migrated into the dispatcher and `core.rs` no longer parses HTML/LaTeX blocks directly; tests are green.

On fenced divs: I started migrating them too, but hit the key architectural issue—we can’t fully move fenced divs into the dispatcher cleanly until the dispatcher has a way to interact with the container stack (open needs to `push(Container::FencedDiv)` and close needs to pop/finish it). I reverted the dispatcher-side fenced-div attempt and restored the original fenced-div parsing in `core.rs`, so behavior is unchanged and we can discuss the right integration approach next.

<sub>⏱️ 118m 58s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

/home/jola/.local/state/.copilot/session-state/8111c140-3b00-44e8-81bb-2bc5c0b6ac76/plan.md (lines 1--1)

<details>
<summary>62 lines</summary>

```
1. # Plan: Block dispatcher “prepared match” refactor
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Current `BlockParser::can_parse()` often does non-trivial work (e.g. fenced code parses fence + `InfoString::parse`, setext allocates, figure uses inline image parsing). This work is frequently repeated because:
5. - several parsers call `try_parse_*` again inside `parse()`
6. - `core.rs` calls `block_registry.detect(...)` multiple times per line in different branches
7. - `core.rs` separately re-implements “is this a block element marker?” checks (e.g. Definition continuation guard), further duplicating detection.
8. 
9. ## Goal
10. Keep the good part of the refactor (Pandoc-like ordered registry + ability to “prepare/flush buffers before emitting”), while eliminating duplicate detection/parsing and reducing `core.rs` complexity.
11. 
12. ## Approach (high level)
13. Introduce a **prepared match** concept: detection returns a match object that caches any expensive parsing results, and emission consumes that cached data (no re-parsing). Then rework `core.rs` so each line does **one** detection pass, decides whether the match is allowed (blank-line rules / interrupt rules), performs preparation, and emits.
14. 
15. ## Key design decision (to confirm)
16. Two viable representations:
17. 1) **Trait objects + opaque payload** (recommended for minimal churn): registry remains `Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>`. Detection returns a `BlockMatch` containing a parser index + interrupt/blank requirements + `Box<dyn Any>` payload; emission downcasts payload.
18. 2) **Enum registry + typed payload** (slightly faster/cleaner types, but larger refactor): registry becomes `Vec<BlockParserKind>` and match payload becomes an enum.
19. 
20. ## Workplan
21. 
22. ### A. Baseline & safety
23. - [x] Run baseline tests/benchmarks to capture current behavior (`cargo test` at minimum).
24. - [ ] Identify a small set of “high-signal” golden cases to watch while refactoring (fenced code, setext, yaml/hrule ambiguity, refdefs).
25. 
26. ### B. Introduce prepared-match API (non-invasive)
27. - [x] Add new types in `src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs`:
28.   - `PreparedBlockMatch` (parser index + detection + cached payload)
29. - [x] Extend `BlockParserRegistry` with new entrypoints (keep old API temporarily):
30.   - `detect_prepared(...) -> Option<PreparedBlockMatch>`
31.   - `parse_prepared(match, ...) -> usize`
32. 
33. ### C. Convert migrated parsers (start with biggest perf win)
34. - [x] Fenced code: detection parses fence + `InfoString` once; parse reuses cached `FenceInfo`.
35. - [x] ATX heading: detection returns cached level; parse uses it (no second `try_parse_atx_heading`).
36. - [ ] YAML: optionally cache “is yaml not hrule” disambiguation.
37. - [ ] Figure: cache validation span from inline image parse so it isn’t repeated.
38. - [x] Setext: remove allocation in detection (no `Vec`).
39. - [x] Reference definition: cache parse tuple from `try_parse_reference_definition` (payload currently unused by emitter).
40. 
41. ### D. Refactor `core.rs` to “single detect per line”
42. - [x] Create one `BlockContext` per line and reuse it (avoid repeated rebuilds).
43. - [x] Replace the multiple dispatcher detections with one flow per line (detect once, then prepare/emit).
44. - [x] Rework the Definition continuation guard (`is_block_element` at ~1041) to delegate to the registry for migrated blocks (keeps special-cases for def/list/bq/html/tex).
45. - [x] Delegate nested-block marker probes (Definition continuation after blank line) to the registry instead of hardcoding fence/div/hrule checks.
46. 
47. Notes:
48. - `BlockContext` no longer stores `&ContainerStack` to avoid long-lived borrows blocking mutation during preparation/emission.
49. 
50. ### E. Consolidate ordering/precedence in the registry
51. - [ ] Move remaining “block start” checks from `core.rs` into the registry progressively (tables/divs/html/latex/etc.), keeping Pandoc ordering notes.
52. - [ ] Ensure special-case precedence rules stay correct (e.g., YAML vs hrule, setext vs hrule, list vs header edge cases).
53. 
54. ### F. Cleanup
55. - [ ] Remove old `can_parse/parse` API once all migrated blocks use prepared matches.
56. - [ ] Remove redundant detection logic left in `core.rs`.
57. - [ ] Run full test suite + clippy + fmt check.
58. 
59. ## Notes / Constraints
60. - Maintain single-pass, lossless CST emission; prepared matches must not emit to the builder.
61. - Prepared payload should avoid borrowing `ctx.content` directly unless lifetimes are carefully managed; prefer storing indices / owned small strings only when needed.
62. - Performance expectation: dynamic dispatch/downcast overhead is negligible vs string scanning/allocs; eliminating repeated `InfoString::parse` etc. is the main win.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 119m 16s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Options for fenced div integration (given the container-stack dependency) boil down to **who owns container mutations**:

1) **“Dispatcher emits, core mutates containers” (recommended, minimal change)**
- Add a tiny “side-effect” signal to the prepared match, e.g. `PreparedBlockMatch { kind: ContainerEffect::OpenFencedDiv | CloseFencedDiv | None, … }`.
- Dispatcher **only emits CST nodes** for `DIV_FENCE_OPEN` / `DIV_FENCE_CLOSE` (and starts `FENCED_DIV`), but `core.rs` performs `containers.push(Container::FencedDiv)` / `close_containers_to(...)` around the dispatcher emission.
- Pros: preserves current ownership model (only `core` touches `containers`), avoids borrow/lifetime traps, keeps BlockContext simple.
- Cons: fenced-div handling remains “split brain” (some logic in dispatcher, some in core), but it’s a clean split.

2) **Pass a `ContainerSink`/callback into `parse_prepared`**
- Extend `parse_prepared(..., hooks: &mut dyn BlockParseHooks)` where hooks has methods like `open_fenced_div()` / `close_fenced_div()`.
- `core.rs` provides the implementation that mutates `self.containers`; dispatcher stays generic.
- Pros: keeps fenced-div logic “inside the fenced-div parser” (more modular).
- Cons: introduces a new interface threaded through registry; slightly more moving parts.

3) **Let block parsers mutate containers directly**
- Make `parse_prepared` take `&mut Parser` (or `&mut ContainerStack`) instead of just builder/lines.
- Pros: simplest inside the parser.
- Cons: reintroduces the borrow-checker problems you already hit (long-lived borrows / tight coupling), and makes the dispatcher much less reusable/testable.

4) **Don’t migrate fenced divs yet (status quo)**
- Keep them in `core.rs` until we have a more general “container-aware block parser” design for other containerized blocks too (lists/footnotes/definition lists).
- Pros: zero risk.
- Cons: fenced div remains a big chunk in `core.rs`.

I updated `plan.md` to mark HTML/LaTeX/line-block as done and to add a fenced-div decision item; next step would be implementing option (1) or (2).

900. 
901. -- parse raw text for one list item, excluding start marker and continuations
902. rawListItem :: PandocMonad m
903.             => Bool -- four space rule
904.             -> MarkdownParser m a
905.             -> MarkdownParser m (Text, Int)
906. rawListItem fourSpaceRule start = try $ do
907.   pos1 <- getPosition
908.   start
909.   pos2 <- getPosition
910.   let continuationIndent = if fourSpaceRule
911.                               then 4
912.                               else sourceColumn pos2 - sourceColumn pos1
913.   first <- anyLine
914.   rest <- many (do notFollowedBy listStart
915.                    notFollowedBy (() <$ codeBlockFenced)
916.                    notFollowedBy blankline
917.                    listLine continuationIndent)
918.   blanks <- manyChar blankline
919.   let result = T.unlines (first:rest) <> blanks
920.   return (result, continuationIndent)
921. 
922. -- continuation of a list item - indented and separated by blankline
923. -- or (in compact lists) endline.
924. -- note: nested lists are parsed as continuations
925. listContinuation :: PandocMonad m => Int -> MarkdownParser m Text
926. listContinuation continuationIndent = try $ do
927.   x <- try $ do
928.          notFollowedBy blankline
929.          notFollowedByHtmlCloser
930.          notFollowedByDivCloser
931.          gobbleSpaces continuationIndent
932.          anyLineNewline
933.   xs <- many $ try $ do
934.          notFollowedBy blankline
935.          notFollowedByHtmlCloser
936.          notFollowedByDivCloser
937.          gobbleSpaces continuationIndent <|> notFollowedBy' listStart
938.          anyLineNewline
939.   blanks <- manyChar blankline
940.   return $ T.concat (x:xs) <> blanks
941. 
942. -- Variant of blanklines that doesn't require blank lines
943. -- before a fence or eof.
944. blanklines' :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m Text
945. blanklines' = blanklines <|> try checkDivCloser
946.   where checkDivCloser = do
947.           guardEnabled Ext_fenced_divs
948.           divLevel <- stateFencedDivLevel <$> getState
949.           guard (divLevel >= 1)
950.           lookAhead divFenceEnd
951.           return ""
952. 
953. notFollowedByDivCloser :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m ()
954. notFollowedByDivCloser =
955.   guardDisabled Ext_fenced_divs <|>
956.   do divLevel <- stateFencedDivLevel <$> getState
957.      guard (divLevel < 1) <|> notFollowedBy divFenceEnd
958. 
959. notFollowedByHtmlCloser :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m ()
960. notFollowedByHtmlCloser = do
961.   inHtmlBlock <- stateInHtmlBlock <$> getState
962.   case inHtmlBlock of
963.         Just t  -> notFollowedBy' $ htmlTag (~== TagClose t)
964.         Nothing -> return ()
965. 
966. listItem :: PandocMonad m
967.          => Bool -- four-space rule
968.          -> MarkdownParser m a
969.          -> MarkdownParser m (F Blocks)
970. listItem fourSpaceRule start = try $ do
971.   -- parsing with ListItemState forces markers at beginning of lines to
972.   -- count as list item markers, even if not separated by blank space.
973.   -- see definition of "endline"
974.   state <- getState
975.   let oldContext = stateParserContext state
976.   setState $ state {stateParserContext = ListItemState}
977.   (first, continuationIndent) <- rawListItem fourSpaceRule start
978.   continuations <- many (listContinuation continuationIndent)
979.   -- parse the extracted block, which may contain various block elements:
980.   let raw = T.concat (first:continuations)
981.   contents <- parseFromString' parseBlocks raw
982.   updateState (\st -> st {stateParserContext = oldContext})
983.   exts <- getOption readerExtensions
984.   return $ B.fromList . taskListItemFromAscii exts . B.toList <$> contents
985. 
986. orderedList :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m (F Blocks)
987. orderedList = try $ do
988.   (start, style, delim) <- lookAhead (orderedListStart Nothing)
989.   unless (style `elem` [DefaultStyle, Decimal, Example] &&
990.           delim `elem` [DefaultDelim, Period]) $
991.     guardEnabled Ext_fancy_lists
992.   when (style == Example) $ guardEnabled Ext_example_lists
993.   fourSpaceRule <- (True <$ guardEnabled Ext_four_space_rule)
994.                <|> return (style == Example)
995.   items <- fmap sequence $ many1 $ listItem fourSpaceRule
996.                  (orderedListStart (Just (style, delim)))
997.   start' <- if style == Example
998.                then return start
999.                else (start <$ guardEnabled Ext_startnum) <|> return 1
1000.   return $ B.orderedListWith (start', style, delim) <$> fmap compactify items
1001. 
1002. bulletList :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m (F Blocks)
1003. bulletList = do
1004.   fourSpaceRule <- (True <$ guardEnabled Ext_four_space_rule)
1005.                <|> return False
1006.   items <- fmap sequence $ many1 $ listItem fourSpaceRule bulletListStart
1007.   return $ B.bulletList <$> fmap compactify items
1008. 
1009. -- definition lists
1010. 
1011. defListStart :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m ()
1012. defListStart = do
1013.   nonindentSpaces
1014.   char ':' <|> char '~'
1015.   gobbleSpaces 1 <|> () <$ lookAhead newline
1016.   try (gobbleAtMostSpaces 3 >> notFollowedBy spaceChar) <|> return ()
1017. 
1018. definitionListItem :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m (F (Inlines, [Blocks]))
1019. definitionListItem = try $ do
1020.   rawLine' <- anyLine
```

</details>

2123. spanHtml :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m (F Inlines)
2124. spanHtml = do
2125.   guardEnabled Ext_native_spans
2126.   try $ do
2127.     (TagOpen _ attrs, _) <- htmlTag (~== TagOpen ("span" :: Text) [])
2128.     contents <- mconcat <$> manyTill inline (htmlTag (~== TagClose ("span" :: Text)))
2129.     let ident = fromMaybe "" $ lookup "id" attrs
2130.     let classes = maybe [] T.words $ lookup "class" attrs
2131.     let keyvals = [(k,v) | (k,v) <- attrs, k /= "id" && k /= "class"]
2132.     return $ wrapSpan (ident, classes, keyvals) <$> contents
2133. 
2134. divHtml :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m (F Blocks)
2135. divHtml = do
2136.   guardEnabled Ext_native_divs
2137.   try $ do
2138.     openpos <- getPosition
2139.     (TagOpen _ attrs, _) <- htmlTag (~== TagOpen ("div" :: Text) [])
2140.     -- we set stateInHtmlBlock so that closing tags that can be either block
2141.     -- or inline will not be parsed as inline tags
2142.     oldInHtmlBlock <- stateInHtmlBlock <$> getState
2143.     updateState $ \st -> st{ stateInHtmlBlock = Just "div" }
2144.     optional blanklines
2145.     contents <- mconcat <$>
2146.                 many (notFollowedBy' (htmlTag (~== TagClose ("div" :: Text)))
2147.                       >> block)
2148.     void (htmlTag (~== TagClose ("div" :: Text))) <|>
2149.        (getPosition >>= report . UnclosedDiv openpos)
2150.     let ident = fromMaybe "" $ lookup "id" attrs
2151.     let classes = maybe [] T.words $ lookup "class" attrs
2152.     let keyvals = [(k,v) | (k,v) <- attrs, k /= "id" && k /= "class"]
2153.     updateState $ \st -> st{ stateInHtmlBlock = oldInHtmlBlock }
2154.     return $ B.divWith (ident, classes, keyvals) <$> contents
2155. 
2156. divFenced :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m (F Blocks)
2157. divFenced = do
2158.   guardEnabled Ext_fenced_divs
2159.   try $ do
2160.     openpos <- getPosition
2161.     string ":::"
2162.     skipMany (char ':')
2163.     skipMany spaceChar
2164.     attribs <- attributes <|> ((\x -> ("",[x],[])) <$> many1Char nonspaceChar)
2165.     skipMany spaceChar
2166.     skipMany (char ':')
2167.     blankline
2168.     updateState $ \st ->
2169.       st{ stateFencedDivLevel = stateFencedDivLevel st + 1 }
2170.     bs <- mconcat <$> many (notFollowedBy divFenceEnd >> block)
2171.     divFenceEnd <|> (getPosition >>= report . UnclosedDiv openpos)
2172.     updateState $ \st ->
2173.       st{ stateFencedDivLevel = stateFencedDivLevel st - 1 }
2174.     return $ B.divWith attribs <$> bs
2175. 
2176. divFenceEnd :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m ()
2177. divFenceEnd = try $ do
2178.   string ":::"
2179.   skipMany (char ':')
2180.   blanklines
2181.   return ()
2182. 
2183. rawHtmlInline :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m (F Inlines)
2184. rawHtmlInline = do
2185.   guardEnabled Ext_raw_html
2186.   inHtmlBlock <- stateInHtmlBlock <$> getState
2187.   let isCloseBlockTag t = case inHtmlBlock of
2188.                                Just t' -> t ~== TagClose t'
2189.                                Nothing -> False
2190.   mdInHtml <- option False $
2191.                 (    guardEnabled Ext_markdown_in_html_blocks
2192.                  <|> guardEnabled Ext_markdown_attribute
2193.                 ) >> return True
2194.   (_,result) <- htmlTag $ if mdInHtml
2195.                              then (\x -> isInlineTag x &&
2196.                                          not (isCloseBlockTag x))
2197.                              else not . isTextTag
2198.   return $ return $ B.rawInline "html" result

1162. rawTeXBlock :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m (F Blocks)
1163. rawTeXBlock = do
1164.   guardEnabled Ext_raw_tex
1165.   result <- (B.rawBlock "tex" . trim . T.concat <$>
1166.                 many1 ((<>) <$> rawConTeXtEnvironment <*> spnl'))
1167.           <|> (B.rawBlock "tex" . trim . T.concat <$>
1168.                 many1 ((<>) <$> rawLaTeXBlock <*> spnl'))
1169.   return $ case B.toList result of
1170.                 [RawBlock _ cs]
1171.                   | T.all (`elem` [' ','\t','\n']) cs -> return mempty
1172.                 -- don't create a raw block for suppressed macro defs
1173.                 _ -> return result
1174. 
1175. rawHtmlBlocks :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m (F Blocks)
1176. rawHtmlBlocks = do
1177.   (TagOpen tagtype _, raw) <- htmlTag isBlockTag
1178.   let selfClosing = "/>" `T.isSuffixOf` raw
1179.   -- we don't want '<td>    text' to be a code block:
1180.   skipMany spaceChar
1181.   tabStop <- getOption readerTabStop
1182.   indentlevel <- option 0 $
1183.                  do blankline
1184.                     sum <$> many ( (1 <$ char ' ')
1185.                                    <|>
1186.                                    (tabStop <$ char '\t') )
1187.   -- try to find closing tag
1188.   -- we set stateInHtmlBlock so that closing tags that can be either block or
1189.   -- inline will not be parsed as inline tags
1190.   oldInHtmlBlock <- stateInHtmlBlock <$> getState
1191.   updateState $ \st -> st{ stateInHtmlBlock = Just tagtype }
1192.   let closer = htmlTag (~== TagClose tagtype)
1193.   let block' = try $ do
1194.                  gobbleAtMostSpaces indentlevel
1195.                  notFollowedBy' closer
1196.                  block
1197.   contents <- if selfClosing
1198.                  then return mempty
1199.                  else mconcat <$> many block'
1200.   result <-
1201.     try
1202.     (do gobbleAtMostSpaces indentlevel
1203.         (_, rawcloser) <- closer
1204.         return (return (B.rawBlock "html" $ stripMarkdownAttribute raw) <>
1205.                 contents <>
1206.                 return (B.rawBlock "html" rawcloser)))
1207.       <|> return (return (B.rawBlock "html" raw) <> contents)
1208.   updateState $ \st -> st{ stateInHtmlBlock = oldInHtmlBlock }
1209.   return result
1210. 
1211. -- remove markdown="1" attribute
1212. stripMarkdownAttribute :: Text -> Text
1213. stripMarkdownAttribute s = renderTags' $ map filterAttrib $ parseTags s
1214.   where filterAttrib (TagOpen t as) = TagOpen t
1215.                                         [(k,v) | (k,v) <- as, k /= "markdown"]
1216.         filterAttrib              x = x
1217. 
1218. --
1219. -- line block
1220. --
1221. 
1222. lineBlock :: PandocMonad m => MarkdownParser m (F Blocks)
1223. lineBlock = do
1224.   guardEnabled Ext_line_blocks
1225.   try $ do
1226.     lines' <- lineBlockLines >>=
1227.               mapM (parseFromString' (trimInlinesF <$> inlines))
1228.     return $ B.lineBlock <$> sequence lines'
1229. 
1230. --

<sub>⏱️ 121m 37s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Yes—the “container” approach is broadly correct for *us*, and Pandoc does something very similar conceptually, just expressed differently because it’s a monadic parser with mutable reader state rather than a CST builder + explicit stack.

Pandoc’s approach in `Markdown.hs`:
- It keeps **reader state flags/counters** that influence block parsing across lines (notably `stateFencedDivLevel`, and also `stateInHtmlBlock`, `stateParserContext`, etc.).
- For fenced divs specifically, it does **not** build a generic “container stack”; it increments/decrements a **fenced div nesting counter** around parsing the div body:
  - On open (`divFenced`): `stateFencedDivLevel += 1`, then parses blocks until `divFenceEnd`, then `stateFencedDivLevel -= 1`.
  - That state is then consulted in other places to adjust “blankline” / continuation behavior (e.g. `blanklines'`, `notFollowedByDivCloser`, list continuation checks).

How this maps to Panache:
- Your `ContainerStack` is effectively a *structured* version of Pandoc’s reader state: instead of “just counters/flags”, you track nested constructs (BlockQuote/ListItem/Paragraph/FencedDiv) and buffer content accordingly.
- That’s a good fit for a lossless CST builder because you need explicit open/close points and buffering anyway; Pandoc can “just parse into an AST” and rely on parser combinator backtracking + state.

So the issue you hit with fenced divs isn’t that containers are wrong—it’s that **Pandoc treats fenced div as a stateful block**, and in Panache that state currently lives in `core.rs` (`ContainerStack`). To migrate fenced divs cleanly, the dispatcher needs a way to trigger a small state transition (push/pop / level inc-dec), analogous to Pandoc’s `stateFencedDivLevel` updates.

---

<sub>⏱️ 123m 50s</sub>

### 👤 User

If we go with the first option, what you recommended, can we eventually switch once we move everything into the dispatcher? Or does it have to stay outside in core? Maybe it's fine that it's there, I don't really know.

---

<sub>⏱️ 123m 57s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Yes—you can switch later: option (1) is a good **bridge** because it keeps container mutations centralized while you migrate block detection/emission into the dispatcher. As migration completes, `core.rs` can shrink into a thin “executor” that just loops lines, calls the dispatcher, and applies returned **effects** (push/pop/close/flush) + emits nodes—at that point the “real parser” is effectively the dispatcher even if the loop still lives in `core`. It’s also totally fine if a tiny bit stays in `core` permanently, because something must own the single-pass loop + container lifecycle; the key is that it becomes generic plumbing, not a pile of block-specific branches.

---

1. //! Block parser dispatcher for organizing block-level parsing.
2. //!
3. //! This module provides a trait-based abstraction for block parsers,
4. //! making it easier to add new block types and reducing duplication in parse_inner_content.
5. //!
6. //! Design principles:
7. //! - Single-pass parsing preserved (no backtracking)
8. //! - Each block parser operates independently
9. //! - Inline parsing still integrated (called from within block parsing)
10. //! - Maintains exact CST structure and losslessness
11. 
12. use crate::config::Config;
13. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
14. use std::any::Any;
15. 
16. use super::blocks::code_blocks::{
17.     CodeBlockType, FenceInfo, InfoString, parse_fenced_code_block, try_parse_fence_open,
18. };
19. use super::blocks::figures::parse_figure;
20. use super::blocks::headings::{
21.     emit_atx_heading, emit_setext_heading, try_parse_atx_heading, try_parse_setext_heading,
22. };
23. use super::blocks::horizontal_rules::{emit_horizontal_rule, try_parse_horizontal_rule};
24. use super::blocks::html_blocks::{HtmlBlockType, parse_html_block, try_parse_html_block_start};
25. use super::blocks::latex_envs::{LatexEnvInfo, parse_latex_environment, try_parse_latex_env_begin};
26. use super::blocks::line_blocks::{parse_line_block, try_parse_line_block_start};
27. use super::blocks::metadata::{try_parse_pandoc_title_block, try_parse_yaml_block};
28. use super::blocks::reference_links::try_parse_reference_definition;
29. use super::inlines::links::try_parse_inline_image;
30. use super::utils::container_stack::byte_index_at_column;
31. use super::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
32. 
33. /// Information about list indentation context.
34. ///
35. /// Used by block parsers that need to handle indentation stripping
36. /// when parsing inside list items (e.g., fenced code blocks).
37. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
38. pub(crate) struct ListIndentInfo {
39.     /// Number of columns to strip for list content
40.     pub content_col: usize,
41. }
42. 
43. /// Context passed to block parsers for decision-making.
44. ///
45. /// Contains immutable references to parser state that block parsers need
46. /// to check conditions (e.g., blank line before, blockquote depth, etc.).
47. pub(crate) struct BlockContext<'a> {
48.     /// Current line content (after blockquote markers stripped if any)
49.     pub content: &'a str,
50. 
51.     /// Whether there was a blank line before this line
52.     pub has_blank_before: bool,
53. 
54.     /// Whether we're at document start (pos == 0)
55.     pub at_document_start: bool,
56. 
57.     /// Current blockquote depth
58.     pub blockquote_depth: usize,
59. 
60.     /// Parser configuration
61.     pub config: &'a Config,
62. 
63.     // NOTE: we intentionally do not store `&ContainerStack` here to avoid
64.     // long-lived borrows of `self` in the main parser loop.
65.     /// Base indentation from container context (footnotes, definitions)
66.     pub content_indent: usize,
67. 
68.     /// List indentation info if inside a list
69.     pub list_indent_info: Option<ListIndentInfo>,
70. 
71.     /// Next line content for lookahead (used by setext headings)
72.     pub next_line: Option<&'a str>,
73. }
74. 
75. /// Result of detecting whether a block can be parsed.
76. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
77. pub(crate) enum BlockDetectionResult {
78.     /// Can parse this block, requires blank line before
79.     Yes,
80. 
81.     /// Can parse this block and can interrupt paragraphs (no blank line needed)
82.     #[allow(dead_code)]
83.     YesCanInterrupt,
84. 
85.     /// Cannot parse this content
86.     No,
87. }
88. 
89. /// A prepared (cached) detection result.
90. ///
91. /// This allows expensive detection logic (e.g., fence parsing) to be performed once,
92. /// while emission happens only after the caller prepares (flushes buffers/closes paragraphs).
93. pub(crate) struct PreparedBlockMatch {
94.     pub parser_index: usize,
95.     pub detection: BlockDetectionResult,
96.     pub payload: Option<Box<dyn Any>>,
97. }
98. 
99. /// Trait for block-level parsers.
100. ///
101. /// Each block type implements this trait with a two-phase approach:
102. /// 1. Detection: Can this block type parse this content? (lightweight, no emission)
103. /// 2. Parsing: Actually parse and emit the block to the builder (called after preparation)
104. ///
105. /// This separation allows the caller to:
106. /// - Prepare for block elements (close paragraphs, flush buffers) BEFORE emission
107. /// - Handle blocks that can interrupt paragraphs vs those that need blank lines
108. /// - Maintain correct CST node ordering
109. ///
110. /// Note: This is purely organizational - the trait doesn't introduce
111. /// backtracking or multiple passes. Each parser operates during the
112. /// single forward pass through the document.
113. pub(crate) trait BlockParser {
114.     /// Detect if this parser can handle the content (no emission).
115.     fn can_parse(
116.         &self,
117.         ctx: &BlockContext,
118.         lines: &[&str],
119.         line_pos: usize,
120.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult;
121. 
122.     /// Prepared detection hook.
123.     ///
124.     /// Default implementation just calls `can_parse()` and returns no payload.
125.     fn detect_prepared(
126.         &self,
127.         ctx: &BlockContext,
128.         lines: &[&str],
129.         line_pos: usize,
130.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
131.         let detection = self.can_parse(ctx, lines, line_pos);
132.         match detection {
133.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes | BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
134.                 Some((detection, None))
135.             }
136.             BlockDetectionResult::No => None,
137.         }
138.     }
139. 
140.     /// Parse and emit this block type to the builder.
141.     fn parse(
142.         &self,
143.         ctx: &BlockContext,
144.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
145.         lines: &[&str],
146.         line_pos: usize,
147.     ) -> usize;
148. 
149.     /// Prepared parse hook.
150.     ///
151.     /// Default implementation ignores payload and calls `parse()`.
152.     fn parse_prepared(
153.         &self,
154.         ctx: &BlockContext,
155.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
156.         lines: &[&str],
157.         line_pos: usize,
158.         _payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
159.     ) -> usize {
160.         self.parse(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos)
161.     }
162. 
163.     /// Name of this block parser (for debugging/logging)
164.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str;
165. }
166. 
167. // ============================================================================
168. // Concrete Block Parser Implementations
169. // ============================================================================
170. 
171. /// Horizontal rule parser
172. pub(crate) struct HorizontalRuleParser;
173. 
174. impl BlockParser for HorizontalRuleParser {
175.     fn can_parse(
176.         &self,
177.         ctx: &BlockContext,
178.         _lines: &[&str],
179.         _line_pos: usize,
180.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
181.         // Must have blank line before
182.         if !ctx.has_blank_before {
183.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
184.         }
185. 
186.         // Check if this looks like a horizontal rule
187.         if try_parse_horizontal_rule(ctx.content).is_some() {
188.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
189.         } else {
190.             BlockDetectionResult::No
191.         }
192.     }
193. 
194.     fn parse(
195.         &self,
196.         ctx: &BlockContext,
197.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
198.         lines: &[&str],
199.         line_pos: usize,
200.     ) -> usize {
201.         // Use ctx.content (blockquote markers already stripped)
202.         // But preserve newline from original line
203.         let (_, newline_str) = strip_newline(lines[line_pos]);
204.         let content_with_newline = if !newline_str.is_empty() {
205.             format!("{}{}", ctx.content.trim_end(), newline_str)
206.         } else {
207.             ctx.content.to_string()
208.         };
209. 
210.         emit_horizontal_rule(builder, &content_with_newline);
211.         1 // Consumed 1 line
212.     }
213. 
214.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
215.         "horizontal_rule"
216.     }
217. }
218. 
219. /// ATX heading parser (# Heading)
220. pub(crate) struct AtxHeadingParser;
```
