```
220. pub(crate) struct AtxHeadingParser;
221. 
222. impl BlockParser for AtxHeadingParser {
223.     fn can_parse(
224.         &self,
225.         ctx: &BlockContext,
226.         lines: &[&str],
227.         line_pos: usize,
228.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
229.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
230.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
231.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
232.     }
233. 
234.     fn detect_prepared(
235.         &self,
236.         ctx: &BlockContext,
237.         _lines: &[&str],
238.         _line_pos: usize,
239.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
240.         if !ctx.has_blank_before {
241.             return None;
242.         }
243. 
244.         let level = try_parse_atx_heading(ctx.content)?;
245.         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, Some(Box::new(level))))
246.     }
247. 
248.     fn parse(
249.         &self,
250.         ctx: &BlockContext,
251.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
252.         lines: &[&str],
253.         line_pos: usize,
254.     ) -> usize {
255.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
256.     }
257. 
258.     fn parse_prepared(
259.         &self,
260.         ctx: &BlockContext,
261.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
262.         lines: &[&str],
263.         line_pos: usize,
264.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
265.     ) -> usize {
266.         let line = lines[line_pos];
267.         let heading_level = payload
268.             .and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<usize>().copied())
269.             .or_else(|| try_parse_atx_heading(ctx.content))
270.             .unwrap_or(1);
271.         emit_atx_heading(builder, line, heading_level, ctx.config);
272.         1
273.     }
274. 
275.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
276.         "atx_heading"
277.     }
278. }
279. 
280. /// Pandoc title block parser (% Title ...)
281. pub(crate) struct PandocTitleBlockParser;
282. 
283. impl BlockParser for PandocTitleBlockParser {
284.     fn can_parse(
285.         &self,
286.         ctx: &BlockContext,
287.         lines: &[&str],
288.         line_pos: usize,
289.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
290.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
291.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
292.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
293.     }
294. 
295.     fn detect_prepared(
296.         &self,
297.         ctx: &BlockContext,
298.         _lines: &[&str],
299.         line_pos: usize,
300.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
301.         // Must be at document start.
302.         if !ctx.at_document_start || line_pos != 0 {
303.             return None;
304.         }
305. 
306.         // Must start with % (allow leading spaces).
307.         if !ctx.content.trim_start().starts_with('%') {
308.             return None;
309.         }
310. 
311.         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, None))
312.     }
313. 
314.     fn parse(
315.         &self,
316.         ctx: &BlockContext,
317.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
318.         lines: &[&str],
319.         line_pos: usize,
320.     ) -> usize {
321.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
322.     }
323. 
324.     fn parse_prepared(
325.         &self,
326.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
327.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
328.         lines: &[&str],
329.         line_pos: usize,
330.         _payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
331.     ) -> usize {
332.         let new_pos =
333.             try_parse_pandoc_title_block(lines, line_pos, builder).unwrap_or(line_pos + 1);
334.         new_pos - line_pos
335.     }
336. 
337.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
338.         "pandoc_title_block"
339.     }
340. }
341. 
342. /// YAML metadata block parser (--- ... ---/...)
343. pub(crate) struct YamlMetadataParser;
344. 
345. impl BlockParser for YamlMetadataParser {
346.     fn can_parse(
347.         &self,
348.         ctx: &BlockContext,
349.         lines: &[&str],
350.         line_pos: usize,
351.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
352.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
353.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
354.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
355.     }
356. 
357.     fn detect_prepared(
358.         &self,
359.         ctx: &BlockContext,
360.         lines: &[&str],
361.         line_pos: usize,
362.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
363.         // Must be at top level (not inside blockquotes)
364.         if ctx.blockquote_depth > 0 {
365.             return None;
366.         }
367. 
368.         // Must start with ---
369.         if ctx.content.trim() != "---" {
370.             return None;
371.         }
372. 
373.         // YAML needs blank line before OR be at document start
374.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
375.             return None;
376.         }
377. 
378.         // Look ahead: next line must NOT be blank (to distinguish from horizontal rule)
379.         let next_line = lines.get(line_pos + 1)?;
380.         if next_line.trim().is_empty() {
381.             // This is a horizontal rule, not YAML
382.             return None;
383.         }
384. 
385.         // Cache the `at_document_start` flag for emission (avoids any ambiguity if ctx changes).
386.         Some((
387.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
388.             Some(Box::new(ctx.at_document_start)),
389.         ))
390.     }
391. 
392.     fn parse(
393.         &self,
394.         ctx: &BlockContext,
395.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
396.         lines: &[&str],
397.         line_pos: usize,
398.     ) -> usize {
399.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
400.     }
401. 
402.     fn parse_prepared(
403.         &self,
404.         ctx: &BlockContext,
405.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
406.         lines: &[&str],
407.         line_pos: usize,
408.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
409.     ) -> usize {
410.         let at_document_start = payload
411.             .and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<bool>().copied())
412.             .unwrap_or(ctx.at_document_start);
413. 
414.         if let Some(new_pos) = try_parse_yaml_block(lines, line_pos, builder, at_document_start) {
415.             new_pos - line_pos
416.         } else {
417.             1
418.         }
419.     }
420. 
421.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
422.         "yaml_metadata"
423.     }
424. }
425. 
426. /// Figure parser (standalone image on its own line)
427. pub(crate) struct FigureParser;
428. 
429. impl BlockParser for FigureParser {
430.     fn can_parse(
431.         &self,
432.         ctx: &BlockContext,
433.         lines: &[&str],
434.         line_pos: usize,
435.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
436.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
437.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
438.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
439.     }
440. 
441.     fn detect_prepared(
442.         &self,
443.         ctx: &BlockContext,
444.         _lines: &[&str],
445.         _line_pos: usize,
446.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
447.         // Must have blank line before
448.         if !ctx.has_blank_before {
449.             return None;
450.         }
451. 
452.         let trimmed = ctx.content.trim();
453.         // Must start with ![
454.         if !trimmed.starts_with("![") {
455.             return None;
456.         }
457. 
458.         // Run the expensive inline-image validation once here.
459.         let (len, _alt, _dest, _attrs) = try_parse_inline_image(trimmed)?;
460.         let after_image = &trimmed[len..];
461.         if !after_image.trim().is_empty() {
462.             return None;
463.         }
464. 
465.         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, Some(Box::new(len))))
466.     }
467. 
468.     fn parse(
469.         &self,
470.         ctx: &BlockContext,
471.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
472.         lines: &[&str],
473.         line_pos: usize,
474.     ) -> usize {
475.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
476.     }
477. 
478.     fn parse_prepared(
479.         &self,
480.         ctx: &BlockContext,
481.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
482.         lines: &[&str],
483.         line_pos: usize,
484.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
485.     ) -> usize {
486.         // If detection succeeded, we already validated that this is a standalone image.
487.         // Payload currently only caches the parsed length (future-proofing).
488.         let _len = payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<usize>().copied());
489. 
490.         let line = lines[line_pos];
491.         parse_figure(builder, line, ctx.config);
492.         1
493.     }
494. 
495.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
496.         "figure"
497.     }
498. }
499.

510.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)

344.     /// Returns true if the line was consumed.
345.     fn parse_line(&mut self, line: &str) -> bool {
346.         // Count blockquote markers on this line
347.         let (bq_depth, inner_content) = count_blockquote_markers(line);
348.         let current_bq_depth = self.current_blockquote_depth();
349. 
350.         log::debug!(
351.             "parse_line [{}]: bq_depth={}, current_bq={}, depth={}, line={:?}",
352.             self.pos,
353.             bq_depth,
354.             current_bq_depth,
355.             self.containers.depth(),
356.             line.trim_end()
357.         );
358. 
359.         // Handle blank lines specially (including blank lines inside blockquotes)
360.         // A line like ">" with nothing after is a blank line inside a blockquote
361.         // Note: lines may end with \n from split_inclusive
362.         // TODO: Does this handle CLRF correctly?
363.         let is_blank = line.trim_end_matches('\n').trim().is_empty()
364.             || (bq_depth > 0 && inner_content.trim_end_matches('\n').trim().is_empty());
365. 
366.         if is_blank {
367.             // Close paragraph if open
368.             self.close_paragraph_if_open();
369. 
370.             // Close Plain node in Definition if open
371.             // Blank lines should close Plain, allowing subsequent content to be siblings
372.             // Emit buffered PLAIN content before continuing
373.             self.emit_buffered_plain_if_needed();
374. 
375.             // Note: Blank lines between terms and definitions are now preserved
376.             // and emitted as part of the term parsing logic
377. 
378.             // For blank lines inside blockquotes, we need to handle them at the right depth
379.             // First, adjust blockquote depth if needed
380.             if bq_depth > current_bq_depth {
381.                 // Open blockquotes
382.                 for _ in current_bq_depth..bq_depth {
383.                     self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::BLOCKQUOTE.into());
384.                     self.containers.push(Container::BlockQuote {});
385.                 }
386.             } else if bq_depth < current_bq_depth {
387.                 // Close blockquotes down to bq_depth (must use Parser close to emit buffers)
388.                 self.close_blockquotes_to_depth(bq_depth);
389.             }
390. 
391.             // Peek ahead to determine what containers to keep open
392.             let mut peek = self.pos + 1;
393.             while peek < self.lines.len() && self.lines[peek].trim().is_empty() {
394.                 peek += 1;
395.             }
396. 
397.             // Determine what containers to keep open based on next line
398.             let levels_to_keep = if peek < self.lines.len() {
399.                 self.compute_levels_to_keep(self.lines[peek])
400.             } else {
401.                 0
402.             };
403.             log::trace!(
404.                 "Blank line: depth={}, levels_to_keep={}, next='{}'",
405.                 self.containers.depth(),
406.                 levels_to_keep,
407.                 if peek < self.lines.len() {
408.                     self.lines[peek]
409.                 } else {
410.                     "<EOF>"
411.                 }
412.             );
413. 
414.             // Check if blank line should be buffered in a ListItem BEFORE closing containers
415. 
416.             // Close containers down to the level we want to keep
417.             while self.containers.depth() > levels_to_keep {
418.                 match self.containers.last() {
419.                     Some(Container::ListItem { .. }) => {
420.                         // levels_to_keep wants to close the ListItem - blank line is between items
421.                         log::debug!(
422.                             "Closing ListItem at blank line (levels_to_keep={} < depth={})",
423.                             levels_to_keep,
424.                             self.containers.depth()
425.                         );
426.                         self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
427.                     }
428.                     Some(Container::List { .. })
429.                     | Some(Container::FootnoteDefinition { .. })
430.                     | Some(Container::Paragraph { .. })
431.                     | Some(Container::Definition { .. })
432.                     | Some(Container::DefinitionItem { .. })
433.                     | Some(Container::DefinitionList { .. }) => {
434.                         log::debug!(
435.                             "Closing {:?} at blank line (depth {} > levels_to_keep {})",
436.                             self.containers.last(),
437.                             self.containers.depth(),
438.                             levels_to_keep
439.                         );
440. 
441.                         self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
442.                     }
443.                     _ => break,
444.                 }
445.             }
446. 
447.             // If we kept a list item open, its first-line text may still be buffered.

1. //! Fenced div parsing utilities.
2. 
3. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::strip_leading_spaces;
4. 
5. /// Information about a detected div fence opening.
6. #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
7. pub(crate) struct DivFenceInfo {
8.     pub attributes: String,
9.     pub fence_count: usize,
10. }
11. 
12. /// Try to detect a fenced div opening from content.
13. /// Returns div fence info if this is a valid opening fence.
14. ///
15. /// Opening fences MUST have attributes (or the fences are treated as closing).
16. /// Format: `::: {.class #id}` or `::: classname` or `::::: {#id} :::::`
17. pub(crate) fn try_parse_div_fence_open(content: &str) -> Option<DivFenceInfo> {
18.     let trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(content);
19. 
20.     // Check for fence opening (:::)
21.     if !trimmed.starts_with(':') {
22.         return None;
23.     }
24. 
25.     let colon_count = trimmed.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ':').count();
26. 
27.     if colon_count < 3 {
28.         return None;
29.     }
30. 
31.     // Get the part after the colons
32.     let after_colons = trimmed[colon_count..].trim_start();
33. 
34.     // Check if there are attributes
35.     // Attributes can be:
36.     // 1. Curly braces: {.class #id key="value"}
37.     // 2. Single word (treated as class): classname
38.     // 3. Attributes followed by more colons (optional): {.class} :::
39. 
40.     let attributes = if after_colons.starts_with('{') {
41.         // Find the closing brace
42.         if let Some(close_idx) = after_colons.find('}') {
43.             after_colons[..=close_idx].to_string()
44.         } else {
45.             // Unclosed brace, not valid
46.             return None;
47.         }
48.     } else if after_colons.is_empty() {
49.         // No attributes, this is a closing fence
50.         return None;
51.     } else {
52.         // Single word or words until optional trailing colons
53.         let content_before_colons = after_colons.trim_end_matches(':').trim_end();
54. 
55.         if content_before_colons.is_empty() {
56.             // Only colons, no attributes
57.             return None;
58.         }
59. 
60.         // Take the first word as the class name
61.         content_before_colons.split_whitespace().next()?.to_string()
62.     };
63. 
64.     Some(DivFenceInfo {
65.         attributes,
66.         fence_count: colon_count,
67.     })
68. }
69. 
70. /// Check if a line is a valid closing fence for a div.
71. /// Closing fences have NO attributes and at least 3 colons.
72. pub(crate) fn is_div_closing_fence(content: &str) -> bool {
73.     let trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(content);
74. 
75.     if !trimmed.starts_with(':') {
76.         return false;
77.     }
78. 
79.     let colon_count = trimmed.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ':').count();
80. 
81.     if colon_count < 3 {
82.         return false;
83.     }
84. 
85.     // Rest of line must be empty (only colons are allowed)
86.     trimmed[colon_count..].trim().is_empty()
87. }
88. 
89. #[cfg(test)]
90. mod tests {
91.     use super::*;
92. 
93.     #[test]
94.     fn test_parse_div_fence_open_with_curly_braces() {
95.         let line = "::: {.callout-note}";
96.         let fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(line).unwrap();
97.         assert_eq!(fence.attributes, "{.callout-note}");
98.     }
99. 
100.     #[test]
101.     fn test_parse_div_fence_open_with_class_name() {
102.         let line = "::: Warning";
103.         let fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(line).unwrap();
104.         assert_eq!(fence.attributes, "Warning");
105.     }
106. 
107.     #[test]
108.     fn test_parse_div_fence_open_with_trailing_colons() {
109.         let line = "::::: {#special .sidebar} :::::";
110.         let fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(line).unwrap();
111.         assert_eq!(fence.attributes, "{#special .sidebar}");
112.     }
113. 
114.     #[test]
115.     fn test_closing_fence_no_attributes() {
116.         let line = ":::";
117.         assert!(try_parse_div_fence_open(line).is_none());
118.         assert!(is_div_closing_fence(line));
119.     }
120. 
121.     #[test]
122.     fn test_closing_fence_many_colons() {
123.         let line = "::::::::::::::";
124.         assert!(try_parse_div_fence_open(line).is_none());
125.         assert!(is_div_closing_fence(line));
126.     }
127. 
128.     #[test]
129.     fn test_not_a_fence_too_few_colons() {
130.         let line = ":: something";
131.         assert!(try_parse_div_fence_open(line).is_none());
132.         assert!(!is_div_closing_fence(line));
133.     }
134. }
```

</details>

1442.             self.prepare_for_block_element();
1443. 
1444.             let bq_depth = self.current_blockquote_depth();
1445.             log::debug!("Parsed indented code block at line {}", self.pos);
1446.             let new_pos = parse_indented_code_block(
1447.                 &mut self.builder,
1448.                 &self.lines,
1449.                 self.pos,
1450.                 bq_depth,
1451.                 content_indent,
1452.             );
1453.             self.pos = new_pos;
1454.             return true;
1455.         }
1456. 
1457.         // Check for fenced div opening
1458.         if has_blank_before && let Some(div_fence) = try_parse_div_fence_open(content) {
1459.             log::debug!(
1460.                 "Parsed fenced div at line {}: {} colons",
1461.                 self.pos,
1462.                 div_fence.fence_count
1463.             );
1464. 
1465.             // Prepare for fenced div
1466.             self.prepare_for_block_element();
1467. 
1468.             // Start FencedDiv node
1469.             self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::FENCED_DIV.into());
1470. 
1471.             // Emit opening fence with attributes as child node to avoid duplication
1472.             self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_FENCE_OPEN.into());
1473. 
1474.             // Get original full line
1475.             let full_line = self.lines[self.pos];
1476.             let trimmed = full_line.trim_start();
1477. 
1478.             // Emit leading whitespace if present
1479.             let leading_ws_len = full_line.len() - trimmed.len();
1480.             if leading_ws_len > 0 {
1481.                 self.builder
1482.                     .token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &full_line[..leading_ws_len]);
1483.             }
1484. 
1485.             // Emit fence colons
1486.             let fence_str: String = ":".repeat(div_fence.fence_count);
1487.             self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), &fence_str);
1488. 
1489.             // Parse everything after colons
1490.             let after_colons = &trimmed[div_fence.fence_count..];
1491.             let (content_before_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(after_colons);
1492. 
1493.             // Emit optional space before attributes
1494.             let has_leading_space = content_before_newline.starts_with(' ');
1495.             if has_leading_space {
1496.                 self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
1497.             }
1498. 
1499.             // Get content after the leading space (if any)
1500.             let content_after_space = if has_leading_space {
1501.                 &content_before_newline[1..]
1502.             } else {
1503.                 content_before_newline
1504.             };
1505. 
1506.             // Emit attributes as DivInfo child node (avoids duplication)
1507.             self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_INFO.into());
1508.             self.builder
1509.                 .token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), &div_fence.attributes);
1510.             self.builder.finish_node(); // DivInfo
1511. 
1512.             // Check for trailing colons after attributes (symmetric fences)
1513.             let (trailing_space, trailing_colons) = if div_fence.attributes.starts_with('{') {
1514.                 // For bracketed attributes like {.class}, find what's after the closing brace
1515.                 if let Some(close_idx) = content_after_space.find('}') {
1516.                     let after_attrs = &content_after_space[close_idx + 1..];
1517.                     let trailing = after_attrs.trim_start();
1518.                     let space_count = after_attrs.len() - trailing.len();
1519.                     if !trailing.is_empty() && trailing.chars().all(|c| c == ':') {
1520.                         (space_count > 0, trailing)
1521.                     } else {
1522.                         (false, "")
1523.                     }
1524.                 } else {
1525.                     (false, "")
1526.                 }
1527.             } else {
1528.                 // For simple class names like "Warning", check after first word
1529.                 // content_after_space starts with the attribute (e.g., "Warning ::::::")
1530.                 let after_attrs = &content_after_space[div_fence.attributes.len()..];
1531.                 if let Some(after_space) = after_attrs.strip_prefix(' ') {
1532.                     if !after_space.is_empty() && after_space.chars().all(|c| c == ':') {
1533.                         (true, after_space)
1534.                     } else {
1535.                         (false, "")
1536.                     }
1537.                 } else {
1538.                     (false, "")
1539.                 }
1540.             };
1541. 
1542.             // Emit space before trailing colons if present
1543.             if trailing_space {
1544.                 self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
1545.             }
1546. 
1547.             // Emit trailing colons if present
1548.             if !trailing_colons.is_empty() {
1549.                 self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), trailing_colons);
1550.             }
1551. 
1552.             // Emit newline
1553.             if !newline_str.is_empty() {
1554.                 self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
1555.             }
1556.             self.builder.finish_node(); // DivFenceOpen
1557. 
1558.             // Push FencedDiv container
1559.             self.containers.push(Container::FencedDiv {});
1560. 
1561.             self.pos += 1;
1562.             return true;
1563.         }
1564. 
1565.         // Check for fenced div closing
1566.         if self.in_fenced_div() && is_div_closing_fence(content) {
1567.             // Close paragraph before closing fenced div
1568.             self.close_paragraph_if_open();
1569. 
1570.             // Emit closing fence - parse to avoid newline duplication
1571.             self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_FENCE_CLOSE.into());
1572. 
1573.             // Get original full line
1574.             let full_line = self.lines[self.pos];
1575.             let trimmed = full_line.trim_start();
1576. 
1577.             // Emit leading whitespace if present
1578.             let leading_ws_len = full_line.len() - trimmed.len();
1579.             if leading_ws_len > 0 {
1580.                 self.builder
1581.                     .token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &full_line[..leading_ws_len]);
1582.             }
1583. 
1584.             // Emit fence content without newline (handle both CRLF and LF)
1585.             let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(trimmed);
1586. 
1587.             self.builder
1588.                 .token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), content_without_newline);
1589. 
1590.             // Emit newline separately if present
1591.             if !line_ending.is_empty() {
1592.                 self.builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
1593.             }
1594.             self.builder.finish_node(); // DivFenceClose
1595. 
1596.             // Pop the FencedDiv container (this will finish the FencedDiv node)
1597.             self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1598. 
1599.             self.pos += 1;
1600.             return true;
1601.         }
1602. 
1603.         // List marker?
1604.         if let Some((marker, marker_len, spaces_after)) =
1605.             try_parse_list_marker(content, self.config)
1606.         {
1607.             let (indent_cols, indent_bytes) = leading_indent(content);
1608.             if indent_cols >= 4 && !lists::in_list(&self.containers) {
1609.                 // Code block at top-level, treat as paragraph
1610.                 paragraphs::start_paragraph_if_needed(&mut self.containers, &mut self.builder);
1611.                 paragraphs::append_paragraph_line(
1612.                     &mut self.containers,
1613.                     &mut self.builder,
1614.                     content,
1615.                     self.config,
1616.                 );
1617.                 self.pos += 1;
1618.                 return true;
1619.             }
1620.
```

</details>

### 💬 Copilot

6. pub(crate) enum Container {
7.     BlockQuote {
8.         // No special tracking needed
9.     },
10.     FencedDiv {
11.         // No special tracking needed - closed by fence marker
12.     },
13.     List {
14.         marker: ListMarker,
15.         base_indent_cols: usize,
16.         has_blank_between_items: bool, // Track if list is loose (blank lines between items)
17.     },
18.     ListItem {
19.         content_col: usize,
20.         buffer: ListItemBuffer, // Buffer for list item content
21.     },
22.     DefinitionList {
23.         // Definition lists don't need special tracking
24.     },
25.     DefinitionItem {
26.         // No special tracking needed
27.     },
28.     Definition {
29.         content_col: usize,
30.         plain_open: bool,
31.         #[allow(dead_code)] // Will be used in Subtask 4
32.         plain_buffer: TextBuffer, // Buffer for accumulating PLAIN content
33.     },
34.     Paragraph {
35.         buffer: ParagraphBuffer, // Interleaved buffer for paragraph content with markers
36.     },
37.     FootnoteDefinition {
38.         content_col: usize,
39.     },
40. }
41. 
42. pub(crate) struct ContainerStack {
43.     pub(crate) stack: Vec<Container>,
44. }
45. 
46. impl ContainerStack {
47.     pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
48.         Self { stack: Vec::new() }
49.     }
50. 
51.     pub(crate) fn depth(&self) -> usize {
52.         self.stack.len()
53.     }
54. 
55.     pub(crate) fn last(&self) -> Option<&Container> {
56.         self.stack.last()
57.     }
58. 
59.     pub(crate) fn push(&mut self, c: Container) {
60.         self.stack.push(c);
61.     }
62. }
63. 
64. /// Expand tabs to columns (tab stop = 4) and return (cols, byte_offset).
65. pub(crate) fn leading_indent(line: &str) -> (usize, usize) {
66.     let mut cols = 0usize;
67.     let mut bytes = 0usize;
68.     for b in line.bytes() {
69.         match b {
70.             b' ' => {
71.                 cols += 1;
72.                 bytes += 1;
73.             }
74.             b'\t' => {
75.                 cols += 4 - (cols % 4);
76.                 bytes += 1;
77.             }
78.             _ => break,
79.         }
80.     }
81.     (cols, bytes)
82. }
83. 
84. /// Return byte index at a given column (tabs = 4).
85. pub(crate) fn byte_index_at_column(line: &str, target_col: usize) -> usize {
86.     let mut col = 0usize;
87.     let mut idx = 0usize;
88.     for (i, b) in line.bytes().enumerate() {
89.         if col >= target_col {
90.             return idx;
91.         }
92.         match b {
93.             b' ' => {
94.                 col += 1;
95.                 idx = i + 1;
96.             }
97.             b'\t' => {
98.                 col += 4 - (col % 4);
99.                 idx = i + 1;
100.             }
101.             _ => break,
102.         }
103.     }
104.     idx
105. }
```

77.     /// Emit buffered PLAIN content if Definition container has open PLAIN.
78.     /// Close containers down to `keep`, emitting buffered content first.
79.     fn close_containers_to(&mut self, keep: usize) {
80.         // Emit buffered PARAGRAPH/PLAIN content before closing
81.         while self.containers.depth() > keep {
82.             match self.containers.stack.last() {
83.                 // Handle ListItem with buffering
84.                 Some(Container::ListItem { buffer, .. }) if !buffer.is_empty() => {
85.                     // Clone buffer to avoid borrow issues
86.                     let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
87. 
88.                     log::debug!(
89.                         "Closing ListItem with buffer (is_empty={}, segment_count={})",
90.                         buffer_clone.is_empty(),
91.                         buffer_clone.segment_count()
92.                     );
93. 
94.                     // Determine if this should be Plain or PARAGRAPH:
95.                     // 1. Check if parent LIST has blank lines between items (list-level loose)
96.                     // 2. OR check if this item has blank lines within its content (item-level loose)
97.                     let parent_list_is_loose = self
98.                         .containers
99.                         .stack
100.                         .iter()
101.                         .rev()
102.                         .find_map(|c| match c {
103.                             Container::List {
104.                                 has_blank_between_items,
105.                                 ..
106.                             } => Some(*has_blank_between_items),
107.                             _ => None,
108.                         })
109.                         .unwrap_or(false);
110. 
111.                     let use_paragraph =
112.                         parent_list_is_loose || buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content();
113. 
114.                     log::debug!(
115.                         "Emitting ListItem buffer: use_paragraph={} (parent_list_is_loose={}, item_has_blanks={})",
116.                         use_paragraph,
117.                         parent_list_is_loose,
118.                         buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content()
119.                     );
120. 
121.                     // Pop container first
122.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
123.                     // Emit buffered content as Plain or PARAGRAPH
124.                     buffer_clone.emit_as_block(&mut self.builder, use_paragraph, self.config);
125.                     self.builder.finish_node(); // Close LIST_ITEM
126.                 }
127.                 // Handle ListItem without content
128.                 Some(Container::ListItem { .. }) => {
129.                     log::debug!("Closing empty ListItem (no buffer content)");
130.                     // Just close normally (empty list item)
131.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
132.                     self.builder.finish_node();
133.                 }
134.                 // Handle Paragraph with buffering
135.                 Some(Container::Paragraph { buffer }) if !buffer.is_empty() => {
136.                     // Clone buffer to avoid borrow issues
137.                     let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
138.                     // Pop container first
139.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
140.                     // Emit buffered content with inline parsing (handles markers)
```

980.         }

982. 
983.     fn parse(
984.         &self,
985.         ctx: &BlockContext,
986.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
987.         lines: &[&str],
988.         pos: usize,
989.     ) -> usize {
990.         // Get text line and underline line
991.         let text_line = lines[pos];
992.         let underline_line = lines[pos + 1];
993. 
994.         // Determine level from underline character (no need to call try_parse again)
995.         // can_parse() already validated this is a valid setext heading
996.         let underline_char = underline_line.trim().chars().next().unwrap_or('=');
997.         let level = if underline_char == '=' { 1 } else { 2 };
998. 
999.         // Emit the setext heading
1000.         emit_setext_heading(builder, text_line, underline_line, level, ctx.config);
1001. 
1002.         // Return lines consumed: text line + underline line
1003.         2
1004.     }
1005. 
1006.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
1007.         "setext_heading"
1008.     }
1009. }
1010. 
1011. // ============================================================================
1012. // Block Parser Registry
1013. // ============================================================================
1014. 
1015. /// Registry of block parsers, ordered by priority.
1016. ///
1017. /// This dispatcher tries each parser in order until one succeeds.
1018. /// The ordering follows Pandoc's approach - explicit list order rather
1019. /// than numeric priorities.
1020. pub(crate) struct BlockParserRegistry {
1021.     parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>,
1022. }
1023. 
1024. impl BlockParserRegistry {
1025.     /// Create a new registry with all block parsers.
1026.     ///
1027.     /// Order matters! Parsers are tried in the order listed here.
1028.     /// This follows Pandoc's design where ordering is explicit and documented.
1029.     ///
1030.     /// **Pandoc reference order** (from pandoc/src/Text/Pandoc/Readers/Markdown.hs:487-515):
1031.     /// 1. blanklines (handled separately in our parser)
1032.     /// 2. codeBlockFenced
1033.     /// 3. yamlMetaBlock' ← YAML metadata comes early!
1034.     /// 4. bulletList
1035.     /// 5. divHtml
1036.     /// 6. divFenced
1037.     /// 7. header ← ATX headings
1038.     /// 8. lhsCodeBlock
1039.     /// 9. htmlBlock
1040.     /// 10. table
1041.     /// 11. codeBlockIndented
1042.     /// 12. rawTeXBlock (LaTeX)
1043.     /// 13. lineBlock
1044.     /// 14. blockQuote
1045.     /// 15. hrule ← Horizontal rules come AFTER headers!
1046.     /// 16. orderedList
1047.     /// 17. definitionList
1048.     /// 18. noteBlock (footnotes)
1049.     /// 19. referenceKey ← Reference definitions
1050.     /// 20. abbrevKey
1051.     /// 21. para
1052.     /// 22. plain
1053.     pub fn new() -> Self {
1054.         let parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>> = vec![
1055.             // Match Pandoc's ordering to ensure correct precedence:
1056.             // (0) Pandoc title block (must be at document start).
1057.             Box::new(PandocTitleBlockParser),
1058.             // (2) Fenced code blocks - can interrupt paragraphs!
1059.             Box::new(FencedCodeBlockParser),
1060.             // (3) YAML metadata - before headers and hrules!
1061.             Box::new(YamlMetadataParser),
1062.             // (7) Setext headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
1063.             // Must come before ATX to properly handle `---` disambiguation
1064.             Box::new(SetextHeadingParser),
1065.             // (7) ATX headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
1066.             Box::new(AtxHeadingParser),
1067.             // (9) HTML blocks
1068.             Box::new(HtmlBlockParser),
1069.             // (12) LaTeX environment blocks
1070.             Box::new(LatexEnvironmentParser),
1071.             // (13) Line blocks
1072.             Box::new(LineBlockParser),
1073.             // (15) Horizontal rules - AFTER headings per Pandoc
1074.             Box::new(HorizontalRuleParser),
1075.             // Figures (standalone images) - Pandoc doesn't have these
1076.             Box::new(FigureParser),
1077.             // (19) Reference definitions
1078.             Box::new(ReferenceDefinitionParser),
1079.             // TODO: Migrate remaining blocks in Pandoc order:
1080.             // - (4-6) Lists and divs (bulletList, divHtml, divFenced)
1081.             // - (10) Tables (grid, multiline, pipe, simple)
1082.             // - (11) Indented code blocks (AFTER fenced!)
1083.             // - (13) Line blocks
1084.             // - (16) Ordered lists
1085.             // - (17) Definition lists
1086.             // - (18) Footnote definitions (noteBlock)
1087.         ];
1088. 
1089.         Self { parsers }
1090.     }
1091. 
1092.     /// Try to parse a block using the registered parsers.
1093.     ///
1094.     /// This method implements the two-phase parsing:
1095.     /// 1. Detection: Check if any parser can handle this content
1096.     /// 2. Caller prepares (closes paragraphs, flushes buffers)
1097.     /// 3. Parser emits the block
1098.     ///
1099.     /// Returns (parser_index, detection_result) if a parser can handle this,
1100.     /// or None if no parser matched.
1101.     #[allow(dead_code)]
1102.     pub fn detect(
1103.         &self,
1104.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1105.         lines: &[&str],
1106.         line_pos: usize,
1107.     ) -> Option<(usize, BlockDetectionResult)> {
1108.         for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
1109.             let result = parser.can_parse(ctx, lines, line_pos);
1110.             match result {
1111.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes | BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
1112.                     log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
1113.                     return Some((i, result));
1114.                 }
1115.                 BlockDetectionResult::No => continue,
1116.             }
1117.         }
1118.         None
1119.     }
1120. 
1121.     /// Like `detect()`, but allows parsers to return cached payload for emission.
1122.     pub fn detect_prepared(
1123.         &self,
1124.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1125.         lines: &[&str],
1126.         line_pos: usize,
1127.     ) -> Option<PreparedBlockMatch> {
1128.         for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
1129.             if let Some((detection, payload)) = parser.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos) {
1130.                 log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
1131.                 return Some(PreparedBlockMatch {
1132.                     parser_index: i,
1133.                     detection,
1134.                     payload,
1135.                 });
1136.             }
1137.         }
1138.         None
1139.     }
1140. 
1141.     /// Parse a block using the specified parser (by index from detect()).
1142.     ///
1143.     /// Should only be called after detect() returns Some and after
1144.     /// caller has prepared for the block element.
1145.     #[allow(dead_code)]
1146.     pub fn parse(
1147.         &self,
1148.         parser_index: usize,
1149.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1150.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1151.         lines: &[&str],
1152.         line_pos: usize,
1153.     ) -> usize {
1154.         let parser = &self.parsers[parser_index];
1155.         log::debug!("Block parsed by: {}", parser.name());
1156.         parser.parse(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos)
1157.     }
1158. 
1159.     pub fn parse_prepared(
1160.         &self,
1161.         block_match: &PreparedBlockMatch,
1162.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1163.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1164.         lines: &[&str],
1165.         line_pos: usize,
1166.     ) -> usize {
1167.         let parser = &self.parsers[block_match.parser_index];
1168.         log::debug!("Block parsed by: {}", parser.name());
1169.         parser.parse_prepared(
1170.             ctx,
1171.             builder,
1172.             lines,
1173.             line_pos,
1174.             block_match.payload.as_deref(),
1175.         )
1176.     }
1177. }
```

</details>

1004.     /// Parse content inside blockquotes (or at top level).

1021.             let (indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(content);

1023.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(content, content_indent);
1024.                 (&content[idx..], Some(&content[..idx]))
1025.             } else {
1026.                 // Line has less indent than required - preserve leading whitespace

1029.                 if ws_len > 0 {
1030.                     (trimmed_start, Some(&content[..ws_len]))

1146.         // We'll update these two fields shortly (after they are computed), but we can still

1156.         // Check for heading (needs blank line before, or at start of container)

1302.             // Try to parse pipe table (check before simple table since pipes are more specific)
1303.             if let Some(lines_consumed) =
1304.                 try_parse_pipe_table(&self.lines, self.pos, &mut self.builder, self.config)
1305.             {
1306.                 log::debug!(
1307.                     "Parsed pipe table at line {} ({} lines)",
1308.                     self.pos,
1309.                     lines_consumed
1310.                 );
1311.                 self.pos += lines_consumed;
1312.                 return true;
1313.             }
1314. 
1315.             // Try to parse simple table
1316.             if let Some(lines_consumed) =
1317.                 try_parse_simple_table(&self.lines, self.pos, &mut self.builder, self.config)
1318.             {
1319.                 log::debug!(
1320.                     "Parsed simple table at line {} ({} lines)",
1321.                     self.pos,
1322.                     lines_consumed
1323.                 );
1324.                 self.pos += lines_consumed;
1325.                 return true;
1326.             }
1327. 
1328.             if let Some(block_match) = dispatcher_match.as_ref() {
1329.                 let detection = block_match.detection;
1330. 
1331.                 match detection {
1332.                     BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
1333.                         self.emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed();
1334.                         if self.is_paragraph_open() {
1335.                             self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1336.                         }
1337.                     }
1338.                     BlockDetectionResult::Yes => {
1339.                         self.prepare_for_block_element();
1340.                     }
1341.                     BlockDetectionResult::No => unreachable!(),
1342.                 }
1343. 
1344.                 let lines_consumed = self.block_registry.parse_prepared(
1345.                     block_match,
1346.                     &dispatcher_ctx,
1347.                     &mut self.builder,
1348.                     &self.lines,
1349.                     self.pos,
1350.                 );
1351.                 self.pos += lines_consumed;
1352.                 return true;
1353.             }
1354.         } else if let Some(block_match) = dispatcher_match.as_ref() {
1355.             // Without blank-before, only allow interrupting blocks OR blocks that are
1356.             // explicitly allowed without blank lines (e.g. reference definitions).
1357.             match block_match.detection {
1358.                 BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
1359.                     self.emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed();
1360.                     if self.is_paragraph_open() {
1361.                         self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1362.                     }
1363.                 }
1364.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes => {
1365.                     // E.g. reference definitions: no preparation (matches previous behavior).
1366.                 }
1367.                 BlockDetectionResult::No => unreachable!(),
1368.             }
1369. 
1370.             if !matches!(block_match.detection, BlockDetectionResult::No) {
1371.                 let lines_consumed = self.block_registry.parse_prepared(
1372.                     block_match,
1373.                     &dispatcher_ctx,
1374.                     &mut self.builder,
1375.                     &self.lines,
1376.                     self.pos,
1377.                 );
1378.                 self.pos += lines_consumed;
1379.                 return true;
1380.             }
1381.         }
1382. 
1383.         // Check for footnote definition: [^id]: content
1384.         // Similar to list items - marker followed by content that can span multiple lines
1385.         // Must check BEFORE reference definitions since both start with [
1386.         if let Some((id, content_start)) = try_parse_footnote_marker(content) {
1387.             // Footnotes have precedence over dispatcher-based reference definitions.
1388.             // (Dispatcher match is evaluated above for performance; we handle footnotes here.)
1389.             log::debug!("Parsed footnote definition at line {}: [^{}]", self.pos, id);
1390. 
1391.             // Close paragraph if one is open
1392.             self.close_paragraph_if_open();
1393. 
1394.             // Close previous footnote if one is open
1395.             while matches!(
1396.                 self.containers.last(),
1397.                 Some(Container::FootnoteDefinition { .. })
1398.             ) {
1399.                 self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1400.             }
1401. 
1402.             // Start the footnote definition container
1403.             self.builder
1404.                 .start_node(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION.into());
1405. 
1406.             // Emit the marker
1407.             let marker_text = &content[..content_start];
1408.             self.builder
1409.                 .token(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_REFERENCE.into(), marker_text);
1410. 
1411.             // Calculate content column (minimum 4 spaces for continuation)
1412.             // The first line can start right after the marker, but subsequent lines
1413.             // need at least 4 spaces of indentation
1414.             let content_col = 4;
1415.             self.containers
```

526.     fn parse(
527.         &self,
528.         ctx: &BlockContext,
529.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
530.         lines: &[&str],
531.         line_pos: usize,
532.     ) -> usize {
533.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
534.     }
535. 
536.     fn parse_prepared(
537.         &self,
538.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
539.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
540.         lines: &[&str],
541.         line_pos: usize,
542.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
543.     ) -> usize {
544.         use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
545. 
546.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::REFERENCE_DEFINITION.into());
547. 
548.         let full_line = lines[line_pos];
549.         let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(full_line);
550. 
551.         // Detection already cached the parsed tuple; emission should not need to re-parse.
552.         // If payload is missing (legacy callsites), we fall back to the old raw emission.
553.         debug_assert!(
554.             payload
555.                 .and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<(usize, String, String, Option<String>)>())
556.                 .is_some()
557.         );
558. 
559.         emit_reference_definition_content(builder, content_without_newline);
560. 
561.         if !line_ending.is_empty() {
562.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
563.         }
564. 
565.         builder.finish_node();
566. 
567.         1
568.     }
569. 
570.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
571.         "reference_definition"
572.     }
573. }
574. 
575. /// Helper function to emit reference definition content with inline structure.
576. fn emit_reference_definition_content(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, text: &str) {
577.     use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
578. 
579.     if !text.starts_with('[') {
580.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
581.         return;
582.     }
583. 
584.     let rest = &text[1..];
585.     if let Some(close_pos) = rest.find(']') {
586.         let label = &rest[..close_pos];
587.         let after_bracket = &rest[close_pos + 1..];
588. 
589.         if after_bracket.starts_with(':') {
590.             // Emit LINK node with the label
591.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK.into());
592. 
593.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into());
594.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into(), "[");
595.             builder.finish_node();
596. 
597.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_TEXT.into());
598.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), label);
599.             builder.finish_node();
600. 
601.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "]");
602.             builder.finish_node(); // LINK
603. 
604.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), after_bracket);
605.             return;
606.         }
607.     }
608. 
609.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
610. }
611.

809. 
810.     fn has_nested_block_structure(&self, content: &str) -> bool {
811.         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
812.             content,
813.             has_blank_before: true,
814.             at_document_start: false,
815.             blockquote_depth: self.current_blockquote_depth(),
816.             config: self.config,
817.             content_indent: 0,
818.             list_indent_info: None,
819.             next_line: None,
820.         };
821. 
822.         // Note: we only use the registry for its “is this a block start?” decision;
823.         // it should not rely on `lines` here (setext is gated by `has_blank_before`).
824.         self.block_registry
825.             .detect_prepared(&block_ctx, &[], 0)
826.             .is_some()
827.     }
828. 
829.     /// Compute how many container levels to keep open based on next line content.
830.     fn compute_levels_to_keep(&self, next_line: &str) -> usize {
831.         let (next_bq_depth, next_inner) = count_blockquote_markers(next_line);
832.         let (raw_indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(next_inner);
833.         let next_marker = try_parse_list_marker(next_inner, self.config);
834. 
835.         // Calculate current blockquote depth for proper indent calculation
836.         let current_bq_depth = self.current_blockquote_depth();
837. 
838.         log::debug!(
839.             "compute_levels_to_keep: next_line indent={}, has_marker={}, stack_depth={}, current_bq={}, next_bq={}",
840.             raw_indent_cols,
```

1. //! Blockquote parsing utilities.
2. //!
3. //! Re-exports marker parsing functions from marker_utils for backward compatibility.
4. 
5. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
6. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
7. 
8. use crate::parser::utils::container_stack::{Container, ContainerStack};
9. 
10. pub(crate) use crate::parser::utils::marker_utils::{
11.     count_blockquote_markers, try_parse_blockquote_marker,
12. };
13. 
14. /// Check if we need a blank line before starting a new blockquote.
15. /// Returns true if a blockquote can start here.
16. pub(in crate::parser) fn can_start_blockquote(pos: usize, lines: &[&str]) -> bool {
17.     // At start of document, no blank line needed
18.     if pos == 0 {
19.         return true;
20.     }
21.     // After a blank line, can start blockquote
22.     if pos > 0 && lines[pos - 1].trim().is_empty() {
23.         return true;
24.     }
25.     // If we're already in a blockquote, nested blockquotes need blank line too
26.     // (blank_before_blockquote extension)
27.     false
28. }
29. 
30. /// Get the current blockquote depth from the container stack.
31. pub(in crate::parser) fn current_blockquote_depth(containers: &ContainerStack) -> usize {
32.     containers
33.         .stack
34.         .iter()
35.         .filter(|c| matches!(c, Container::BlockQuote { .. }))
36.         .count()
37. }
38. 
39. /// Strip exactly n blockquote markers from a line, returning the rest.
40. pub(in crate::parser) fn strip_n_blockquote_markers(line: &str, n: usize) -> &str {
41.     let mut remaining = line;
42.     for _ in 0..n {
43.         if let Some((_, content_start)) = try_parse_blockquote_marker(remaining) {
44.             remaining = &remaining[content_start..];
45.         } else {
46.             break;
47.         }
48.     }
49.     remaining
50. }
51. 
52. /// Emit one blockquote marker with its whitespace.
53. pub(in crate::parser) fn emit_one_blockquote_marker(
54.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
55.     leading_spaces: usize,
56.     has_trailing_space: bool,
57. ) {
58.     if leading_spaces > 0 {
59.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &" ".repeat(leading_spaces));
60.     }
61.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::BLOCKQUOTE_MARKER.into(), ">");
62.     if has_trailing_space {
63.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
64.     }
65. }
```

</details>

1. //! Indented code block parsing utilities.
2. //!
3. //! A block of text indented four spaces (or one tab) is treated as verbatim text.
4. //! The initial (four space or one tab) indentation is not considered part of the
5. //! verbatim text and is removed in the output.
6. //!
7. //! Note: blank lines in the verbatim text need not begin with four spaces.
8. 
9. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
10. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
11. 
12. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
13. 
14. /// Check if a line is indented enough to be part of an indented code block.
15. /// Returns true if the line starts with 4+ spaces or 1+ tab.
16. pub(crate) fn is_indented_code_line(content: &str) -> bool {
17.     if content.is_empty() {
18.         return false;
19.     }
20. 
21.     // Check for tab
22.     if content.starts_with('\t') {
23.         return true;
24.     }
25. 
26.     // Check for 4+ spaces
27.     let spaces = content.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ' ').count();
28.     spaces >= 4
29. }
30. 
31. /// Parse an indented code block, consuming lines from the parser.
32. /// Returns the new position after the code block.
33. ///
34. /// An indented code block consists of consecutive lines that are either:
35. /// - Indented by 4+ spaces or 1+ tab
36. /// - Blank lines (which don't need indentation)
37. ///
38. /// The block ends when we hit a non-blank line that isn't indented enough.
39. /// Parse an indented code block, consuming lines from the parser.
40. /// Returns the new position after the code block.
41. ///
42. /// An indented code block consists of consecutive lines that are either:
43. /// - Indented by 4+ spaces or 1+ tab (beyond base_indent)
44. /// - Blank lines (which don't need indentation)
45. ///
46. /// The block ends when we hit a non-blank line that isn't indented enough.
47. pub(crate) fn parse_indented_code_block(
48.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
49.     lines: &[&str],
50.     start_pos: usize,
51.     bq_depth: usize,
52.     base_indent: usize,
53. ) -> usize {
54.     use super::blockquotes::count_blockquote_markers;
55. 
56.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::CODE_BLOCK.into());
57.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::CODE_CONTENT.into());
58. 
59.     let mut current_pos = start_pos;
60.     // Total indent needed: base (e.g., footnote) + 4 for code
61.     let code_indent = base_indent + 4;
62. 
63.     while current_pos < lines.len() {
64.         let line = lines[current_pos];
65. 
66.         // Strip blockquote markers to get inner content
67.         let (line_bq_depth, inner) = count_blockquote_markers(line);
68. 
69.         // If blockquote depth decreases, code block ends (we've left the blockquote)
70.         if line_bq_depth < bq_depth {
71.             break;
72.         }
73. 
74.         // Blank lines need look-ahead: only include if next non-blank line continues the code
75.         if inner.trim().is_empty() {
76.             // Check if code continues after this blank line
77.             let mut look_pos = current_pos + 1;
78.             let mut continues = false;
79.             while look_pos < lines.len() {
80.                 let (look_bq_depth, look_inner) = count_blockquote_markers(lines[look_pos]);
81.                 if look_bq_depth < bq_depth {
82.                     break;
83.                 }
84.                 if look_inner.trim_end_matches('\n').trim().is_empty() {
85.                     look_pos += 1;
86.                     continue;
87.                 }
88.                 let (look_indent, _) = leading_indent(look_inner);
89.                 if look_indent >= code_indent {
90.                     continues = true;
91.                 }
92.                 break;
93.             }
94.             if !continues {
95.                 break;
96.             }
97.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "");
98.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), "\n");
99.             current_pos += 1;
100.             continue;
101.         }
102. 
103.         // Check if line is indented enough (base_indent + 4 for code)
104.         let (indent_cols, indent_bytes) = leading_indent(inner);
105.         if indent_cols < code_indent {
106.             break;
107.         }
108. 
109.         // For losslessness: emit ALL indentation as WHITESPACE, then emit remaining content
110.         // The formatter can decide how to handle the indentation
111.         if indent_bytes > 0 {
112.             let indent_str = &inner[..indent_bytes];
113.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), indent_str);
114.         }
115. 
116.         // Get the content after the indentation
117.         let content = &inner[indent_bytes..];
118. 
119.         // Split off trailing newline if present (from split_inclusive)
120.         let (content_without_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(content);
```

</details>

612. /// Fenced code block parser (``` or ~~~)
613. pub(crate) struct FencedCodeBlockParser;
614. 
615. impl BlockParser for FencedCodeBlockParser {
616.     fn can_parse(
617.         &self,
618.         ctx: &BlockContext,
619.         _lines: &[&str],
620.         _line_pos: usize,
621.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
622.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, _lines, _line_pos)
623.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
624.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
625.     }
626. 
627.     fn detect_prepared(
628.         &self,
629.         ctx: &BlockContext,
630.         _lines: &[&str],
631.         _line_pos: usize,
632.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
633.         // Calculate content to check - may need to strip list indentation
634.         let content_to_check = if let Some(list_info) = ctx.list_indent_info {
635.             if list_info.content_col > 0 && !ctx.content.is_empty() {
636.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(ctx.content, list_info.content_col);
637.                 &ctx.content[idx..]
638.             } else {
639.                 ctx.content
640.             }
641.         } else {
642.             ctx.content
643.         };
644. 
645.         let fence = try_parse_fence_open(content_to_check)?;
646. 
647.         // Parse info string to determine block type (expensive, but now cached via fence)
648.         let info = InfoString::parse(&fence.info_string);
649. 
650.         let is_executable = matches!(info.block_type, CodeBlockType::Executable { .. });
651.         let is_pandoc_like = matches!(
652.             ctx.config.flavor,
653.             crate::config::Flavor::Pandoc
654.                 | crate::config::Flavor::CommonMark
655.                 | crate::config::Flavor::Gfm
656.         );
657.         if is_executable && is_pandoc_like {
658.             return None;
659.         }
660. 
661.         // Fenced code blocks can interrupt paragraphs only if they have an info string.
662.         let has_info = !fence.info_string.trim().is_empty();
663.         let detection = if has_info {
664.             BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
665.         } else if ctx.has_blank_before {
666.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
667.         } else {
668.             BlockDetectionResult::No
669.         };
670. 
671.         match detection {
672.             BlockDetectionResult::No => None,
673.             _ => Some((detection, Some(Box::new(fence)))),
674.         }
675.     }
676. 
677.     fn parse(
678.         &self,
679.         ctx: &BlockContext,
680.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
681.         lines: &[&str],
682.         line_pos: usize,
683.     ) -> usize {
684.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
685.     }
686. 
687.     fn parse_prepared(
688.         &self,
689.         ctx: &BlockContext,
690.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
691.         lines: &[&str],
692.         line_pos: usize,
693.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
694.     ) -> usize {
695.         let list_indent_stripped = ctx.list_indent_info.map(|i| i.content_col).unwrap_or(0);
696. 
697.         let fence = if let Some(fence) = payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<FenceInfo>()) {
698.             fence.clone()
699.         } else {
700.             // Backward-compat: if called via legacy `parse()`, recompute.
701.             let content_to_check = if list_indent_stripped > 0 && !ctx.content.is_empty() {
702.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(ctx.content, list_indent_stripped);
703.                 &ctx.content[idx..]
704.             } else {
705.                 ctx.content
706.             };
707.             try_parse_fence_open(content_to_check).expect("Fence should exist")
708.         };
709. 
710.         // Calculate total indent: base content indent + list indent
711.         let total_indent = ctx.content_indent + list_indent_stripped;
712. 
713.         let new_pos = parse_fenced_code_block(
714.             builder,
715.             lines,
716.             line_pos,
717.             fence,
718.             ctx.blockquote_depth,
719.             total_indent,
720.         );
721. 
722.         new_pos - line_pos
723.     }
724. 
725.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
726.         "fenced_code_block"
727.     }
728. }
729. 
730. // ============================================================================
731. // HTML Block Parser (position #9)
732. // ============================================================================
733. 
734. pub(crate) struct HtmlBlockParser;
735. 
736. impl BlockParser for HtmlBlockParser {
737.     fn can_parse(
738.         &self,
739.         ctx: &BlockContext,
740.         lines: &[&str],
741.         line_pos: usize,
742.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
743.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
744.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
745.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
746.     }
747. 
748.     fn detect_prepared(
749.         &self,
750.         ctx: &BlockContext,
751.         _lines: &[&str],
752.         _line_pos: usize,
753.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
754.         if !ctx.config.extensions.raw_html {
755.             return None;
756.         }
757. 
758.         let block_type = try_parse_html_block_start(ctx.content)?;
759. 
760.         // Match previous behavior (and Pandoc-ish semantics): HTML blocks can interrupt
761.         // paragraphs; blank lines are not required.
762.         let detection = if ctx.has_blank_before || ctx.at_document_start {
763.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
764.         } else {
765.             BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
766.         };
767. 
768.         Some((detection, Some(Box::new(block_type))))
769.     }
770. 
771.     fn parse(
772.         &self,
773.         ctx: &BlockContext,
774.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
775.         lines: &[&str],
776.         line_pos: usize,
777.     ) -> usize {
778.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
779.     }
780. 
781.     fn parse_prepared(
782.         &self,
783.         ctx: &BlockContext,
784.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
785.         lines: &[&str],
786.         line_pos: usize,
787.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
788.     ) -> usize {
789.         let block_type = if let Some(bt) = payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<HtmlBlockType>()) {
790.             bt.clone()
791.         } else {
792.             try_parse_html_block_start(ctx.content).expect("HTML block type should exist")
793.         };
794. 
795.         let new_pos = parse_html_block(builder, lines, line_pos, block_type, ctx.blockquote_depth);
796.         new_pos - line_pos
797.     }
798. 
799.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
800.         "html_block"
801.     }
802. }
803. 
804. // ============================================================================
805. // LaTeX Environment Parser (position #12)
806. // ============================================================================
807. 
808. pub(crate) struct LatexEnvironmentParser;
809. 
810. impl BlockParser for LatexEnvironmentParser {
811.     fn can_parse(
812.         &self,
813.         ctx: &BlockContext,
814.         lines: &[&str],
815.         line_pos: usize,
816.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
817.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
818.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
819.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
820.     }
````

1. //! Consolidated utilities for parsing block markers (blockquotes, lists, definitions).
2. //!
3. //! This module provides common functionality for parsing markers that follow
4. //! similar patterns: optional leading spaces, marker character(s), optional trailing space.
5. 
6. /// Information about a single blockquote marker.
7. #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
8. pub(crate) struct BlockQuoteMarkerInfo {
9.     pub leading_spaces: usize,
10.     pub has_trailing_space: bool,
11. }
12. 
13. /// Check if line starts with a blockquote marker (up to 3 spaces + >).
14. /// Returns (marker_end_byte, content_start_byte) if found.
15. pub(crate) fn try_parse_blockquote_marker(line: &str) -> Option<(usize, usize)> {
16.     let bytes = line.as_bytes();
17.     let mut i = 0;
18. 
19.     // Skip up to 3 spaces
20.     let mut spaces = 0;
21.     while i < bytes.len() && bytes[i] == b' ' && spaces < 3 {
22.         spaces += 1;
23.         i += 1;
24.     }
25. 
26.     // Must have > next
27.     if i >= bytes.len() || bytes[i] != b'>' {
28.         return None;
29.     }
30.     let marker_end = i + 1;
31. 
32.     // Optional space after >
33.     let content_start = if marker_end < bytes.len() && bytes[marker_end] == b' ' {
34.         marker_end + 1
35.     } else {
36.         marker_end
37.     };
38. 
39.     Some((marker_end, content_start))
40. }
41. 
42. /// Count how many blockquote levels a line has, returning (depth, remaining_content).
43. pub(crate) fn count_blockquote_markers(line: &str) -> (usize, &str) {
44.     let mut depth = 0;
45.     let mut remaining = line;
46. 
47.     while let Some((_, content_start)) = try_parse_blockquote_marker(remaining) {
48.         depth += 1;
49.         remaining = &remaining[content_start..];
50.     }
51. 
52.     (depth, remaining)
53. }
54. 
55. /// Parse all blockquote markers from a line and return detailed info about each.
56. /// Returns Vec of BlockQuoteMarkerInfo for each marker found.
57. /// This is useful for lossless parsing where we need to preserve exact whitespace.
58. pub(crate) fn parse_blockquote_marker_info(line: &str) -> Vec<BlockQuoteMarkerInfo> {
59.     let mut markers = Vec::new();
60.     let mut remaining = line;
61. 
62.     loop {
63.         let bytes = remaining.as_bytes();
64.         let mut i = 0;
65. 
66.         // Count leading whitespace (up to 3 spaces before >)
67.         let mut spaces = 0;
68.         while i < bytes.len() && bytes[i] == b' ' && spaces < 3 {
69.             spaces += 1;
70.             i += 1;
71.         }
72. 
73.         // Check if there's a > marker
74.         if i >= bytes.len() || bytes[i] != b'>' {
75.             break;
76.         }
77.         i += 1; // skip '>'
78. 
79.         // Check for optional space after >
80.         let has_trailing_space = i < bytes.len() && bytes[i] == b' ';
81.         if has_trailing_space {
82.             i += 1;
83.         }
84. 
85.         markers.push(BlockQuoteMarkerInfo {
86.             leading_spaces: spaces,
87.             has_trailing_space,
88.         });
89.         remaining = &remaining[i..];
90.     }
91. 
92.     markers
93. }
94. 
95. #[cfg(test)]
96. mod tests {
97.     use super::*;
98. 
99.     #[test]
100.     fn test_simple_marker() {
101.         assert_eq!(try_parse_blockquote_marker("> text"), Some((1, 2)));
102.     }
103. 
104.     #[test]
105.     fn test_marker_no_space() {
106.         assert_eq!(try_parse_blockquote_marker(">text"), Some((1, 1)));
107.     }
108. 
109.     #[test]
110.     fn test_marker_with_leading_spaces() {
111.         assert_eq!(try_parse_blockquote_marker("   > text"), Some((4, 5)));
112.     }
113. 
114.     #[test]
115.     fn test_four_spaces_not_blockquote() {
116.         assert_eq!(try_parse_blockquote_marker("    > text"), None);
117.     }
118. 
119.     #[test]
120.     fn test_count_nested() {
```

230.         {
231.             plain_buffer.clear();
232.             *plain_open = false;
233.         }
234.     }
235. 
236.     /// Close blockquotes down to a target depth.
237.     ///
238.     /// Must use `Parser::close_containers_to` (not `ContainerStack::close_to`) so list/paragraph
239.     /// buffers are emitted for losslessness.
240.     fn close_blockquotes_to_depth(&mut self, target_depth: usize) {
241.         let mut current = self.current_blockquote_depth();
242.         while current > target_depth {
243.             while !matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::BlockQuote { .. })) {
244.                 if self.containers.depth() == 0 {
245.                     break;
246.                 }
247.                 self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
248.             }
249.             if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::BlockQuote { .. })) {
250.                 self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
251.                 current -= 1;
252.             } else {
253.                 break;
254.             }
255.         }
256.     }
257. 
258.     /// Emit buffered list item content if we're in a ListItem and it has content.
259.     /// This is used before starting block-level elements inside list items.
260.     fn emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed(&mut self) {
261.         if let Some(Container::ListItem { buffer, .. }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
262.             && !buffer.is_empty()
263.         {
264.             let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
265.             buffer.clear();
266.             let use_paragraph = buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content();
267.             buffer_clone.emit_as_block(&mut self.builder, use_paragraph, self.config);
268.         }
269.     }
270. 
271.     /// Check if a paragraph is currently open.
272.     fn is_paragraph_open(&self) -> bool {
273.         matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Paragraph { .. }))
274.     }
275. 
276.     /// Close paragraph if one is currently open.
277.     fn close_paragraph_if_open(&mut self) {
278.         if self.is_paragraph_open() {
279.             self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
280.         }
281.     }
282. 
283.     /// Prepare for a block-level element by flushing buffers and closing paragraphs.
284.     /// This is a common pattern before starting tables, code blocks, divs, etc.
285.     fn prepare_for_block_element(&mut self) {
286.         self.emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed();
287.         self.close_paragraph_if_open();
288.     }
289. 
290.     /// Get current blockquote depth from container stack.
291.     fn current_blockquote_depth(&self) -> usize {
292.         blockquotes::current_blockquote_depth(&self.containers)
293.     }
294. 
295.     /// Emit or buffer a blockquote marker depending on parser state.
296.     ///
297.     /// If a paragraph is open and we're using integrated parsing, buffer the marker.
298.     /// Otherwise emit it directly to the builder.
299.     fn emit_or_buffer_blockquote_marker(
300.         &mut self,
301.         leading_spaces: usize,
302.         has_trailing_space: bool,
303.     ) {
304.         // If paragraph is open, buffer the marker (it will be emitted at correct position)
305.         if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Paragraph { .. })) {
306.             // Buffer the marker in the paragraph
307.             paragraphs::append_paragraph_marker(
308.                 &mut self.containers,
309.                 leading_spaces,
310.                 has_trailing_space,
311.             );
312.         } else {
313.             // Emit directly
314.             blockquotes::emit_one_blockquote_marker(
315.                 &mut self.builder,
316.                 leading_spaces,
317.                 has_trailing_space,
318.             );
319.         }
320.     }
321. 
322.     fn parse_document_stack(&mut self) {
323.         self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DOCUMENT.into());
324. 
325.         log::debug!("Starting document parse");
326. 
327.         // Pandoc title block is handled via the block dispatcher.
328. 
329.         while self.pos < self.lines.len() {
330.             let line = self.lines[self.pos];
331. 
332.             log::debug!("Parsing line {}: {}", self.pos + 1, line);
333. 
334.             if self.parse_line(line) {
335.                 continue;
336.             }
337.             self.pos += 1;
338.         }
339. 
340.         self.close_containers_to(0);
```

</details>

70. 
71.     /// Next line content for lookahead (used by setext headings)

74. 
75. /// Result of detecting whether a block can be parsed.
76. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
77. pub(crate) enum BlockDetectionResult {
78.     /// Can parse this block, requires blank line before
79.     Yes,
80. 
81.     /// Can parse this block and can interrupt paragraphs (no blank line needed)
82.     #[allow(dead_code)]
83.     YesCanInterrupt,
84. 
85.     /// Cannot parse this content
86.     No,
87. }
88. 
89. /// A prepared (cached) detection result.
90. ///
91. /// This allows expensive detection logic (e.g., fence parsing) to be performed once,
92. /// while emission happens only after the caller prepares (flushes buffers/closes paragraphs).
93. pub(crate) struct PreparedBlockMatch {
94.     pub parser_index: usize,
95.     pub detection: BlockDetectionResult,
96.     pub payload: Option<Box<dyn Any>>,
97. }
98. 
99. /// Trait for block-level parsers.
100. ///
101. /// Each block type implements this trait with a two-phase approach:
102. /// 1. Detection: Can this block type parse this content? (lightweight, no emission)
103. /// 2. Parsing: Actually parse and emit the block to the builder (called after preparation)
104. ///
105. /// This separation allows the caller to:
106. /// - Prepare for block elements (close paragraphs, flush buffers) BEFORE emission
107. /// - Handle blocks that can interrupt paragraphs vs those that need blank lines
108. /// - Maintain correct CST node ordering
109. ///
110. /// Note: This is purely organizational - the trait doesn't introduce
111. /// backtracking or multiple passes. Each parser operates during the
112. /// single forward pass through the document.
113. pub(crate) trait BlockParser {
114.     /// Detect if this parser can handle the content (no emission).
115.     fn can_parse(
116.         &self,
117.         ctx: &BlockContext,
118.         lines: &[&str],
119.         line_pos: usize,
120.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult;
```

<sub>⏱️ 141m 41s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Implementing option (1) by adding a small “effect” to prepared dispatcher matches, then migrating fenced div open/close emission into the dispatcher while keeping container push/pop in `core.rs`.
