821. // ============================================================================
822. // LaTeX Environment Parser (position #12)
823. // ============================================================================
824. 
825. pub(crate) struct LatexEnvironmentParser;
826. 
827. impl BlockParser for LatexEnvironmentParser {
828.     fn can_parse(
829.         &self,
830.         ctx: &BlockContext,
831.         lines: &[&str],
832.         line_pos: usize,
833.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
834.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
835.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
836.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
837.     }
838. 
839.     fn detect_prepared(
840.         &self,
841.         ctx: &BlockContext,
842.         _lines: &[&str],
843.         _line_pos: usize,
844.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
845.         if !ctx.config.extensions.raw_tex {
846.             return None;
847.         }
848. 
849.         let env_info = try_parse_latex_env_begin(ctx.content)?;
850. 
851.         // Like HTML blocks, raw TeX blocks should be able to interrupt paragraphs.
852.         let detection = if ctx.has_blank_before || ctx.at_document_start {
853.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
854.         } else {
855.             BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
856.         };
857. 
858.         Some((detection, Some(Box::new(env_info))))
859.     }
860. 
861.     fn parse(
862.         &self,
863.         ctx: &BlockContext,
864.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
865.         lines: &[&str],
866.         line_pos: usize,
867.     ) -> usize {
868.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
869.     }
870. 
871.     fn parse_prepared(
872.         &self,
873.         ctx: &BlockContext,
874.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
875.         lines: &[&str],
876.         line_pos: usize,
877.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
878.     ) -> usize {
879.         let env_info = if let Some(info) = payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<LatexEnvInfo>()) {
880.             info.clone()
881.         } else {
882.             try_parse_latex_env_begin(ctx.content).expect("LaTeX env info should exist")
883.         };
884. 
885.         let new_pos =
886.             parse_latex_environment(builder, lines, line_pos, env_info, ctx.blockquote_depth);
887.         new_pos - line_pos
888.     }
889. 
890.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
891.         "latex_environment"
892.     }
893. }
894. 
895. // ============================================================================
896. // Line Block Parser (position #13)
897. // ============================================================================
898. 
899. pub(crate) struct LineBlockParser;
900. 
901. impl BlockParser for LineBlockParser {
902.     fn can_parse(
903.         &self,
904.         ctx: &BlockContext,
905.         lines: &[&str],
906.         line_pos: usize,
907.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
908.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
909.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
910.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
911.     }
912. 
913.     fn detect_prepared(
914.         &self,
915.         ctx: &BlockContext,
916.         _lines: &[&str],
917.         _line_pos: usize,
918.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
919.         if !ctx.config.extensions.line_blocks {
920.             return None;
921.         }
922. 
923.         if try_parse_line_block_start(ctx.content).is_none() {
924.             return None;
925.         }
926. 
927.         // Line blocks can interrupt paragraphs.
928.         let detection = if ctx.has_blank_before || ctx.at_document_start {
929.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
930.         } else {
931.             BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
932.         };
933. 
934.         Some((detection, None))
935.     }
936. 
937.     fn parse(
938.         &self,
939.         ctx: &BlockContext,
940.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
941.         lines: &[&str],
942.         line_pos: usize,
943.     ) -> usize {
944.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
945.     }
946. 
947.     fn parse_prepared(
948.         &self,
949.         ctx: &BlockContext,
950.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
951.         lines: &[&str],
952.         line_pos: usize,
953.         _payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
954.     ) -> usize {
955.         let new_pos = parse_line_block(lines, line_pos, builder, ctx.config);
956.         new_pos - line_pos
957.     }
958. 
959.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
960.         "line_block"
961.     }
962. }
963. 
964. // ============================================================================
965. // Setext Heading Parser (position #3)
966. // ============================================================================
967. 
968. pub(crate) struct SetextHeadingParser;
969. 
970. impl BlockParser for SetextHeadingParser {
971.     fn can_parse(
972.         &self,
973.         ctx: &BlockContext,
974.         _lines: &[&str],
975.         _line_pos: usize,
976.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
977.         // Setext headings require blank line before (unless at document start)
978.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
979.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
980.         }
```

</details>

443. /// Figure parser (standalone image on its own line)
444. pub(crate) struct FigureParser;
445. 
446. impl BlockParser for FigureParser {
447.     fn can_parse(
448.         &self,
449.         ctx: &BlockContext,
450.         lines: &[&str],
451.         line_pos: usize,
452.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
453.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
454.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
455.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
456.     }
457. 
458.     fn detect_prepared(
459.         &self,
460.         ctx: &BlockContext,
461.         _lines: &[&str],
462.         _line_pos: usize,
463.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
464.         // Must have blank line before
465.         if !ctx.has_blank_before {
466.             return None;
467.         }
468. 
469.         let trimmed = ctx.content.trim();
470.         // Must start with ![
471.         if !trimmed.starts_with("![") {
472.             return None;
473.         }
474. 
475.         // Run the expensive inline-image validation once here.
476.         let (len, _alt, _dest, _attrs) = try_parse_inline_image(trimmed)?;
477.         let after_image = &trimmed[len..];
478.         if !after_image.trim().is_empty() {
479.             return None;
480.         }
481. 
482.         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, Some(Box::new(len))))
483.     }
484. 
485.     fn parse(
486.         &self,
487.         ctx: &BlockContext,
488.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
489.         lines: &[&str],
490.         line_pos: usize,
491.     ) -> usize {
492.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
493.     }

506. 
507.         let line = lines[line_pos];
508.         parse_figure(builder, line, ctx.config);
509.         1
510.     }
511. 
512.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
513.         "figure"
514.     }
515. }

520. impl BlockParser for ReferenceDefinitionParser {
521.     fn can_parse(
522.         &self,
523.         ctx: &BlockContext,
524.         lines: &[&str],
525.         line_pos: usize,
526.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
527.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
528.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
529.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
530.     }
531. 
532.     fn detect_prepared(
533.         &self,
534.         ctx: &BlockContext,
535.         _lines: &[&str],
536.         _line_pos: usize,
537.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
538.         // Parse once and cache for emission.
539.         let parsed = try_parse_reference_definition(ctx.content)?;
540.         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, Some(Box::new(parsed))))
541.     }
542. 
543.     fn parse(
544.         &self,
545.         ctx: &BlockContext,
546.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
547.         lines: &[&str],
548.         line_pos: usize,
549.     ) -> usize {
550.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
551.     }
552. 
553.     fn parse_prepared(
554.         &self,
555.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
556.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
557.         lines: &[&str],
558.         line_pos: usize,
559.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
560.     ) -> usize {
561.         use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
562. 
563.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::REFERENCE_DEFINITION.into());
564. 
565.         let full_line = lines[line_pos];
566.         let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(full_line);
567. 
568.         // Detection already cached the parsed tuple; emission should not need to re-parse.
569.         // If payload is missing (legacy callsites), we fall back to the old raw emission.
570.         debug_assert!(
571.             payload
572.                 .and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<(usize, String, String, Option<String>)>())
573.                 .is_some()
574.         );
575. 
576.         emit_reference_definition_content(builder, content_without_newline);
577. 
578.         if !line_ending.is_empty() {
579.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
580.         }
581. 
582.         builder.finish_node();
583. 
584.         1
585.     }
586. 
587.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
588.         "reference_definition"
589.     }
590. }
591. 
592. /// Helper function to emit reference definition content with inline structure.
593. fn emit_reference_definition_content(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, text: &str) {
594.     use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
595. 
596.     if !text.starts_with('[') {
597.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
598.         return;
599.     }
600. 
601.     let rest = &text[1..];
602.     if let Some(close_pos) = rest.find(']') {
603.         let label = &rest[..close_pos];
604.         let after_bracket = &rest[close_pos + 1..];
605. 
606.         if after_bracket.starts_with(':') {
607.             // Emit LINK node with the label
608.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK.into());
609. 
610.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into());
611.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into(), "[");
612.             builder.finish_node();
613. 
614.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_TEXT.into());
615.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), label);
616.             builder.finish_node();
617. 
618.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "]");
619.             builder.finish_node(); // LINK
620.
```

</details>

 }

+pub(crate) enum BlockEffect {

+    CloseFencedDiv,
+}

 /// Trait for block-level parsers.
 ///
 /// Each block type implements this trait with a two-phase approach:

         &self,

@@ -944,6 +965,251 @@ impl BlockParser for LineBlockParser {
     }
 }
 
+// ============================================================================
+// Fenced Div Parsers (position #6)
+// ============================================================================
+
+pub(crate) struct FencedDivOpenParser;
+
+impl BlockParser for FencedDivOpenParser {
+    fn effect(&self) -> BlockEffect {
+        BlockEffect::OpenFencedDiv
+    }
+
+    fn can_parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        _lines: &[&str],
+        _line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
+        self.detect_prepared(ctx, _lines, _line_pos)
+            .map(|(d, _)| d)
+            .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
+    }
+
+    fn detect_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        _lines: &[&str],
+        _line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
+        if !ctx.config.extensions.fenced_divs {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        let div_fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(ctx.content)?;
+        Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, Some(Box::new(div_fence))))
+    }
+
+    fn parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> usize {
+        self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
+    }
+
+    fn parse_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+        payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
+    ) -> usize {
+        use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
+
+        let div_fence = payload
+            .and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<DivFenceInfo>())
+            .cloned()
+            .or_else(|| try_parse_div_fence_open(ctx.content));
+
+        let Some(div_fence) = div_fence else {
+            return 1;
+        };
+
+        // Start FENCED_DIV node (container push happens in core based on `effect`).
+        builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::FENCED_DIV.into());
+
+        // Emit opening fence with attributes as child node to avoid duplication.
+        builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_FENCE_OPEN.into());
+
+        // Use full original line to preserve indentation and newline.
+        let full_line = lines[line_pos];
+        let line_no_bq = strip_n_blockquote_markers(full_line, ctx.blockquote_depth);
+        let trimmed = line_no_bq.trim_start();
+
+        // Leading whitespace
+        let leading_ws_len = line_no_bq.len() - trimmed.len();
+        if leading_ws_len > 0 {
+            builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &line_no_bq[..leading_ws_len]);
+        }
+
+        // Fence colons
+        let fence_str: String = ":".repeat(div_fence.fence_count);
+        builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), &fence_str);
+
+        // Everything after colons
+        let after_colons = &trimmed[div_fence.fence_count..];
+        let (content_before_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(after_colons);
+
+        // Optional space before attributes
+        let has_leading_space = content_before_newline.starts_with(' ');
+        if has_leading_space {
+            builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
+        }
+
+        let content_after_space = if has_leading_space {
+            &content_before_newline[1..]
+        } else {
+            content_before_newline
+        };
+
+        // Attributes
+        builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_INFO.into());
+        builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), &div_fence.attributes);
+        builder.finish_node();
+
+        // Trailing colons (symmetric fences)
+        let (trailing_space, trailing_colons) = if div_fence.attributes.starts_with('{') {
+            if let Some(close_idx) = content_after_space.find('}') {
+                let after_attrs = &content_after_space[close_idx + 1..];
+                let trailing = after_attrs.trim_start();
+                let space_count = after_attrs.len() - trailing.len();
+                if !trailing.is_empty() && trailing.chars().all(|c| c == ':') {
+                    (space_count > 0, trailing)
+                } else {
+                    (false, "")
+                }
+            } else {
+                (false, "")
+            }
+        } else {
+            let after_attrs = &content_after_space[div_fence.attributes.len()..];
+            if let Some(after_space) = after_attrs.strip_prefix(' ') {
+                if !after_space.is_empty() && after_space.chars().all(|c| c == ':') {
+                    (true, after_space)
+                } else {
+                    (false, "")
+                }
+            } else {
+                (false, "")
+            }
+        };
+
+        if trailing_space {
+            builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
+        }
+        if !trailing_colons.is_empty() {
+            builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), trailing_colons);
+        }
+
+        if !newline_str.is_empty() {
+            builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
+        }
+
+        builder.finish_node(); // DIV_FENCE_OPEN
+
+        1
+    }
+
+    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
+        "fenced_div_open"
+    }
+}
+
+pub(crate) struct FencedDivCloseParser;
+
+impl BlockParser for FencedDivCloseParser {
+    fn effect(&self) -> BlockEffect {
+        BlockEffect::CloseFencedDiv
+    }
+
+    fn can_parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        _lines: &[&str],
+        _line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
+        self.detect_prepared(ctx, _lines, _line_pos)
+            .map(|(d, _)| d)
+            .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
+    }
+
+    fn detect_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        _lines: &[&str],
+        _line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
+        if !ctx.config.extensions.fenced_divs {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        if !ctx.in_fenced_div {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        if !is_div_closing_fence(ctx.content) {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        Some((BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt, None))
+    }
+
+    fn parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> usize {
+        self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
+    }
+
+    fn parse_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+        _payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
+    ) -> usize {
+        use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
+
+        builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_FENCE_CLOSE.into());
+
+        let full_line = lines[line_pos];
+        let line_no_bq = strip_n_blockquote_markers(full_line, ctx.blockquote_depth);
+        let trimmed = line_no_bq.trim_start();
+
+        let leading_ws_len = line_no_bq.len() - trimmed.len();
+        if leading_ws_len > 0 {
+            builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &line_no_bq[..leading_ws_len]);
+        }
+
+        let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(trimmed);
+        builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), content_without_newline);
+
+        if !line_ending.is_empty() {
+            builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
+        }
+
+        builder.finish_node();
+        1
+    }
+
+    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
+        "fenced_div_close"
+    }
+}
+
 // ============================================================================
 // Setext Heading Parser (position #3)
 // ============================================================================
@@ -1059,6 +1325,9 @@ impl BlockParserRegistry {
             Box::new(FencedCodeBlockParser),
             // (3) YAML metadata - before headers and hrules!
             Box::new(YamlMetadataParser),
+            // (6) Fenced divs ::: (open/close)
+            Box::new(FencedDivCloseParser),
+            Box::new(FencedDivOpenParser),
             // (7) Setext headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
             // Must come before ATX to properly handle `---` disambiguation
             Box::new(SetextHeadingParser),
@@ -1077,10 +1346,9 @@ impl BlockParserRegistry {
             // (19) Reference definitions
             Box::new(ReferenceDefinitionParser),
             // TODO: Migrate remaining blocks in Pandoc order:
-            // - (4-6) Lists and divs (bulletList, divHtml, divFenced)
+            // - (4-6) Lists and divs (bulletList, divHtml)
             // - (10) Tables (grid, multiline, pipe, simple)
             // - (11) Indented code blocks (AFTER fenced!)
-            // - (13) Line blocks

             // - (18) Footnote definitions (noteBlock)
@@ -1131,6 +1399,7 @@ impl BlockParserRegistry {
                 return Some(PreparedBlockMatch {
                     parser_index: i,
                     detection,
+                    effect: parser.effect(),
                     payload,
                 });
             }

<sub>⏱️ 145m 19s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Implemented **option 1** for fenced divs: the dispatcher now detects/emits `DIV_FENCE_OPEN`/`DIV_FENCE_CLOSE` and returns a `BlockEffect` (`OpenFencedDiv`/`CloseFencedDiv`), while `core.rs` remains the sole owner of container mutations (push/pop/close).

Changes:
- `block_dispatcher.rs`: added `BlockEffect`, `PreparedBlockMatch.effect`, `BlockContext.in_fenced_div`, plus `FencedDivOpenParser` and `FencedDivCloseParser` wired into registry order.
- `core.rs`: passes `in_fenced_div` into dispatcher context, applies `block_match.effect` after emission, and removed the old inline fenced-div branches.

Validation: `cargo test -q` passes (including golden `fenced_divs`).

---

<sub>⏱️ 146m 22s</sub>

### 👤 User

Okay! Let's keep going.

Moving indented code blocks into the dispatcher next (it’s stateless aside from indentation + blockquote depth) and wiring a strict-blank-line flag through BlockContext to preserve current behavior.

1196.         let has_blank_before_strict = at_document_start || prev_line_blank;
1197. 
1198.         // Check if this line looks like a table caption followed by a table
1199.         // If so, try to parse the table (which will include the caption)
1200.         if is_caption_followed_by_table(&self.lines, self.pos) {
1201.             log::debug!("Found caption followed by table at line {}", self.pos);
1202. 
1203.             // Prepare for table
1204.             self.prepare_for_block_element();
1205. 
1206.             let caption_start = self.pos;
1207. 
1208.             // The caption is at self.pos. We need to find where the actual table starts.
1209.             // Skip non-blank lines (caption continuation) and one blank line
1210.             let mut table_pos = self.pos + 1;
```

</details>

1219. // Indented Code Block Parser (position #11)
1220. // ============================================================================
1221. 
1222. pub(crate) struct IndentedCodeBlockParser;
1223. 
1224. impl BlockParser for IndentedCodeBlockParser {
1225.     fn can_parse(
1226.         &self,
1227.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1228.         _lines: &[&str],
1229.         _line_pos: usize,
1230.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
1231.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, _lines, _line_pos)
1232.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
1233.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
1234.     }
1235. 
1236.     fn detect_prepared(
1237.         &self,
1238.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1239.         _lines: &[&str],
1240.         _line_pos: usize,
1241.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
1242.         // Indented code blocks require a strict blank line before (or doc start).
1243.         if !ctx.has_blank_before_strict {
1244.             return None;
1245.         }
1246. 
1247.         // Don't treat as code if it's a list marker (list takes precedence).
1248.         if try_parse_list_marker(ctx.content, ctx.config).is_some() {
1249.             return None;
1250.         }
1251. 
1252.         if !is_indented_code_line(ctx.content) {
1253.             return None;
1254.         }
1255. 
1256.         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, None))
1257.     }
1258. 
1259.     fn parse(
1260.         &self,
1261.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1262.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1263.         lines: &[&str],
1264.         line_pos: usize,
1265.     ) -> usize {
1266.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
1267.     }
1268. 
1269.     fn parse_prepared(
1270.         &self,
1271.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1272.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1273.         lines: &[&str],
1274.         line_pos: usize,
1275.         _payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
1276.     ) -> usize {
1277.         let new_pos = parse_indented_code_block(
1278.             builder,
1279.             lines,
1280.             line_pos,
1281.             ctx.blockquote_depth,
1282.             ctx.content_indent,
1283.         );
1284.         new_pos - line_pos
1285.     }
1286. 
1287.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
1288.         "indented_code_block"
1289.     }
1290. }
1291. 
1292. // ============================================================================
1293. // Setext Heading Parser (position #3)
1294. // ============================================================================
1295. 
1296. pub(crate) struct SetextHeadingParser;
1297. 
1298. impl BlockParser for SetextHeadingParser {
1299.     fn can_parse(
1300.         &self,
1301.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1302.         _lines: &[&str],
1303.         _line_pos: usize,
1304.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
1305.         // Setext headings require blank line before (unless at document start)
1306.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
1307.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
1308.         }
1309. 
1310.         // Need next line for lookahead
1311.         let next_line = match ctx.next_line {
1312.             Some(line) => line,
1313.             None => return BlockDetectionResult::No,
1314.         };
1315. 
1316.         // Create lines array for detection function (avoid allocation)
1317.         let lines = [ctx.content, next_line];
1318. 
1319.         // Try to detect setext heading
1320.         if try_parse_setext_heading(&lines, 0).is_some() {
1321.             // Setext headings need blank line before (normal case)
1322.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
1323.         } else {
1324.             BlockDetectionResult::No
1325.         }

1. # PLAIN Continuation Edge Cases
2. 
3. ## Multi-line Definition
4. 
5. Term 1
6. :   First line of definition
7.     continues here with proper indent
8.     and even a third line.
9. 
10. Term 2
11. :   Single line definition.
12. 
13. ## Definition with Blank Lines
14. 
15. Term with blank
16. :   Definition starts here
17. 
18.     Continues after blank line
19.     with more content.
20. 
21. ## Definition Followed by Nested List
22. 
23. Term before list
24. :   Definition content here
25.     continues on next line
26.     
27.     - Nested list inside definition
28.     - Second item
29. 
30. ## Definition with Inline Markup
31. 
32. **Bold term**
33. :   Definition with *emphasis* and **bold**
34.     continues with `code` on next line
35.     and [link](http://example.com) here.
36. 
37. ## Definition Followed by Blockquote
38. 
39. Regular term
40. :   Definition text
41.     continues here
42. 
43. > Blockquote after definition
44. 
45. ## Lazy Continuation
46. 
47. Lazy term
48. :   Definition line one
49. continues without indent (lazy continuation)
50. still part of the definition.
51. 
52. ## Multiple Definitions for Same Term
53. 
54. Shared term
55. :   First definition
56.     continues here
57. 
58. :   Second definition
59.     also continues
60. 
61. ## End with Definition
62. 
63. Final term
64. :   Last definition continues
65.     to the very end.
66.
```

```
1. //! Indented code block parsing utilities.
2. //!
3. //! A block of text indented four spaces (or one tab) is treated as verbatim text.
4. //! The initial (four space or one tab) indentation is not considered part of the
5. //! verbatim text and is removed in the output.
6. //!
7. //! Note: blank lines in the verbatim text need not begin with four spaces.
8. 
9. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
10. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
11. 
12. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
13. 
14. /// Check if a line is indented enough to be part of an indented code block.
15. /// Returns true if the line starts with 4+ spaces or 1+ tab.
16. pub(crate) fn is_indented_code_line(content: &str) -> bool {
17.     if content.is_empty() {
18.         return false;
19.     }
20. 
21.     // Check for tab
22.     if content.starts_with('\t') {
23.         return true;
24.     }
25. 
26.     // Check for 4+ spaces
27.     let spaces = content.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ' ').count();
28.     spaces >= 4
29. }
30. 
31. /// Parse an indented code block, consuming lines from the parser.
32. /// Returns the new position after the code block.
33. ///
34. /// An indented code block consists of consecutive lines that are either:
35. /// - Indented by 4+ spaces or 1+ tab
36. /// - Blank lines (which don't need indentation)
37. ///
38. /// The block ends when we hit a non-blank line that isn't indented enough.
39. /// Parse an indented code block, consuming lines from the parser.
40. /// Returns the new position after the code block.
41. ///
42. /// An indented code block consists of consecutive lines that are either:
43. /// - Indented by 4+ spaces or 1+ tab (beyond base_indent)
44. /// - Blank lines (which don't need indentation)
45. ///
46. /// The block ends when we hit a non-blank line that isn't indented enough.
47. pub(crate) fn parse_indented_code_block(
48.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
49.     lines: &[&str],
50.     start_pos: usize,
51.     bq_depth: usize,
52.     base_indent: usize,
53. ) -> usize {
54.     use super::blockquotes::count_blockquote_markers;
55. 
56.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::CODE_BLOCK.into());
57.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::CODE_CONTENT.into());
58. 
59.     let mut current_pos = start_pos;
60.     // Total indent needed: base (e.g., footnote) + 4 for code
61.     let code_indent = base_indent + 4;
62. 
63.     while current_pos < lines.len() {
64.         let line = lines[current_pos];
65. 
66.         // Strip blockquote markers to get inner content
67.         let (line_bq_depth, inner) = count_blockquote_markers(line);
68. 
69.         // If blockquote depth decreases, code block ends (we've left the blockquote)
70.         if line_bq_depth < bq_depth {
71.             break;
72.         }
73. 
74.         // Blank lines need look-ahead: only include if next non-blank line continues the code
75.         if inner.trim().is_empty() {
76.             // Check if code continues after this blank line
77.             let mut look_pos = current_pos + 1;
78.             let mut continues = false;
79.             while look_pos < lines.len() {
80.                 let (look_bq_depth, look_inner) = count_blockquote_markers(lines[look_pos]);
81.                 if look_bq_depth < bq_depth {
82.                     break;
83.                 }
84.                 if look_inner.trim_end_matches('\n').trim().is_empty() {
85.                     look_pos += 1;
86.                     continue;
87.                 }
88.                 let (look_indent, _) = leading_indent(look_inner);
89.                 if look_indent >= code_indent {
90.                     continues = true;
91.                 }
92.                 break;
93.             }
94.             if !continues {
95.                 break;
96.             }
97.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "");
98.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), "\n");
99.             current_pos += 1;
100.             continue;
101.         }
102. 
103.         // Check if line is indented enough (base_indent + 4 for code)
104.         let (indent_cols, indent_bytes) = leading_indent(inner);
105.         if indent_cols < code_indent {
106.             break;
107.         }
108. 
109.         // For losslessness: emit ALL indentation as WHITESPACE, then emit remaining content
110.         // The formatter can decide how to handle the indentation
111.         if indent_bytes > 0 {
112.             let indent_str = &inner[..indent_bytes];
113.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), indent_str);
114.         }
115. 
116.         // Get the content after the indentation
117.         let content = &inner[indent_bytes..];
118. 
119.         // Split off trailing newline if present (from split_inclusive)
120.         let (content_without_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(content);
```

</details>

120.         let (content_without_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(content);
121. 
122.         if !content_without_newline.is_empty() {
123.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), content_without_newline);
124.         }
125. 
126.         if !newline_str.is_empty() {
127.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
128.         }
129. 
130.         current_pos += 1;
131.     }
132. 
133.     builder.finish_node(); // CodeContent
134.     builder.finish_node(); // CodeBlock
135. 
136.     current_pos
137. }

139. use crate::parser::utils::container_stack::leading_indent;
140. 
141. #[cfg(test)]
142. mod tests {
143.     use super::*;
144. 
145.     #[test]
146.     fn test_is_indented_code_line() {
147.         assert!(is_indented_code_line("    code"));
148.         assert!(is_indented_code_line("        code"));
149.         assert!(is_indented_code_line("\tcode"));
150.         assert!(!is_indented_code_line("   not enough"));
151.         assert!(!is_indented_code_line(""));
152.         assert!(!is_indented_code_line("no indent"));
153.     }
154. 
155.     #[test]
156.     fn test_parse_simple_code_block() {
157.         let input = vec!["    code line 1", "    code line 2"];
158.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
159.         let new_pos = parse_indented_code_block(&mut builder, &input, 0, 0, 0);
160.         assert_eq!(new_pos, 2);
161.     }
162. 
163.     #[test]
164.     fn test_parse_code_block_with_blank_line() {
165.         let input = vec!["    code line 1", "", "    code line 2"];
166.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
167.         let new_pos = parse_indented_code_block(&mut builder, &input, 0, 0, 0);
168.         assert_eq!(new_pos, 3);
169.     }
170. 
171.     #[test]
172.     fn test_parse_code_block_stops_at_unindented() {
173.         let input = vec!["    code line 1", "    code line 2", "not code"];
174.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
175.         let new_pos = parse_indented_code_block(&mut builder, &input, 0, 0, 0);
176.         assert_eq!(new_pos, 2);
177.     }
178. 
179.     #[test]
180.     fn test_parse_code_block_with_tab() {
181.         let input = vec!["\tcode with tab", "\tanother line"];
182.         let mut builder = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
183.         let new_pos = parse_indented_code_block(&mut builder, &input, 0, 0, 0);
184.         assert_eq!(new_pos, 2);
185.     }
186. }

5. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
6. pub(crate) enum Container {

8.         // No special tracking needed

11.         // No special tracking needed - closed by fence marker
12.     },

1802.             // Start new definition item

1804.             self.containers.push(Container::DefinitionItem {});
1805. 
1806.             // Emit term
1807.             emit_term(&mut self.builder, content, self.config);
1808.             self.pos += 1;

<sub>⏱️ 153m 45s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’m going to fix the regression by ensuring an unindented blank line inside a definition isn’t treated as a plain continuation (so the following indented lines can become an indented code block).

---

342.     /// Returns true if the line was consumed.
343.     fn parse_line(&mut self, line: &str) -> bool {
344.         // Count blockquote markers on this line
345.         let (bq_depth, inner_content) = count_blockquote_markers(line);
346.         let current_bq_depth = self.current_blockquote_depth();
347. 
348.         log::debug!(
349.             "parse_line [{}]: bq_depth={}, current_bq={}, depth={}, line={:?}",

354.             line.trim_end()
355.         );
356. 
357.         // Handle blank lines specially (including blank lines inside blockquotes)
358.         // A line like ">" with nothing after is a blank line inside a blockquote
359.         // Note: lines may end with \n from split_inclusive
360.         // TODO: Does this handle CLRF correctly?

362.             || (bq_depth > 0 && inner_content.trim_end_matches('\n').trim().is_empty());
363. 
364.         if is_blank {
365.             // Close paragraph if open
366.             self.close_paragraph_if_open();
367. 
368.             // Close Plain node in Definition if open
369.             // Blank lines should close Plain, allowing subsequent content to be siblings
370.             // Emit buffered PLAIN content before continuing
371.             self.emit_buffered_plain_if_needed();
372. 
373.             // Note: Blank lines between terms and definitions are now preserved
374.             // and emitted as part of the term parsing logic
375. 
376.             // For blank lines inside blockquotes, we need to handle them at the right depth

379.                 // Open blockquotes

382.                     self.containers.push(Container::BlockQuote {});

384.             } else if bq_depth < current_bq_depth {
385.                 // Close blockquotes down to bq_depth (must use Parser close to emit buffers)

203.     /// Emit buffered PLAIN content if there's an open PLAIN in a Definition.
204.     /// This is used when we need to close PLAIN but keep the Definition container open.
205.     fn emit_buffered_plain_if_needed(&mut self) {
206.         // Check if we have an open PLAIN with buffered content
207.         if let Some(Container::Definition {
208.             plain_open: true,
209.             plain_buffer,
210.             ..
211.         }) = self.containers.stack.last()
212.             && !plain_buffer.is_empty()
213.         {
214.             // Emit PLAIN node with buffered inline-parsed content
215.             self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::PLAIN.into());
216.             let text = plain_buffer.get_accumulated_text();
217.             inline_emission::emit_inlines(&mut self.builder, &text, self.config);
218.             self.builder.finish_node();
219.         }
220. 
221.         // Mark PLAIN as closed and clear buffer
222.         if let Some(Container::Definition {
223.             plain_open,
224.             plain_buffer,
225.             ..
226.         }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
227.             && *plain_open
228.         {
229.             plain_buffer.clear();
230.             *plain_open = false;
231.         }
232.     }
233. 
234.     /// Close blockquotes down to a target depth.
235.     ///
236.     /// Must use `Parser::close_containers_to` (not `ContainerStack::close_to`) so list/paragraph
237.     /// buffers are emitted for losslessness.
238.     fn close_blockquotes_to_depth(&mut self, target_depth: usize) {
239.         let mut current = self.current_blockquote_depth();
240.         while current > target_depth {
241.             while !matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::BlockQuote { .. })) {
242.                 if self.containers.depth() == 0 {
243.                     break;
244.                 }
245.                 self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
246.             }
247.             if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::BlockQuote { .. })) {
248.                 self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
249.                 current -= 1;
250.             } else {
251.                 break;
252.             }
253.         }
254.     }

829.     /// Compute how many container levels to keep open based on next line content.
830.     fn compute_levels_to_keep(&self, next_line: &str) -> usize {
831.         let (next_bq_depth, next_inner) = count_blockquote_markers(next_line);
832.         let (raw_indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(next_inner);
833.         let next_marker = try_parse_list_marker(next_inner, self.config);

838.         log::debug!(
839.             "compute_levels_to_keep: next_line indent={}, has_marker={}, stack_depth={}, current_bq={}, next_bq={}",

841.             next_marker.is_some(),
842.             self.containers.depth(),
843.             current_bq_depth,
844.             next_bq_depth
845.         );
846. 
847.         let mut keep_level = 0;
848.         let mut content_indent_so_far = 0usize;
849. 
850.         // First, account for blockquotes
851.         for (i, c) in self.containers.stack.iter().enumerate() {
852.             match c {
853.                 Container::BlockQuote { .. } => {
854.                     // Count blockquotes up to this point
855.                     let bq_count = self.containers.stack[..=i]
856.                         .iter()
857.                         .filter(|x| matches!(x, Container::BlockQuote { .. }))
858.                         .count();

864.                     // Track footnote indent for nested containers

867.                     // (or at the content column if content started after marker)

890.                         let has_list_marker =

902.                 }

904.                     marker,
905.                     base_indent_cols,
906.                     ..
907.                 } => {
908.                     // Adjust indent for footnote context

911.                         markers_match(marker, nm) && effective_indent <= base_indent_cols + 3
912.                     } else {
913.                         // For non-list-marker lines, must be indented past list content

915.                             .containers
916.                             .stack
917.                             .get(i + 1)

919.                                 Container::ListItem { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
920.                                 _ => None,
921.                             })

931.                     // Keep list item if next line is indented to content column
932.                     // BUT NOT if it's a new list item marker at an outer level
933.

935.                     // those extra markers count as "content" that should be indented for list continuation.

937.                     // indicate list continuation, and the second > is content.

808.     fn has_nested_block_structure(&self, content: &str) -> bool {
809.         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
810.             content,
811.             has_blank_before: true,
812.             has_blank_before_strict: true,
813.             at_document_start: false,
814.             in_fenced_div: self.in_fenced_div(),

817.             content_indent: 0,
818.             list_indent_info: None,
819.             next_line: None,
820.         };
821. 
822.         // Note: we only use the registry for its “is this a block start?” decision;
823.         // it should not rely on `lines` here (setext is gated by `has_blank_before`).
824.         self.block_registry
825.             .detect_prepared(&block_ctx, &[], 0)
826.             .is_some()
827.     }

1. use crate::config::Config;
2. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
3. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
4. 
5. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
6. use crate::parser::utils::inline_emission;
7. 
8. /// Tries to parse a definition list marker (`:` or `~`)
9. ///
10. /// Returns Some((marker_char, indent, spaces_after)) if found, None otherwise.
11. /// The marker can be indented 0-3 spaces and must be followed by whitespace.
12. pub(crate) fn try_parse_definition_marker(line: &str) -> Option<(char, usize, usize)> {
13.     // Count leading spaces (0-3 allowed)
14.     let indent = line.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ' ').count();
15.     if indent > 3 {
16.         return None;
17.     }
18. 
19.     let after_indent = &line[indent..];
20. 
21.     // Check for : or ~ marker
22.     let marker = after_indent.chars().next()?;
23.     if !matches!(marker, ':' | '~') {
24.         return None;
25.     }
26. 
27.     let after_marker = &after_indent[1..];
28. 
29.     // Must be followed by whitespace
30.     if !after_marker.starts_with(' ') && !after_marker.starts_with('\t') && !after_marker.is_empty()
31.     {
32.         return None;
33.     }
34. 
35.     let spaces_after = after_marker
36.         .chars()
37.         .take_while(|&c| c == ' ' || c == '\t')
38.         .count();
39. 
40.     Some((marker, indent, spaces_after))
41. }
42. 
43. /// Emit a term line into the syntax tree
44. pub(crate) fn emit_term(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, line: &str, config: &Config) {
45.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::TERM.into());
46.     // Strip trailing newline from line (it will be emitted separately)
47.     let (text, newline_str) = strip_newline(line);
48.     let trimmed_text = text.trim_end();
49. 
50.     if !trimmed_text.is_empty() {
51.         inline_emission::emit_inlines(builder, trimmed_text, config);
52.     }
53. 
54.     if !newline_str.is_empty() {
55.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
56.     }
57.     builder.finish_node(); // Term
58. }
59. 
60. /// Emit a definition marker
61. pub(crate) fn emit_definition_marker(
62.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
63.     marker: char,
64.     indent: usize,
65. ) {
66.     if indent > 0 {
67.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &" ".repeat(indent));
68.     }
69.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::DEFINITION_MARKER.into(), &marker.to_string());
70. }
71. 
72. // Helper functions for definition list management in Parser
73. 
74. use crate::parser::utils::container_stack::{Container, ContainerStack};
75. 
76. /// Check if we're in a definition list.
77. pub(in crate::parser) fn in_definition_list(containers: &ContainerStack) -> bool {
78.     containers
79.         .stack
80.         .iter()
81.         .any(|c| matches!(c, Container::DefinitionList { .. }))
82. }
83. 
84. /// Look ahead past blank lines to find a definition marker.
85. /// Returns Some(blank_line_count) if found, None otherwise.
86. pub(in crate::parser) fn next_line_is_definition_marker(
87.     lines: &[&str],
88.     pos: usize,
89. ) -> Option<usize> {
90.     let mut check_pos = pos + 1;
91.     let mut blank_count = 0;
92.     while check_pos < lines.len() {
93.         let line = lines[check_pos];
94.         if line.trim().is_empty() {
95.             blank_count += 1;
96.             check_pos += 1;
97.             continue;
98.         }
99.         if try_parse_definition_marker(line).is_some() {
100.             return Some(blank_count);
101.         } else {
102.             return None;
103.         }
104.     }
105.     None
106. }
107. 
108. #[cfg(test)]
109. mod tests {
110.     use super::*;
111. 
112.     #[test]
113.     fn test_parse_definition_marker_colon() {
114.         assert_eq!(
115.             try_parse_definition_marker(":   Definition"),
116.             Some((':', 0, 3))
117.         );
118.     }
119. 
120.     #[test]
121.     fn test_parse_definition_marker_tilde() {
122.         assert_eq!(
123.             try_parse_definition_marker("~   Definition"),
124.             Some(('~', 0, 3))
125.         );
126.     }
127. 
128.     #[test]
129.     fn test_parse_definition_marker_indented() {
130.         assert_eq!(
131.             try_parse_definition_marker("  : Definition"),
132.             Some((':', 2, 1))
133.         );
134.         assert_eq!(
135.             try_parse_definition_marker("   ~ Definition"),
136.             Some(('~', 3, 1))
137.         );
138.     }
139. 
140.     #[test]
141.     fn test_parse_definition_marker_too_indented() {
142.         assert_eq!(try_parse_definition_marker("    : Definition"), None);
143.     }
144. 
145.     #[test]
146.     fn test_parse_definition_marker_no_space_after() {
147.         assert_eq!(try_parse_definition_marker(":Definition"), None);
148.     }
149. 
150.     #[test]
151.     fn test_parse_definition_marker_at_eol() {
152.         assert_eq!(try_parse_definition_marker(":"), Some((':', 0, 0)));
153.     }
154. }

992.     /// Get the total indentation to strip from content containers (footnotes + definitions).
993.     fn content_container_indent_to_strip(&self) -> usize {

995.             .stack

997.             .filter_map(|c| match c {
998.                 Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
999.                 Container::Definition { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
1000.                 _ => None,
1001.             })
1002.             .sum()
1003.     }

10. pub(crate) enum ListMarker {
11.     Bullet(char),
12.     Ordered(OrderedMarker),
13. }
14. 
15. #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
16. pub(crate) enum OrderedMarker {
17.     Decimal {
18.         number: String,
19.         style: ListDelimiter,
20.     },

23.         letter: char,

49. 
50. /// Parse a Roman numeral (lower or upper case).
51. /// Returns (numeral_string, length) if valid, None otherwise.
52. fn try_parse_roman_numeral(text: &str, uppercase: bool) -> Option<(String, usize)> {
53.     let valid_chars = if uppercase { "IVXLCDM" } else { "ivxlcdm" };
54. 
55.     let count = text
56.         .chars()
57.         .take_while(|c| valid_chars.contains(*c))
58.         .count();
59. 
60.     if count == 0 {
61.         return None;
62.     }
63. 
64.     let numeral = &text[..count];
65.     let numeral_upper = numeral.to_uppercase();
66. 
67.     // Only consider chars that are valid Roman numeral symbols
68.     // Reject if it contains only non-Roman letters (a-z except i, v, x, l, c, d, m)
69.     let has_only_roman_chars = numeral_upper.chars().all(|c| "IVXLCDM".contains(c));
70.     if !has_only_roman_chars {
71.         return None;
72.     }
73. 
74.     // For single-character numerals, only accept the most common ones to avoid
75.     // ambiguity with alphabetic list markers (a-z, A-Z).
76.     // Single L, C, D, M are valid Roman numerals but unlikely in list contexts.
77.     if count == 1 {
78.         let ch = numeral_upper.chars().next().unwrap();
79.         if !matches!(ch, 'I' | 'V' | 'X') {
80.             return None;
81.         }
82.     }
83. 
84.     // Validate it's a proper Roman numeral (basic validation)
85.     // Must not have more than 3 consecutive same characters (except M)
86.     if numeral_upper.contains("IIII")
87.         || numeral_upper.contains("XXXX")
88.         || numeral_upper.contains("CCCC")
89.         || numeral_upper.contains("VV")
90.         || numeral_upper.contains("LL")
91.         || numeral_upper.contains("DD")
92.     {
93.         return None;
94.     }
95. 
96.     // Must have valid subtractive notation (I before V/X, X before L/C, C before D/M)
97.     // V, L, D can never appear before a larger numeral (no subtractive use)
98.     let chars: Vec<char> = numeral_upper.chars().collect();
99.     for i in 0..chars.len().saturating_sub(1) {
100.         let curr = chars[i];
101.         let next = chars[i + 1];
102. 
103.         // Get Roman numeral values for comparison
104.         let curr_val = match curr {
105.             'I' => 1,
106.             'V' => 5,
107.             'X' => 10,
108.             'L' => 50,
109.             'C' => 100,
110.             'D' => 500,
111.             'M' => 1000,
112.             _ => return None,
113.         };
114.         let next_val = match next {
115.             'I' => 1,
116.             'V' => 5,
117.             'X' => 10,
118.             'L' => 50,
119.             'C' => 100,
120.             'D' => 500,
```

140. pub(crate) fn try_parse_list_marker(
141.     line: &str,
142.     config: &Config,
143. ) -> Option<(ListMarker, usize, usize)> {
144.     let trimmed = line.trim_start_matches([' ', '\t']);
145. 
146.     // Try bullet markers (including task lists)
147.     if let Some(ch) = trimmed.chars().next()
148.         && matches!(ch, '*' | '+' | '-')
149.     {
150.         let after_marker = &trimmed[1..];
151. 
152.         // Check for task list: [ ] or [x] or [X]
153.         let trimmed_after = after_marker.trim_start();
154.         let is_task = trimmed_after.starts_with('[')
155.             && trimmed_after.len() >= 3
156.             && matches!(
157.                 trimmed_after.chars().nth(1),
158.                 Some(' ') | Some('x') | Some('X')
159.             )
160.             && trimmed_after.chars().nth(2) == Some(']');
161. 
162.         // Must be followed by whitespace (or be task list)
163.         if after_marker.starts_with(' ')
164.             || after_marker.starts_with('\t')
165.             || after_marker.is_empty()
166.             || is_task
167.         {
168.             let spaces_after = after_marker
169.                 .chars()
170.                 .take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace())
171.                 .count();
172.             return Some((ListMarker::Bullet(ch), 1, spaces_after));
173.         }
174.     }
175. 
176.     // Try ordered markers
177.     if let Some(after_marker) = trimmed.strip_prefix("#.")
178.         && (after_marker.starts_with(' ')
179.             || after_marker.starts_with('\t')
180.             || after_marker.is_empty())
181.     {
182.         let spaces_after = after_marker
183.             .chars()
184.             .take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace())
185.             .count();
186.         return Some((ListMarker::Ordered(OrderedMarker::Hash), 2, spaces_after));
187.     }
188. 
189.     // Try example lists: (@) or (@label)
190.     if config.extensions.example_lists
191.         && let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix("(@")
192.     {
193.         // Check if it has a label or is just (@)
194.         let label_end = rest
195.             .chars()
196.             .take_while(|c| c.is_alphanumeric() || *c == '_' || *c == '-')
197.             .count();
198. 
199.         // Must be followed by ')'
200.         if rest.len() > label_end && rest.chars().nth(label_end) == Some(')') {
201.             let label = if label_end > 0 {
202.                 Some(rest[..label_end].to_string())
203.             } else {
204.                 None
205.             };
206. 
207.             let after_marker = &rest[label_end + 1..];
208.             if after_marker.starts_with(' ')
209.                 || after_marker.starts_with('\t')
210.                 || after_marker.is_empty()
211.             {
212.                 let spaces_after = after_marker
213.                     .chars()
214.                     .take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace())
215.                     .count();
216.                 let marker_len = 2 + label_end + 1; // "(@" + label + ")"
217.                 return Some((
218.                     ListMarker::Ordered(OrderedMarker::Example { label }),
219.                     marker_len,
220.                     spaces_after,
221.                 ));
222.             }
223.         }
224.     }
225. 
226.     // Try parenthesized markers: (2), (a), (ii)
227.     if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix('(') {
228.         // Try decimal: (2)
229.         let digit_count = rest.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_ascii_digit()).count();
230.         if digit_count > 0 && rest.len() > digit_count && rest.chars().nth(digit_count) == Some(')')
231.         {
232.             let number = &rest[..digit_count];
233.             let after_marker = &rest[digit_count + 1..];
234.             if after_marker.starts_with(' ')
235.                 || after_marker.starts_with('\t')
236.                 || after_marker.is_empty()
237.             {
238.                 let spaces_after = after_marker
239.                     .chars()
240.                     .take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace())
```

import os,sys,difflib
out1=os.environ['out1']
out2=os.environ['out2']
if out1==out2:
    print('idempotent')
    sys.exit(0)
for line in difflib.unified_diff(out1.splitlines(True), out2.splitlines(True), fromfile='once', tofile='twice'):
    sys.stdout.write(line)

<details>

```
1. //! Golden test cases for panache formatter.
2. //!
3. //! Each test case is a directory under `tests/cases/` containing:
4. //! - `input.*` - Source file (`.md`, `.qmd`, or `.Rmd`)
5. //! - `expected.*` - Expected formatted output (same extension as input)
6. //! - `cst.txt` - (Optional) Expected CST structure for parse regression testing
7. //! - `panache.toml` - (Optional) Config to test specific flavors/extensions
8. //!
9. //! Run with `UPDATE_EXPECTED=1 cargo test` to regenerate expected outputs.
10. //! Run with `UPDATE_CST=1 cargo test` to regenerate CST files.
11. //! Run with both flags to update both: `UPDATE_EXPECTED=1 UPDATE_CST=1 cargo test`.
12. 
13. use panache::{Config, format, parse};
14. use std::{
15.     fs,
16.     path::{Path, PathBuf},
17. };
18. 
19. /// Find a file with given base name and any supported extension.
20. fn find_file_with_extension(dir: &Path, base: &str) -> Option<PathBuf> {
21.     for ext in &["md", "qmd", "Rmd"] {
22.         let path = dir.join(format!("{}.{}", base, ext));
23.         if path.exists() {
24.             return Some(path);
25.         }
26.     }
27.     None
28. }
29. 
30. /// Load config from test case directory if it exists.
31. fn load_test_config(dir: &Path) -> Option<Config> {
32.     let config_path = dir.join("panache.toml");
33.     if config_path.exists() {
34.         let content = fs::read_to_string(config_path).ok()?;
35.         toml::from_str(&content).ok()
36.     } else {
37.         None
38.     }
39. }
40. 
41. /// Run a single golden test case.
42. fn run_golden_case(case_name: &str) {
43.     let dir = Path::new(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"))
44.         .join("tests")
45.         .join("cases")
46.         .join(case_name);
47. 
48.     let update_expected = std::env::var_os("UPDATE_EXPECTED").is_some();
49.     let update_cst = std::env::var_os("UPDATE_CST").is_some();
50. 
51.     // Find input file with any supported extension
52.     let input_path = find_file_with_extension(&dir, "input")
53.         .unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("No input file found in {}", case_name));
54. 
55.     // Determine expected path based on input extension
56.     let input_ext = input_path
57.         .extension()
58.         .and_then(|e| e.to_str())
59.         .unwrap_or("qmd");
60.     let expected_path = dir.join(format!("expected.{}", input_ext));
61. 
62.     let cst_path = dir.join("cst.txt");
63. 
64.     // Load optional config
65.     let config = load_test_config(&dir);
66. 
67.     // Read input file - preserve line endings exactly
68.     let input = fs::read_to_string(&input_path).unwrap();
69. 
70.     // Test losslessness: parser must preserve input byte-for-byte
71.     // This is critical for LSP, linting, and range formatting
72.     let ast = parse(&input, config.clone());
73.     let tree_text = ast.text().to_string();
74. 
75.     // Use similar_asserts for nice diff output showing exactly where bytes are lost
76.     similar_asserts::assert_eq!(
77.         input,
78.         tree_text,
79.         "losslessness check failed for {} (tree text does not match input, diff: {:+} bytes)",
80.         case_name,
81.         tree_text.len() as i64 - input.len() as i64
82.     );
83. 
84.     // Test formatting
85.     let output = format(&input, config.clone(), None);
86. 
87.     // Idempotency: formatting twice should equal once
88.     let output_twice = format(&output, config.clone(), None);
89.     similar_asserts::assert_eq!(output, output_twice, "idempotency: {}", case_name);
90. 
91.     // Test CST parsing (if cst.txt exists or we're updating CST)
92.     if cst_path.exists() || update_cst {
93.         let cst_output = format!("{:#?}\n", ast);
94. 
95.         if update_cst {
96.             fs::write(&cst_path, &cst_output).unwrap();
97.         } else {
98.             let expected_cst = fs::read_to_string(&cst_path)
99.                 .unwrap_or_else(|_| panic!("Failed to read cst.txt in {}", case_name));
100.             similar_asserts::assert_eq!(expected_cst, cst_output, "CST mismatch: {}", case_name);
101.         }
102.     }
103. 
104.     if update_expected {
105.         fs::write(&expected_path, &output).unwrap();
106.         return;
107.     }
108. 
109.     let expected = fs::read_to_string(&expected_path).unwrap_or_else(|_| input.clone());
110. 
111.     similar_asserts::assert_eq!(expected, output, "case: {}", case_name);
112. }
113. 
114. /// Macro to generate individual test functions for each golden case.
115. ///
116. /// Usage: `golden_test_cases!(case1, case2, case3);`
117. ///
118. /// This generates separate test functions named `golden_case1`, `golden_case2`, etc.
119. /// Each test runs independently, so failures don't stop other tests from running.
120. macro_rules! golden_test_cases {
121.     ($($case:ident),+ $(,)?) => {
122.         $(
123.             #[test]
124.             fn $case() {
125.                 run_golden_case(stringify!($case));
126.             }
127.         )+
128.     };
129. }
130.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 159m 36s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Build tiny repro using panache::format like tests do**

$ cat > /tmp/repro.rs <<'RS'
use std::fs;

fn main() {
    let input = fs::read_to_string("tests/cases/plain_continuation_edge_cases/input.md").unwrap();
    let out1 = panache::format(&input, None, None);
    let out2 = panache::format(&out1, None, None);
    if out1 == out2 {
        println!("idempotent");
        return;
    }
    println!("not idempotent");
    for diff in diffy::create_patch(&out1, &out2).to_string().lines().take(200) {
        println!("{}", diff);
    }
}

958. /// Checks if the content after a list marker is exactly another bullet marker.
959. /// Returns the nested bullet marker character if detected.
960. pub(in crate::parser) fn is_content_nested_bullet_marker(
961.     content: &str,
962.     marker_len: usize,
963.     spaces_after: usize,
964. ) -> Option<char> {
965.     let indent_bytes = content.len() - content.trim_start().len();
966.     let content_start = indent_bytes + marker_len + spaces_after;
967. 
968.     if content_start >= content.len() {
969.         return None;
970.     }
971. 
972.     let remaining = &content[content_start..];
973.     let (text_part, _) = strip_newline(remaining);
974.     let trimmed = text_part.trim();
975. 
976.     // Check if it's exactly one of the bullet marker characters
977.     if trimmed.len() == 1 {
978.         let ch = trimmed.chars().next().unwrap();
979.         if matches!(ch, '*' | '+' | '-') {
980.             return Some(ch);
981.         }
982.     }
983. 
984.     None
985. }
986. 
987. /// Add a list item that contains a nested empty list (for cases like `- *`).
988. /// This creates: LIST_ITEM (outer) -> LIST (nested) -> LIST_ITEM (empty inner)
989. #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
990. pub(in crate::parser) fn add_list_item_with_nested_empty_list(
991.     containers: &mut ContainerStack,
992.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
993.     content: &str,
994.     marker_len: usize,
995.     spaces_after: usize,
996.     indent_cols: usize,
997.     indent_bytes: usize,
998.     nested_marker: char,
999. ) {
1000.     // First, emit the outer list item (just marker + whitespace)
1001.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LIST_ITEM.into());
1002. 
1003.     // Emit leading indentation for lossless parsing
1004.     if indent_bytes > 0 {
1005.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &content[..indent_bytes]);
1006.     }
1007. 
1008.     let marker_text = &content[indent_bytes..indent_bytes + marker_len];
1009.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::LIST_MARKER.into(), marker_text);
1010. 
1011.     if spaces_after > 0 {
1012.         let space_start = indent_bytes + marker_len;
1013.         let space_end = space_start + spaces_after;
1014.         if space_end <= content.len() {
1015.             builder.token(
1016.                 SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(),
1017.                 &content[space_start..space_end],
1018.             );
1019.         }
1020.     }
1021. 
1022.     // Now start the nested list inside this item
1023.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LIST.into());
1024. 
1025.     // Add empty list item to the nested list
1026.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LIST_ITEM.into());
1027.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::LIST_MARKER.into(), &nested_marker.to_string());
1028. 
1029.     // Extract and emit the newline from original content (lossless)
1030.     let content_start = indent_bytes + marker_len + spaces_after;
1031.     if content_start < content.len() {
1032.         let remaining = &content[content_start..];
1033.         // Skip the nested marker character (1 byte) and get the newline
1034.         if remaining.len() > 1 {
1035.             let (_, newline_str) = strip_newline(&remaining[1..]);
1036.             if !newline_str.is_empty() {
1037.                 builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
1038.             }
1039.         }
1040.     }
1041. 
1042.     builder.finish_node(); // Close nested LIST_ITEM
1043.     builder.finish_node(); // Close nested LIST
1044. 
1045.     // Push container for the outer list item
1046.     let content_col = indent_cols + marker_len + spaces_after;
1047.     containers.push(Container::ListItem {
1048.         content_col,
1049.         buffer: ListItemBuffer::new(),
1050.     });
1051. }
1052. 
1053. /// Add a list item to the current list.
1054. pub(in crate::parser) fn add_list_item(
1055.     containers: &mut ContainerStack,
1056.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1057.     content: &str,
1058.     marker_len: usize,
1059.     spaces_after: usize,
1060.     indent_cols: usize,
1061.     indent_bytes: usize,
1062. ) {
1063.     let (content_col, text_to_buffer) = emit_list_item(
1064.         builder,
1065.         content,
1066.         marker_len,
1067.         spaces_after,
1068.         indent_cols,
1069.         indent_bytes,
1070.     );
1071. 
1072.     log::debug!(
1073.         "add_list_item: content={:?}, text_to_buffer={:?}",
1074.         content,
1075.         text_to_buffer
1076.     );
1077. 
1078.     let mut buffer = ListItemBuffer::new();
1079.     if !text_to_buffer.is_empty() {
1080.         buffer.push_text(text_to_buffer);
1081.     }
1082.     containers.push(Container::ListItem {
1083.         content_col,
1084.         buffer,
1085.     });
1086. }

561. /// Emit a list item node to the builder (marker and whitespace only).
562. /// Returns (content_col, text_to_buffer) where text_to_buffer is the content that should be
563. /// added to the list item buffer for later inline parsing.
564. pub(crate) fn emit_list_item(
565.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
566.     content: &str,
567.     marker_len: usize,
568.     spaces_after: usize,
569.     indent_cols: usize,
570.     indent_bytes: usize,
571. ) -> (usize, String) {
572.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LIST_ITEM.into());
573. 
574.     // Emit leading indentation for lossless parsing
575.     if indent_bytes > 0 {
576.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &content[..indent_bytes]);
577.     }
578. 
579.     let marker_text = &content[indent_bytes..indent_bytes + marker_len];
580.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::LIST_MARKER.into(), marker_text);
581. 
582.     if spaces_after > 0 {
583.         let space_start = indent_bytes + marker_len;
584.         let space_end = space_start + spaces_after;
585.         if space_end <= content.len() {
586.             builder.token(
587.                 SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(),
588.                 &content[space_start..space_end],
589.             );
590.         }
591.     }
592. 
593.     let content_col = indent_cols + marker_len + spaces_after;
594.     let content_start = indent_bytes + marker_len + spaces_after;
595. 
596.     // Extract text content to be buffered (instead of emitting it directly).
597.     // If the item starts with a task checkbox, emit it as a dedicated token so it
598.     // doesn't get parsed as a link.
599.     let text_to_buffer = if content_start < content.len() {
600.         let rest = &content[content_start..];
601.         if (rest.starts_with("[ ]") || rest.starts_with("[x]") || rest.starts_with("[X]"))
602.             && rest
603.                 .as_bytes()
604.                 .get(3)
605.                 .is_some_and(|b| (*b as char).is_whitespace())
606.         {
607.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TASK_CHECKBOX.into(), &rest[..3]);
608.             rest[3..].to_string()
609.         } else {
610.             rest.to_string()
611.         }
612.     } else {
613.         String::new()
614.     };
615. 
616.     (content_col, text_to_buffer)
617. }
618. 
619. #[cfg(test)]
620. mod tests {
621.     use super::*;
622.     use crate::config::Config;
623. 
624.     #[test]
625.     fn detects_bullet_markers() {
626.         let config = Config::default();
627.         assert!(try_parse_list_marker("* item", &config).is_some());
628.     }
629. 
630.     #[test]
631.     fn detects_fancy_alpha_markers() {
632.         let mut config = Config::default();
633.         config.extensions.fancy_lists = true;
634. 
635.         // Test lowercase alpha period
636.         assert!(
637.             try_parse_list_marker("a. item", &config).is_some(),
638.             "a. should parse"
639.         );
640.         assert!(
641.             try_parse_list_marker("b. item", &config).is_some(),
642.             "b. should parse"
643.         );
644.         assert!(
645.             try_parse_list_marker("c. item", &config).is_some(),
646.             "c. should parse"
647.         );
648. 
649.         // Test lowercase alpha right paren
650.         assert!(
651.             try_parse_list_marker("a) item", &config).is_some(),
652.             "a) should parse"
653.         );
654.         assert!(
655.             try_parse_list_marker("b) item", &config).is_some(),
656.             "b) should parse"
657.         );
658.     }
659. }

<sub>⏱️ 162m 45s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’m going to reproduce the non-idempotent formatting region and then adjust the blank-line “levels_to_keep” logic so list items inside definition lists remain stable across multiple format passes.

---

<sub>⏱️ 162m 46s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Format twice and print the failing section**

$ set -euo pipefail
in=tests/cases/plain_continuation_edge_cases/input.md
out1=$(cat "$in" | cargo run -q -- format)
out2=$(printf "%s" "$out1" | cargo run -q -- format)
python - <<'PY'
import os,re
out1=os.environ['out1']
out2=os.environ['out2']
pat=re.compile(r"## Definition Followed by Nested List.*?(?=\n## )",re.S)
m1=pat.search(out1)
m2=pat.search(out2)
print('--- once ---')
print(m1.group(0).strip() if m1 else 'missing')
print('--- twice ---')
print(m2.group(0).strip() if m2 else 'missing')
PY

<sub>⏱️ 162m 52s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Format twice and display nested-list section**

$ python - <<'PY'
import subprocess,re
in_path='tests/cases/plain_continuation_edge_cases/input.md'
input_text=open(in_path).read()
cmd=['cargo','run','-q','--','format']
out1=subprocess.check_output(cmd, input=input_text.encode()).decode()
out2=subprocess.check_output(cmd, input=out1.encode()).decode()
pat=re.compile(r"## Definition Followed by Nested List.*?(?=\n## )",re.S)
for label,txt in [('once',out1),('twice',out2)]:
    m=pat.search(txt)
    print('---',label,'---')
    print((m.group(0) if m else 'missing').strip())
PY

<sub>⏱️ 163m 8s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Parse formatted output and show CST around nested list**

$ python - <<'PY'
import subprocess,re
cmd_parse=['cargo','run','-q','--','parse']
cmd_format=['cargo','run','-q','--','format']
input_text=open('tests/cases/plain_continuation_edge_cases/input.md').read()
out1=subprocess.check_output(cmd_format, input=input_text.encode()).decode()
# parse formatted output
cst=subprocess.check_output(cmd_parse, input=out1.encode()).decode()
# Print around nested list nodes
lines=cst.splitlines()
# locate heading "Definition Followed" span in debug? We'll just find first LIST after that heading text.
idx=None
for i,l in enumerate(lines):
    if 'Definition Followed b' in l:
        idx=i
        break
if idx is None:
    print('heading not found')
    exit()
# find first LIST line after idx
j=None
for i in range(idx, min(idx+400, len(lines))):
    if 'LIST@' in lines[i] or 'LIST_ITEM@' in lines[i]:
        j=i
        break
print('\n'.join(lines[max(0,(j or idx)-20):min(len(lines),(j or idx)+60)]))
PY

import subprocess

        end=i+60

    start=idx

print('\n'.join(lines[start:end]))

320.         _lines: &[&str],

322.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
323.         // Must be at document start.
324.         if !ctx.at_document_start || line_pos != 0 {
325.             return None;
326.         }
327. 
328.         // Must start with % (allow leading spaces).
329.         if !ctx.content.trim_start().starts_with('%') {
330.             return None;
331.         }
332. 
333.         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, None))
334.     }

336.     fn parse(
337.         &self,
338.         ctx: &BlockContext,
339.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,

364. /// YAML metadata block parser (--- ... ---/...)
365. pub(crate) struct YamlMetadataParser;
366. 
367. impl BlockParser for YamlMetadataParser {
368.     fn can_parse(
369.         &self,
370.         ctx: &BlockContext,
371.         lines: &[&str],
372.         line_pos: usize,
373.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
374.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
375.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
376.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
377.     }
378. 
379.     fn detect_prepared(
380.         &self,
381.         ctx: &BlockContext,
382.         lines: &[&str],
383.         line_pos: usize,
384.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
385.         // Must be at top level (not inside blockquotes)
386.         if ctx.blockquote_depth > 0 {
387.             return None;
388.         }
389. 
390.         // Must start with ---
391.         if ctx.content.trim() != "---" {
392.             return None;
393.         }
394. 
395.         // YAML needs blank line before OR be at document start
396.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
397.             return None;
398.         }
399. 
400.         // Look ahead: next line must NOT be blank (to distinguish from horizontal rule)
401.         let next_line = lines.get(line_pos + 1)?;
402.         if next_line.trim().is_empty() {
403.             // This is a horizontal rule, not YAML
404.             return None;
405.         }
406. 
407.         // Cache the `at_document_start` flag for emission (avoids any ambiguity if ctx changes).
408.         Some((
409.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
410.             Some(Box::new(ctx.at_document_start)),
411.         ))
412.     }
413. 
414.     fn parse(
415.         &self,
416.         ctx: &BlockContext,
417.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
418.         lines: &[&str],

420.     ) -> usize {

```
1360. /// Registry of block parsers, ordered by priority.
1361. ///
1362. /// This dispatcher tries each parser in order until one succeeds.
1363. /// The ordering follows Pandoc's approach - explicit list order rather
1364. /// than numeric priorities.
1365. pub(crate) struct BlockParserRegistry {
1366.     parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>,
1367. }
1368. 
1369. impl BlockParserRegistry {
1370.     /// Create a new registry with all block parsers.
1371.     ///
1372.     /// Order matters! Parsers are tried in the order listed here.
1373.     /// This follows Pandoc's design where ordering is explicit and documented.
1374.     ///
1375.     /// **Pandoc reference order** (from pandoc/src/Text/Pandoc/Readers/Markdown.hs:487-515):
1376.     /// 1. blanklines (handled separately in our parser)
1377.     /// 2. codeBlockFenced
1378.     /// 3. yamlMetaBlock' ← YAML metadata comes early!
1379.     /// 4. bulletList
1380.     /// 5. divHtml
1381.     /// 6. divFenced
1382.     /// 7. header ← ATX headings
1383.     /// 8. lhsCodeBlock
1384.     /// 9. htmlBlock
1385.     /// 10. table
1386.     /// 11. codeBlockIndented
1387.     /// 12. rawTeXBlock (LaTeX)
1388.     /// 13. lineBlock
1389.     /// 14. blockQuote
1390.     /// 15. hrule ← Horizontal rules come AFTER headers!
1391.     /// 16. orderedList
1392.     /// 17. definitionList
1393.     /// 18. noteBlock (footnotes)
1394.     /// 19. referenceKey ← Reference definitions
1395.     /// 20. abbrevKey
1396.     /// 21. para
1397.     /// 22. plain
1398.     pub fn new() -> Self {
1399.         let parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>> = vec![
1400.             // Match Pandoc's ordering to ensure correct precedence:
1401.             // (0) Pandoc title block (must be at document start).
1402.             Box::new(PandocTitleBlockParser),
1403.             // (2) Fenced code blocks - can interrupt paragraphs!
1404.             Box::new(FencedCodeBlockParser),
1405.             // (3) YAML metadata - before headers and hrules!
1406.             Box::new(YamlMetadataParser),
1407.             // (6) Fenced divs ::: (open/close)
1408.             Box::new(FencedDivCloseParser),
1409.             Box::new(FencedDivOpenParser),
1410.             // (7) Setext headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
1411.             // Must come before ATX to properly handle `---` disambiguation
1412.             Box::new(SetextHeadingParser),
1413.             // (7) ATX headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
1414.             Box::new(AtxHeadingParser),
1415.             // (9) HTML blocks
1416.             Box::new(HtmlBlockParser),
1417.             // (12) LaTeX environment blocks
1418.             Box::new(LatexEnvironmentParser),
1419.             // (13) Line blocks
1420.             Box::new(LineBlockParser),
1421.             // (11) Indented code blocks (AFTER fenced!)
1422.             Box::new(IndentedCodeBlockParser),
1423.             // (15) Horizontal rules - AFTER headings per Pandoc
1424.             Box::new(HorizontalRuleParser),
1425.             // Figures (standalone images) - Pandoc doesn't have these
1426.             Box::new(FigureParser),
1427.             // (19) Reference definitions
1428.             Box::new(ReferenceDefinitionParser),
1429.             // TODO: Migrate remaining blocks in Pandoc order:
1430.             // - (4-6) Lists and divs (bulletList, divHtml)
1431.             // - (10) Tables (grid, multiline, pipe, simple)
1432.             // - (16) Ordered lists
1433.             // - (17) Definition lists
1434.             // - (18) Footnote definitions (noteBlock)
1435.         ];
1436. 
1437.         Self { parsers }
1438.     }
1439. 
1440.     /// Try to parse a block using the registered parsers.
1441.     ///
1442.     /// This method implements the two-phase parsing:
1443.     /// 1. Detection: Check if any parser can handle this content
1444.     /// 2. Caller prepares (closes paragraphs, flushes buffers)
1445.     /// 3. Parser emits the block
1446.     ///
1447.     /// Returns (parser_index, detection_result) if a parser can handle this,
1448.     /// or None if no parser matched.
1449.     #[allow(dead_code)]
1450.     pub fn detect(
1451.         &self,
1452.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1453.         lines: &[&str],
1454.         line_pos: usize,
1455.     ) -> Option<(usize, BlockDetectionResult)> {
1456.         for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
1457.             let result = parser.can_parse(ctx, lines, line_pos);
1458.             match result {
1459.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes | BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
1460.                     log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
1461.                     return Some((i, result));
1462.                 }
1463.                 BlockDetectionResult::No => continue,
1464.             }
1465.         }
```

</details>
