<sub>⏱️ 166m 8s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’m going to tweak the “keep Definition open across blank line” logic so nested lists remain stable across formatting, while still letting indented code after a blank line terminate the definition (fixing the golden idempotency regression).

+// ============================================================================
+// Indented Code Block Parser (position #11)
+// ============================================================================
+
+pub(crate) struct IndentedCodeBlockParser;
+
+impl BlockParser for IndentedCodeBlockParser {
+    fn can_parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        _lines: &[&str],
+        _line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
+        self.detect_prepared(ctx, _lines, _line_pos)
+            .map(|(d, _)| d)
+            .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
+    }
+
+    fn detect_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        _lines: &[&str],
+        _line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
+        // Indented code blocks require a strict blank line before (or doc start).
+        if !ctx.has_blank_before_strict {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        // Don't treat as code if it's a list marker (list takes precedence).
+        if try_parse_list_marker(ctx.content, ctx.config).is_some() {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        if !is_indented_code_line(ctx.content) {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, None))
+    }
+
+    fn parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> usize {
+        self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
+    }
+
+    fn parse_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+        _payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
+    ) -> usize {
+        let new_pos = parse_indented_code_block(
+            builder,
+            lines,
+            line_pos,
+            ctx.blockquote_depth,
+            ctx.content_indent,
+        );
+        new_pos - line_pos
+    }
+
+    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
+        "indented_code_block"
+    }
+}
+

 // Setext Heading Parser (position #3)
 // ============================================================================

<sub>⏱️ 166m 36s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Fixed the compile break and migrated **indented code blocks** into the dispatcher (with `has_blank_before_strict` plumbed through `BlockContext`).

While doing that, we hit a real semantic edge case: **definition-list blank-line continuation** was incorrectly swallowing indented code (and later caused a formatter idempotency failure); I fixed it by (1) not treating “indented code” as “nested structure” for the blank-line keep-open heuristic, and (2) ensuring definition “plain continuation” doesn’t eat an outdented blank line.

All tests are green again: `cargo test -q` passes.

---

<sub>⏱️ 172m 11s</sub>

### 👤 User

Pandoc has quite nice logic for continuation lines, but it feels like ours is scattered and somewhat ad-hoc. Is that your impression as well?

---

<sub>⏱️ 172m 20s</sub>

I’ll review and update the session plan to reflect indented-code migration + the continuation/idempotency fixes.

<sub>⏱️ 172m 20s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

/home/jola/.local/state/.copilot/session-state/8111c140-3b00-44e8-81bb-2bc5c0b6ac76/plan.md

<details>
<summary>67 lines</summary>

```
1. # Plan: Block dispatcher “prepared match” refactor
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Current `BlockParser::can_parse()` often does non-trivial work (e.g. fenced code parses fence + `InfoString::parse`, setext allocates, figure uses inline image parsing). This work is frequently repeated because:
5. - several parsers call `try_parse_*` again inside `parse()`
6. - `core.rs` calls `block_registry.detect(...)` multiple times per line in different branches
7. - `core.rs` separately re-implements “is this a block element marker?” checks (e.g. Definition continuation guard), further duplicating detection.
8. 
9. ## Goal
10. Keep the good part of the refactor (Pandoc-like ordered registry + ability to “prepare/flush buffers before emitting”), while eliminating duplicate detection/parsing and reducing `core.rs` complexity.
11. 
12. ## Approach (high level)
13. Introduce a **prepared match** concept: detection returns a match object that caches any expensive parsing results, and emission consumes that cached data (no re-parsing). Then rework `core.rs` so each line does **one** detection pass, decides whether the match is allowed (blank-line rules / interrupt rules), performs preparation, and emits.
14. 
15. ## Key design decision (to confirm)
16. Two viable representations:
17. 1) **Trait objects + opaque payload** (recommended for minimal churn): registry remains `Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>`. Detection returns a `BlockMatch` containing a parser index + interrupt/blank requirements + `Box<dyn Any>` payload; emission downcasts payload.
18. 2) **Enum registry + typed payload** (slightly faster/cleaner types, but larger refactor): registry becomes `Vec<BlockParserKind>` and match payload becomes an enum.
19. 
20. ## Workplan
21. 
22. ### A. Baseline & safety
23. - [x] Run baseline tests/benchmarks to capture current behavior (`cargo test` at minimum).
24. - [ ] Identify a small set of “high-signal” golden cases to watch while refactoring (fenced code, setext, yaml/hrule ambiguity, refdefs).
25. 
26. ### B. Introduce prepared-match API (non-invasive)
27. - [x] Add new types in `src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs`:
28.   - `PreparedBlockMatch` (parser index + detection + cached payload)
29. - [x] Extend `BlockParserRegistry` with new entrypoints (keep old API temporarily):
30.   - `detect_prepared(...) -> Option<PreparedBlockMatch>`
31.   - `parse_prepared(match, ...) -> usize`
32. 
33. ### C. Convert migrated parsers (start with biggest perf win)
34. - [x] Fenced code: detection parses fence + `InfoString` once; parse reuses cached `FenceInfo`.
35. - [x] ATX heading: detection returns cached level; parse uses it (no second `try_parse_atx_heading`).
36. - [ ] YAML: optionally cache “is yaml not hrule” disambiguation.
37. - [ ] Figure: cache validation span from inline image parse so it isn’t repeated.
38. - [x] Setext: remove allocation in detection (no `Vec`).
39. - [x] Reference definition: cache parse tuple from `try_parse_reference_definition` (payload currently unused by emitter).
40. 
41. ### D. Refactor `core.rs` to “single detect per line”
42. - [x] Create one `BlockContext` per line and reuse it (avoid repeated rebuilds).
43. - [x] Replace the multiple dispatcher detections with one flow per line (detect once, then prepare/emit).
44. - [x] Rework the Definition continuation guard (`is_block_element` at ~1041) to delegate to the registry for migrated blocks (keeps special-cases for def/list/bq/html/tex).
45. - [x] Delegate nested-block marker probes (Definition continuation after blank line) to the registry instead of hardcoding fence/div/hrule checks.
46. 
47. Notes:
48. - `BlockContext` no longer stores `&ContainerStack` to avoid long-lived borrows blocking mutation during preparation/emission.
49. 
50. ### E. Consolidate ordering/precedence in the registry
51. - [x] Migrate HTML blocks into the registry (interrupting).
52. - [x] Migrate LaTeX environment blocks into the registry (interrupting).
53. - [x] Migrate line blocks into the registry (interrupting).
54. - [x] Decide fenced-div integration approach (needs container-stack interaction), then migrate (Option 1: dispatcher emits + core mutates containers).
55. - [ ] Move remaining “block start” checks from `core.rs` into the registry progressively (tables, indented code, lists, etc.), keeping Pandoc ordering notes.
56. - [ ] Ensure special-case precedence rules stay correct (e.g., YAML vs hrule, setext vs hrule, list vs header edge cases).
57. 
58. ### F. Cleanup
59. - [ ] Remove old `can_parse/parse` API once all migrated blocks use prepared matches.
60. - [ ] Remove redundant detection logic left in `core.rs`.
61. - [ ] Run full test suite + clippy + fmt check.
62. 
63. ## Notes / Constraints
64. - Maintain single-pass, lossless CST emission; prepared matches must not emit to the builder.
65. - Prepared payload should avoid borrowing `ctx.content` directly unless lifetimes are carefully managed; prefer storing indices / owned small strings only when needed.
66. - Performance expectation: dynamic dispatch/downcast overhead is negligible vs string scanning/allocs; eliminating repeated `InfoString::parse` etc. is the main win.
67.
```

</details>


---

<sub>⏱️ 172m 58s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Yes—Pandoc’s continuation handling is much more *centralized and state-driven*, while ours is currently split across at least three places (`parse_line` blank handling + `compute_levels_to_keep` + ad-hoc “definition continuation” in `parse_inner_content`). The indented-code migration made that pretty obvious: we had to touch multiple heuristics (definition plain continuation, blank-line keep-open, and “nested structure” probing) to restore correct behavior + idempotency.

I updated `plan.md` to mark **indented code migrated** and removed it from the remaining “block start” checklist.

<sub>⏱️ 173m 50s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Agreed—doing a **small, behavior-preserving refactor now** is worth it, because it prevents exactly the kind of cross-cutting regressions we just saw with indented code.

Next “obvious win” I’d do:
- Extract a single “continuation policy” helper (e.g. `src/parser/continuation.rs`) that owns:
  - “is this blank line inside container?” decisions (`compute_levels_to_keep`)
  - definition “plain continuation vs new block” decision
  - the “nested structure probe” wrapper (with the right ctx flags)
- Then make `core.rs` call that in exactly one place, instead of duplicating ad-hoc checks.

If you want, I’ll start by introducing the helper with unit tests that pin current behavior (esp. the `plain_continuation_edge_cases` scenario) and then do a mechanical move of logic into it.

````
1. //! Parser module for Pandoc/Quarto documents.
2. //!
3. //! This module implements a single-pass parser that constructs a lossless syntax tree (CST) for
4. //! Quarto documents.
5. 
6. use crate::config::Config;
7. use crate::syntax::SyntaxNode;
8. 
9. pub mod blocks;
10. pub mod inlines;
11. pub mod utils;
12. 
13. mod block_dispatcher;
14. mod core;
15. 
16. // Re-export main parser
17. pub use core::Parser;
18. 
19. /// Parses a Quarto document string into a syntax tree.
20. ///
21. /// Single-pass architecture: blocks emit inline structure during parsing.
22. ///
23. /// # Examples
24. ///
25. /// ```rust
26. /// use panache::parser::parse;
27. ///
28. /// let input = "# Heading\n\nParagraph text.";
29. /// let tree = parse(input, None);
30. /// println!("{:#?}", tree);
31. /// ```
32. ///
33. /// # Arguments
34. ///
35. /// * `input` - The Quarto document content to parse
36. /// * `config` - Optional configuration. If None, uses default config.
37. pub fn parse(input: &str, config: Option<Config>) -> SyntaxNode {
38.     let config = config.unwrap_or_default();
39.     Parser::new(input, &config).parse()
40. }
````

</details>

1028.         // Check if this line looks like a table caption followed by a table
1029.         // If so, try to parse the table (which will include the caption)
1030.         if is_caption_followed_by_table(&self.lines, self.pos) {
1031.             log::debug!("Found caption followed by table at line {}", self.pos);
1032. 
1033.             // Prepare for table
1034.             self.prepare_for_block_element();
1035. 
1036.             let caption_start = self.pos;
1037. 
1038.             // The caption is at self.pos. We need to find where the actual table starts.
1039.             // Skip non-blank lines (caption continuation) and one blank line
1040.             let mut table_pos = self.pos + 1;
1041.             while table_pos < self.lines.len() && !self.lines[table_pos].trim().is_empty() {
1042.                 table_pos += 1;
1043.             }
1044.             // Skip one blank line if present
1045.             if table_pos < self.lines.len() && self.lines[table_pos].trim().is_empty() {
1046.                 table_pos += 1;
1047.             }
1048. 
1049.             // Now table_pos should be at the table start (separator, header, or grid fence)
1050.             // Try to parse the table from this position
1051.             if table_pos < self.lines.len() {
1052.                 if let Some(lines_consumed) =
1053.                     try_parse_grid_table(&self.lines, table_pos, &mut self.builder, self.config)
1054.                 {
1055.                     log::debug!(
1056.                         "Parsed grid table (with caption) starting at line {} ({} lines total from caption)",
1057.                         table_pos,
1058.                         lines_consumed
1059.                     );
1060.                     // lines_consumed is from table_pos, but includes the caption found by find_caption_before_table
1061.                     // So we advance from caption_start by lines_consumed
1062.                     self.pos = caption_start + lines_consumed;
1063.                     return true;
1064.                 }
1065. 
1066.                 if let Some(lines_consumed) = try_parse_multiline_table(
1067.                     &self.lines,
1068.                     table_pos,
1069.                     &mut self.builder,
1070.                     self.config,
1071.                 ) {
1072.                     log::debug!(
1073.                         "Parsed multiline table (with caption) starting at line {} ({} lines total from caption)",
1074.                         table_pos,
1075.                         lines_consumed
1076.                     );
1077.                     self.pos = caption_start + lines_consumed;
1078.                     return true;
1079.                 }
1080. 
1081.                 if let Some(lines_consumed) =
1082.                     try_parse_pipe_table(&self.lines, table_pos, &mut self.builder, self.config)
1083.                 {
1084.                     log::debug!(
1085.                         "Parsed pipe table (with caption) starting at line {} ({} lines total from caption)",
1086.                         table_pos,
1087.                         lines_consumed
1088.                     );
1089.                     self.pos = caption_start + lines_consumed;
1090.                     return true;
1091.                 }
1092. 
1093.                 if let Some(lines_consumed) =
1094.                     try_parse_simple_table(&self.lines, table_pos, &mut self.builder, self.config)
1095.                 {
1096.                     log::debug!(
1097.                         "Parsed simple table (with caption) starting at line {} ({} lines total from caption)",
1098.                         table_pos,
1099.                         lines_consumed
1100.                     );
1101.                     self.pos = caption_start + lines_consumed;
1102.                     return true;
1103.                 }
1104.             }
1105.         }
1106. 
1107.         if has_blank_before {
1108.             // Try to parse grid table (check before pipe/simple since + is most specific)
1109.             if let Some(lines_consumed) =
1110.                 try_parse_grid_table(&self.lines, self.pos, &mut self.builder, self.config)
1111.             {
1112.                 log::debug!(
1113.                     "Parsed grid table at line {} ({} lines)",
1114.                     self.pos,
1115.                     lines_consumed
1116.                 );
1117.                 // Prepare for grid table
1118.                 self.prepare_for_block_element();
1119.                 self.pos += lines_consumed;
1120.                 return true;
1121.             }
1122. 
1123.             // Try to parse multiline table (check before pipe/simple since full-width dashes are specific)
1124.             if let Some(lines_consumed) =
1125.                 try_parse_multiline_table(&self.lines, self.pos, &mut self.builder, self.config)

840.         let content_indent = self.content_container_indent_to_strip();
841.         let (stripped_content, indent_to_emit) = if content_indent > 0 {
842.             let (indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(content);
843.             if indent_cols >= content_indent {
844.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(content, content_indent);
845.                 (&content[idx..], Some(&content[..idx]))
846.             } else {
847.                 // Line has less indent than required - preserve leading whitespace
848.                 let trimmed_start = content.trim_start();
849.                 let ws_len = content.len() - trimmed_start.len();
850.                 if ws_len > 0 {
851.                     (trimmed_start, Some(&content[..ws_len]))
852.                 } else {
853.                     (content, None)
854.                 }
855.             }
856.         } else {
857.             (content, None)
858.         };
859. 
860.         // Check if we're in a Definition container (with or without an open PLAIN)
861.         // Continuation lines should be added to PLAIN, not treated as new blocks
862.         // BUT: Don't treat lines with block element markers as continuations
863.         if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Definition { .. })) {
864.             // A blank line that isn't indented to the definition content column ends the definition.
865.             // (Otherwise indented code blocks after a blank line get swallowed as PLAIN continuation.)
866.             let (indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(content);
867.             if content.trim().is_empty() && indent_cols < content_indent {
868.                 // Fall through to normal blank-line handling.
869.             } else {
870.                 // Check if this line starts with any block element marker
871.                 // Use stripped_content so we check AFTER removing footnote/definition indent
872.                 let is_block_element = try_parse_definition_marker(stripped_content).is_some()
873.                     || try_parse_list_marker(stripped_content, self.config).is_some()
874.                     || count_blockquote_markers(stripped_content).0 > 0
875.                     || (self.config.extensions.raw_html

877.                     || (self.config.extensions.raw_tex
878.                         && try_parse_latex_env_begin(stripped_content).is_some())
879.                     || {
880.                         let next_line = if self.pos + 1 < self.lines.len() {
881.                             Some(self.lines[self.pos + 1])
882.                         } else {
883.                             None
884.                         };
885. 
886.                         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
887.                             content: stripped_content,
888.                             has_blank_before: true,
889.                             has_blank_before_strict: true,
890.                             at_document_start: self.pos == 0
891.                                 && self.current_blockquote_depth() == 0,
892.                             in_fenced_div: self.in_fenced_div(),
893.                             blockquote_depth: self.current_blockquote_depth(),
894.                             config: self.config,
895.                             content_indent,
896.                             list_indent_info: None,
897.                             next_line,
898.                         };
899. 
900.                         self.block_registry
901.                             .detect_prepared(&block_ctx, &self.lines, self.pos)
902.                             .is_some()
903.                     };
904. 
905.                 if is_block_element {
906.                     // Close any open Plain block before processing the block element
907.                     // Buffered PLAIN content will be emitted by emit_buffered_plain_if_needed()
908.                     // Fall through to parse the block element
909.                 } else {
910.                     // This is a continuation line - add to PLAIN (start one if needed)
911.                     let full_line = self.lines[self.pos];
912.                     let (text_without_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(full_line);
913. 
914.                     // Buffer the line for later inline parsing
915.                     if let Some(Container::Definition {
916.                         plain_open,
917.                         plain_buffer,
918.                         ..
919.                     }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
920.                     {
921.                         // Include the newline in the buffered text for losslessness
922.                         let line_with_newline = if !newline_str.is_empty() {
923.                             format!("{}{}", text_without_newline, newline_str)
924.                         } else {
925.                             text_without_newline.to_string()

1. //! Continuation/blank-line handling policy.
2. //!
3. //! This module centralizes the parser's "should this line continue an existing container?"
4. //! logic (especially across blank lines). Keeping this logic in one place reduces the
5. //! risk of scattered ad-hoc heuristics diverging as blocks move into the dispatcher.
6. 
7. use crate::config::Config;
8. 
9. use crate::parser::block_dispatcher::{BlockContext, BlockParserRegistry};
10. use crate::parser::blocks::{definition_lists, html_blocks, latex_envs, lists};
11. use crate::parser::blocks::blockquotes::{count_blockquote_markers, strip_n_blockquote_markers};
12. use crate::parser::core::Parser;
13. use crate::parser::utils::container_stack::{byte_index_at_column, leading_indent, ContainerStack, Container};
14. 
15. pub(crate) struct ContinuationPolicy<'a, 'cfg> {
16.     config: &'cfg Config,
17.     block_registry: &'a BlockParserRegistry,
18. }
19. 
20. impl<'a, 'cfg> ContinuationPolicy<'a, 'cfg> {
21.     pub(crate) fn new(config: &'cfg Config, block_registry: &'a BlockParserRegistry) -> Self {
22.         Self {
23.             config,
24.             block_registry,
25.         }
26.     }
27. 
28.     /// Registry-based "does this look like the start of some nested block structure" probe.
29.     ///
30.     /// Important: this is intended for *blank-line keep-open decisions*, so it uses
31.     /// `has_blank_before_strict = false` to avoid treating indented code blocks as nested.
32.     pub(crate) fn has_nested_block_structure(&self, parser: &Parser<'cfg>, content: &str) -> bool {
33.         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
34.             content,
35.             has_blank_before: true,
36.             // For blank-line container-keep decisions we do NOT want indented code blocks
37.             // to count as “nested structure” (that would keep definitions open incorrectly).
38.             has_blank_before_strict: false,
39.             at_document_start: false,
40.             in_fenced_div: parser.in_fenced_div(),
41.             blockquote_depth: parser.current_blockquote_depth(),
42.             config: self.config,
43.             content_indent: 0,
44.             list_indent_info: None,
45.             next_line: None,
46.         };
47. 
48.         // Note: we only use the registry for its “is this a block start?” decision;
49.         // it should not rely on `lines` here (setext is gated by `has_blank_before`).
50.         self.block_registry
51.             .detect_prepared(&block_ctx, &parser.lines, parser.pos)
52.             .is_some()
53.     }
54. 
55.     pub(crate) fn compute_levels_to_keep(
56.         &self,
57.         parser: &Parser<'cfg>,
58.         containers: &ContainerStack,
59.         next_line: &str,
60.     ) -> usize {
61.         let (next_bq_depth, next_inner) = count_blockquote_markers(next_line);
62.         let (raw_indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(next_inner);
63.         let next_marker = lists::try_parse_list_marker(next_inner, self.config);
64. 
65.         // Calculate current blockquote depth for proper indent calculation
66.         let current_bq_depth = parser.current_blockquote_depth();
67. 
68.         let mut keep_level = 0;
69.         let mut content_indent_so_far = 0usize;
70. 
71.         // First, account for blockquotes
72.         for (i, c) in containers.stack.iter().enumerate() {
73.             match c {
74.                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::BlockQuote { .. } => {
75.                     let bq_count = containers.stack[..=i]
76.                         .iter()
77.                         .filter(|x| {
78.                             matches!(
79.                                 x,
80.                                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::BlockQuote { .. }
81.                             )
82.                         })
83.                         .count();
84.                     if bq_count <= next_bq_depth {
85.                         keep_level = i + 1;
86.                     }
87.                 }
88.                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::FootnoteDefinition {
89.                     content_col,
90.                     ..
91.                 } => {
92.                     content_indent_so_far += *content_col;
93.                     let min_indent = (*content_col).max(4);
94.                     if raw_indent_cols >= min_indent {
95.                         keep_level = i + 1;
96.                     }
97.                 }
98.                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::Definition { content_col, .. } => {
99.                     let min_indent = (*content_col).max(4);
100.                     if raw_indent_cols >= min_indent {
101.                         let after_content_indent = if raw_indent_cols >= content_indent_so_far {
102.                             let idx = byte_index_at_column(next_line, content_indent_so_far);
103.                             &next_line[idx..]
104.                         } else {
105.                             next_line
106.                         };
107. 
108.                         let has_definition_marker =
109.                             definition_lists::try_parse_definition_marker(after_content_indent)
110.                                 .is_some();
111.                         let has_list_marker =
112.                             lists::try_parse_list_marker(after_content_indent, self.config).is_some();
113.                         let has_block_structure = has_list_marker
114.                             || count_blockquote_markers(after_content_indent).0 > 0
115.                             || self.has_nested_block_structure(parser, after_content_indent);
116. 
117.                         if !has_definition_marker && has_block_structure {
118.                             keep_level = i + 1;
119.                         }
120.                     }
121.                 }
122.                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::List {
123.                     marker,
124.                     base_indent_cols,
125.                     ..
126.                 } => {
127.                     let effective_indent = raw_indent_cols.saturating_sub(content_indent_so_far);
128.                     let continues_list = if let Some((ref nm, _, _)) = next_marker {
129.                         lists::markers_match(marker, nm) && effective_indent <= base_indent_cols + 3
130.                     } else {
131.                         let item_content_col = containers
132.                             .stack
133.                             .get(i + 1)
134.                             .and_then(|c| match c {
135.                                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::ListItem {
136.                                     content_col,
137.                                     ..
138.                                 } => Some(*content_col),
139.                                 _ => None,
140.                             })
141.                             .unwrap_or(1);
142.                         effective_indent >= item_content_col
143.                     };
144.                     if continues_list {
145.                         keep_level = i + 1;
146.                     }
147.                 }
148.                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::ListItem { content_col, .. } => {
149.                     let effective_indent = if next_bq_depth > current_bq_depth {
150.                         let after_current_bq =
151.                             strip_n_blockquote_markers(next_line, current_bq_depth);
152.                         let (spaces_before_next_marker, _) = leading_indent(after_current_bq);
153.                         spaces_before_next_marker.saturating_sub(content_indent_so_far)
154.                     } else {
155.                         raw_indent_cols.saturating_sub(content_indent_so_far)
156.                     };
157. 
158.                     let is_new_item_at_outer_level = if next_marker.is_some() {
159.                         effective_indent < *content_col
160.                     } else {
161.                         false
162.                     };
163. 
164.                     if !is_new_item_at_outer_level && effective_indent >= *content_col {
165.                         keep_level = i + 1;
166.                     }
167.                 }
168.                 _ => {}
169.             }
170.         }
171. 
172.         keep_level
173.     }
174. 
175.     /// Checks whether a line inside a definition should be treated as a plain continuation
176.     /// (and buffered into the definition PLAIN), rather than parsed as a new block.
177.     pub(crate) fn definition_plain_can_continue(
178.         &self,
179.         parser: &Parser<'cfg>,
180.         stripped_content: &str,
181.         raw_content: &str,
182.         content_indent: usize,
183.     ) -> bool {
184.         debug_assert!(matches!(parser.containers.last(), Some(Container::Definition { .. })));
185.         // A blank line that isn't indented to the definition content column ends the definition.
186.         let (indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(raw_content);
187.         if raw_content.trim().is_empty() && indent_cols < content_indent {
188.             return false;
189.         }
190. 
191.         // If it's a block element marker, don't continue as plain.
192.         if definition_lists::try_parse_definition_marker(stripped_content).is_some() {
193.             return false;
194.         }
195.         if lists::try_parse_list_marker(stripped_content, self.config).is_some() {
196.             return false;
197.         }
198.         if count_blockquote_markers(stripped_content).0 > 0 {
199.             return false;
200.         }
201.         if self.config.extensions.raw_html
202.             && html_blocks::try_parse_html_block_start(stripped_content).is_some()
203.         {
204.             return false;
205.         }
206.         if self.config.extensions.raw_tex
207.             && latex_envs::try_parse_latex_env_begin(stripped_content).is_some()
208.         {
209.             return false;
210.         }
211. 
212.         let next_line = if parser.pos + 1 < parser.lines.len() {
213.             Some(parser.lines[parser.pos + 1])
214.         } else {
215.             None
216.         };
217. 
218.         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
219.             content: stripped_content,
220.             has_blank_before: true,
221.             has_blank_before_strict: true,
222.             at_document_start: parser.pos == 0 && parser.current_blockquote_depth() == 0,
223.             in_fenced_div: parser.in_fenced_div(),
224.             blockquote_depth: parser.current_blockquote_depth(),
225.             config: self.config,
226.             content_indent,
227.             list_indent_info: None,
228.             next_line,
229.         };
230. 
231.         !self
232.             .block_registry

1. use crate::config::Config;
2. use crate::syntax::{SyntaxKind, SyntaxNode};
3. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
4. 
5. use super::block_dispatcher::{
6.     BlockContext, BlockDetectionResult, BlockEffect, BlockParserRegistry,
7. };
8. use super::blocks::blockquotes;
9. use super::blocks::definition_lists;
10. use super::blocks::html_blocks;
11. use super::blocks::latex_envs;
12. use super::blocks::line_blocks;
13. use super::blocks::lists;
14. use super::blocks::paragraphs;
15. use super::blocks::reference_links;
16. use super::blocks::tables;
17. use super::utils::container_stack;
18. use super::utils::helpers::{split_lines_inclusive, strip_newline};
19. use super::utils::inline_emission;
20. use super::utils::marker_utils;
21. use super::utils::text_buffer;
22. 
23. use container_stack::{Container, ContainerStack, byte_index_at_column, leading_indent};
24. use super::utils::continuation::ContinuationPolicy;
25. use definition_lists::{emit_definition_marker, emit_term, try_parse_definition_marker};
26. use html_blocks::try_parse_html_block_start;
27. use latex_envs::try_parse_latex_env_begin;
28. use line_blocks::{parse_line_block, try_parse_line_block_start};
29. use lists::{is_content_nested_bullet_marker, markers_match, try_parse_list_marker};
30. use marker_utils::{count_blockquote_markers, parse_blockquote_marker_info};
31. use reference_links::try_parse_footnote_marker;
32. use tables::{
33.     is_caption_followed_by_table, try_parse_grid_table, try_parse_multiline_table,
34.     try_parse_pipe_table, try_parse_simple_table,
35. };
36. use text_buffer::TextBuffer;
37. 
38. fn init_logger() {
39.     let _ = env_logger::builder().is_test(true).try_init();
40. }
41. 
42. pub struct Parser<'a> {
43.     lines: Vec<&'a str>,
44.     pos: usize,
45.     builder: GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
46.     containers: ContainerStack,
47.     config: &'a Config,
48.     block_registry: BlockParserRegistry,
49. }
50. 
51. impl<'a> Parser<'a> {
52.     pub fn new(input: &'a str, config: &'a Config) -> Self {
53.         // Use split_lines_inclusive to preserve line endings (both LF and CRLF)
54.         let lines = split_lines_inclusive(input);
55.         Self {
56.             lines,
57.             pos: 0,
58.             builder: GreenNodeBuilder::new(),
59.             containers: ContainerStack::new(),
60.             config,
61.             block_registry: BlockParserRegistry::new(),
62.         }
63.     }
64. 
65.     pub fn parse(mut self) -> SyntaxNode {
66.         #[cfg(debug_assertions)]
67.         {
68.             init_logger();
69.         }
70. 
71.         self.parse_document_stack();
72. 
73.         SyntaxNode::new_root(self.builder.finish())
74.     }
75. 
76.     /// Emit buffered PLAIN content if Definition container has open PLAIN.
77.     /// Close containers down to `keep`, emitting buffered content first.
78.     fn close_containers_to(&mut self, keep: usize) {
79.         // Emit buffered PARAGRAPH/PLAIN content before closing
80.         while self.containers.depth() > keep {
81.             match self.containers.stack.last() {
82.                 // Handle ListItem with buffering
83.                 Some(Container::ListItem { buffer, .. }) if !buffer.is_empty() => {
84.                     // Clone buffer to avoid borrow issues
85.                     let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
86. 
87.                     log::debug!(
88.                         "Closing ListItem with buffer (is_empty={}, segment_count={})",
89.                         buffer_clone.is_empty(),
90.                         buffer_clone.segment_count()
91.                     );
92. 
93.                     // Determine if this should be Plain or PARAGRAPH:
94.                     // 1. Check if parent LIST has blank lines between items (list-level loose)
95.                     // 2. OR check if this item has blank lines within its content (item-level loose)
96.                     let parent_list_is_loose = self
97.                         .containers
98.                         .stack
99.                         .iter()
100.                         .rev()
101.                         .find_map(|c| match c {
102.                             Container::List {
103.                                 has_blank_between_items,
104.                                 ..
105.                             } => Some(*has_blank_between_items),
106.                             _ => None,
107.                         })
108.                         .unwrap_or(false);
109. 
110.                     let use_paragraph =
111.                         parent_list_is_loose || buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content();
112. 
113.                     log::debug!(
114.                         "Emitting ListItem buffer: use_paragraph={} (parent_list_is_loose={}, item_has_blanks={})",
115.                         use_paragraph,
116.                         parent_list_is_loose,
117.                         buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content()
118.                     );
119. 
120.                     // Pop container first
121.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
122.                     // Emit buffered content as Plain or PARAGRAPH
123.                     buffer_clone.emit_as_block(&mut self.builder, use_paragraph, self.config);
124.                     self.builder.finish_node(); // Close LIST_ITEM
125.                 }
126.                 // Handle ListItem without content
127.                 Some(Container::ListItem { .. }) => {
128.                     log::debug!("Closing empty ListItem (no buffer content)");
129.                     // Just close normally (empty list item)
130.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
131.                     self.builder.finish_node();
132.                 }
133.                 // Handle Paragraph with buffering
134.                 Some(Container::Paragraph { buffer }) if !buffer.is_empty() => {
135.                     // Clone buffer to avoid borrow issues
136.                     let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
137.                     // Pop container first
138.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
139.                     // Emit buffered content with inline parsing (handles markers)
140.                     buffer_clone.emit_with_inlines(&mut self.builder, self.config);
```

</details>

173. 
174.     /// Checks whether a line inside a definition should be treated as a plain continuation
175.     /// (and buffered into the definition PLAIN), rather than parsed as a new block.
176.     pub(crate) fn definition_plain_can_continue(
177.         &self,
178.         stripped_content: &str,
179.         raw_content: &str,
180.         content_indent: usize,
181.         block_ctx: &BlockContext,
182.         lines: &[&str],
183.         pos: usize,
184.     ) -> bool {
185.         // A blank line that isn't indented to the definition content column ends the definition.
186.         let (indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(raw_content);
187.         if raw_content.trim().is_empty() && indent_cols < content_indent {
188.             return false;
189.         }
190. 
191.         // If it's a block element marker, don't continue as plain.
192.         if definition_lists::try_parse_definition_marker(stripped_content).is_some() {
193.             return false;
194.         }
195.         if lists::try_parse_list_marker(stripped_content, self.config).is_some() {
196.             return false;
197.         }
198.         if count_blockquote_markers(stripped_content).0 > 0 {
199.             return false;
200.         }
201.         if self.config.extensions.raw_html
202.             && html_blocks::try_parse_html_block_start(stripped_content).is_some()
203.         {
204.             return false;
205.         }
206.         if self.config.extensions.raw_tex
207.             && latex_envs::try_parse_latex_env_begin(stripped_content).is_some()
208.         {
209.             return false;
210.         }
211. 
212.         let next_line = if parser.pos + 1 < parser.lines.len() {
213.             Some(parser.lines[parser.pos + 1])
214.         } else {
215.             None
216.         };
217. 
218.         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
219.             content: stripped_content,
220.             has_blank_before: true,
221.             has_blank_before_strict: true,
222.             at_document_start: parser.pos == 0 && parser.current_blockquote_depth() == 0,
223.             in_fenced_div: parser.in_fenced_div(),
224.             blockquote_depth: parser.current_blockquote_depth(),
225.             config: self.config,
226.             content_indent,
227.             list_indent_info: None,
228.             next_line,
229.         };
230. 
231.         !self
232.             .block_registry
233.             .detect_prepared(&block_ctx, &parser.lines, parser.pos)
234.             .is_some()
235.     }
236. }
```

</details>

365.         if is_blank {

367.             self.close_paragraph_if_open();
368. 
369.             // Close Plain node in Definition if open
370.             // Blank lines should close Plain, allowing subsequent content to be siblings
371.             // Emit buffered PLAIN content before continuing
372.             self.emit_buffered_plain_if_needed();
373. 
374.             // Note: Blank lines between terms and definitions are now preserved
375.             // and emitted as part of the term parsing logic
376. 
377.             // For blank lines inside blockquotes, we need to handle them at the right depth
378.             // First, adjust blockquote depth if needed
379.             if bq_depth > current_bq_depth {

381.                 for _ in current_bq_depth..bq_depth {

384.                 }
385.             } else if bq_depth < current_bq_depth {
386.                 // Close blockquotes down to bq_depth (must use Parser close to emit buffers)
387.                 self.close_blockquotes_to_depth(bq_depth);
388.             }
389. 
390.             // Peek ahead to determine what containers to keep open
391.             let mut peek = self.pos + 1;
392.             while peek < self.lines.len() && self.lines[peek].trim().is_empty() {
393.                 peek += 1;
394.             }
395. 
396.             // Determine what containers to keep open based on next line
397.             let levels_to_keep = if peek < self.lines.len() {
398.                 ContinuationPolicy::new(self.config, &self.block_registry)
399.                     .compute_levels_to_keep(self, &self.containers, self.lines[peek])
400.             } else {
401.                 0
402.             };
403.             log::trace!(
404.                 "Blank line: depth={}, levels_to_keep={}, next='{}'",
405.                 self.containers.depth(),
406.                 levels_to_keep,
407.                 if peek < self.lines.len() {
408.                     self.lines[peek]
409.                 } else {
410.                     "<EOF>"
411.                 }
412.             );
413. 
414.             // Check if blank line should be buffered in a ListItem BEFORE closing containers
415. 
416.             // Close containers down to the level we want to keep
417.             while self.containers.depth() > levels_to_keep {
418.                 match self.containers.last() {
419.                     Some(Container::ListItem { .. }) => {
420.                         // levels_to_keep wants to close the ListItem - blank line is between items

830.     ///                    If None, uses self.lines[self.pos]
831.     fn parse_inner_content(&mut self, content: &str, line_to_append: Option<&str>) -> bool {
832.         log::debug!(
833.             "parse_inner_content [{}]: depth={}, last={:?}, content={:?}",
834.             self.pos,
835.             self.containers.depth(),
836.             self.containers.last(),
837.             content.trim_end()
838.         );
839.         // Calculate how much indentation should be stripped for content containers
840.         // (definitions, footnotes) FIRST, so we can check for block markers correctly
841.         let content_indent = self.content_container_indent_to_strip();
842.         let (stripped_content, indent_to_emit) = if content_indent > 0 {
843.             let (indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(content);
844.             if indent_cols >= content_indent {
845.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(content, content_indent);
846.                 (&content[idx..], Some(&content[..idx]))
847.             } else {
848.                 // Line has less indent than required - preserve leading whitespace
849.                 let trimmed_start = content.trim_start();
850.                 let ws_len = content.len() - trimmed_start.len();
851.                 if ws_len > 0 {
852.                     (trimmed_start, Some(&content[..ws_len]))
853.                 } else {
854.                     (content, None)
855.                 }
856.             }
857.         } else {
858.             (content, None)
859.         };
860. 
861.         // Check if we're in a Definition container (with or without an open PLAIN)
862.         // Continuation lines should be added to PLAIN, not treated as new blocks
863.         // BUT: Don't treat lines with block element markers as continuations
864.         if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Definition { .. })) {
865.             let policy = ContinuationPolicy::new(self.config, &self.block_registry);
866. 
867.             if policy.definition_plain_can_continue(
868.                 stripped_content,
869.                 content,
870.                 content_indent,
871.                 &block_ctx,
872.                 &self.lines,
873.                 self.pos,
874.             ) {
875.                 let full_line = self.lines[self.pos];
876.                 let (text_without_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(full_line);
877. 
878.                 if let Some(Container::Definition {
879.                     plain_open,
880.                     plain_buffer,
881.                     ..
882.                 }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
883.                 {
884.                     let line_with_newline = if !newline_str.is_empty() {
885.                         format!("{}{}", text_without_newline, newline_str)
886.                     } else {
887.                         text_without_newline.to_string()
888.                     };
889.                     plain_buffer.push_line(line_with_newline);
890.                     *plain_open = true;
891.                 }
892. 
893.                 self.pos += 1;
894.                 return true;
895.             }
896.         }
897. 
898.         // Store the stripped content for later use
899.         let content = stripped_content;
900.

<sub>⏱️ 179m 4s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

/home/jola/.local/state/.copilot/session-state/8111c140-3b00-44e8-81bb-2bc5c0b6ac76/plan.md (lines 1-120)

<details>
<summary>68 lines</summary>

```
1. # Plan: Block dispatcher “prepared match” refactor
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Current `BlockParser::can_parse()` often does non-trivial work (e.g. fenced code parses fence + `InfoString::parse`, setext allocates, figure uses inline image parsing). This work is frequently repeated because:
5. - several parsers call `try_parse_*` again inside `parse()`
6. - `core.rs` calls `block_registry.detect(...)` multiple times per line in different branches
7. - `core.rs` separately re-implements “is this a block element marker?” checks (e.g. Definition continuation guard), further duplicating detection.
8. 
9. ## Goal
10. Keep the good part of the refactor (Pandoc-like ordered registry + ability to “prepare/flush buffers before emitting”), while eliminating duplicate detection/parsing and reducing `core.rs` complexity.
11. 
12. ## Approach (high level)
13. Introduce a **prepared match** concept: detection returns a match object that caches any expensive parsing results, and emission consumes that cached data (no re-parsing). Then rework `core.rs` so each line does **one** detection pass, decides whether the match is allowed (blank-line rules / interrupt rules), performs preparation, and emits.
14. 
15. ## Key design decision (to confirm)
16. Two viable representations:
17. 1) **Trait objects + opaque payload** (recommended for minimal churn): registry remains `Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>`. Detection returns a `BlockMatch` containing a parser index + interrupt/blank requirements + `Box<dyn Any>` payload; emission downcasts payload.
18. 2) **Enum registry + typed payload** (slightly faster/cleaner types, but larger refactor): registry becomes `Vec<BlockParserKind>` and match payload becomes an enum.
19. 
20. ## Workplan
21. 
22. ### A. Baseline & safety
23. - [x] Run baseline tests/benchmarks to capture current behavior (`cargo test` at minimum).
24. - [ ] Identify a small set of “high-signal” golden cases to watch while refactoring (fenced code, setext, yaml/hrule ambiguity, refdefs).
25. 
26. ### B. Introduce prepared-match API (non-invasive)
27. - [x] Add new types in `src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs`:
28.   - `PreparedBlockMatch` (parser index + detection + cached payload)
29. - [x] Extend `BlockParserRegistry` with new entrypoints (keep old API temporarily):
30.   - `detect_prepared(...) -> Option<PreparedBlockMatch>`
31.   - `parse_prepared(match, ...) -> usize`
32. 
33. ### C. Convert migrated parsers (start with biggest perf win)
34. - [x] Fenced code: detection parses fence + `InfoString` once; parse reuses cached `FenceInfo`.
35. - [x] ATX heading: detection returns cached level; parse uses it (no second `try_parse_atx_heading`).
36. - [ ] YAML: optionally cache “is yaml not hrule” disambiguation.
37. - [ ] Figure: cache validation span from inline image parse so it isn’t repeated.
38. - [x] Setext: remove allocation in detection (no `Vec`).
39. - [x] Reference definition: cache parse tuple from `try_parse_reference_definition` (payload currently unused by emitter).
40. - [x] Indented code: migrated into dispatcher (uses `has_blank_before_strict`).
41. 
42. ### D. Refactor `core.rs` to “single detect per line”
43. - [x] Create one `BlockContext` per line and reuse it (avoid repeated rebuilds).
44. - [x] Replace the multiple dispatcher detections with one flow per line (detect once, then prepare/emit).
45. - [x] Rework the Definition continuation guard (`is_block_element` at ~1041) to delegate to the registry for migrated blocks (keeps special-cases for def/list/bq/html/tex).
46. - [x] Delegate nested-block marker probes (Definition continuation after blank line) to the registry instead of hardcoding fence/div/hrule checks.
47. - [x] Fix scattered continuation heuristics surfaced by indented-code migration (definition continuation + blank-line keep-open).
48. 
49. Notes:
50. - `BlockContext` no longer stores `&ContainerStack` to avoid long-lived borrows blocking mutation during preparation/emission.
51. 
52. ### E. Consolidate ordering/precedence in the registry
53. - [x] Migrate HTML blocks into the registry (interrupting).
54. - [x] Migrate LaTeX environment blocks into the registry (interrupting).
55. - [x] Migrate line blocks into the registry (interrupting).
56. - [x] Decide fenced-div integration approach (needs container-stack interaction), then migrate (Option 1: dispatcher emits + core mutates containers).
57. - [ ] Move remaining “block start” checks from `core.rs` into the registry progressively (tables, lists, etc.), keeping Pandoc ordering notes.
58. - [ ] Ensure special-case precedence rules stay correct (e.g., YAML vs hrule, setext vs hrule, list vs header edge cases).
59. 
60. ### F. Cleanup
61. - [ ] Remove old `can_parse/parse` API once all migrated blocks use prepared matches.
62. - [ ] Remove redundant detection logic left in `core.rs`.
63. - [ ] Run full test suite + clippy + fmt check.
64. 
65. ## Notes / Constraints
66. - Maintain single-pass, lossless CST emission; prepared matches must not emit to the builder.
67. - Prepared payload should avoid borrowing `ctx.content` directly unless lifetimes are carefully managed; prefer storing indices / owned small strings only when needed.
68. - Performance expectation: dynamic dispatch/downcast overhead is negligible vs string scanning/allocs; eliminating repeated `InfoString::parse` etc. is the main win.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 179m 8s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I finished wiring the Definition “plain continuation vs new block” decision through the centralized `ContinuationPolicy` (and removed the duplicated ad-hoc detection logic from `core.rs` for that case).

Changes:
- `src/parser/core.rs`: Definition continuation now calls `ContinuationPolicy::definition_plain_can_continue(...)`.
- `src/parser/utils/continuation.rs`: `ContinuationPolicy` no longer pokes private `Parser` fields/methods; it takes the needed `BlockContext/lines/pos` explicitly.
- Also cleaned up now-unused imports in `core.rs`.

Validation:
- `cargo test -q` passes (all tests green).

1094.                 }

1106.             } else {
1107.                 (false, "")
1108.             }

1110. 
1111.         if trailing_space {
1112.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
1113.         }
1114.         if !trailing_colons.is_empty() {
1115.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), trailing_colons);
1116.         }
1117.

1159.                 match block_match.effect {
1160.                     BlockEffect::None => {}
1161.                     BlockEffect::OpenFencedDiv => {
1162.                         self.containers.push(Container::FencedDiv {});
1163.                     }
1164.                     BlockEffect::CloseFencedDiv => {
1165.                         self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth().saturating_sub(1));
1166.                     }
1167.                 }
1168. 
1169.                 self.pos += lines_consumed;
1170.                 return true;
1171.             }
1172.         } else if let Some(block_match) = dispatcher_match.as_ref() {
1173.             // Without blank-before, only allow interrupting blocks OR blocks that are
1174.             // explicitly allowed without blank lines (e.g. reference definitions).
1175.             match block_match.detection {
1176.                 BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
1177.                     self.emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed();
1178.                     if self.is_paragraph_open() {
1179.                         self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1180.                     }
1181.                 }
1182.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes => {
1183.                     // E.g. reference definitions: no preparation (matches previous behavior).
1184.                 }
1185.                 BlockDetectionResult::No => unreachable!(),
1186.             }
1187. 
1188.             if !matches!(block_match.detection, BlockDetectionResult::No) {
1189.                 let lines_consumed = self.block_registry.parse_prepared(
1190.                     block_match,
1191.                     &dispatcher_ctx,
1192.                     &mut self.builder,
1193.                     &self.lines,
1194.                     self.pos,
1195.                 );
1196. 
1197.                 match block_match.effect {
1198.                     BlockEffect::None => {}
1199.                     BlockEffect::OpenFencedDiv => {
1200.                         self.containers.push(Container::FencedDiv {});
1201.                     }
1202.                     BlockEffect::CloseFencedDiv => {
1203.                         self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth().saturating_sub(1));
1204.                     }
1205.                 }
1206. 
1207.                 self.pos += lines_consumed;
1208.                 return true;
1209.             }
1210.         }
1211. 
1212.         // Check for footnote definition: [^id]: content
1213.         // Similar to list items - marker followed by content that can span multiple lines
1214.         // Must check BEFORE reference definitions since both start with [
1215.         if let Some((id, content_start)) = try_parse_footnote_marker(content) {
1216.             // Footnotes have precedence over dispatcher-based reference definitions.
1217.             // (Dispatcher match is evaluated above for performance; we handle footnotes here.)
1218.             log::debug!("Parsed footnote definition at line {}: [^{}]", self.pos, id);
1219. 
1220.             // Close paragraph if one is open
1221.             self.close_paragraph_if_open();
1222. 
1223.             // Close previous footnote if one is open
1224.             while matches!(
1225.                 self.containers.last(),
1226.                 Some(Container::FootnoteDefinition { .. })
1227.             ) {
1228.                 self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
1229.             }
1230. 
1231.             // Start the footnote definition container
1232.             self.builder
1233.                 .start_node(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION.into());
1234. 
1235.             // Emit the marker
```

</details>

```
1. //! Fenced div parsing utilities.
2. 
3. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::strip_leading_spaces;
4. 
5. /// Information about a detected div fence opening.
6. #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
7. pub(crate) struct DivFenceInfo {
8.     pub attributes: String,
9.     pub fence_count: usize,
10. }
11. 
12. /// Try to detect a fenced div opening from content.
13. /// Returns div fence info if this is a valid opening fence.
14. ///
15. /// Opening fences MUST have attributes (or the fences are treated as closing).
16. /// Format: `::: {.class #id}` or `::: classname` or `::::: {#id} :::::`
17. pub(crate) fn try_parse_div_fence_open(content: &str) -> Option<DivFenceInfo> {
18.     let trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(content);
19. 
20.     // Check for fence opening (:::)
21.     if !trimmed.starts_with(':') {
22.         return None;
23.     }
24. 
25.     let colon_count = trimmed.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ':').count();
26. 
27.     if colon_count < 3 {
28.         return None;
29.     }
30. 
31.     // Get the part after the colons
32.     let after_colons = trimmed[colon_count..].trim_start();
33. 
34.     // Check if there are attributes
35.     // Attributes can be:
36.     // 1. Curly braces: {.class #id key="value"}
37.     // 2. Single word (treated as class): classname
38.     // 3. Attributes followed by more colons (optional): {.class} :::
39. 
40.     let attributes = if after_colons.starts_with('{') {
41.         // Find the closing brace
42.         if let Some(close_idx) = after_colons.find('}') {
43.             after_colons[..=close_idx].to_string()
44.         } else {
45.             // Unclosed brace, not valid
46.             return None;
47.         }
48.     } else if after_colons.is_empty() {
49.         // No attributes, this is a closing fence
50.         return None;
51.     } else {
52.         // Single word or words until optional trailing colons
53.         let content_before_colons = after_colons.trim_end_matches(':').trim_end();
54. 
55.         if content_before_colons.is_empty() {
56.             // Only colons, no attributes
57.             return None;
58.         }
59. 
60.         // Take the first word as the class name
61.         content_before_colons.split_whitespace().next()?.to_string()
62.     };
63. 
64.     Some(DivFenceInfo {
65.         attributes,
66.         fence_count: colon_count,
67.     })
68. }
69. 
70. /// Check if a line is a valid closing fence for a div.
71. /// Closing fences have NO attributes and at least 3 colons.
72. pub(crate) fn is_div_closing_fence(content: &str) -> bool {
73.     let trimmed = strip_leading_spaces(content);
74. 
75.     if !trimmed.starts_with(':') {
76.         return false;
77.     }
78. 
79.     let colon_count = trimmed.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ':').count();
80. 
81.     if colon_count < 3 {
82.         return false;
83.     }
84. 
85.     // Rest of line must be empty (only colons are allowed)
86.     trimmed[colon_count..].trim().is_empty()
87. }
88. 
89. #[cfg(test)]
90. mod tests {
91.     use super::*;
92. 
93.     #[test]
94.     fn test_parse_div_fence_open_with_curly_braces() {
95.         let line = "::: {.callout-note}";
96.         let fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(line).unwrap();
97.         assert_eq!(fence.attributes, "{.callout-note}");
98.     }
99. 
100.     #[test]
101.     fn test_parse_div_fence_open_with_class_name() {
102.         let line = "::: Warning";
103.         let fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(line).unwrap();
104.         assert_eq!(fence.attributes, "Warning");
105.     }
106. 
107.     #[test]
108.     fn test_parse_div_fence_open_with_trailing_colons() {
109.         let line = "::::: {#special .sidebar} :::::";
110.         let fence = try_parse_div_fence_open(line).unwrap();
111.         assert_eq!(fence.attributes, "{#special .sidebar}");
112.     }
113. 
114.     #[test]
115.     fn test_closing_fence_no_attributes() {
116.         let line = ":::";
117.         assert!(try_parse_div_fence_open(line).is_none());
118.         assert!(is_div_closing_fence(line));
119.     }
120. 
121.     #[test]
122.     fn test_closing_fence_many_colons() {
123.         let line = "::::::::::::::";
124.         assert!(try_parse_div_fence_open(line).is_none());
125.         assert!(is_div_closing_fence(line));
126.     }
127. 
128.     #[test]
129.     fn test_not_a_fence_too_few_colons() {
130.         let line = ":: something";
131.         assert!(try_parse_div_fence_open(line).is_none());
132.         assert!(!is_div_closing_fence(line));
133.     }
134. }
```

</details>

1. use super::list_item_buffer::ListItemBuffer;
2. use super::text_buffer::{ParagraphBuffer, TextBuffer};
3. use crate::parser::blocks::lists::ListMarker;
4. 
5. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
6. pub(crate) enum Container {
7.     BlockQuote {
8.         // No special tracking needed
9.     },
10.     FencedDiv {
11.         // No special tracking needed - closed by fence marker
12.     },
13.     List {
14.         marker: ListMarker,
15.         base_indent_cols: usize,
16.         has_blank_between_items: bool, // Track if list is loose (blank lines between items)
17.     },
18.     ListItem {
19.         content_col: usize,
20.         buffer: ListItemBuffer, // Buffer for list item content
21.     },
22.     DefinitionList {
23.         // Definition lists don't need special tracking
24.     },
25.     DefinitionItem {
26.         // No special tracking needed
27.     },
28.     Definition {
29.         content_col: usize,
30.         plain_open: bool,
31.         #[allow(dead_code)] // Will be used in Subtask 4
32.         plain_buffer: TextBuffer, // Buffer for accumulating PLAIN content
33.     },
34.     Paragraph {
35.         buffer: ParagraphBuffer, // Interleaved buffer for paragraph content with markers
36.     },
37.     FootnoteDefinition {
38.         content_col: usize,
39.     },
40. }
41. 
42. pub(crate) struct ContainerStack {
43.     pub(crate) stack: Vec<Container>,
44. }
45. 
46. impl ContainerStack {
47.     pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
48.         Self { stack: Vec::new() }
49.     }
50. 
51.     pub(crate) fn depth(&self) -> usize {
52.         self.stack.len()
53.     }
54. 
55.     pub(crate) fn last(&self) -> Option<&Container> {
56.         self.stack.last()
57.     }
58. 
59.     pub(crate) fn push(&mut self, c: Container) {
60.         self.stack.push(c);
61.     }
62. }
63. 
64. /// Expand tabs to columns (tab stop = 4) and return (cols, byte_offset).
65. pub(crate) fn leading_indent(line: &str) -> (usize, usize) {
66.     let mut cols = 0usize;
67.     let mut bytes = 0usize;
68.     for b in line.bytes() {
69.         match b {
70.             b' ' => {
71.                 cols += 1;
72.                 bytes += 1;
73.             }
74.             b'\t' => {
75.                 cols += 4 - (cols % 4);
76.                 bytes += 1;
77.             }
78.             _ => break,
79.         }
80.     }
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 182m 31s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

/home/jola/projects/panache/src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs (lines 1320-1470)

<details>
<summary>151 lines</summary>

```
1320.         if try_parse_setext_heading(&lines, 0).is_some() {
1321.             // Setext headings need blank line before (normal case)
1322.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
1323.         } else {
1324.             BlockDetectionResult::No
1325.         }
1326.     }
1327. 
1328.     fn parse(
1329.         &self,
1330.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1331.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1332.         lines: &[&str],
1333.         pos: usize,
1334.     ) -> usize {
1335.         // Get text line and underline line
1336.         let text_line = lines[pos];
1337.         let underline_line = lines[pos + 1];
1338. 
1339.         // Determine level from underline character (no need to call try_parse again)
1340.         // can_parse() already validated this is a valid setext heading
1341.         let underline_char = underline_line.trim().chars().next().unwrap_or('=');
1342.         let level = if underline_char == '=' { 1 } else { 2 };
1343. 
1344.         // Emit the setext heading
1345.         emit_setext_heading(builder, text_line, underline_line, level, ctx.config);
1346. 
1347.         // Return lines consumed: text line + underline line
1348.         2
1349.     }
1350. 
1351.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
1352.         "setext_heading"
1353.     }
1354. }
1355. 
1356. // ============================================================================
1357. // Block Parser Registry
1358. // ============================================================================
1359. 
1360. /// Registry of block parsers, ordered by priority.
1361. ///
1362. /// This dispatcher tries each parser in order until one succeeds.
1363. /// The ordering follows Pandoc's approach - explicit list order rather
1364. /// than numeric priorities.
1365. pub(crate) struct BlockParserRegistry {
1366.     parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>,
1367. }
1368. 
1369. impl BlockParserRegistry {
1370.     /// Create a new registry with all block parsers.
1371.     ///
1372.     /// Order matters! Parsers are tried in the order listed here.
1373.     /// This follows Pandoc's design where ordering is explicit and documented.
1374.     ///
1375.     /// **Pandoc reference order** (from pandoc/src/Text/Pandoc/Readers/Markdown.hs:487-515):
1376.     /// 1. blanklines (handled separately in our parser)
1377.     /// 2. codeBlockFenced
1378.     /// 3. yamlMetaBlock' ← YAML metadata comes early!
1379.     /// 4. bulletList
1380.     /// 5. divHtml
1381.     /// 6. divFenced
1382.     /// 7. header ← ATX headings
1383.     /// 8. lhsCodeBlock
1384.     /// 9. htmlBlock
1385.     /// 10. table
1386.     /// 11. codeBlockIndented
1387.     /// 12. rawTeXBlock (LaTeX)
1388.     /// 13. lineBlock
1389.     /// 14. blockQuote
1390.     /// 15. hrule ← Horizontal rules come AFTER headers!
1391.     /// 16. orderedList
1392.     /// 17. definitionList
1393.     /// 18. noteBlock (footnotes)
1394.     /// 19. referenceKey ← Reference definitions
1395.     /// 20. abbrevKey
1396.     /// 21. para
1397.     /// 22. plain
1398.     pub fn new() -> Self {
1399.         let parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>> = vec![
1400.             // Match Pandoc's ordering to ensure correct precedence:
1401.             // (0) Pandoc title block (must be at document start).
1402.             Box::new(PandocTitleBlockParser),
1403.             // (2) Fenced code blocks - can interrupt paragraphs!
1404.             Box::new(FencedCodeBlockParser),
1405.             // (3) YAML metadata - before headers and hrules!
1406.             Box::new(YamlMetadataParser),
1407.             // (6) Fenced divs ::: (open/close)
1408.             Box::new(FencedDivCloseParser),
1409.             Box::new(FencedDivOpenParser),
1410.             // (7) Setext headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
1411.             // Must come before ATX to properly handle `---` disambiguation
1412.             Box::new(SetextHeadingParser),
1413.             // (7) ATX headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
1414.             Box::new(AtxHeadingParser),
1415.             // (9) HTML blocks
1416.             Box::new(HtmlBlockParser),
1417.             // (12) LaTeX environment blocks
1418.             Box::new(LatexEnvironmentParser),
1419.             // (13) Line blocks
1420.             Box::new(LineBlockParser),
1421.             // (11) Indented code blocks (AFTER fenced!)
1422.             Box::new(IndentedCodeBlockParser),
1423.             // (15) Horizontal rules - AFTER headings per Pandoc
1424.             Box::new(HorizontalRuleParser),
1425.             // Figures (standalone images) - Pandoc doesn't have these
1426.             Box::new(FigureParser),
1427.             // (19) Reference definitions
1428.             Box::new(ReferenceDefinitionParser),
1429.             // TODO: Migrate remaining blocks in Pandoc order:
1430.             // - (4-6) Lists and divs (bulletList, divHtml)
1431.             // - (10) Tables (grid, multiline, pipe, simple)
1432.             // - (16) Ordered lists
1433.             // - (17) Definition lists
1434.             // - (18) Footnote definitions (noteBlock)
1435.         ];
1436. 
1437.         Self { parsers }
1438.     }
1439. 
1440.     /// Try to parse a block using the registered parsers.
1441.     ///
1442.     /// This method implements the two-phase parsing:
1443.     /// 1. Detection: Check if any parser can handle this content
1444.     /// 2. Caller prepares (closes paragraphs, flushes buffers)
1445.     /// 3. Parser emits the block
1446.     ///
1447.     /// Returns (parser_index, detection_result) if a parser can handle this,
1448.     /// or None if no parser matched.
1449.     #[allow(dead_code)]
1450.     pub fn detect(
1451.         &self,
1452.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1453.         lines: &[&str],
1454.         line_pos: usize,
1455.     ) -> Option<(usize, BlockDetectionResult)> {
1456.         for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
1457.             let result = parser.can_parse(ctx, lines, line_pos);
1458.             match result {
1459.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes | BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
1460.                     log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
1461.                     return Some((i, result));
1462.                 }
1463.                 BlockDetectionResult::No => continue,
1464.             }
1465.         }
1466.         None
1467.     }
1468. 
1469.     /// Like `detect()`, but allows parsers to return cached payload for emission.
1470.     pub fn detect_prepared(
```

1. //! Block parser dispatcher for organizing block-level parsing.
2. //!
3. //! This module provides a trait-based abstraction for block parsers,
4. //! making it easier to add new block types and reducing duplication in parse_inner_content.
5. //!
6. //! Design principles:
7. //! - Single-pass parsing preserved (no backtracking)
8. //! - Each block parser operates independently
9. //! - Inline parsing still integrated (called from within block parsing)
10. //! - Maintains exact CST structure and losslessness
11. 
12. use crate::config::Config;
13. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
14. use std::any::Any;
15. 
16. use super::blocks::blockquotes::strip_n_blockquote_markers;
17. use super::blocks::code_blocks::{
18.     CodeBlockType, FenceInfo, InfoString, parse_fenced_code_block, try_parse_fence_open,
19. };
20. use super::blocks::fenced_divs::{DivFenceInfo, is_div_closing_fence, try_parse_div_fence_open};
21. use super::blocks::figures::parse_figure;
22. use super::blocks::headings::{
23.     emit_atx_heading, emit_setext_heading, try_parse_atx_heading, try_parse_setext_heading,
24. };
25. use super::blocks::horizontal_rules::{emit_horizontal_rule, try_parse_horizontal_rule};
26. use super::blocks::html_blocks::{HtmlBlockType, parse_html_block, try_parse_html_block_start};
27. use super::blocks::indented_code::{is_indented_code_line, parse_indented_code_block};
28. use super::blocks::latex_envs::{LatexEnvInfo, parse_latex_environment, try_parse_latex_env_begin};
29. use super::blocks::line_blocks::{parse_line_block, try_parse_line_block_start};
30. use super::blocks::lists::try_parse_list_marker;
31. use super::blocks::metadata::{try_parse_pandoc_title_block, try_parse_yaml_block};
32. use super::blocks::reference_links::try_parse_reference_definition;
33. use super::inlines::links::try_parse_inline_image;
34. use super::utils::container_stack::byte_index_at_column;
35. use super::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
36. 
37. /// Information about list indentation context.
38. ///
39. /// Used by block parsers that need to handle indentation stripping
40. /// when parsing inside list items (e.g., fenced code blocks).
41. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
42. pub(crate) struct ListIndentInfo {
43.     /// Number of columns to strip for list content
44.     pub content_col: usize,
45. }
46. 
47. /// Context passed to block parsers for decision-making.
48. ///
49. /// Contains immutable references to parser state that block parsers need
50. /// to check conditions (e.g., blank line before, blockquote depth, etc.).
51. pub(crate) struct BlockContext<'a> {
52.     /// Current line content (after blockquote markers stripped if any)
53.     pub content: &'a str,
54. 
55.     /// Whether there was a blank line before this line (relaxed, container-aware)
56.     pub has_blank_before: bool,
57. 
58.     /// Whether there was a strict blank line before this line (no container exceptions)
59.     pub has_blank_before_strict: bool,
60. 
61.     /// Whether we're currently inside a fenced div (container-owned state)
62.     pub in_fenced_div: bool,
63. 
64.     /// Whether we're at document start (pos == 0)
65.     pub at_document_start: bool,
66. 
67.     /// Current blockquote depth
68.     pub blockquote_depth: usize,
69. 
70.     /// Parser configuration
71.     pub config: &'a Config,
72. 
73.     // NOTE: we intentionally do not store `&ContainerStack` here to avoid
74.     // long-lived borrows of `self` in the main parser loop.
75.     /// Base indentation from container context (footnotes, definitions)
76.     pub content_indent: usize,
77. 
78.     /// List indentation info if inside a list
79.     pub list_indent_info: Option<ListIndentInfo>,
80. 
81.     /// Next line content for lookahead (used by setext headings)
82.     pub next_line: Option<&'a str>,
83. }
84. 
85. /// Result of detecting whether a block can be parsed.
86. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
87. pub(crate) enum BlockDetectionResult {
88.     /// Can parse this block, requires blank line before
89.     Yes,
90. 
91.     /// Can parse this block and can interrupt paragraphs (no blank line needed)
92.     #[allow(dead_code)]
93.     YesCanInterrupt,
94. 
95.     /// Cannot parse this content
96.     No,
97. }
98. 
99. /// A prepared (cached) detection result.
100. ///
101. /// This allows expensive detection logic (e.g., fence parsing) to be performed once,
102. /// while emission happens only after the caller prepares (flushes buffers/closes paragraphs).
103. pub(crate) struct PreparedBlockMatch {
104.     pub parser_index: usize,
105.     pub detection: BlockDetectionResult,
106.     pub effect: BlockEffect,
107.     pub payload: Option<Box<dyn Any>>,
108. }
109.

111. pub(crate) enum BlockEffect {

114.     CloseFencedDiv,
115. }

120. /// 1. Detection: Can this block type parse this content? (lightweight, no emission)
