1960. // ============================================================================
1961. 
1962. /// Check if a line is a multiline table separator (continuous dashes).
1963. /// Multiline table separators span the full width and are all dashes.
1964. /// Returns Some(columns) if valid, None otherwise.
1965. fn try_parse_multiline_separator(line: &str) -> Option<Vec<Column>> {
1966.     let trimmed = line.trim_start();
1967.     let leading_spaces = line.len() - trimmed.len();
1968. 
1969.     // Must have leading spaces <= 3 to not be a code block
1970.     if leading_spaces > 3 {
1971.         return None;
1972.     }
1973. 
1974.     let trimmed = trimmed.trim_end();
1975. 
1976.     // Must be all dashes (continuous line of dashes)
1977.     if trimmed.is_empty() || !trimmed.chars().all(|c| c == '-') {
1978.         return None;
1979.     }
1980. 
1981.     // Must have at least 3 dashes
1982.     if trimmed.len() < 3 {
1983.         return None;
1984.     }
1985. 
1986.     // This is a full-width separator - columns will be determined by column separator lines
1987.     Some(vec![Column {
1988.         start: leading_spaces,
1989.         end: leading_spaces + trimmed.len(),
1990.         alignment: Alignment::Default,
1991.     }])
1992. }
1993. 
1994. /// Check if a line is a column separator line for multiline tables.
1995. /// Column separators have dashes with spaces between them to define columns.
1996. fn is_column_separator(line: &str) -> bool {
1997.     try_parse_table_separator(line).is_some()
1998. }
1999. 
2000. /// Try to parse a multiline table starting at the given position.
2001. /// Returns the number of lines consumed if successful.
2002. pub(crate) fn try_parse_multiline_table(
2003.     lines: &[&str],
2004.     start_pos: usize,
2005.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
2006.     config: &Config,
2007. ) -> Option<usize> {
2008.     if start_pos >= lines.len() {
2009.         return None;
2010.     }
2011. 
2012.     let first_line = lines[start_pos];
2013. 
2014.     // First line can be either:
2015.     // 1. A full-width dash separator (for tables with headers)
2016.     // 2. A column separator (for headerless tables)
2017.     let is_full_width_start = try_parse_multiline_separator(first_line).is_some();
2018.     let is_column_sep_start = !is_full_width_start && is_column_separator(first_line);
2019. 
2020.     if !is_full_width_start && !is_column_sep_start {
2021.         return None;
2022.     }
2023. 
2024.     // Look ahead to find the structure
2025.     let mut pos = start_pos + 1;
2026.     let mut found_column_sep = is_column_sep_start; // Already found if headerless
2027.     let mut column_sep_pos = if is_column_sep_start { start_pos } else { 0 };
2028.     let mut has_header = false;
2029.     let mut found_blank_line = false;
2030.     let mut found_closing_sep = false;
2031. 
2032.     // Scan for header section and column separator
2033.     while pos < lines.len() {
2034.         let line = lines[pos];
2035. 
2036.         // Check for column separator (defines columns) - only if we started with full-width
2037.         if is_full_width_start && is_column_separator(line) && !found_column_sep {
2038.             found_column_sep = true;
2039.             column_sep_pos = pos;
2040.             has_header = pos > start_pos + 1; // Has header if there's content before column sep
2041.             pos += 1;
2042.             continue;
2043.         }
2044. 
2045.         // Check for blank line (row separator in body)
2046.         if line.trim().is_empty() {
2047.             found_blank_line = true;
2048.             pos += 1;
2049.             // Check if next line is closing dashes (full-width or column sep for headerless)
2050.             if pos < lines.len() {
2051.                 let next = lines[pos];
2052.                 if try_parse_multiline_separator(next).is_some()
2053.                     || (is_column_sep_start && is_column_separator(next))
2054.                 {
2055.                     found_closing_sep = true;
2056.                     pos += 1; // Include the closing separator
2057.                     break;
2058.                 }
2059.             }
2060.             continue;
2061.         }
2062. 
2063.         // Check for closing full-width dashes
2064.         if try_parse_multiline_separator(line).is_some() {
2065.             found_closing_sep = true;
```

1500. 
1501.     // Split by | to find cells (similar to pipe table parsing)
1502.     let mut parts: Vec<&str> = trimmed.split('|').collect();
1503. 
1504.     // Remove empty first and last parts if line starts/ends with |
1505.     if !parts.is_empty() && parts[0].is_empty() {
1506.         parts.remove(0);
1507.     }
1508.     if !parts.is_empty() && parts[parts.len() - 1].is_empty() {
1509.         parts.pop();
1510.     }
1511. 
1512.     // Emit leading pipe
1513.     if trimmed.starts_with('|') {
1514.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "|");
1515.     }
1516. 
1517.     // Emit each cell
1518.     for (idx, cell_content) in cell_contents.iter().enumerate() {
1519.         if idx < parts.len() {
1520.             let part = parts[idx];
1521. 
1522.             // Emit leading whitespace in cell
1523.             let cell_trimmed = part.trim();
1524.             let ws_start_len = part.len() - part.trim_start().len();
1525.             if ws_start_len > 0 {
1526.                 builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &part[..ws_start_len]);
1527.             }
1528. 
1529.             // Emit TABLE_CELL with inline parsing
1530.             emit_table_cell(builder, cell_content, config);
1531. 
1532.             // Emit trailing whitespace in cell
1533.             let ws_end_start = ws_start_len + cell_trimmed.len();
1534.             if ws_end_start < part.len() {
1535.                 builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &part[ws_end_start..]);
1536.             }
1537.         }
1538. 
1539.         // Emit pipe separator (unless this is the last cell and line doesn't end with |)
1540.         if idx < cell_contents.len() - 1 || trimmed.ends_with('|') {
1541.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "|");
1542.         }
1543.     }
1544. 
1545.     // Emit trailing whitespace before newline
1546.     let trailing_ws_start = leading_ws_len + trimmed.len();
1547.     if trailing_ws_start < line_without_newline.len() {
1548.         builder.token(
1549.             SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(),
1550.             &line_without_newline[trailing_ws_start..],
1551.         );
1552.     }
1553. 
1554.     // Emit newline
1555.     if !newline_str.is_empty() {
1556.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
1557.     }
1558. 
1559.     // Emit continuation lines as TEXT for losslessness
1560.     for line in lines.iter().skip(1) {
1561.         emit_line_tokens(builder, line);
1562.     }
1563. 
1564.     builder.finish_node();

1566. 
1567. /// Try to parse a grid table starting at the given position.
1568. /// Returns the number of lines consumed if successful.
1569. pub(crate) fn try_parse_grid_table(
1570.     lines: &[&str],
1571.     start_pos: usize,
1572.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1573.     config: &Config,
1574. ) -> Option<usize> {
1575.     if start_pos >= lines.len() {
1576.         return None;
1577.     }
1578. 
1579.     // Check if this line is a caption followed by a table
1580.     // If so, the actual table starts after the caption and blank line
1581.     let (actual_start, has_caption_before) = if is_caption_followed_by_table(lines, start_pos) {
1582.         // Skip caption line
1583.         let mut pos = start_pos + 1;
1584.         // Skip blank line if present
1585.         while pos < lines.len() && lines[pos].trim().is_empty() {
1586.             pos += 1;
1587.         }
1588.         (pos, true)
1589.     } else {
1590.         (start_pos, false)
1591.     };
1592. 
1593.     if actual_start >= lines.len() {
1594.         return None;
1595.     }
1596. 
1597.     // First line must be a grid separator
1598.     let first_line = lines[actual_start];
1599.     let _columns = try_parse_grid_separator(first_line)?;
1600. 
1601.     // Track table structure
1602.     let mut end_pos = actual_start + 1;
1603.     let mut found_header_sep = false;
1604.     let mut in_footer = false;
1605. 
1606.     // Scan table lines
1607.     while end_pos < lines.len() {
1608.         let line = lines[end_pos];
1609. 
1610.         // Check for blank line (table ends)
1611.         if line.trim().is_empty() {
1612.             break;
1613.         }
1614. 
1615.         // Check for separator line
1616.         if let Some(sep_cols) = try_parse_grid_separator(line) {
1617.             // Check if this is a header separator (=)
1618.             if sep_cols.iter().any(|c| c.is_header_separator) {
1619.                 if !found_header_sep {
1620.                     found_header_sep = true;
1621.                 } else if !in_footer {
1622.                     // Second = separator starts footer
1623.                     in_footer = true;
1624.                 }
1625.             }
```

933. /// Try to parse a pipe table starting at the given position.
934. /// Returns the number of lines consumed if successful.
935. pub(crate) fn try_parse_pipe_table(
936.     lines: &[&str],
937.     start_pos: usize,
938.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
939.     config: &Config,
940. ) -> Option<usize> {
941.     if start_pos + 1 >= lines.len() {
942.         return None;
943.     }
944. 
945.     // Check if this line is a caption followed by a table
946.     // If so, the actual table starts after the caption and blank line
947.     let (actual_start, has_caption_before) = if is_caption_followed_by_table(lines, start_pos) {
948.         // Skip caption line
949.         let mut pos = start_pos + 1;
950.         // Skip blank line if present
951.         while pos < lines.len() && lines[pos].trim().is_empty() {
952.             pos += 1;
953.         }
954.         (pos, true)
955.     } else {
956.         (start_pos, false)
957.     };
958. 
959.     if actual_start + 1 >= lines.len() {
960.         return None;
961.     }
962. 
963.     // First line should have pipes (potential header)
964.     let header_line = lines[actual_start];
965.     if !header_line.contains('|') {
966.         return None;
967.     }
968. 
969.     // Second line should be separator
970.     let separator_line = lines[actual_start + 1];
971.     let alignments = try_parse_pipe_separator(separator_line)?;
972. 
973.     // Parse header cells
974.     let header_cells = parse_pipe_table_row(header_line);
975. 
976.     // Number of columns should match (approximately - be lenient)
977.     if header_cells.len() != alignments.len() && !header_cells.is_empty() {
978.         // Only fail if very different
979.         if header_cells.len() < alignments.len() / 2 || header_cells.len() > alignments.len() * 2 {
980.             return None;
981.         }
982.     }
983. 
984.     // Find table end (first blank line or end of input)
985.     let mut end_pos = actual_start + 2;
986.     while end_pos < lines.len() {
987.         let line = lines[end_pos];
988.         if line.trim().is_empty() {
989.             break;
990.         }
991.         // Row should have pipes
992.         if !line.contains('|') {
993.             break;
994.         }
995.         end_pos += 1;
996.     }
997. 
998.     // Must have at least one data row
999.     if end_pos <= actual_start + 2 {
1000.         return None;
1001.     }
1002. 
1003.     // Check for caption before table (only if we didn't already detect it)

1005.         Some((start_pos, start_pos + 1)) // Single-line caption detected earlier

1007.         find_caption_before_table(lines, actual_start)
1008.     };

1014.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::PIPE_TABLE.into());
1015. 
1016.     // Emit caption before if present
1017.     if let Some((cap_start, cap_end)) = caption_before {
1018.         emit_table_caption(builder, lines, cap_start, cap_end, config);
1019.         // Emit blank line between caption and table if present
1020.         if cap_end < actual_start {
1021.             for line in lines.iter().take(actual_start).skip(cap_end) {
1022.                 if line.trim().is_empty() {

453. /// Try to parse a simple table starting at the given position.
454. /// Returns the number of lines consumed if successful.
455. pub(crate) fn try_parse_simple_table(
456.     lines: &[&str],
457.     start_pos: usize,
458.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
459.     config: &Config,
460. ) -> Option<usize> {
461.     log::debug!("try_parse_simple_table at line {}", start_pos + 1);
462. 
463.     if start_pos >= lines.len() {
464.         return None;
465.     }
466. 
467.     // Look for a separator line
468.     let separator_pos = find_separator_line(lines, start_pos)?;
469.     log::debug!("  found separator at line {}", separator_pos + 1);
470. 
471.     let separator_line = lines[separator_pos];
472.     let mut columns = try_parse_table_separator(separator_line)?;
473. 
474.     // Determine if there's a header (separator not at start)
475.     let has_header = separator_pos > start_pos;
476.     let header_line = if has_header {
477.         Some(lines[separator_pos - 1])
478.     } else {
479.         None
480.     };
481. 
482.     // Determine alignments
483.     determine_alignments(&mut columns, separator_line, header_line);
484. 
485.     // Find table end (blank line or end of input)
486.     let end_pos = find_table_end(lines, separator_pos + 1);
487. 
488.     // Must have at least one data row (or it's just a separator)
489.     let data_rows = end_pos - separator_pos - 1;
490. 
491.     if data_rows == 0 {
492.         return None;
493.     }
494. 
495.     // Check for caption before table
496.     let caption_before = find_caption_before_table(lines, start_pos);
497. 
498.     // Check for caption after table
499.     let caption_after = find_caption_after_table(lines, end_pos);
500. 
501.     // Build the table
502.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::SIMPLE_TABLE.into());
503. 
504.     // Emit caption before if present
505.     if let Some((cap_start, cap_end)) = caption_before {
506.         emit_table_caption(builder, lines, cap_start, cap_end, config);
507.     }
508. 
509.     // Emit header if present
510.     if has_header {
511.         emit_table_row(
512.             builder,
513.             lines[separator_pos - 1],
514.             &columns,
515.             SyntaxKind::TABLE_HEADER,
516.             config,
517.         );
518.     }
519. 
520.     // Emit separator
```

```
150. 
151. /// Check if a line could be the start of a grid table.
152. /// Grid tables start with a separator line like +---+---+ or +===+===+
153. fn is_grid_table_start(line: &str) -> bool {
154.     try_parse_grid_separator(line).is_some()
155. }
156. 
157. /// Check if a line could be the start of a multiline table.
158. /// Multiline tables start with either:
159. /// - A full-width dash separator (----)
160. /// - A column separator with dashes and spaces (---- ---- ----)
161. fn is_multiline_table_start(line: &str) -> bool {
162.     try_parse_multiline_separator(line).is_some() || is_column_separator(line)
163. }
164. 
165. /// Check if there's a table following a potential caption at this position.
166. /// This is used to avoid parsing a caption as a paragraph when it belongs to a table.
167. pub(crate) fn is_caption_followed_by_table(lines: &[&str], caption_pos: usize) -> bool {
168.     if caption_pos >= lines.len() {
169.         return false;
170.     }
171. 
172.     // Caption must start with a caption prefix
173.     if !is_table_caption_start(lines[caption_pos]) {
174.         return false;
175.     }
176. 
177.     let mut pos = caption_pos + 1;
178. 
179.     // Skip continuation lines of caption (non-blank lines)
180.     while pos < lines.len() && !lines[pos].trim().is_empty() {
181.         // If we hit a table separator, we found a table
182.         if try_parse_table_separator(lines[pos]).is_some() {
183.             return true;
184.         }
185.         pos += 1;
186.     }
187. 
188.     // Skip one blank line
189.     if pos < lines.len() && lines[pos].trim().is_empty() {
190.         pos += 1;
191.     }
192. 
193.     // Check for table at next position
194.     if pos < lines.len() {
195.         let line = lines[pos];
196. 
197.         // Check for grid table start (+---+---+ or +===+===+)
198.         if is_grid_table_start(line) {
199.             return true;
200.         }
201. 
202.         // Check for multiline table start (---- or ---- ---- ----)
203.         if is_multiline_table_start(line) {
204.             return true;
205.         }
206. 
207.         // Could be a separator line (simple/pipe table, headerless)
208.         if try_parse_table_separator(line).is_some() {
209.             return true;
210.         }
211. 
212.         // Or could be a header line followed by separator (simple/pipe table with header)
213.         if pos + 1 < lines.len() && !line.trim().is_empty() {
214.             let next_line = lines[pos + 1];
215.             if try_parse_table_separator(next_line).is_some()
216.                 || try_parse_pipe_separator(next_line).is_some()
217.             {
218.                 return true;
219.             }
220.         }
```

</details>

563. /// Find the position of a separator line starting from pos.
564. fn find_separator_line(lines: &[&str], start_pos: usize) -> Option<usize> {
565.     log::debug!("  find_separator_line from line {}", start_pos + 1);
566. 
567.     // Check first line
568.     log::debug!("    checking first line: {:?}", lines[start_pos]);
569.     if try_parse_table_separator(lines[start_pos]).is_some() {
570.         log::debug!("    separator found at first line");
571.         return Some(start_pos);
572.     }
573. 
574.     // Check second line (for table with header)
575.     if start_pos + 1 < lines.len()
576.         && !lines[start_pos].trim().is_empty()
577.         && try_parse_table_separator(lines[start_pos + 1]).is_some()
578.     {
579.         return Some(start_pos + 1);
580.     }
581. 
582.     None
583. }
584. 
585. /// Find where the table ends (first blank line or end of input).
586. fn find_table_end(lines: &[&str], start_pos: usize) -> usize {
587.     for i in start_pos..lines.len() {
588.         if lines[i].trim().is_empty() {
589.             return i;
590.         }
591.         // Check if this could be a closing separator
592.         if try_parse_table_separator(lines[i]).is_some() {
593.             // Check if next line is blank or end
594.             if i + 1 >= lines.len() || lines[i + 1].trim().is_empty() {
595.                 return i + 1;
596.             }
597.         }
598.     }
599.     lines.len()
600. }

634.             0

1720.                 builder.finish_node();
1721.             }
1722.         } else if is_grid_content_row(line) {
1723.             // Content row - accumulate for multi-line cells
1724.             current_row_kind = if !past_header_sep && found_header_sep {
1725.                 SyntaxKind::TABLE_HEADER
1726.             } else if in_footer_section {
1727.                 SyntaxKind::TABLE_FOOTER
1728.             } else {
1729.                 SyntaxKind::TABLE_ROW
1730.             };
1731. 
1732.             current_row_lines.push(line);
1733.         }
1734.     }
1735. 
1736.     // Emit any remaining accumulated row
1737.     if !current_row_lines.is_empty() {
1738.         // Use first separator's columns for cell boundaries
1739.         if let Some(sep_cols) = try_parse_grid_separator(lines[actual_start]) {
1740.             emit_grid_table_row(
1741.                 builder,
1742.                 &current_row_lines,
1743.                 &sep_cols,
1744.                 current_row_kind,
1745.                 config,
1746.             );
1747.         }
1748.     }
1749. 
1750.     // Emit caption after if present
1751.     if let Some((cap_start, cap_end)) = caption_after {
1752.         if cap_start > end_pos {
1753.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::BLANK_LINE.into());
1754.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::BLANK_LINE.into(), "\n");
1755.             builder.finish_node();
1756.         }
1757.         emit_table_caption(builder, lines, cap_start, cap_end, config);
1758.     }
1759. 
1760.     builder.finish_node(); // GRID_TABLE
1761. 
1762.     // Calculate lines consumed
1763.     let table_start = caption_before
1764.         .map(|(start, _)| start)
1765.         .unwrap_or(actual_start);
1766.     let table_end = if let Some((_, cap_end)) = caption_after {
1767.         cap_end
1768.     } else {
1769.         end_pos
1770.     };
1771. 
1772.     Some(table_end - table_start)
1773. }
1774. 
1775. #[cfg(test)]
```

</details>

9. /// The flavor of Markdown to parse and format.

12. #[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
13. pub enum Flavor {

33. pub struct Extensions {
34.     // ===== Block-level extensions =====

54.     pub task_lists: bool,

57.

62.     pub fenced_code_blocks: bool,
63.     /// Attributes on fenced code blocks {.language #id}
64.     pub fenced_code_attributes: bool,
65.     /// Attributes on inline code
66.     pub inline_code_attributes: bool,
67.

589.         emit_reference_definition_content(builder, content_without_newline);
590. 
591.         if !line_ending.is_empty() {
592.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
593.         }
594. 
595.         builder.finish_node();
596. 
597.         1
598.     }
599. 
600.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
601.         "reference_definition"
602.     }
603. }
604. 
605. /// Helper function to emit reference definition content with inline structure.
606. fn emit_reference_definition_content(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, text: &str) {
607.     use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
608. 
609.     if !text.starts_with('[') {
610.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
611.         return;
612.     }
613. 
614.     let rest = &text[1..];
615.     if let Some(close_pos) = rest.find(']') {
616.         let label = &rest[..close_pos];
617.         let after_bracket = &rest[close_pos + 1..];
618. 
619.         if after_bracket.starts_with(':') {
620.             // Emit LINK node with the label
621.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK.into());
622. 
623.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into());
624.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into(), "[");
625.             builder.finish_node();
626. 
627.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_TEXT.into());
628.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), label);
629.             builder.finish_node();
630. 
631.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "]");
632.             builder.finish_node(); // LINK
633. 
634.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), after_bracket);
635.             return;
636.         }
637.     }
638. 
639.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
640. }
641. 
642. /// Fenced code block parser (``` or ~~~)
643. pub(crate) struct FencedCodeBlockParser;
644. 
645. impl BlockParser for FencedCodeBlockParser {
646.     fn can_parse(
647.         &self,
648.         ctx: &BlockContext,
649.         _lines: &[&str],
650.         _line_pos: usize,
651.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
652.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, _lines, _line_pos)
653.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
654.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
655.     }
656. 
657.     fn detect_prepared(
658.         &self,
659.         ctx: &BlockContext,
660.         _lines: &[&str],
661.         _line_pos: usize,
662.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
663.         // Calculate content to check - may need to strip list indentation
664.         let content_to_check = if let Some(list_info) = ctx.list_indent_info {
665.             if list_info.content_col > 0 && !ctx.content.is_empty() {
666.                 let idx = byte_index_at_column(ctx.content, list_info.content_col);
667.                 &ctx.content[idx..]
668.             } else {
669.                 ctx.content
670.             }
671.         } else {
672.             ctx.content
673.         };
674. 
675.         let fence = try_parse_fence_open(content_to_check)?;
676. 
677.         // Parse info string to determine block type (expensive, but now cached via fence)
678.         let info = InfoString::parse(&fence.info_string);
679. 
680.         let is_executable = matches!(info.block_type, CodeBlockType::Executable { .. });
681.         let is_pandoc_like = matches!(
682.             ctx.config.flavor,
683.             crate::config::Flavor::Pandoc
684.                 | crate::config::Flavor::CommonMark
685.                 | crate::config::Flavor::Gfm
686.         );
687.         if is_executable && is_pandoc_like {
688.             return None;
689.         }
690. 
691.         // Fenced code blocks can interrupt paragraphs only if they have an info string.
692.         let has_info = !fence.info_string.trim().is_empty();
693.         let detection = if has_info {
694.             BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt
695.         } else if ctx.has_blank_before {
696.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
697.         } else {
698.             BlockDetectionResult::No
699.         };
700. 
701.         match detection {
702.             BlockDetectionResult::No => None,
703.             _ => Some((detection, Some(Box::new(fence)))),
704.         }
705.     }
706. 
707.     fn parse(
708.         &self,
709.         ctx: &BlockContext,
710.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
711.         lines: &[&str],
712.         line_pos: usize,
713.     ) -> usize {
714.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
715.     }
716. 
717.     fn parse_prepared(
718.         &self,
719.         ctx: &BlockContext,
720.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
````

</details>

1. //! Syntax tree types and AST node wrappers for Quarto/Pandoc documents.
2. //!
3. //! This module provides a typed API over the raw concrete syntax tree (CST)
4. //! produced by the parser. The CST is based on the `rowan` library and uses
5. //! the red-green tree pattern for efficient incremental parsing.
6. 
7. mod ast;
8. mod chunk_options;
9. mod headings;
10. mod kind;
11. mod links;
12. mod lists;
13. mod references;
14. mod tables;
15. 
16. pub use ast::*;
17. pub use chunk_options::*;
18. pub use headings::*;
19. pub use kind::*;
20. pub use links::*;
21. pub use lists::*;
22. pub use references::*;
23. pub use tables::*;
24. 
25. pub type SyntaxNode = rowan::SyntaxNode<QuartoLanguage>;
26. pub type SyntaxToken = rowan::SyntaxToken<QuartoLanguage>;
27. pub type SyntaxElement = rowan::SyntaxElement<QuartoLanguage>;
28. 
29. #[cfg(test)]
30. mod tests {
31.     use super::*;
32. 
33.     #[test]
34.     fn test_heading_wrapper() {
35.         use crate::Config;
36.         use crate::parser::parse;
37. 
38.         let input = "# Hello World\n\nParagraph.";
39.         let tree = parse(input, Some(Config::default()));
40. 
41.         // Find heading using typed wrapper (root is now DOCUMENT directly)
42.         let heading = tree
43.             .children()
44.             .find_map(Heading::cast)
45.             .expect("should find heading");
46. 
47.         assert_eq!(heading.level(), 1);
48.         assert_eq!(heading.text(), "Hello World");
49.     }
50. 
51.     #[test]
52.     fn test_link_wrapper() {
53.         use crate::Config;
54.         use crate::parser::parse;
55. 
56.         let input = "Click [here](https://example.com).";
57.         let tree = parse(input, Some(Config::default()));
58. 
59.         // Find link using typed wrapper
60.         let link = tree
61.             .descendants()
62.             .find_map(Link::cast)
63.             .expect("should find link");
64. 
65.         assert_eq!(
66.             link.text().map(|t| t.text_content()),
67.             Some("here".to_string())
68.         );
69.         assert_eq!(
70.             link.dest().map(|d| d.url_content()),
71.             Some("https://example.com".to_string())
72.         );
73.     }
74. 
75.     #[test]
76.     fn test_image_wrapper() {
77.         use crate::Config;
78.         use crate::parser::parse;
79. 
80.         let input = "![Alt text](image.png)";
81.         let tree = parse(input, Some(Config::default()));
82. 
83.         let image = tree
84.             .descendants()
85.             .find_map(ImageLink::cast)
86.             .expect("should find image");
87. 
88.         assert_eq!(image.alt().map(|a| a.text()), Some("Alt text".to_string()));
89.     }
90. 
91.     #[test]
92.     fn test_table_wrapper() {
93.         use crate::Config;
94.         use crate::parser::parse;
95. 
96.         let input = r#"| A | B |
97. |---|---|
98. | 1 | 2 |
99. 
100. Table: My caption
101. "#;

247. mod tests {
248.     use super::*;

251.     #[test]
252.     fn test_heading_hierarchy() {
253.         let content = "# H1\n\n## H2\n\n### H3\n\n## H2 Again\n\n# H1 Again";
254.         let config = Config::default();
255.         let tree = crate::parser::parse(content, Some(config));
256.         let symbols = build_document_symbols(&tree, content);
257. 
258.         assert_eq!(symbols.len(), 2); // Two H1 headings
259. 
260.         let h1_first = &symbols[0];
261.         assert_eq!(h1_first.name, "H1");
262.         assert_eq!(h1_first.kind, SymbolKind::STRING);
263.         assert_eq!(h1_first.children.as_ref().unwrap().len(), 2); // Two H2 children
264. 
265.         let h2_first = &h1_first.children.as_ref().unwrap()[0];
266.         assert_eq!(h2_first.name, "H2");
267.         assert_eq!(h2_first.children.as_ref().unwrap().len(), 1); // One H3 child
268. 
269.         let h3 = &h2_first.children.as_ref().unwrap()[0];
270.         assert_eq!(h3.name, "H3");
271. 
272.         let h2_second = &h1_first.children.as_ref().unwrap()[1];
273.         assert_eq!(h2_second.name, "H2 Again");
274. 
275.         let h1_second = &symbols[1];
276.         assert_eq!(h1_second.name, "H1 Again");
277.     }
278. 
279.     #[test]
280.     fn test_table_under_heading() {
281.         let content = "# Heading\n\n| col1 | col2 |\n|------|------|\n| a    | b    |\n";
282.         let config = Config::default();
283.         let tree = crate::parser::parse(content, Some(config));
284.         let symbols = build_document_symbols(&tree, content);
285. 
286.         assert_eq!(symbols.len(), 1);
287.         let heading = &symbols[0];
288.         assert_eq!(heading.name, "Heading");
289. 
290.         let children = heading.children.as_ref().unwrap();
291.         assert_eq!(children.len(), 1);
292.         assert_eq!(children[0].name, "Table");
293.         assert_eq!(children[0].kind, SymbolKind::ARRAY);
294.     }
295. 
296.     #[test]
297.     fn test_table_with_caption() {
298.         let content = "# Heading\n\n| col1 | col2 |\n|------|------|\n| a    | b    |\n: Results\n";
299.         let config = Config::default();
300.         let tree = crate::parser::parse(content, Some(config));
301.         let symbols = build_document_symbols(&tree, content);
302. 
303.         assert_eq!(symbols.len(), 1);
304.         let children = symbols[0].children.as_ref().unwrap();
305.         assert_eq!(children.len(), 1);
306.         assert!(children[0].name.starts_with("Table:"));
307.     }

309.     #[test]
310.     fn test_figure() {

312.         let config = Config::default();
313.         let tree = crate::parser::parse(content, Some(config));
314.         let symbols = build_document_symbols(&tree, content);

367. 
368. Some text here.
369. 
370. | col1 | col2 |
371. |------|------|
372. | a    | b    |
373. 
374. ## Methods
375.

377. 
378. ### Subsection
379. 
380. Another table:

383. |---|---|
384. | 1 | 2 |

393.         assert_eq!(h1.name, "Introduction");
394. 
395.         let h1_children = h1.children.as_ref().unwrap();
396.         // Should have: table + h2
397.         assert!(h1_children.len() >= 2);
398. 
399.         // Find the H2
400.         let h2 = h1_children.iter().find(|s| s.name == "Methods").unwrap();
401.         let h2_children = h2.children.as_ref().unwrap();
402. 
403.         // H2 should have figure + h3
404.         assert!(h2_children.iter().any(|s| s.name.starts_with("Figure:")));
405.         assert!(h2_children.iter().any(|s| s.name == "Subsection"));
406.     }
407. }

276. /// Find caption after table (if any).
277. /// Returns (caption_start, caption_end) positions, or None.
278. fn find_caption_after_table(lines: &[&str], table_end: usize) -> Option<(usize, usize)> {
279.     if table_end >= lines.len() {
280.         return None;
281.     }
282. 
283.     let mut pos = table_end;
284. 
285.     // Skip one blank line if present
286.     if pos < lines.len() && lines[pos].trim().is_empty() {
287.         pos += 1;
288.     }
289. 
290.     if pos >= lines.len() {
291.         return None;
292.     }
293. 
294.     // Check if this line is a caption
295.     if is_table_caption_start(lines[pos]) {
296.         let caption_start = pos;
297.         // Find end of caption (continues until blank line)
298.         let mut caption_end = caption_start + 1;
299.         while caption_end < lines.len() && !lines[caption_end].trim().is_empty() {
300.             caption_end += 1;
301.         }
302.         Some((caption_start, caption_end))
303.     } else {
304.         None
305.     }
306. }
307. 
308. /// Emit a table caption node.
309. fn emit_table_caption(
310.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
311.     lines: &[&str],
312.     start: usize,
313.     end: usize,
314.     config: &Config,
315. ) {
316.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::TABLE_CAPTION.into());
317. 
318.     for (i, line) in lines[start..end].iter().enumerate() {
319.         if i == 0 {
320.             // First line - parse and emit prefix separately
321.             let trimmed = line.trim_start();
322.             let leading_ws_len = line.len() - trimmed.len();
323. 
324.             // Emit leading whitespace if present
325.             if leading_ws_len > 0 {
326.                 builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &line[..leading_ws_len]);
327.             }
328. 
329.             // Check for caption prefix and emit separately
330.             // Calculate where the prefix ends (after trimmed content)
331.             let prefix_and_rest = if line.ends_with('\n') {
332.                 &line[leading_ws_len..line.len() - 1] // Exclude newline
333.             } else {
334.                 &line[leading_ws_len..]
335.             };
336. 
337.             let (prefix_len, prefix_text) = if prefix_and_rest.starts_with("Table: ") {
338.                 (7, "Table: ")
339.             } else if prefix_and_rest.starts_with("table: ") {
340.                 (7, "table: ")
341.             } else if prefix_and_rest.starts_with(": ") {
342.                 (2, ": ")
343.             } else if prefix_and_rest.starts_with(':') {
344.                 (1, ":")
345.             } else {
346.                 (0, "")
347.             };
348. 
349.             if prefix_len > 0 {
350.                 builder.token(SyntaxKind::TABLE_CAPTION_PREFIX.into(), prefix_text);
351. 
352.                 // Emit rest of line after prefix
353.                 let rest_start = leading_ws_len + prefix_len;
354.                 if rest_start < line.len() {
355.                     // Get the caption text (excluding newline)
356.                     let (caption_text, newline_str) = strip_newline(&line[rest_start..]);
357. 
358.                     if !caption_text.is_empty() {
359.                         inline_emission::emit_inlines(builder, caption_text, config);
360.                     }
```

</details>

366.             } else {
367.                 // No recognized prefix, emit whole trimmed line

395. /// Emit a table cell with inline content parsing.
396. /// This is the core helper for Phase 7.1 table inline parsing migration.
397. fn emit_table_cell(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, cell_text: &str, config: &Config) {
398.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::TABLE_CELL.into());
399. 
400.     // Parse inline content within the cell
401.     if !cell_text.is_empty() {
402.         inline_emission::emit_inlines(builder, cell_text, config);
403.     }
404. 
405.     builder.finish_node(); // TABLE_CELL
406. }
407. 
408. /// Determine column alignments based on separator and optional header.
409. fn determine_alignments(columns: &mut [Column], separator_line: &str, header_line: Option<&str>) {
410.     for col in columns.iter_mut() {
411.         let sep_slice = &separator_line[col.start..col.end];
412. 
413.         if let Some(header) = header_line {
414.             // Extract header text for this column
415.             let header_text = if col.end <= header.len() {
416.                 header[col.start..col.end].trim()
417.             } else if col.start < header.len() {
418.                 header[col.start..].trim()
419.             } else {
420.                 ""
421.             };
422. 
423.             if header_text.is_empty() {
424.                 col.alignment = Alignment::Default;
425.                 continue;
426.             }
427. 
428.             // Find where the header text starts and ends within the column
429.             let header_in_col = &header[col.start..col.end.min(header.len())];
430.             let text_start = header_in_col.len() - header_in_col.trim_start().len();
```

121-/// Trait for block-level parsers.
122-///
123-/// Each block type implements this trait with a two-phase approach:
124-/// 1. Detection: Can this block type parse this content? (lightweight, no emission)
125-/// 2. Parsing: Actually parse and emit the block to the builder (called after preparation)
126-///
127-/// This separation allows the caller to:
128-/// - Prepare for block elements (close paragraphs, flush buffers) BEFORE emission
129-/// - Handle blocks that can interrupt paragraphs vs those that need blank lines
130-/// - Maintain correct CST node ordering
131-///
132-/// Note: This is purely organizational - the trait doesn't introduce
133-/// backtracking or multiple passes. Each parser operates during the
134-/// single forward pass through the document.
135-pub(crate) trait BlockParser {
136-    /// Detect if this parser can handle the content (no emission).
137-    fn can_parse(
138-        &self,
139-        ctx: &BlockContext,
140-        lines: &[&str],
141-        line_pos: usize,
142-    ) -> BlockDetectionResult;
143-
144-    fn effect(&self) -> BlockEffect {
145-        BlockEffect::None
146-    }
147-
148-    /// Prepared detection hook.
149-    ///
150-    /// Default implementation just calls `can_parse()` and returns no payload.
151-    fn detect_prepared(
152-        &self,
153-        ctx: &BlockContext,
154-        lines: &[&str],
155-        line_pos: usize,
156-    ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
157-        let detection = self.can_parse(ctx, lines, line_pos);
158-        match detection {
159-            BlockDetectionResult::Yes | BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
160-                Some((detection, None))
161-            }
162-            BlockDetectionResult::No => None,
163-        }
164-    }
165-
166-    /// Parse and emit this block type to the builder.
167-    fn parse(
168-        &self,
169-        ctx: &BlockContext,
170-        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
171-        lines: &[&str],
172-        line_pos: usize,
173-    ) -> usize;
174-
175-    /// Prepared parse hook.
176-    ///
177-    /// Default implementation ignores payload and calls `parse()`.
178-    fn parse_prepared(
179-        &self,
180-        ctx: &BlockContext,
181-        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
182-        lines: &[&str],
183-        line_pos: usize,
184-        _payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
185-    ) -> usize {
186-        self.parse(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos)
187-    }
--
1669:    pub fn detect_prepared(
1670-        &self,
1671-        ctx: &BlockContext,
1672-        lines: &[&str],
1673-        line_pos: usize,
1674-    ) -> Option<PreparedBlockMatch> {
1675-        for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
1676-            if let Some((detection, payload)) = parser.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos) {
1677-                log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
1678-                return Some(PreparedBlockMatch {
1679-                    parser_index: i,
1680-                    detection,
1681-                    effect: parser.effect(),
1682-                    payload,
1683-                });
1684-            }
1685-        }
1686-        None
1687-    }
1688-
1689-    /// Parse a block using the specified parser (by index from detect()).
1690-    ///
1691-    /// Should only be called after detect() returns Some and after
1692-    /// caller has prepared for the block element.
1693-    #[allow(dead_code)]
1694-    pub fn parse(
1695-        &self,
1696-        parser_index: usize,
1697-        ctx: &BlockContext,
1698-        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1699-        lines: &[&str],
1700-        line_pos: usize,
1701-    ) -> usize {
1702-        let parser = &self.parsers[parser_index];
1703-        log::debug!("Block parsed by: {}", parser.name());
1704-        parser.parse(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos)
1705-    }
1706-
1707:    pub fn parse_prepared(
1708-        &self,
1709-        block_match: &PreparedBlockMatch,
1710-        ctx: &BlockContext,
1711-        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1712-        lines: &[&str],
1713-        line_pos: usize,
1714-    ) -> usize {
1715-        let parser = &self.parsers[block_match.parser_index];
1716-        log::debug!("Block parsed by: {}", parser.name());
1717-        parser.parse_prepared(
1718-            ctx,
1719-            builder,
1720-            lines,
1721-            line_pos,
1722-            block_match.payload.as_deref(),
1723-        )
1724-    }
1725-}

711.     fn parse(
712.         &self,
713.         ctx: &BlockContext,
714.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
715.         lines: &[&str],
716.         line_pos: usize,
717.     ) -> usize {
718.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
719.     }
720. 
721.     fn parse_prepared(
722.         &self,
723.         ctx: &BlockContext,
724.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
725.         lines: &[&str],
726.         line_pos: usize,
727.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
728.     ) -> usize {
729.         let prepared = payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<TablePrepared>().copied());
730. 
731.         let try_kind = |kind: TableKind,
732.                         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>|
733.          -> Option<usize> {
734.             match kind {
735.                 TableKind::Grid => {
736.                     if ctx.config.extensions.grid_tables {
737.                         try_parse_grid_table(lines, line_pos, builder, ctx.config)
738.                     } else {
739.                         None
740.                     }
741.                 }
742.                 TableKind::Multiline => {
743.                     if ctx.config.extensions.multiline_tables {
744.                         try_parse_multiline_table(lines, line_pos, builder, ctx.config)
745.                     } else {
746.                         None
747.                     }
748.                 }
749.                 TableKind::Pipe => {
750.                     if ctx.config.extensions.pipe_tables {
751.                         try_parse_pipe_table(lines, line_pos, builder, ctx.config)
752.                     } else {
753.                         None
754.                     }
755.                 }
756.                 TableKind::Simple => {
757.                     if ctx.config.extensions.simple_tables {
758.                         try_parse_simple_table(lines, line_pos, builder, ctx.config)
759.                     } else {
760.                         None
761.                     }
762.                 }
763.             }
764.         };
765. 
766.         if let Some(prepared) = prepared {
767.             if let Some(n) = try_kind(prepared.kind, builder) {
768.                 return n;
769.             }
770.         }
771. 
772.         // Fallback (should be rare) - match core order.
773.         if let Some(n) = try_kind(TableKind::Grid, builder) {
774.             return n;
775.         }
776.         if let Some(n) = try_kind(TableKind::Multiline, builder) {
777.             return n;
778.         }
779.         if let Some(n) = try_kind(TableKind::Pipe, builder) {
780.             return n;
781.         }
782.         if let Some(n) = try_kind(TableKind::Simple, builder) {
783.             return n;
784.         }
785. 
786.         debug_assert!(false, "TableParser::parse called without a matching table");
787.         1
788.     }
789. 
790.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
791.         "table"
792.     }
793. }
794. 
795. /// Helper function to emit reference definition content with inline structure.
796. fn emit_reference_definition_content(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, text: &str) {
797.     use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
798. 
799.     if !text.starts_with('[') {
800.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
801.         return;
802.     }
803. 
804.     let rest = &text[1..];
805.     if let Some(close_pos) = rest.find(']') {
806.         let label = &rest[..close_pos];
807.         let after_bracket = &rest[close_pos + 1..];
808. 
809.         if after_bracket.starts_with(':') {
810.             // Emit LINK node with the label
811.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK.into());
812. 
813.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into());
814.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into(), "[");
815.             builder.finish_node();
816. 
817.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_TEXT.into());
818.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), label);
819.             builder.finish_node();
820.
```

933. /// Try to parse a pipe table starting at the given position.
934. /// Returns the number of lines consumed if successful.
935. pub(crate) fn try_parse_pipe_table(
936.     lines: &[&str],
937.     start_pos: usize,
938.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
939.     config: &Config,
940. ) -> Option<usize> {
941.     if start_pos + 1 >= lines.len() {
942.         return None;
943.     }
944. 
945.     // Check if this line is a caption followed by a table
946.     // If so, the actual table starts after the caption and blank line
947.     let (actual_start, has_caption_before) = if is_caption_followed_by_table(lines, start_pos) {
948.         // Skip caption line
949.         let mut pos = start_pos + 1;
950.         // Skip blank line if present
951.         while pos < lines.len() && lines[pos].trim().is_empty() {
952.             pos += 1;
953.         }
954.         (pos, true)
955.     } else {
956.         (start_pos, false)
957.     };
958. 
959.     if actual_start + 1 >= lines.len() {
960.         return None;
961.     }
962. 
963.     // First line should have pipes (potential header)
964.     let header_line = lines[actual_start];
965.     if !header_line.contains('|') {
966.         return None;
967.     }
968. 
969.     // Second line should be separator
970.     let separator_line = lines[actual_start + 1];
971.     let alignments = try_parse_pipe_separator(separator_line)?;
972. 
973.     // Parse header cells
974.     let header_cells = parse_pipe_table_row(header_line);
975. 
976.     // Number of columns should match (approximately - be lenient)
977.     if header_cells.len() != alignments.len() && !header_cells.is_empty() {
978.         // Only fail if very different
979.         if header_cells.len() < alignments.len() / 2 || header_cells.len() > alignments.len() * 2 {
980.             return None;
981.         }
982.     }
983. 
984.     // Find table end (first blank line or end of input)
985.     let mut end_pos = actual_start + 2;
986.     while end_pos < lines.len() {
987.         let line = lines[end_pos];
988.         if line.trim().is_empty() {
989.             break;
990.         }
991.         // Row should have pipes
992.         if !line.contains('|') {
993.             break;
994.         }
995.         end_pos += 1;
996.     }
997. 
998.     // Must have at least one data row
999.     if end_pos <= actual_start + 2 {
1000.         return None;
1001.     }
1002. 
1003.     // Check for caption before table (only if we didn't already detect it)
1004.     let caption_before = if has_caption_before {
1005.         Some((start_pos, start_pos + 1)) // Single-line caption detected earlier
1006.     } else {
1007.         find_caption_before_table(lines, actual_start)
1008.     };
1009. 
1010.     // Check for caption after table
1011.     let caption_after = find_caption_after_table(lines, end_pos);
1012. 
1013.     // Build the pipe table
1014.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::PIPE_TABLE.into());
1015. 
1016.     // Emit caption before if present
1017.     if let Some((cap_start, cap_end)) = caption_before {
1018.         emit_table_caption(builder, lines, cap_start, cap_end, config);
1019.         // Emit blank line between caption and table if present
1020.         if cap_end < actual_start {
1021.             for line in lines.iter().take(actual_start).skip(cap_end) {
1022.                 if line.trim().is_empty() {
1023.                     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::BLANK_LINE.into());
1024.                     builder.token(SyntaxKind::BLANK_LINE.into(), line);
1025.                     builder.finish_node();
1026.                 }
1027.             }
1028.         }
1029.     }
1030. 
1031.     // Emit header row with inline-parsed cells
1032.     emit_pipe_table_row(builder, header_line, SyntaxKind::TABLE_HEADER, config);
1033. 
1034.     // Emit separator
1035.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::TABLE_SEPARATOR.into());
1036.     emit_line_tokens(builder, separator_line);
1037.     builder.finish_node();
1038.

1567. /// Try to parse a grid table starting at the given position.
1568. /// Returns the number of lines consumed if successful.
1569. pub(crate) fn try_parse_grid_table(
1570.     lines: &[&str],
1571.     start_pos: usize,
1572.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1573.     config: &Config,
1574. ) -> Option<usize> {
1575.     if start_pos >= lines.len() {
1576.         return None;
1577.     }
1578. 
1579.     // Check if this line is a caption followed by a table
1580.     // If so, the actual table starts after the caption and blank line
1581.     let (actual_start, has_caption_before) = if is_caption_followed_by_table(lines, start_pos) {
1582.         // Skip caption line
1583.         let mut pos = start_pos + 1;
1584.         // Skip blank line if present
1585.         while pos < lines.len() && lines[pos].trim().is_empty() {
1586.             pos += 1;
1587.         }
1588.         (pos, true)
1589.     } else {
1590.         (start_pos, false)
1591.     };
1592. 
1593.     if actual_start >= lines.len() {
1594.         return None;
1595.     }
1596. 
1597.     // First line must be a grid separator
1598.     let first_line = lines[actual_start];
1599.     let _columns = try_parse_grid_separator(first_line)?;
1600. 
1601.     // Track table structure
1602.     let mut end_pos = actual_start + 1;
1603.     let mut found_header_sep = false;
1604.     let mut in_footer = false;
1605. 
1606.     // Scan table lines
1607.     while end_pos < lines.len() {
1608.         let line = lines[end_pos];
1609. 
1610.         // Check for blank line (table ends)
1611.         if line.trim().is_empty() {
1612.             break;
1613.         }
1614. 
1615.         // Check for separator line
1616.         if let Some(sep_cols) = try_parse_grid_separator(line) {
1617.             // Check if this is a header separator (=)
1618.             if sep_cols.iter().any(|c| c.is_header_separator) {
1619.                 if !found_header_sep {
1620.                     found_header_sep = true;
1621.                 } else if !in_footer {
1622.                     // Second = separator starts footer
1623.                     in_footer = true;
1624.                 }
1625.             }
1626.             end_pos += 1;
1627.             continue;
1628.         }
1629. 
1630.         // Check for content row
1631.         if is_grid_content_row(line) {

1633.             continue;

1635.

1644.     }
1645. 
1646.     // Last consumed line should be a separator for a well-formed table

1671.                     builder.finish_node();

1674.         }
1675.     }

1681.     let mut current_row_kind = SyntaxKind::TABLE_HEADER;
1682. 
1683.     // Emit table rows - accumulate multi-line cells

1690.                     &current_row_lines,
1691.                     &sep_cols,
1692.                     current_row_kind,

1705.                     builder.finish_node();

2000. /// Try to parse a multiline table starting at the given position.
2001. /// Returns the number of lines consumed if successful.
2002. pub(crate) fn try_parse_multiline_table(
2003.     lines: &[&str],
2004.     start_pos: usize,
2005.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
2006.     config: &Config,
2007. ) -> Option<usize> {
2008.     if start_pos >= lines.len() {
2009.         return None;
2010.     }
2011. 
2012.     let first_line = lines[start_pos];
2013. 
2014.     // First line can be either:
2015.     // 1. A full-width dash separator (for tables with headers)
2016.     // 2. A column separator (for headerless tables)
2017.     let is_full_width_start = try_parse_multiline_separator(first_line).is_some();
2018.     let is_column_sep_start = !is_full_width_start && is_column_separator(first_line);
2019. 
2020.     if !is_full_width_start && !is_column_sep_start {
2021.         return None;
2022.     }
2023. 
2024.     // Look ahead to find the structure
2025.     let mut pos = start_pos + 1;
2026.     let mut found_column_sep = is_column_sep_start; // Already found if headerless
2027.     let mut column_sep_pos = if is_column_sep_start { start_pos } else { 0 };
2028.     let mut has_header = false;
2029.     let mut found_blank_line = false;
2030.     let mut found_closing_sep = false;
2031. 
2032.     // Scan for header section and column separator
2033.     while pos < lines.len() {
2034.         let line = lines[pos];
2035. 
2036.         // Check for column separator (defines columns) - only if we started with full-width
2037.         if is_full_width_start && is_column_separator(line) && !found_column_sep {
2038.             found_column_sep = true;
2039.             column_sep_pos = pos;
2040.             has_header = pos > start_pos + 1; // Has header if there's content before column sep
2041.             pos += 1;
2042.             continue;
2043.         }
2044. 
2045.         // Check for blank line (row separator in body)
2046.         if line.trim().is_empty() {
2047.             found_blank_line = true;
2048.             pos += 1;
2049.             // Check if next line is closing dashes (full-width or column sep for headerless)
2050.             if pos < lines.len() {
2051.                 let next = lines[pos];
2052.                 if try_parse_multiline_separator(next).is_some()
2053.                     || (is_column_sep_start && is_column_separator(next))
2054.                 {
2055.                     found_closing_sep = true;
2056.                     pos += 1; // Include the closing separator
2057.                     break;
2058.                 }
2059.             }
2060.             continue;
2061.         }
2062. 
2063.         // Check for closing full-width dashes
2064.         if try_parse_multiline_separator(line).is_some() {
2065.             found_closing_sep = true;
2066.             pos += 1;
2067.             break;
2068.         }
2069. 
2070.         // Check for closing column separator (for headerless tables)
2071.         if is_column_sep_start && is_column_separator(line) && found_blank_line {
2072.             found_closing_sep = true;
2073.             pos += 1;
2074.             break;
2075.         }
2076. 
2077.         // Content row
2078.         pos += 1;
2079.     }
2080. 
2081.     // Must have found a column separator to be a valid multiline table
2082.     if !found_column_sep {
2083.         return None;
2084.     }
2085. 
2086.     // Must have had at least one blank line between rows (distinguishes from simple tables)

150. 
151. /// Check if a line could be the start of a grid table.
152. /// Grid tables start with a separator line like +---+---+ or +===+===+
153. fn is_grid_table_start(line: &str) -> bool {
154.     try_parse_grid_separator(line).is_some()
155. }
156. 
157. /// Check if a line could be the start of a multiline table.
158. /// Multiline tables start with either:
159. /// - A full-width dash separator (----)
160. /// - A column separator with dashes and spaces (---- ---- ----)
161. fn is_multiline_table_start(line: &str) -> bool {
162.     try_parse_multiline_separator(line).is_some() || is_column_separator(line)
163. }
164. 
165. /// Check if there's a table following a potential caption at this position.
166. /// This is used to avoid parsing a caption as a paragraph when it belongs to a table.
167. pub(crate) fn is_caption_followed_by_table(lines: &[&str], caption_pos: usize) -> bool {
168.     if caption_pos >= lines.len() {
169.         return false;
170.     }
171. 
172.     // Caption must start with a caption prefix
173.     if !is_table_caption_start(lines[caption_pos]) {
174.         return false;
175.     }
176. 
177.     let mut pos = caption_pos + 1;
178. 
179.     // Skip continuation lines of caption (non-blank lines)
180.     while pos < lines.len() && !lines[pos].trim().is_empty() {
181.         // If we hit a table separator, we found a table
182.         if try_parse_table_separator(lines[pos]).is_some() {
183.             return true;
184.         }
185.         pos += 1;
186.     }
187. 
188.     // Skip one blank line
189.     if pos < lines.len() && lines[pos].trim().is_empty() {
190.         pos += 1;
191.     }
192. 
193.     // Check for table at next position
194.     if pos < lines.len() {
195.         let line = lines[pos];
196. 
197.         // Check for grid table start (+---+---+ or +===+===+)
198.         if is_grid_table_start(line) {
199.             return true;
200.         }
201. 
202.         // Check for multiline table start (---- or ---- ---- ----)
203.         if is_multiline_table_start(line) {
204.             return true;
205.         }
206. 
207.         // Could be a separator line (simple/pipe table, headerless)
208.         if try_parse_table_separator(line).is_some() {
209.             return true;
210.         }
211. 
212.         // Or could be a header line followed by separator (simple/pipe table with header)
213.         if pos + 1 < lines.len() && !line.trim().is_empty() {
214.             let next_line = lines[pos + 1];
215.             if try_parse_table_separator(next_line).is_some()
216.                 || try_parse_pipe_separator(next_line).is_some()
217.             {
218.                 return true;
219.             }
220.         }
221.     }
222. 
223.     false
224. }
225. 
226. /// Find caption before table (if any).
227. /// Returns (caption_start, caption_end) positions, or None.
228. fn find_caption_before_table(lines: &[&str], table_start: usize) -> Option<(usize, usize)> {
229.     if table_start == 0 {
230.         return None;
231.     }
232. 
233.     // Look backward for a caption
234.     // Caption must be immediately before table (with possible blank line between)
235.     let mut pos = table_start - 1;
236. 
237.     // Skip one blank line if present
238.     if lines[pos].trim().is_empty() {
239.         if pos == 0 {
240.             return None;
241.         }
242.         pos -= 1;
243.     }
244. 
245.     // Now pos points to the last non-blank line before the table
246.     // This could be the last line of a multiline caption, or a single-line caption
247.     let caption_end = pos + 1; // End is exclusive
248. 
249.     // If this line is NOT a caption start, it might be a continuation line
250.     // Scan backward through non-blank lines to find the caption start
251.     if !is_table_caption_start(lines[pos]) {
252.         // Not a caption start - check if there's a caption start above
253.         let mut scan_pos = pos;
254.         while scan_pos > 0 {
255.             scan_pos -= 1;
256.             let line = lines[scan_pos];
257. 
258.             // If we hit a blank line, we've gone too far
259.             if line.trim().is_empty() {
260.                 return None;
261.             }
262. 
263.             // If we find a caption start, this is the beginning of the multiline caption
264.             if is_table_caption_start(line) {
265.                 return Some((scan_pos, caption_end));
266.             }
267.         }
268.         // Scanned to beginning without finding caption start
269.         None
270.     } else {
271.         // This line is a caption start - return the range
272.         Some((pos, caption_end))
273.     }
274. }
275. 
276. /// Find caption after table (if any).
277. /// Returns (caption_start, caption_end) positions, or None.
278. fn find_caption_after_table(lines: &[&str], table_end: usize) -> Option<(usize, usize)> {
279.     if table_end >= lines.len() {
280.         return None;
281.     }
282. 
283.     let mut pos = table_end;
284. 
285.     // Skip one blank line if present
286.     if pos < lines.len() && lines[pos].trim().is_empty() {
287.         pos += 1;
288.     }
289. 
290.     if pos >= lines.len() {
291.         return None;
292.     }
293. 
294.     // Check if this line is a caption
295.     if is_table_caption_start(lines[pos]) {
296.         let caption_start = pos;
297.         // Find end of caption (continues until blank line)
298.         let mut caption_end = caption_start + 1;
299.         while caption_end < lines.len() && !lines[caption_end].trim().is_empty() {
300.             caption_end += 1;
301.         }
302.         Some((caption_start, caption_end))
303.     } else {
304.         None
305.     }
306. }
307. 
308. /// Emit a table caption node.
309. fn emit_table_caption(

314.     config: &Config,

334.                 &line[leading_ws_len..]
335.             };
336. 
337.             let (prefix_len, prefix_text) = if prefix_and_rest.starts_with("Table: ") {
338.                 (7, "Table: ")
339.             } else if prefix_and_rest.starts_with("table: ") {
340.                 (7, "table: ")

2240. fn extract_first_line_cell_contents(line: &str, columns: &[Column]) -> Vec<String> {

2250.         } else {
2251.             ""
2252.         };
2253. 
2254.         cells.push(cell_text.to_string());

2256. 
2257.     cells

453. /// Try to parse a simple table starting at the given position.
454. /// Returns the number of lines consumed if successful.
455. pub(crate) fn try_parse_simple_table(
456.     lines: &[&str],
457.     start_pos: usize,
458.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
459.     config: &Config,
460. ) -> Option<usize> {
461.     log::debug!("try_parse_simple_table at line {}", start_pos + 1);
462. 
463.     if start_pos >= lines.len() {
464.         return None;
465.     }
466. 
467.     // Look for a separator line
468.     let separator_pos = find_separator_line(lines, start_pos)?;
469.     log::debug!("  found separator at line {}", separator_pos + 1);
470. 
471.     let separator_line = lines[separator_pos];
472.     let mut columns = try_parse_table_separator(separator_line)?;
473. 
474.     // Determine if there's a header (separator not at start)
475.     let has_header = separator_pos > start_pos;
476.     let header_line = if has_header {
477.         Some(lines[separator_pos - 1])
478.     } else {
479.         None
480.     };
481. 
482.     // Determine alignments
483.     determine_alignments(&mut columns, separator_line, header_line);
484. 
485.     // Find table end (blank line or end of input)
486.     let end_pos = find_table_end(lines, separator_pos + 1);
487. 
488.     // Must have at least one data row (or it's just a separator)
489.     let data_rows = end_pos - separator_pos - 1;
490. 
491.     if data_rows == 0 {
492.         return None;
493.     }
494. 
495.     // Check for caption before table
496.     let caption_before = find_caption_before_table(lines, start_pos);
497. 
498.     // Check for caption after table
499.     let caption_after = find_caption_after_table(lines, end_pos);
500. 
501.     // Build the table
502.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::SIMPLE_TABLE.into());
503. 
504.     // Emit caption before if present
505.     if let Some((cap_start, cap_end)) = caption_before {
506.         emit_table_caption(builder, lines, cap_start, cap_end, config);
507.     }
508. 
509.     // Emit header if present
510.     if has_header {
511.         emit_table_row(
512.             builder,
513.             lines[separator_pos - 1],
514.             &columns,
515.             SyntaxKind::TABLE_HEADER,
516.             config,
517.         );
518.     }
519. 
520.     // Emit separator
521.     builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::TABLE_SEPARATOR.into());
522.     emit_line_tokens(builder, separator_line);
523.     builder.finish_node();
524. 
525.     // Emit data rows
526.     for line in lines.iter().take(end_pos).skip(separator_pos + 1) {
527.         emit_table_row(builder, line, &columns, SyntaxKind::TABLE_ROW, config);
528.     }
529. 
530.     // Emit caption after if present
531.     if let Some((cap_start, cap_end)) = caption_after {
532.         // Emit blank line before caption if needed
533.         if cap_start > end_pos {
534.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::BLANK_LINE.into());
535.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::BLANK_LINE.into(), "\n");
536.             builder.finish_node();
537.         }
538.         emit_table_caption(builder, lines, cap_start, cap_end, config);
539.     }
540. 
541.     builder.finish_node(); // SimpleTable
542. 
543.     // Calculate lines consumed (including captions)
544.     let table_start = if let Some((cap_start, _)) = caption_before {
545.         cap_start
546.     } else if has_header {
547.         separator_pos - 1
548.     } else {
549.         separator_pos
550.     };
551. 
552.     let table_end = if let Some((_, cap_end)) = caption_after {
553.         cap_end
554.     } else {
555.         end_pos
556.     };
557. 
558.     let lines_consumed = table_end - table_start;
559. 
560.     Some(lines_consumed)
561. }
562. 
563. /// Find the position of a separator line starting from pos.
564. fn find_separator_line(lines: &[&str], start_pos: usize) -> Option<usize> {
565.     log::debug!("  find_separator_line from line {}", start_pos + 1);
566. 
567.     // Check first line
568.     log::debug!("    checking first line: {:?}", lines[start_pos]);
569.     if try_parse_table_separator(lines[start_pos]).is_some() {
570.         log::debug!("    separator found at first line");
571.         return Some(start_pos);
572.     }
573. 
574.     // Check second line (for table with header)
575.     if start_pos + 1 < lines.len()
576.         && !lines[start_pos].trim().is_empty()
577.         && try_parse_table_separator(lines[start_pos + 1]).is_some()
578.     {
579.         return Some(start_pos + 1);
580.     }
581. 
582.     None
583. }
584. 
585. /// Find where the table ends (first blank line or end of input).
586. fn find_table_end(lines: &[&str], start_pos: usize) -> usize {
587.     for i in start_pos..lines.len() {
588.         if lines[i].trim().is_empty() {
589.             return i;
590.         }
591.         // Check if this could be a closing separator
592.         if try_parse_table_separator(lines[i]).is_some() {
593.             // Check if next line is blank or end
594.             if i + 1 >= lines.len() || lines[i + 1].trim().is_empty() {
595.                 return i + 1;
596.             }
597.         }
598.     }
599.     lines.len()
600. }
601.

612.

614.

617.     let leading_ws_len = line_without_newline.len() - trimmed.len();
618.     if leading_ws_len > 0 {

1. //! Simple table parsing for Pandoc's simple_tables extension.
2. 
3. use crate::config::Config;
4. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
5. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
6. 
7. use crate::parser::utils::helpers::{emit_line_tokens, strip_newline};
8. use crate::parser::utils::inline_emission;
9. 
10. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
11. pub enum Alignment {
12.     Left,
13.     Right,
14.     Center,
15.     Default,
16. }
17. 
18. /// Column information extracted from the separator line.
19. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
20. pub(crate) struct Column {
21.     /// Start position (byte index) in the line
22.     start: usize,
23.     /// End position (byte index) in the line
24.     end: usize,
25.     /// Column alignment
26.     alignment: Alignment,
27. }
28. 
29. /// Try to detect if a line is a table separator line.
30. /// Returns Some(column positions) if it's a valid separator.
31. pub(crate) fn try_parse_table_separator(line: &str) -> Option<Vec<Column>> {
32.     let trimmed = line.trim_start();
33.     // Strip trailing newline if present (CRLF or LF)
34.     let (trimmed, newline_str) = strip_newline(trimmed);
35.     let leading_spaces = line.len() - trimmed.len() - newline_str.len();
36. 
37.     // Must have leading spaces <= 3 to not be a code block
38.     if leading_spaces > 3 {
39.         return None;
40.     }
41. 
42.     // Must contain at least one dash
43.     if !trimmed.contains('-') {
44.         return None;
45.     }
46. 
47.     // A separator line consists of dashes and spaces
48.     if !trimmed.chars().all(|c| c == '-' || c == ' ') {
49.         return None;
50.     }
51. 
52.     // Must not be a horizontal rule (needs spaces between dash groups)
53.     // Horizontal rules are continuous dashes (possibly with leading spaces)
54.     if trimmed.chars().filter(|&c| c == '-').count() >= 3
55.         && !trimmed.contains("  ") // no double spaces = likely horizontal rule
56.         && trimmed.chars().all(|c| c == '-' || c == ' ')
57.     {
58.         // Could be horizontal rule, check if there are clear column separations
59.         let dash_groups: Vec<_> = trimmed.split(' ').filter(|s| !s.is_empty()).collect();
60. 
61.         // If only one group of dashes, it's a horizontal rule
62.         if dash_groups.len() == 1 {
63.             return None;
64.         }
65.     }
66. 
67.     // Extract column positions from dash groups
68.     let columns = extract_columns(trimmed, leading_spaces);
69. 
70.     if columns.is_empty() {
71.         return None;
72.     }
73. 
74.     Some(columns)
75. }
76. 
77. /// Extract column positions from a separator line.
78. fn extract_columns(separator: &str, offset: usize) -> Vec<Column> {
79.     let mut columns = Vec::new();
80.     let mut in_dashes = false;
81.     let mut col_start = 0;
82. 
83.     for (i, ch) in separator.char_indices() {
84.         match ch {
85.             '-' => {
86.                 if !in_dashes {
87.                     col_start = i + offset;
88.                     in_dashes = true;
89.                 }
90.             }
91.             ' ' => {
92.                 if in_dashes {
93.                     columns.push(Column {
94.                         start: col_start,
95.                         end: i + offset,
96.                         alignment: Alignment::Default, // Will be determined later
97.                     });
98.                     in_dashes = false;
99.                 }
100.             }
101.             _ => {}
102.         }
103.     }
104. 
105.     // Handle last column
106.     if in_dashes {
107.         columns.push(Column {
108.             start: col_start,
109.             end: separator.len() + offset,
110.             alignment: Alignment::Default,
111.         });
112.     }
113. 
114.     columns
115. }
116.

118. /// Returns Some((prefix_len, caption_text)) if it's a caption.
119. fn try_parse_caption_prefix(line: &str) -> Option<(usize, &str)> {
120.     let trimmed = line.trim_start();
```

116. 
117. /// Try to parse a table caption from a line.
118. /// Returns Some((prefix_len, caption_text)) if it's a caption.
119. fn try_parse_caption_prefix(line: &str) -> Option<(usize, &str)> {
120.     let trimmed = line.trim_start();
121.     let leading_spaces = line.len() - trimmed.len();
122. 
123.     // Must have leading spaces <= 3 to not be a code block
124.     if leading_spaces > 3 {
125.         return None;
126.     }
127. 
128.     // Check for "Table:" or "table:" or just ":"
129.     if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix("Table:") {
130.         Some((leading_spaces + 6, rest))
131.     } else if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix("table:") {
132.         Some((leading_spaces + 6, rest))
133.     } else if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix(':') {
134.         // Just ":" - but need to be careful not to match definition list markers
135.         // A caption with just ":" should have content or be followed by content
136.         if !rest.trim().is_empty() || rest.starts_with(' ') {
137.             Some((leading_spaces + 1, rest))
138.         } else {
139.             None
140.         }
141.     } else {
142.         None
143.     }
144. }
145. 
146. /// Check if a line could be the start of a table caption.
147. fn is_table_caption_start(line: &str) -> bool {
148.     try_parse_caption_prefix(line).is_some()
149. }
150. 
151. /// Check if a line could be the start of a grid table.
152. /// Grid tables start with a separator line like +---+---+ or +===+===+
153. fn is_grid_table_start(line: &str) -> bool {
154.     try_parse_grid_separator(line).is_some()
155. }
156. 
157. /// Check if a line could be the start of a multiline table.
158. /// Multiline tables start with either:
159. /// - A full-width dash separator (----)
160. /// - A column separator with dashes and spaces (---- ---- ----)
161. fn is_multiline_table_start(line: &str) -> bool {
162.     try_parse_multiline_separator(line).is_some() || is_column_separator(line)
163. }
164. 
165. /// Check if there's a table following a potential caption at this position.
166. /// This is used to avoid parsing a caption as a paragraph when it belongs to a table.
167. pub(crate) fn is_caption_followed_by_table(lines: &[&str], caption_pos: usize) -> bool {
168.     if caption_pos >= lines.len() {

        // Check for HTML block (if raw_html extension is enabled)

            // Emit buffer before HTML block
            self.emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed();

            // Close paragraph before opening HTML block
            if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Paragraph { .. })) {

            }

        // Check if this line looks like a table caption followed by a table
        // If so, try to parse the table (which will include the caption)
        if is_caption_followed_by_table(&self.lines, self.pos) {
            log::debug!("Found caption followed by table at line {}", self.pos);

            // Emit buffer before starting table
            self.emit_list_item_buffer_if_needed();

            let caption_start = self.pos;

            // The caption is at self.pos. We need to find where the actual table starts.
            // Skip non-blank lines (caption continuation) and one blank line
            let mut table_pos = self.pos + 1;
            while table_pos < self.lines.len() && !self.lines[table_pos].trim().is_empty() {
                table_pos += 1;
            }
            // Skip one blank line if present
            if table_pos < self.lines.len() && self.lines[table_pos].trim().is_empty() {
                table_pos += 1;
            }

            // Now table_pos should be at the table start (separator, header, or grid fence)
            // Try to parse the table from this position
            if table_pos < self.lines.len() {
                if let Some(lines_consumed) =
                    try_parse_grid_table(&self.lines, table_pos, &mut self.builder, self.config)
                {
                    log::debug!(
                        "Parsed grid table (with caption) starting at line {} ({} lines total from caption)",
                        table_pos,
                        lines_consumed
                    );
                    // lines_consumed is from table_pos, but includes the caption found by find_caption_before_table
                    // So we advance from caption_start by lines_consumed
                    self.pos = caption_start + lines_consumed;
                    return true;
                }

                if let Some(lines_consumed) = try_parse_multiline_table(
                    &self.lines,
                    table_pos,
                    &mut self.builder,
                    self.config,
                ) {
                    log::debug!(
                        "Parsed multiline table (with caption) starting at line {} ({} lines total from caption)",
                        table_pos,
                        lines_consumed

                    self.pos = caption_start + lines_consumed;

if matches!(self.containers.last(), Some(Container::Paragraph { .. })) {
                self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);

            if !definition_lists::in_definition_list(&self.containers) {
                self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DEFINITION_LIST.into());
                self.containers.push(Container::DefinitionList {});
            }

            // Close previous definition item if exists
            while matches!(
                self.containers.last(),
                Some(Container::Definition { .. }) | Some(Container::DefinitionItem { .. })
            ) {
                self.close_containers_to(self.containers.depth() - 1);
            }

            // Start new definition item
            self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DEFINITION_ITEM.into());
            self.containers.push(Container::DefinitionItem {});

            // Emit term
            emit_term(&mut self.builder, content, self.config);
            self.pos += 1;

            // Emit blank lines between term and definition marker

### 💬 Copilot

I’m going to make the table dispatcher recognize “Table:” caption lines as the start of a table block (like the old core special-case), so captions don’t get parsed twice and idempotency is restored.

608. pub(crate) struct TableParser;
609.
