621. }
622. 
623. impl BlockParser for TableParser {
624.     fn effect(&self) -> BlockEffect {
625.         BlockEffect::None
626.     }
627. 
628.     fn can_parse(
629.         &self,
630.         ctx: &BlockContext,
631.         lines: &[&str],
632.         line_pos: usize,
633.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
634.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
635.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
636.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
637.     }
638. 
639.     fn detect_prepared(
640.         &self,
641.         ctx: &BlockContext,
642.         lines: &[&str],
643.         line_pos: usize,
644.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
645.         if !(ctx.config.extensions.simple_tables
646.             || ctx.config.extensions.multiline_tables
647.             || ctx.config.extensions.grid_tables
648.             || ctx.config.extensions.pipe_tables)
649.         {
650.             return None;
651.         }
652. 
653.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
654.             return None;
655.         }
656. 
657.         // Correctness first: only claim a match if a real parse would succeed.
658.         // (Otherwise we can steal list items/paragraphs and drop content.)
659.         let mut tmp = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
660. 
661.         if ctx.config.extensions.grid_tables
662.             && try_parse_grid_table(lines, line_pos, &mut tmp, ctx.config).is_some()
663.         {
664.             return Some((
665.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
666.                 Some(Box::new(TablePrepared {
667.                     kind: TableKind::Grid,
668.                 })),
669.             ));
670.         }
671. 
672.         if ctx.config.extensions.multiline_tables
673.             && try_parse_multiline_table(lines, line_pos, &mut tmp, ctx.config).is_some()
674.         {
675.             return Some((
676.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
677.                 Some(Box::new(TablePrepared {
678.                     kind: TableKind::Multiline,
679.                 })),
680.             ));
681.         }
682.

685.         {
686.             return Some((
687.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
688.                 Some(Box::new(TablePrepared {
689.                     kind: TableKind::Pipe,
690.                 })),
691.             ));
692.         }
693. 
694.         if ctx.config.extensions.simple_tables
695.             && try_parse_simple_table(lines, line_pos, &mut tmp, ctx.config).is_some()
696.         {
697.             return Some((
698.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
699.                 Some(Box::new(TablePrepared {
700.                     kind: TableKind::Simple,
701.                 })),
702.             ));
703.         }
704. 
705.         // (Optional) Caption-only lookahead without table parse shouldn't match.
706.         // The real parsers already handle captions when invoked on the caption line.
707. 
708.         None
709.     }

280.     fn test_table_under_heading() {

293.         assert_eq!(children[0].kind, SymbolKind::ARRAY);

diff --git a/src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs b/src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs
index a1f9a92..51da50b 100644
--- a/src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs
+++ b/src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs
@@ -30,6 +30,10 @@ use super::blocks::line_blocks::{parse_line_block, try_parse_line_block_start};
 use super::blocks::lists::try_parse_list_marker;
 use super::blocks::metadata::{try_parse_pandoc_title_block, try_parse_yaml_block};
 use super::blocks::reference_links::try_parse_reference_definition;
+use super::blocks::tables::{
+    is_caption_followed_by_table, try_parse_grid_table, try_parse_multiline_table,
+    try_parse_pipe_table, try_parse_simple_table,
+};
 use super::inlines::links::try_parse_inline_image;
 use super::utils::container_stack::byte_index_at_column;
 use super::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
@@ -598,6 +602,273 @@ impl BlockParser for ReferenceDefinitionParser {
     }
 }
 
+// ============================================================================
+// Table Parser (position #10)
+// ============================================================================
+
+pub(crate) struct TableParser;
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
+enum TableKind {
+    Grid,
+    Multiline,
+    Pipe,
+    Simple,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
+struct TablePrepared {
+    kind: TableKind,
+}
+
+impl BlockParser for TableParser {
+    fn effect(&self) -> BlockEffect {
+        BlockEffect::None
+    }
+
+    fn can_parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
+        self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
+            .map(|(d, _)| d)
+            .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
+    }
+
+    fn detect_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
+        if !(ctx.config.extensions.simple_tables
+            || ctx.config.extensions.multiline_tables
+            || ctx.config.extensions.grid_tables
+            || ctx.config.extensions.pipe_tables)
+        {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        // Correctness first: only claim a match if a real parse would succeed.
+        // (Otherwise we can steal list items/paragraphs and drop content.)
+        let mut tmp = GreenNodeBuilder::new();
+
+        // Handle caption-before-table lines by matching the *table kind* starting
+        // after the caption, but parsing from the caption line so the caption is
+        // included and consumed.
+        if ctx.config.extensions.table_captions && is_caption_followed_by_table(lines, line_pos) {
+            // Skip caption continuation lines and one optional blank line.
+            let mut table_pos = line_pos + 1;
+            while table_pos < lines.len() && !lines[table_pos].trim().is_empty() {
+                table_pos += 1;
+            }
+            if table_pos < lines.len() && lines[table_pos].trim().is_empty() {
+                table_pos += 1;
+            }
+
+            if ctx.config.extensions.grid_tables
+                && try_parse_grid_table(lines, table_pos, &mut tmp, ctx.config).is_some()
+            {
+                return Some((
+                    BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
+                    Some(Box::new(TablePrepared {
+                        kind: TableKind::Grid,
+                    })),
+                ));
+            }
+
+            if ctx.config.extensions.multiline_tables
+                && try_parse_multiline_table(lines, table_pos, &mut tmp, ctx.config).is_some()
+            {
+                return Some((
+                    BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
+                    Some(Box::new(TablePrepared {
+                        kind: TableKind::Multiline,
+                    })),
+                ));
+            }
+
+            if ctx.config.extensions.pipe_tables
+                && try_parse_pipe_table(lines, table_pos, &mut tmp, ctx.config).is_some()
+            {
+                return Some((
+                    BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
+                    Some(Box::new(TablePrepared {
+                        kind: TableKind::Pipe,
+                    })),
+                ));
+            }
+
+            if ctx.config.extensions.simple_tables
+                && try_parse_simple_table(lines, table_pos, &mut tmp, ctx.config).is_some()
+            {
+                return Some((
+                    BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
+                    Some(Box::new(TablePrepared {
+                        kind: TableKind::Simple,
+                    })),
+                ));
+            }
+
+            return None;
+        }
+
+        if ctx.config.extensions.grid_tables
+            && try_parse_grid_table(lines, line_pos, &mut tmp, ctx.config).is_some()
+        {
+            return Some((
+                BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
+                Some(Box::new(TablePrepared {
+                    kind: TableKind::Grid,
+                })),
+            ));
+        }
+
+        if ctx.config.extensions.multiline_tables
+            && try_parse_multiline_table(lines, line_pos, &mut tmp, ctx.config).is_some()
+        {
+            return Some((
+                BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
+                Some(Box::new(TablePrepared {
+                    kind: TableKind::Multiline,
+                })),
+            ));
+        }
+
+        if ctx.config.extensions.pipe_tables
+            && try_parse_pipe_table(lines, line_pos, &mut tmp, ctx.config).is_some()
+        {
+            return Some((
+                BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
+                Some(Box::new(TablePrepared {
+                    kind: TableKind::Pipe,
+                })),
+            ));
+        }
+
+        if ctx.config.extensions.simple_tables
+            && try_parse_simple_table(lines, line_pos, &mut tmp, ctx.config).is_some()
+        {
+            return Some((
+                BlockDetectionResult::Yes,
+                Some(Box::new(TablePrepared {
+                    kind: TableKind::Simple,
+                })),
+            ));
+        }
+
+        // (Optional) Caption-only lookahead without table parse shouldn't match.
+        // The real parsers already handle captions when invoked on the caption line.
+
+        None
+    }
+
+    fn parse(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+    ) -> usize {
+        self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
+    }
+
+    fn parse_prepared(
+        &self,
+        ctx: &BlockContext,
+        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
+        lines: &[&str],
+        line_pos: usize,
+        payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
+    ) -> usize {
+        let prepared = payload.and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<TablePrepared>().copied());
+
+        let table_pos = if ctx.config.extensions.table_captions
+            && is_caption_followed_by_table(lines, line_pos)
+        {
+            // Skip caption continuation lines and one optional blank line.
+            let mut pos = line_pos + 1;
+            while pos < lines.len() && !lines[pos].trim().is_empty() {
+                pos += 1;
+            }
+            if pos < lines.len() && lines[pos].trim().is_empty() {
+                pos += 1;
+            }
+            pos
+        } else {
+            line_pos
+        };
+
+        let try_kind = |kind: TableKind,
+                        builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>|
+         -> Option<usize> {
+            match kind {
+                TableKind::Grid => {
+                    if ctx.config.extensions.grid_tables {
+                        try_parse_grid_table(lines, table_pos, builder, ctx.config)
+                    } else {
+                        None
+                    }
+                }
+                TableKind::Multiline => {
+                    if ctx.config.extensions.multiline_tables {
+                        try_parse_multiline_table(lines, table_pos, builder, ctx.config)
+                    } else {
+                        None
+                    }
+                }
+                TableKind::Pipe => {
+                    if ctx.config.extensions.pipe_tables {
+                        try_parse_pipe_table(lines, table_pos, builder, ctx.config)
+                    } else {
+                        None
+                    }
+                }
+                TableKind::Simple => {
+                    if ctx.config.extensions.simple_tables {
+                        try_parse_simple_table(lines, table_pos, builder, ctx.config)
+                    } else {
+                        None
+                    }
+                }
+            }
+        };
+
+        if let Some(prepared) = prepared {
+            if let Some(n) = try_kind(prepared.kind, builder) {
+                return n;
+            }
+        }
+
+        // Fallback (should be rare) - match core order.
+        if let Some(n) = try_kind(TableKind::Grid, builder) {
+            return n;
+        }
+        if let Some(n) = try_kind(TableKind::Multiline, builder) {
+            return n;
+        }
+        if let Some(n) = try_kind(TableKind::Pipe, builder) {
+            return n;
+        }
+        if let Some(n) = try_kind(TableKind::Simple, builder) {
+            return n;
+        }
+
+        debug_assert!(false, "TableParser::parse called without a matching table");
+        1
+    }
+
+    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
+        "table"
+    }
+}
+

<sub>⏱️ 210m 54s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Fixed the table migration regressions by teaching `TableParser` to properly handle **caption-before-table** detection without duplicating the caption as a paragraph.

Changes:
- `src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs`: `TableParser::detect_prepared()` now detects caption-before-table by probing the *real table start* (after caption + optional blank), but still parses from the caption line; `parse_prepared()` mirrors that by passing `table_pos` into `try_parse_*_table`.
- `src/parser/core.rs`: removed the old table parsing branches (including caption pre-scan) since the dispatcher now owns this.
- `src/parser/blocks/tables.rs`: restored `:` caption support (needed for existing tests + LSP symbol naming).

Validation: `cargo test -q` now passes (all tests).

1. # Plan: Block dispatcher “prepared match” refactor
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Current `BlockParser::can_parse()` often does non-trivial work (e.g. fenced code parses fence + `InfoString::parse`, setext allocates, figure uses inline image parsing). This work is frequently repeated because:
5. - several parsers call `try_parse_*` again inside `parse()`
6. - `core.rs` calls `block_registry.detect(...)` multiple times per line in different branches
7. - `core.rs` separately re-implements “is this a block element marker?” checks (e.g. Definition continuation guard), further duplicating detection.
8. 
9. ## Goal
10. Keep the good part of the refactor (Pandoc-like ordered registry + ability to “prepare/flush buffers before emitting”), while eliminating duplicate detection/parsing and reducing `core.rs` complexity.
11. 
12. ## Approach (high level)
13. Introduce a **prepared match** concept: detection returns a match object that caches any expensive parsing results, and emission consumes that cached data (no re-parsing). Then rework `core.rs` so each line does **one** detection pass, decides whether the match is allowed (blank-line rules / interrupt rules), performs preparation, and emits.
14. 
15. ## Key design decision (to confirm)
16. Two viable representations:
17. 1) **Trait objects + opaque payload** (recommended for minimal churn): registry remains `Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>`. Detection returns a `BlockMatch` containing a parser index + interrupt/blank requirements + `Box<dyn Any>` payload; emission downcasts payload.
18. 2) **Enum registry + typed payload** (slightly faster/cleaner types, but larger refactor): registry becomes `Vec<BlockParserKind>` and match payload becomes an enum.
19. 
20. ## Workplan
21. 
22. ### A. Baseline & safety
23. - [x] Run baseline tests/benchmarks to capture current behavior (`cargo test` at minimum).
24. - [ ] Identify a small set of “high-signal” golden cases to watch while refactoring (fenced code, setext, yaml/hrule ambiguity, refdefs).
25. 
26. ### B. Introduce prepared-match API (non-invasive)
27. - [x] Add new types in `src/parser/block_dispatcher.rs`:
28.   - `PreparedBlockMatch` (parser index + detection + cached payload)
29. - [x] Extend `BlockParserRegistry` with new entrypoints (keep old API temporarily):
30.   - `detect_prepared(...) -> Option<PreparedBlockMatch>`
31.   - `parse_prepared(match, ...) -> usize`
32. 
33. ### C. Convert migrated parsers (start with biggest perf win)
34. - [x] Fenced code: detection parses fence + `InfoString` once; parse reuses cached `FenceInfo`.
35. - [x] ATX heading: detection returns cached level; parse uses it (no second `try_parse_atx_heading`).
36. - [ ] YAML: optionally cache “is yaml not hrule” disambiguation.
37. - [ ] Figure: cache validation span from inline image parse so it isn’t repeated.
38. - [x] Setext: remove allocation in detection (no `Vec`).
39. - [x] Reference definition: cache parse tuple from `try_parse_reference_definition` (payload currently unused by emitter).
40. - [x] Indented code: migrated into dispatcher (uses `has_blank_before_strict`).
41. 
42. ### D. Refactor `core.rs` to “single detect per line”
43. - [x] Create one `BlockContext` per line and reuse it (avoid repeated rebuilds).
44. - [x] Replace the multiple dispatcher detections with one flow per line (detect once, then prepare/emit).
45. - [x] Rework the Definition continuation guard (`is_block_element` at ~1041) to delegate to the registry for migrated blocks (keeps special-cases for def/list/bq/html/tex).
46. - [x] Delegate nested-block marker probes (Definition continuation after blank line) to the registry instead of hardcoding fence/div/hrule checks.
47. - [x] Fix scattered continuation heuristics surfaced by indented-code migration (definition continuation + blank-line keep-open).
48. 
49. Notes:
50. - `BlockContext` no longer stores `&ContainerStack` to avoid long-lived borrows blocking mutation during preparation/emission.
51. 
52. ### E. Consolidate ordering/precedence in the registry
53. - [x] Migrate HTML blocks into the registry (interrupting).
54. - [x] Migrate LaTeX environment blocks into the registry (interrupting).
55. - [x] Migrate line blocks into the registry (interrupting).
56. - [x] Decide fenced-div integration approach (needs container-stack interaction), then migrate (Option 1: dispatcher emits + core mutates containers).
57. - [ ] Move remaining “block start” checks from `core.rs` into the registry progressively (tables, lists, etc.), keeping Pandoc ordering notes.
58. - [ ] Ensure special-case precedence rules stay correct (e.g., YAML vs hrule, setext vs hrule, list vs header edge cases).
59. 
60. ### F. Cleanup
61. - [ ] Remove old `can_parse/parse` API once all migrated blocks use prepared matches.
62. - [ ] Remove redundant detection logic left in `core.rs`.
63. - [x] Consolidate definition/blank-line continuation heuristics into `ContinuationPolicy`.
64. - [ ] Run full test suite + clippy + fmt check.
65. 
66. ## Notes / Constraints
67. - Maintain single-pass, lossless CST emission; prepared matches must not emit to the builder.
68. - Prepared payload should avoid borrowing `ctx.content` directly unless lifetimes are carefully managed; prefer storing indices / owned small strings only when needed.
69. - Performance expectation: dynamic dispatch/downcast overhead is negligible vs string scanning/allocs; eliminating repeated `InfoString::parse` etc. is the main win.
```

</details>

1563. // Setext Heading Parser (position #3)

1566. pub(crate) struct SetextHeadingParser;
1567. 
1568. impl BlockParser for SetextHeadingParser {
1569.     fn can_parse(
1570.         &self,
1571.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1572.         _lines: &[&str],
1573.         _line_pos: usize,
1574.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
1575.         // Setext headings require blank line before (unless at document start)
1576.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
1577.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
1578.         }
1579. 
1580.         // Need next line for lookahead
1581.         let next_line = match ctx.next_line {
1582.             Some(line) => line,
1583.             None => return BlockDetectionResult::No,
1584.         };
1585. 
1586.         // Create lines array for detection function (avoid allocation)
1587.         let lines = [ctx.content, next_line];
1588. 
1589.         // Try to detect setext heading
1590.         if try_parse_setext_heading(&lines, 0).is_some() {
1591.             // Setext headings need blank line before (normal case)
1592.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
1593.         } else {
1594.             BlockDetectionResult::No
1595.         }
1596.     }
1597. 
1598.     fn parse(
1599.         &self,
1600.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1601.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1602.         lines: &[&str],
1603.         pos: usize,
1604.     ) -> usize {
1605.         // Get text line and underline line
1606.         let text_line = lines[pos];
1607.         let underline_line = lines[pos + 1];
1608. 
1609.         // Determine level from underline character (no need to call try_parse again)
1610.         // can_parse() already validated this is a valid setext heading
1611.         let underline_char = underline_line.trim().chars().next().unwrap_or('=');
1612.         let level = if underline_char == '=' { 1 } else { 2 };
1613. 
1614.         // Emit the setext heading
1615.         emit_setext_heading(builder, text_line, underline_line, level, ctx.config);
1616. 
1617.         // Return lines consumed: text line + underline line
1618.         2
1619.     }
1620. 
1621.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
1622.         "setext_heading"
1623.     }
1624. }
1625. 
1626. // ============================================================================
1627. // Block Parser Registry
1628. // ============================================================================
1629. 
1630. /// Registry of block parsers, ordered by priority.
1631. ///
1632. /// This dispatcher tries each parser in order until one succeeds.
1633. /// The ordering follows Pandoc's approach - explicit list order rather
1634. /// than numeric priorities.
1635. pub(crate) struct BlockParserRegistry {
1636.     parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>,
1637. }
1638. 
1639. impl BlockParserRegistry {
1640.     /// Create a new registry with all block parsers.
1641.     ///
1642.     /// Order matters! Parsers are tried in the order listed here.
1643.     /// This follows Pandoc's design where ordering is explicit and documented.
1644.     ///
1645.     /// **Pandoc reference order** (from pandoc/src/Text/Pandoc/Readers/Markdown.hs:487-515):
1646.     /// 1. blanklines (handled separately in our parser)
1647.     /// 2. codeBlockFenced
1648.     /// 3. yamlMetaBlock' ← YAML metadata comes early!
1649.     /// 4. bulletList
1650.     /// 5. divHtml
1651.     /// 6. divFenced
1652.     /// 7. header ← ATX headings
1653.     /// 8. lhsCodeBlock
1654.     /// 9. htmlBlock
1655.     /// 10. table
1656.     /// 11. codeBlockIndented
1657.     /// 12. rawTeXBlock (LaTeX)
1658.     /// 13. lineBlock
1659.     /// 14. blockQuote
1660.     /// 15. hrule ← Horizontal rules come AFTER headers!
1661.     /// 16. orderedList
1662.     /// 17. definitionList
1663.     /// 18. noteBlock (footnotes)
1664.     /// 19. referenceKey ← Reference definitions
1665.     /// 20. abbrevKey
1666.     /// 21. para
1667.     /// 22. plain
1668.     pub fn new() -> Self {
1669.         let parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>> = vec![
1670.             // Match Pandoc's ordering to ensure correct precedence:
1671.             // (0) Pandoc title block (must be at document start).
1672.             Box::new(PandocTitleBlockParser),
1673.             // (2) Fenced code blocks - can interrupt paragraphs!
1674.             Box::new(FencedCodeBlockParser),
1675.             // (3) YAML metadata - before headers and hrules!
1676.             Box::new(YamlMetadataParser),
1677.             // (6) Fenced divs ::: (open/close)
1678.             Box::new(FencedDivCloseParser),
1679.             Box::new(FencedDivOpenParser),
1680.             // (7) Setext headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
1681.             // Must come before ATX to properly handle `---` disambiguation
1682.             Box::new(SetextHeadingParser),
1683.             // (7) ATX headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
1684.             Box::new(AtxHeadingParser),
1685.             // (9) HTML blocks
1686.             Box::new(HtmlBlockParser),
1687.             // (12) LaTeX environment blocks
1688.             Box::new(LatexEnvironmentParser),
1689.             // (10) Tables
1690.             Box::new(TableParser),
1691.             // (13) Line blocks
1692.             Box::new(LineBlockParser),
1693.             // (11) Indented code blocks (AFTER fenced!)
1694.             Box::new(IndentedCodeBlockParser),
1695.             // (15) Horizontal rules - AFTER headings per Pandoc
1696.             Box::new(HorizontalRuleParser),
1697.             // Figures (standalone images) - Pandoc doesn't have these
1698.             Box::new(FigureParser),
1699.             // (19) Reference definitions
1700.             Box::new(ReferenceDefinitionParser),
1701.             // TODO: Migrate remaining blocks in Pandoc order:
1702.             // - (4-6) Lists and divs (bulletList, divHtml)
1703.             // - (10) Tables (grid, multiline, pipe, simple) (migrated)
1704.             // - (16) Ordered lists
1705.             // - (17) Definition lists
1706.             // - (18) Footnote definitions (noteBlock)
1707.         ];
1708. 
1709.         Self { parsers }
1710.     }
1711. 
1712.     /// Try to parse a block using the registered parsers.
1713.     ///
1714.     /// This method implements the two-phase parsing:
1715.     /// 1. Detection: Check if any parser can handle this content
1716.     /// 2. Caller prepares (closes paragraphs, flushes buffers)
1717.     /// 3. Parser emits the block
1718.     ///
1719.     /// Returns (parser_index, detection_result) if a parser can handle this,
1720.     /// or None if no parser matched.
1721.     #[allow(dead_code)]
1722.     pub fn detect(
1723.         &self,
1724.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1725.         lines: &[&str],
1726.         line_pos: usize,
1727.     ) -> Option<(usize, BlockDetectionResult)> {
1728.         for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
1729.             let result = parser.can_parse(ctx, lines, line_pos);
1730.             match result {
1731.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes | BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
1732.                     log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
1733.                     return Some((i, result));
1734.                 }
1735.                 BlockDetectionResult::No => continue,
1736.             }
1737.         }
1738.         None
1739.     }
1740. 
1741.     /// Like `detect()`, but allows parsers to return cached payload for emission.
1742.     pub fn detect_prepared(
1743.         &self,
1744.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1745.         lines: &[&str],
1746.         line_pos: usize,
1747.     ) -> Option<PreparedBlockMatch> {
1748.         for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
1749.             if let Some((detection, payload)) = parser.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos) {
1750.                 log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
1751.                 return Some(PreparedBlockMatch {
1752.                     parser_index: i,
1753.                     detection,
1754.                     effect: parser.effect(),
1755.                     payload,
```

</details>

1293.     fn parse_prepared(
1294.         &self,
1295.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1296.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1297.         lines: &[&str],
1298.         line_pos: usize,
1299.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
1300.     ) -> usize {
1301.         use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
1302. 
1303.         let div_fence = payload
1304.             .and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<DivFenceInfo>())
1305.             .cloned()
1306.             .or_else(|| try_parse_div_fence_open(ctx.content));
1307. 
1308.         let Some(div_fence) = div_fence else {
1309.             return 1;
1310.         };
1311. 
1312.         // Start FENCED_DIV node (container push happens in core based on `effect`).
1313.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::FENCED_DIV.into());
1314. 
1315.         // Emit opening fence with attributes as child node to avoid duplication.
1316.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_FENCE_OPEN.into());
1317. 
1318.         // Use full original line to preserve indentation and newline.
1319.         let full_line = lines[line_pos];
1320.         let line_no_bq = strip_n_blockquote_markers(full_line, ctx.blockquote_depth);
1321.         let trimmed = line_no_bq.trim_start();
1322. 
1323.         // Leading whitespace
1324.         let leading_ws_len = line_no_bq.len() - trimmed.len();
1325.         if leading_ws_len > 0 {
1326.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), &line_no_bq[..leading_ws_len]);
1327.         }
1328. 
1329.         // Fence colons
1330.         let fence_str: String = ":".repeat(div_fence.fence_count);
1331.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), &fence_str);
1332. 
1333.         // Everything after colons
1334.         let after_colons = &trimmed[div_fence.fence_count..];
1335.         let (content_before_newline, newline_str) = strip_newline(after_colons);
1336. 
1337.         // Optional space before attributes
1338.         let has_leading_space = content_before_newline.starts_with(' ');
1339.         if has_leading_space {
1340.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
1341.         }
1342. 
1343.         let content_after_space = if has_leading_space {
1344.             &content_before_newline[1..]
1345.         } else {
1346.             content_before_newline
1347.         };
1348. 
1349.         // Attributes
1350.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::DIV_INFO.into());
1351.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), &div_fence.attributes);
1352.         builder.finish_node();
1353. 
1354.         // Trailing colons (symmetric fences)
1355.         let (trailing_space, trailing_colons) = if div_fence.attributes.starts_with('{') {
1356.             if let Some(close_idx) = content_after_space.find('}') {
1357.                 let after_attrs = &content_after_space[close_idx + 1..];
1358.                 let trailing = after_attrs.trim_start();
1359.                 let space_count = after_attrs.len() - trailing.len();
1360.                 if !trailing.is_empty() && trailing.chars().all(|c| c == ':') {
1361.                     (space_count > 0, trailing)
1362.                 } else {
1363.                     (false, "")
1364.                 }
1365.             } else {
1366.                 (false, "")
1367.             }
1368.         } else {
1369.             let after_attrs = &content_after_space[div_fence.attributes.len()..];
1370.             if let Some(after_space) = after_attrs.strip_prefix(' ') {
1371.                 if !after_space.is_empty() && after_space.chars().all(|c| c == ':') {
1372.                     (true, after_space)
1373.                 } else {
1374.                     (false, "")
1375.                 }
1376.             } else {
1377.                 (false, "")
1378.             }
1379.         };
1380. 
1381.         if trailing_space {
1382.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::WHITESPACE.into(), " ");
1383.         }
1384.         if !trailing_colons.is_empty() {
1385.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), trailing_colons);
1386.         }
1387. 
1388.         if !newline_str.is_empty() {
1389.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), newline_str);
1390.         }
1391. 
1392.         builder.finish_node(); // DIV_FENCE_OPEN
1393. 
1394.         1
1395.     }
1396. 
1397.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
1398.         "fenced_div_open"
1399.     }
1400. }

<sub>⏱️ 751m 29s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Exploring blockquote migration**

I’m thinking about the blockquote parsing and how it currently uses a container stack. It's tricky, but maybe we can treat it as an effect with OpenBlockQuote/Close. The user wants to migrate the remaining blocks, specifically block_quotes to the dispatcher, so I’ll search for blockquote parsing in the core and existing blocks module. I need to keep my response short, so I’ll explore the current module and see how to implement a BlockQuoteParser in the dispatcher.**Assessing blockquote handling**

I'm evaluating if blockquotes can work with containers. It seems easier to maintain the core's container logic but detect if a line starts with `\>` for blockquote identification. The dispatcher contract expects parse() to return consumed lines and emit nodes directly. Since blockquotes require nested parsing, I could keep blockquote handling in the core for now and prioritize migrating lists instead. However, I could consider modularizing blockquote handling, similar to fenced divs, while focusing on per-line markers.**Considering footnote migration**

I’m thinking that migrating the core parse loop might be tough, so I should consider moving OrderedList, DefinitionList, and Footnote definitions instead. Footnote definitions could utilize `try_parse_footnote_marker` for handling in the core, or perhaps I can move them to the dispatcher. I need to check the module `src/parser/blocks/footnotes.rs` and look for the structure around footnote handling to see if it can manage interruptions and blank lines. Next, I’ll decide whether to migrate `FootnoteDefinitionParser` and how to parse those into `FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION` nodes.**Proceeding with tool calls**

I’m thinking about the next steps, so I’ll start with the tool calls: using glob to identify block files and grep to locate the footnote definitions. I also plan to examine the relevant sections in the core to gather more context. After gathering this information, I’ll be in a better position to decide how to proceed with the migration. I’ll do these tasks in parallel to save time!*

````
1. //! Reference definition and footnote parsing functions.
2. //!
3. //! Reference definitions have the form:
4. //! ```markdown
5. //! [label]: url "optional title"
6. //! [label]: url 'optional title'
7. //! [label]: url (optional title)
8. //! [label]: <url> "title"
9. //! ```
10. //!
11. //! Footnote definitions have the form:
12. //! ```markdown
13. //! [^id]: Footnote content here.
14. //!     Can continue on multiple lines
15. //!     as long as they're indented.
16. //! ```
17. 
18. /// Try to parse a reference definition starting at the current position.
19. /// Returns Some((length, label, url, title)) if successful.
20. ///
21. /// Syntax:
22. /// ```markdown
23. /// [label]: url "title"
24. /// [label]: <url> 'title'
25. /// [label]: url
26. ///          (title on next line)
27. /// ```
28. pub fn try_parse_reference_definition(
29.     text: &str,
30. ) -> Option<(usize, String, String, Option<String>)> {
31.     let bytes = text.as_bytes();
32. 
33.     // Must start at beginning of line with [
34.     if bytes.is_empty() || bytes[0] != b'[' {
35.         return None;
36.     }
37. 
38.     // Check if it's a footnote definition [^id]: - not a reference definition
39.     if bytes.len() >= 2 && bytes[1] == b'^' {
40.         return None;
41.     }
42. 
43.     // Find the closing ] for the label
44.     let mut pos = 1;
45.     let mut escape_next = false;
46. 
47.     while pos < bytes.len() {
48.         if escape_next {
49.             escape_next = false;
50.             pos += 1;
51.             continue;
52.         }
53. 
54.         match bytes[pos] {
55.             b'\\' => {
56.                 escape_next = true;
57.                 pos += 1;
58.             }
59.             b']' => {
60.                 break;
61.             }
62.             b'\n' => {
63.                 // Labels can't span lines
64.                 return None;
65.             }
66.             _ => {
67.                 pos += 1;
68.             }
69.         }
70.     }
71. 
72.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b']' {
73.         return None;
74.     }
75. 
76.     let label = &text[1..pos];
77.     if label.is_empty() {
78.         return None;
79.     }
80. 
81.     pos += 1; // Skip ]
82. 
83.     // Must be followed by :
84.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b':' {
85.         return None;
86.     }
87.     pos += 1;
88. 
89.     // Skip whitespace
90.     while pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[pos], b' ' | b'\t') {
91.         pos += 1;
92.     }
93. 
94.     // Parse URL
95.     let url_start = pos;
96.     let url_end;
97. 
98.     // Check for angle-bracketed URL <url>
99.     if pos < bytes.len() && bytes[pos] == b'<' {
100.         pos += 1;
101.         let url_content_start = pos;
102.         // Find closing >
103.         while pos < bytes.len() && bytes[pos] != b'>' && bytes[pos] != b'\n' && bytes[pos] != b'\r'
104.         {
105.             pos += 1;
106.         }
107.         if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b'>' {
108.             return None;
109.         }
110.         url_end = pos;
111.         let url = text[url_content_start..url_end].to_string();
112.         pos += 1; // Skip >
113. 
114.         // Parse optional title
115.         let title = parse_title(text, bytes, &mut pos)?;
116. 
117.         Some((pos, label.to_string(), url, title))
118.     } else {
119.         // Parse unbracketed URL (until whitespace or newline)
120.         while pos < bytes.len() && !matches!(bytes[pos], b' ' | b'\t' | b'\n' | b'\r') {
121.             pos += 1;
122.         }
123. 
124.         url_end = pos;
125.         if url_start == url_end {
126.             return None; // No URL found
127.         }
128. 
129.         let url = text[url_start..url_end].to_string();
130. 
131.         // Parse optional title
132.         let title = parse_title(text, bytes, &mut pos)?;
133. 
134.         Some((pos, label.to_string(), url, title))
135.     }
136. }
137. 
138. /// Parse an optional title after the URL.
139. /// Titles can be in double quotes, single quotes, or parentheses.
140. /// Returns Some(Some(title)) if title found, Some(None) if no title, None if malformed.
141. fn parse_title(text: &str, bytes: &[u8], pos: &mut usize) -> Option<Option<String>> {
142.     let base_pos = *pos;
143. 
144.     // Skip whitespace (including newlines for multi-line titles)
145.     while *pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[*pos], b' ' | b'\t' | b'\n' | b'\r') {
146.         *pos += 1;
147.     }
148. 
149.     // Check if there's a title
150.     if *pos >= bytes.len() {
151.         return Some(None);
152.     }
153. 
154.     let quote_char = bytes[*pos];
155.     if !matches!(quote_char, b'"' | b'\'' | b'(') {
156.         // No title, that's okay
157.         *pos = base_pos; // Reset position
158.         return Some(None);
159.     }
160. 
161.     let closing_char = if quote_char == b'(' { b')' } else { quote_char };
162. 
163.     *pos += 1; // Skip opening quote
164.     let title_start = *pos;
165. 
166.     // Find closing quote
167.     let mut escape_next = false;
168.     while *pos < bytes.len() {
169.         if escape_next {
170.             escape_next = false;
171.             *pos += 1;
172.             continue;
173.         }
174. 
175.         match bytes[*pos] {
176.             b'\\' => {
177.                 escape_next = true;
178.                 *pos += 1;
179.             }
180.             c if c == closing_char => {
181.                 let title_end = *pos;
182.                 *pos += 1; // Skip closing quote
183. 
184.                 // Skip trailing whitespace to end of line
185.                 while *pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[*pos], b' ' | b'\t') {
186.                     *pos += 1;
187.                 }
188. 
189.                 // Extract title from the original text using correct indices
190.                 let title = text[title_start..title_end].to_string();
191.                 return Some(Some(title));
192.             }
193.             b'\n' if quote_char == b'(' => {
194.                 // Parenthetical titles can span lines
195.                 *pos += 1;
196.             }
197.             _ => {
198.                 *pos += 1;
199.             }
200.         }
201.     }
202. 
203.     // No closing quote found
204.     None
205. }
206. 
207. /// Try to parse just the footnote marker [^id]: from a line.
208. /// Returns Some((id, content_start_col)) if the line starts with a footnote marker.
209. ///
210. /// Syntax:
211. /// ```markdown
212. /// [^id]: Footnote content.
213. /// ```
214. pub fn try_parse_footnote_marker(line: &str) -> Option<(String, usize)> {
215.     let bytes = line.as_bytes();
216. 
217.     // Must start with [^
218.     if bytes.len() < 4 || bytes[0] != b'[' || bytes[1] != b'^' {
219.         return None;
220.     }
221. 
222.     // Find the closing ] for the ID
223.     let mut pos = 2;
224.     while pos < bytes.len() && bytes[pos] != b']' && bytes[pos] != b'\n' && bytes[pos] != b'\r' {
225.         pos += 1;
226.     }
227. 
228.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b']' {
229.         return None;
230.     }
231. 
232.     let id = &line[2..pos];
233.     if id.is_empty() {
234.         return None;
235.     }
236. 
237.     pos += 1; // Skip ]
238. 
239.     // Must be followed by :
240.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b':' {
241.         return None;
242.     }
243.     pos += 1;
244. 
245.     // Skip spaces/tabs until content (or end of line)
246.     while pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[pos], b' ' | b'\t') {
247.         pos += 1;
248.     }
249. 
250.     Some((id.to_string(), pos))
251. }
````

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 752m 8s</sub>

***Planning migration steps**

I need to outline the next steps for the migration process. The user mentioned to keep pushing forward. I’m thinking we'll focus on migrating footnote definitions next since they have a specific handling case. From the Pandoc order, footnote definitions come after some other blocks. There are also fenced divs pending to discuss, but we’ve already integrated those. My main task now is to work on implementing BlockParser for the FootnoteDefinition in the dispatcher.**Considering footnote parsing logic**

Footnote parsing is pretty tricky since it's closely tied to container management and indentation. Right now, the core has custom logic to handle footnote definitions by opening a FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION node and managing content with specific rules. It seems best to keep this container approach in the core while letting the dispatcher manage the initial detection and emission of the opening marker and first line. There’s a potential idea to extend BlockEffect for footnote handling, which could work. I need to ensure I close the previous footnote container too when making these changes.**Structuring footnote handling**

I need to manage core behavior for handling footnote definitions, especially ensuring that any open footnotes are closed before starting a new one. It’s a bit different from how fenced divs work, as I can't rely on a specific close marker. The effect should guide the core to close previous footnotes if they’re open. I might implement an OpenFootnoteDefinition effect in the core, ensuring we close previous footnotes before parsing to prevent nesting. I’ll need to figure out the best way to incorporate this into the preparation and effect handling processes.**Exploring footnote migration**

The user has indicated they want to keep moving forward with more blocks migrated, even though I was thinking about making the smallest changes. I plan to implement footnote definitions using a dispatcher parser while keeping the core logic to a minimum. When there's a match, the core will prepare and call parse_prepared to emit the footnote definition node along with the first line. I'll need to check how the continuation for footnote containers works, so I'll search the core for Container::FootnoteDefinition handling and reuse existing code where I can.*

90. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
91. pub(crate) enum BlockDetectionResult {
92.     /// Can parse this block, requires blank line before
93.     Yes,
94. 
95.     /// Can parse this block and can interrupt paragraphs (no blank line needed)
96.     #[allow(dead_code)]
97.     YesCanInterrupt,
98. 
99.     /// Cannot parse this content
100.     No,
101. }
102. 
103. /// A prepared (cached) detection result.
104. ///
105. /// This allows expensive detection logic (e.g., fence parsing) to be performed once,
106. /// while emission happens only after the caller prepares (flushes buffers/closes paragraphs).
107. pub(crate) struct PreparedBlockMatch {
108.     pub parser_index: usize,
109.     pub detection: BlockDetectionResult,
110.     pub effect: BlockEffect,
111.     pub payload: Option<Box<dyn Any>>,
112. }
113. 
114. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
115. pub(crate) enum BlockEffect {
116.     None,
117.     OpenFencedDiv,
118.     CloseFencedDiv,
119. }
120. 
121. /// Trait for block-level parsers.
122. ///
123. /// Each block type implements this trait with a two-phase approach:
124. /// 1. Detection: Can this block type parse this content? (lightweight, no emission)
125. /// 2. Parsing: Actually parse and emit the block to the builder (called after preparation)
126. ///
127. /// This separation allows the caller to:
128. /// - Prepare for block elements (close paragraphs, flush buffers) BEFORE emission
129. /// - Handle blocks that can interrupt paragraphs vs those that need blank lines
130. /// - Maintain correct CST node ordering
131. ///
132. /// Note: This is purely organizational - the trait doesn't introduce
133. /// backtracking or multiple passes. Each parser operates during the
134. /// single forward pass through the document.
135. pub(crate) trait BlockParser {
136.     /// Detect if this parser can handle the content (no emission).
137.     fn can_parse(
138.         &self,
139.         ctx: &BlockContext,
140.         lines: &[&str],
141.         line_pos: usize,
142.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult;
143. 
144.     fn effect(&self) -> BlockEffect {
145.         BlockEffect::None
146.     }
147. 
148.     /// Prepared detection hook.
149.     ///
150.     /// Default implementation just calls `can_parse()` and returns no payload.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 752m 56s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Evaluating footnote logic**

I’m really diving into the footnote logic here. The container stack doesn’t seem to include a Paragraph container, which could lead to issues with subsequent indented lines starting new paragraphs. I see that within the footnote definition, the first line content stays open for continuation lines. Since footnote continuation lines are indented, they could be treated as part of the same paragraph, which relies on proper indentation handling. I’m considering whether to treat the first line as standalone or part of the same paragraph.**Analyzing footnote continuation**

I'm considering how footnote continuation lines work, particularly their indentation and how they interact with paragraph formatting. The core's total indentation stripping treats indented lines as normal lines, but paragraph continuation can create a new paragraph if needed. I’m wondering if subsequent indented lines should append to the original. In Pandoc, continuation lines with the same indentation indeed become part of the footnote. We need to keep the paragraph open, so the container stack must track its state after the first line. It seems like the BlockParser might need adjustments.**Exploring footnote container manipulation**

I'm thinking about how to use effects for container manipulation after parsing, especially with footnotes. After opening a footnote container, we could check if the first line has content and then start a paragraph container to append that content. The block parser might emit a FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION start with the marker token and necessary payload data. The core could then push the Container::FootnoteDefinition and begin a paragraph if there’s inline content. This approach aligns with earlier core logic, allowing for effective paragraph handling while migrating to a dispatcher. We’ll need a new effect variant for this.**Examining footnote definition handling**

I’m considering how to update the handling of footnote definitions. We need to add a match for OpenFootnoteDefinition to close existing definitions, push a new container, and possibly append content. But handling effects is tricky since it currently occurs after parsing. The old core closed the paragraph before starting the footnote, so we must determine if footnote definitions can interrupt paragraphs. The old code allowed footnotes to interrupt without requiring a blank line, but I'm not sure about that. I think detection should indicate if footnotes can interrupt, perhaps as YesCanInterrupt.**Constructing footnote detection logic**

I’m thinking it’s best to preserve old behavior by treating footnotes as YesCanInterrupt, allowing parsing without requiring a blank line. In the current dispatching logic, blocks marked as YesCanInterrupt will close the paragraph if there's no blank line. We can implement detection to always return YesCanInterrupt but ensure that footnotes take precedence over reference definitions. I’ll need to check how content is passed to the context after stripping indentations. The parse preparation should start FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION nodes while also managing marker text and indentation properly. I'll examine the old core code for insights on handling these cases.*

<sub>⏱️ 754m 4s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Considering migration strategy**

I'm thinking about minimizing changes for now, maybe we should keep footnote definitions managed in the core while we migrate lists. The user said to keep going. Should I work on ordered lists or bullet lists next? Both seem to require a heavy container stack. Maybe blockquotes are the next step since they also involve that stack. It looks like I need to decide on keeping container-manipulating blocks outside the dispatcher while migrating “leaf” blocks. The user wants everything migrated eventually, so I can consider a hybrid approach where the dispatcher can detect these blocks and manage them with existing logic.**Planning footnote migration**

I'm considering the user's "keep going" request and the previous notes on fenced divs, which we already have integrated. The next items on my to-do list are bullet and ordered lists, definition lists, and footnote definitions. I wonder if I could migrate footnote definition detection into the dispatcher while keeping the parsing in core. I might add a parser to return an effect like OpenFootnoteDefinition, with a payload to avoid re-parsing. But, hmm, I should ensure BlockParserRegistry doesn't directly parse but returns something useful. I'll need to find a way to handle this effectively.**Structuring footnote definitions**

I'm exploring how to handle both footnote definitions and container pushes in parsing. For footnote definitions, I realize the parsing needs to happen before the container push. The process would involve starting a node with builder.start_node(FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION) and having the core push the container. As the builder stays inside the node, any inner content will become children of that node. This means parse_prepared should start the node but not finish it, letting the core finalize when closing the container, which aligns with how other container types work.**Outlining footnote definition handling**

I’m realizing that the footnote definition block can follow a specific pattern: the parser will start the FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION node and emit the marker tokens, but it won’t finish that node. The core will push the Container::FootnoteDefinition, keeping the node open until the container is popped. I still need to parse the first line's inline content after the marker, which may require a container stack. I’m considering deferring the inline parsing, treating the remainder of the marker line as a plain TEXT token inside FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION. However, tests might expect inline parsing, so I'll quickly check the cst for the reference_footnotes case.*

17. [^3]: Third footnote here.

19. [^bold]: Footnote after bold text.
20. 
21. [^code]: Footnote after code span.
22. 
23. [^multi]: This is a multi-line footnote
24.     that continues on the next line.
25. 
26.     It can even have multiple paragraphs
27.     with proper indentation.
28. 
29.     - And lists
30.     - With multiple items
31. 
32.     All indented by 4 spaces.
33. 
34. [^longnote]: Here's one with multiple blocks.
35. 
36.     Subsequent paragraphs are indented to show that they
37. belong to the previous footnote.
38. 
39.         { some.code }
40. 
41.     The whole paragraph can be indented, or just the first
42.     line.  In this way, multi-paragraph footnotes work like
43.     multi-paragraph list items.
44. 
45. This paragraph won't be part of the note, because it
46. isn't indented.
47.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 754m 34s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Migrating content blocks**

I'm working on migrating blocks and weighing whether to handle footnote definitions or lists next. I see that according to the plan, there should be a discussion about fenced divs after dealing with HTML, LaTeX, or line items, but it looks like fenced divs are already integrated into the workflow. The next step for Pandoc is to manage bullet lists and HTML blocks. I’ve realized that footnote definitions could be pushed to the dispatcher to reduce special cases and improve registry ordering. This might need a new BlockEffect variant for OpenFootnoteDefinition.**Managing footnote definitions**

I need to ensure that I close previous footnotes and open new nodes during the migration process. One option is to implement a FootnoteDefinitionParser that outputs a FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION node, but it seems like parse doesn’t have container access. Maybe I should tell the core to push the container based on some payload. Introducing a BlockEffect::OpenFootnoteDefinition could work, using PreparedBlockMatch to manage content starts. In the core, I could close any previous footnote containers before opening new ones to keep things minimal. After migrating, I'll streamline footnote handling through the registry.**Implementing FootnoteDefinitionParser**

I see that according to Pandoc's order, the noteBlock should come before the referenceKey, so I need to position the FootnoteDefinitionParser before the ReferenceDefinitionParser in the new() function. That sounds good! Now, to create the FootnoteDefinitionParser, I need to check if footnotes are enabled in the config. If the context doesn't have the footnotes extension, I should search for it in the config extensions using grep. Let's get that structured out clearly!*

---

1680.         _line_pos: usize,
1681.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
1682.         // Setext headings require blank line before (unless at document start)
1683.         if !ctx.has_blank_before && !ctx.at_document_start {
1684.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
1685.         }
1686. 
1687.         // Need next line for lookahead
1688.         let next_line = match ctx.next_line {
1689.             Some(line) => line,
1690.             None => return BlockDetectionResult::No,
1691.         };
1692. 
1693.         // Create lines array for detection function (avoid allocation)
1694.         let lines = [ctx.content, next_line];
1695. 
1696.         // Try to detect setext heading
1697.         if try_parse_setext_heading(&lines, 0).is_some() {
1698.             // Setext headings need blank line before (normal case)
1699.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
1700.         } else {
1701.             BlockDetectionResult::No
1702.         }
1703.     }
1704. 
1705.     fn parse(
1706.         &self,
1707.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1708.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1709.         lines: &[&str],
1710.         pos: usize,
1711.     ) -> usize {
1712.         // Get text line and underline line
1713.         let text_line = lines[pos];
1714.         let underline_line = lines[pos + 1];
1715. 
1716.         // Determine level from underline character (no need to call try_parse again)
1717.         // can_parse() already validated this is a valid setext heading
1718.         let underline_char = underline_line.trim().chars().next().unwrap_or('=');
1719.         let level = if underline_char == '=' { 1 } else { 2 };
1720. 
1721.         // Emit the setext heading
1722.         emit_setext_heading(builder, text_line, underline_line, level, ctx.config);
1723. 
1724.         // Return lines consumed: text line + underline line
1725.         2
```

1737. /// Registry of block parsers, ordered by priority.
1738. ///
1739. /// This dispatcher tries each parser in order until one succeeds.
1740. /// The ordering follows Pandoc's approach - explicit list order rather
1741. /// than numeric priorities.
1742. pub(crate) struct BlockParserRegistry {
1743.     parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>,
1744. }
1745. 
1746. impl BlockParserRegistry {
1747.     /// Create a new registry with all block parsers.
1748.     ///
1749.     /// Order matters! Parsers are tried in the order listed here.
1750.     /// This follows Pandoc's design where ordering is explicit and documented.
1751.     ///
1752.     /// **Pandoc reference order** (from pandoc/src/Text/Pandoc/Readers/Markdown.hs:487-515):
1753.     /// 1. blanklines (handled separately in our parser)
1754.     /// 2. codeBlockFenced
1755.     /// 3. yamlMetaBlock' ← YAML metadata comes early!
1756.     /// 4. bulletList
1757.     /// 5. divHtml
1758.     /// 6. divFenced
1759.     /// 7. header ← ATX headings
1760.     /// 8. lhsCodeBlock
1761.     /// 9. htmlBlock
1762.     /// 10. table
1763.     /// 11. codeBlockIndented
1764.     /// 12. rawTeXBlock (LaTeX)
1765.     /// 13. lineBlock
1766.     /// 14. blockQuote
1767.     /// 15. hrule ← Horizontal rules come AFTER headers!
1768.     /// 16. orderedList
1769.     /// 17. definitionList
1770.     /// 18. noteBlock (footnotes)
1771.     /// 19. referenceKey ← Reference definitions
1772.     /// 20. abbrevKey
1773.     /// 21. para
1774.     /// 22. plain

1775.     pub fn new() -> Self {
1776.         let parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>> = vec![
1777.             // Match Pandoc's ordering to ensure correct precedence:
1778.             // (0) Pandoc title block (must be at document start).
1779.             Box::new(PandocTitleBlockParser),

<sub>⏱️ 756m 10s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Considering migration adjustments**

The user wants me to keep going with the migrations, but we broke tests before, so we need a minimal fix to restore behavior. One option is to revert the footnote definition migration by removing the FootnoteDefinitionParser and reintroducing the core logic for footnote ignoring. That could be a small tweak to get back on track. However, to properly implement footnotes in the dispatcher, we might need a new BlockEffect variant and modifications in core handling. It’s a bigger task, but doable!**Revising node handling for footnotes**

The old core started a node and kept it open until the container was later closed in close_containers. This means that ContainerStack::close_containers_to knows how to handle FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION nodes. I need to ensure that in parse_prepared, I start the node and emit a marker and perhaps the first line of content, but keep it open by pushing the container in core later. This approach mirrors how we handle fenced divs, where the node stays open until closed. For footnotes, the same logic applies.**Improving node management in the parser**

In the current parser, parse_prepared returns a usize, which doesn't allow me to indicate that a node is left open. However, it doesn't need to call finish_node(). For fenced divs, the parser starts FENCED_DIV and DIV_FENCE_OPEN without finishing the FENCED_DIV, which is fine. I can apply the same principle to footnotes by starting FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION in parse_prepared and not finishing it. The core will then push Container::FootnoteDefinition when the footnote ends. To handle this, new BlockEffect variants like OpenFootnoteDefinition could be useful, incorporating content_col in the payload for core pushing.**Enhancing footnote handling in parsing**

I see that we can downcast in effect handling, which is a bit messy but could work as a minimal solution. Alternatively, I might keep BlockEffect::None and detect footnotes in the core using block_match.name, but that isn't ideal since the core doesn't know that yet. Adding new BlockEffect variants like OpenFootnoteDefinition and CloseFootnoteDefinition could be a better path. We also need to ensure footnote definitions take precedence over reference definitions and allow them to interrupt paragraphs without requiring a blank line. We'll remove any outdated core logic regarding footnote definitions once the new parser is functioning properly.**Implementing footnote parsing**

I need to work on the footnote parser's parse_prepared to replicate the old functionality. The container stack expects a FootnoteDefinition variant. When closing, it finishes the FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION node, which was previously constructed in the core. I plan to implement FootnoteDefinitionParser in block_dispatcher.rs, storing a payload with necessary content. It's crucial to ensure first line content is treated like paragraph content and to manage indentation correctly.

I might have to leave the first line content to the core for processing, in a way that traditionally handled paragraphs, without complicating the emission logic. This could help us maintain expected output and behavior in line with prior implementations.**Clarifying footnote content handling**

Footnote definitions should consume one line, including the marker line. The core effect handling will then append the first line content, but I've realized we advance the position after returning true from the main loop. Since the position is incremented after effect handling, we can append to the builder using the content substring from the original content. I need to consider how indentation and markers are handled, especially in list contexts. 

For footnotes, it seems I'll need to ensure proper handling of marker text and newline preservation, adjusting as needed based on the core's processing rules. To be precise, I'll inspect how the content is defined earlier in the core.*

<sub>⏱️ 757m 3s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Evaluating footnote parser issues**

I’ve added a footnote parser, but it only handles the first line and doesn’t manage continuation indentation or stack properly. This is causing LSP tests to fail since footnote definitions aren’t parsed as multiple blocks correctly. It seems the dispatcher is outputting only the current line, which confuses subsequent indented lines, treating them like regular paragraphs. I need to integrate the footnote parser properly into the ContainerStack to maintain its context. There’s a lot to adjust for a clean solution!**Refining footnote handling**

I’m considering extending BlockEffect to include StartFootnoteDefinition with the content column. This would involve the core pushing a FootnoteDefinition container and the builder node starting as before. I realize the footnote definition wraps its content and closes automatically, with the closing logic likely handled by the container stack.

The dispatcher should ease the creation of this node and push the container via the effect, similar to how a fenced div works. I need to figure out storing the effect since Copy is currently an issue; it might require some restructuring, which I find a bit messy!**Updating BlockEffect for footnotes**

I'm thinking it’s better to extend BlockEffect to include OpenFootnoteDefinition and store content_col directly in the payload. Perhaps I can set content_col as a constant value of 4 since it's always that way. This would allow the core to push Container::FootnoteDefinition with content_col fixed at 4 and maintain the continuation behavior.

I need to ensure that the builder node starts correctly and that the first line content is processed via the paragraphs module. The overall behavior should mimic how the core operates, especially how it handles content inside the footnote definition container.**Addressing dispatcher limitations**

I need the dispatcher parse to function like the old core: starting a node, emitting a marker token, and pushing the container. If the first line isn't empty, it should call paragraphs::start_paragraph_if_needed. However, this requires access to the ContainerStack, which block parsers currently lack. This poses a significant issue because the dispatcher can only manage builder and context, not containers beyond the effect.

To keep things minimal, I could leave footnote handling in the core, but the user prefers to continue migrating the rest. Maybe footnotes need a deeper integration where the dispatcher can handle detection while the core manages container pushes and paragraph creation.**Revising the footnote parser approach**

I'm considering a different method for the footnote parser. It might emit the FOOTNOTE_REFERENCE marker token first, and then the core could handle the remainder. One option is splitting the parsing into two stages: first, emitting the start node and marker, and then allowing the core to manage the container and first line content by appending it to a paragraph buffer.

I need to ensure the tokens stay in the correct order, possibly keeping it simple by emitting only the FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION start node initially. Also, I should check how the paragraphs module handles inline parsing, especially for single-line paragraphs versus more complex structures.*

```
1. //! Paragraph handling utilities.
2. //!
3. //! Note: Most paragraph logic is in the main Parser since paragraphs
4. //! are tightly integrated with container handling.
5. 
6. use crate::config::Config;
7. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
8. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
9. 
10. use crate::parser::utils::container_stack::{Container, ContainerStack};
11. use crate::parser::utils::text_buffer::ParagraphBuffer;
12. 
13. /// Start a paragraph if not already in one.
14. pub(in crate::parser) fn start_paragraph_if_needed(
15.     containers: &mut ContainerStack,
16.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
17. ) {
18.     if !matches!(containers.last(), Some(Container::Paragraph { .. })) {
19.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::PARAGRAPH.into());
20.         containers.push(Container::Paragraph {
21.             buffer: ParagraphBuffer::new(),
22.         });
23.     }
24. }
25. 
26. /// Append a line to the current paragraph (preserving losslessness).
27. pub(in crate::parser) fn append_paragraph_line(
28.     containers: &mut ContainerStack,
29.     _builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
30.     line: &str,
31.     _config: &Config,
32. ) {
33.     // Buffer the line (with newline for losslessness)
34.     // Works for ALL paragraphs including those in blockquotes
35.     if let Some(Container::Paragraph { buffer }) = containers.stack.last_mut() {
36.         buffer.push_text(line);
37.     }
38. }
39. 
40. /// Buffer a blockquote marker in the current paragraph.
41. ///
42. /// Called when processing blockquote continuation lines while a paragraph is open
43. /// and using integrated inline parsing. The marker will be emitted at the correct
44. /// position when the paragraph is closed.
45. pub(in crate::parser) fn append_paragraph_marker(
46.     containers: &mut ContainerStack,
47.     leading_spaces: usize,
48.     has_trailing_space: bool,
49. ) {
50.     if let Some(Container::Paragraph { buffer }) = containers.stack.last_mut() {
51.         buffer.push_marker(leading_spaces, has_trailing_space);
52.     }
53. }
54. 
55. /// Get the current content column from the container stack.
56. pub(in crate::parser) fn current_content_col(containers: &ContainerStack) -> usize {
57.     containers
58.         .stack
59.         .iter()
60.         .rev()
61.         .find_map(|c| match c {
62.             Container::ListItem { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
63.             Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
64.             _ => None,
65.         })
66.         .unwrap_or(0)
67. }
```

</details>

1. use super::list_item_buffer::ListItemBuffer;
2. use super::text_buffer::{ParagraphBuffer, TextBuffer};
3. use crate::parser::blocks::lists::ListMarker;
4. 
5. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
6. pub(crate) enum Container {
7.     BlockQuote {
8.         // No special tracking needed
9.     },
10.     FencedDiv {
11.         // No special tracking needed - closed by fence marker
12.     },
13.     List {
14.         marker: ListMarker,
15.         base_indent_cols: usize,
16.         has_blank_between_items: bool, // Track if list is loose (blank lines between items)
17.     },
18.     ListItem {
19.         content_col: usize,
20.         buffer: ListItemBuffer, // Buffer for list item content
21.     },
22.     DefinitionList {
23.         // Definition lists don't need special tracking
24.     },
25.     DefinitionItem {
26.         // No special tracking needed
27.     },
28.     Definition {
29.         content_col: usize,
30.         plain_open: bool,
31.         #[allow(dead_code)] // Will be used in Subtask 4
32.         plain_buffer: TextBuffer, // Buffer for accumulating PLAIN content
33.     },
34.     Paragraph {
35.         buffer: ParagraphBuffer, // Interleaved buffer for paragraph content with markers
36.     },
37.     FootnoteDefinition {
38.         content_col: usize,
39.     },
40. }
41. 
42. pub(crate) struct ContainerStack {
43.     pub(crate) stack: Vec<Container>,
44. }
45. 
46. impl ContainerStack {
47.     pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
48.         Self { stack: Vec::new() }
49.     }
50. 
51.     pub(crate) fn depth(&self) -> usize {
52.         self.stack.len()
53.     }
54. 
55.     pub(crate) fn last(&self) -> Option<&Container> {
56.         self.stack.last()
57.     }
58. 
59.     pub(crate) fn push(&mut self, c: Container) {
60.         self.stack.push(c);
61.     }
62. }
63. 
64. /// Expand tabs to columns (tab stop = 4) and return (cols, byte_offset).
65. pub(crate) fn leading_indent(line: &str) -> (usize, usize) {
66.     let mut cols = 0usize;
67.     let mut bytes = 0usize;
68.     for b in line.bytes() {
69.         match b {
70.             b' ' => {
71.                 cols += 1;
72.                 bytes += 1;
73.             }
74.             b'\t' => {
75.                 cols += 4 - (cols % 4);
76.                 bytes += 1;
77.             }
78.             _ => break,
79.         }
80.     }
81.     (cols, bytes)
82. }
83. 
84. /// Return byte index at a given column (tabs = 4).
85. pub(crate) fn byte_index_at_column(line: &str, target_col: usize) -> usize {
86.     let mut col = 0usize;
87.     let mut idx = 0usize;
88.     for (i, b) in line.bytes().enumerate() {
89.         if col >= target_col {
90.             return idx;
91.         }
92.         match b {
93.             b' ' => {
94.                 col += 1;
95.                 idx = i + 1;
96.             }
97.             b'\t' => {
98.                 col += 4 - (col % 4);
99.                 idx = i + 1;
100.             }
101.             _ => break,
102.         }
103.     }
104.     idx
105. }
```

</details>

55.     pub fn parse(mut self) -> SyntaxNode {

66.     /// Emit buffered PLAIN content if Definition container has open PLAIN.
67.     /// Close containers down to `keep`, emitting buffered content first.
68.     fn close_containers_to(&mut self, keep: usize) {
69.         // Emit buffered PARAGRAPH/PLAIN content before closing
70.         while self.containers.depth() > keep {
71.             match self.containers.stack.last() {
72.                 // Handle ListItem with buffering
73.                 Some(Container::ListItem { buffer, .. }) if !buffer.is_empty() => {
74.                     // Clone buffer to avoid borrow issues
75.                     let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
76. 
77.                     log::debug!(
78.                         "Closing ListItem with buffer (is_empty={}, segment_count={})",
79.                         buffer_clone.is_empty(),
80.                         buffer_clone.segment_count()
81.                     );
82. 
83.                     // Determine if this should be Plain or PARAGRAPH:
84.                     // 1. Check if parent LIST has blank lines between items (list-level loose)
85.                     // 2. OR check if this item has blank lines within its content (item-level loose)
86.                     let parent_list_is_loose = self
87.                         .containers
88.                         .stack
89.                         .iter()
90.                         .rev()
91.                         .find_map(|c| match c {
92.                             Container::List {
93.                                 has_blank_between_items,
94.                                 ..
95.                             } => Some(*has_blank_between_items),
96.                             _ => None,
97.                         })
98.                         .unwrap_or(false);
99. 
100.                     let use_paragraph =
101.                         parent_list_is_loose || buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content();
102. 
103.                     log::debug!(
104.                         "Emitting ListItem buffer: use_paragraph={} (parent_list_is_loose={}, item_has_blanks={})",
105.                         use_paragraph,
106.                         parent_list_is_loose,
107.                         buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content()
108.                     );
109. 
110.                     // Pop container first
111.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
112.                     // Emit buffered content as Plain or PARAGRAPH
113.                     buffer_clone.emit_as_block(&mut self.builder, use_paragraph, self.config);
114.                     self.builder.finish_node(); // Close LIST_ITEM
115.                 }
116.                 // Handle ListItem without content
117.                 Some(Container::ListItem { .. }) => {
118.                     log::debug!("Closing empty ListItem (no buffer content)");
119.                     // Just close normally (empty list item)
120.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
```

<sub>⏱️ 758m 36s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Evaluating footnote migration**

I tried migrating footnote definitions to the dispatcher, but I implemented a too simplistic parse that doesn't push the Container::FootnoteDefinition. This caused failures in subsequent tests because indented lines weren't stripped. So, I need to make footnote a container effect, not just emit nodes. The dispatcher architecture currently supports effects only for fenced div features, and I’m thinking of extending BlockEffect to include OpenFootnoteDefinition, but I should be careful with how we handle closing previous footnote containers.**Refining footnote emission**

I need to handle the emission of footnote markers and the first line similarly to the old logic, using paragraph buffering and the container stack for multi-line content and nested blocks. It feels more efficient to reuse old logic instead of creating something new in the dispatcher. In parse_prepared, we should emit the FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION and FOOTNOTE_REFERENCE tokens, then push the container with content_col. However, the dispatcher doesn’t have access to the ContainerStack, so I've got to manage that in the core based on the effect.**Deciding on footnote parsing**

I have a few options for handling footnote parsing. First, I could keep it in the core instead of moving it to the dispatcher. Alternatively, I might expand the BlockParser trait to access the container stack, but that seems too complicated. Another idea is to have the footnote parser effect trigger a specialized hook in core to build the footnote container and first line content. Given the constraints and recent test failures, the simplest approach could be to revert the footnote migration and keep processing in core. However, it's worth exploring other blocks for migration too.**Moving forward with footnote migration**

I don't want to lose progress, so I think we can still implement footnote migration correctly with minimal changes by extending effects and core. I noticed that the container stack has a FootnoteDefinition variant, but currently, we're getting warnings about it never being constructed since we removed the core logic. 

My approach is to move footnote detection to the dispatcher while keeping the paragraph buffering in core. The FootnoteDefinitionParser should handle detection and return a payload that includes marker_end and possibly marker_text. We need to ensure that the original line text is preserved for losslessness, just like it was done before.**Refining footnote implementation**

I realize that the content parameter is stripped_content after removing container indent. For footnotes at the top level, this is fine, but it may differ inside a blockquote or list. Footnote definitions likely work within those as well, which means we should maintain indentation to properly emit marker_text.

When dealing with the CST, I noticed it expects FOOTNOTE_REFERENCE tokens to maintain specific formatting, including indentation. It’s crucial to preserve original line text for losslessness, which means carefully managing leading whitespace. To simplify this process, I think it might be best to revert changes and keep parsing within core for now while integrating some of the new logic.**Implementing footnote definitions**

I need to ensure that marker_text captures both the marker and any spaces following it, but not the newline. The first_line_content must include the newline from the original content. The parse_prepared function should use ctx.content to generate the marker and first line values, while the previous logic focused on handling stripped_content carefully.

I'll begin the FootnoteDefinitionParser implementation by creating the payload that includes marker_end and potentially content_start for the first line. The function should initiate the FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION node, emit the FOOTNOTE_REFERENCE token from ctx.content, and manage any associated paragraph states on the core side. We must ensure existing footnote containers are closed before starting a new one to prevent nesting issues. I might need to adjust the sequence so that we close the old containers prior to the parse_prepared call for footnotes.**Refining footnote logic**

I’m thinking about adjusting the effect handling to ensure we close old footnote containers before calling parse_prepared. This approach reflects the past logic where previous containers were closed prior to starting a new footnote node. I’ll implement a "pre_effect" stage, specifically for OpenFootnoteDefinition.

I'll also create a BlockEffect enum to manage this. Before calling parse_prepared, if the effect matches OpenFootnoteDefinition, I need to ensure any currently open footnote containers are closed. After that, parse_prepared will start the FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION node and handle first line content for paragraphs appropriately, ensuring that indentation rules are respected to close containers at the right time. I might need to dive deeper into the parse_line function to clarify when footnote containers will close under indentation conditions.*

847.         } else {
848.             (content, None)
