569.         Some((
570.             detection,
571.             Some(Box::new(FootnotePrepared {
572.                 marker_end,
573.             })),
574.         ))
575.     }
576. 
577.     fn parse(
578.         &self,
579.         ctx: &BlockContext,
580.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
581.         lines: &[&str],
582.         line_pos: usize,
583.     ) -> usize {
584.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
585.     }
586. 
587.     fn parse_prepared(
588.         &self,
589.         ctx: &BlockContext,
590.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
591.         lines: &[&str],
592.         line_pos: usize,
593.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
594.     ) -> usize {
595.         use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
596. 
597.         let marker_end = payload
598.             .and_then(|p| p.downcast_ref::<FootnotePrepared>())
599.             .map(|p| p.marker_end)
600.             .or_else(|| try_parse_footnote_marker(ctx.content).map(|(_, end)| end))
601.             .unwrap_or(ctx.content.len());
602. 
603.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION.into());
604. 
605.         let full_line = lines[line_pos];
606.         let (content_without_newline, line_ending) = strip_newline(full_line);
607. 
608.         let marker_text = &content_without_newline[..marker_end.min(content_without_newline.len())];
609.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_REFERENCE.into(), marker_text);
610. 
611.         let first_line_content = &content_without_newline[marker_end.min(content_without_newline.len())..];
612.         if !first_line_content.trim().is_empty() {
613.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::PARAGRAPH.into());
614.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), first_line_content);
615.             if !line_ending.is_empty() {
616.                 builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
617.             }
618.             builder.finish_node();
619.         } else if !line_ending.is_empty() {
620.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::NEWLINE.into(), line_ending);
621.         }
622. 
623.         builder.finish_node();
624. 
625.         1
626.     }
627. 
628.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
629.         "footnote_definition"
630.     }
631. }
632. 
633. /// Reference definition parser ([label]: url "title")
634. pub(crate) struct ReferenceDefinitionParser;
635. 
636. impl BlockParser for ReferenceDefinitionParser {
637.     fn effect(&self) -> BlockEffect {
638.         BlockEffect::None
639.     }
640. 
641.     fn can_parse(
642.         &self,
643.         ctx: &BlockContext,
644.         lines: &[&str],
645.         line_pos: usize,
646.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
647.         self.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos)
648.             .map(|(d, _)| d)
649.             .unwrap_or(BlockDetectionResult::No)
650.     }
651. 
652.     fn detect_prepared(
653.         &self,
654.         ctx: &BlockContext,
655.         _lines: &[&str],
656.         _line_pos: usize,
657.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
658.         // Parse once and cache for emission.
659.         let parsed = try_parse_reference_definition(ctx.content)?;
660.         Some((BlockDetectionResult::Yes, Some(Box::new(parsed))))
661.     }
662. 
663.     fn parse(
664.         &self,
665.         ctx: &BlockContext,
666.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
667.         lines: &[&str],
668.         line_pos: usize,
669.     ) -> usize {
670.         self.parse_prepared(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos, None)
671.     }
672. 
673.     fn parse_prepared(
674.         &self,
675.         _ctx: &BlockContext,
676.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
677.         lines: &[&str],
678.         line_pos: usize,
679.         payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
680.     ) -> usize {
```

<sub>⏱️ 760m 8s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Inspecting footnote handling**

In Pandoc, a footnote starts with the line `[^id]:`, and its content continues if indented with four spaces or if subsequent lines carry that indentation. This structure resembles a Definition container. It seems that the ContainerStack in our parser likely manages closing based on indentation and blank lines. To confirm this, I should take a look at container_stack.rs and see how it's utilized, especially focusing on FootnoteDefinition.*

---

1. use super::list_item_buffer::ListItemBuffer;
2. use super::text_buffer::{ParagraphBuffer, TextBuffer};
3. use crate::parser::blocks::lists::ListMarker;
4. 
5. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
6. pub(crate) enum Container {
7.     BlockQuote {
8.         // No special tracking needed
9.     },
10.     FencedDiv {
11.         // No special tracking needed - closed by fence marker
12.     },
13.     List {
14.         marker: ListMarker,
15.         base_indent_cols: usize,
16.         has_blank_between_items: bool, // Track if list is loose (blank lines between items)
17.     },
18.     ListItem {
19.         content_col: usize,
20.         buffer: ListItemBuffer, // Buffer for list item content
21.     },
22.     DefinitionList {
23.         // Definition lists don't need special tracking
24.     },
25.     DefinitionItem {
26.         // No special tracking needed
27.     },
28.     Definition {
29.         content_col: usize,
30.         plain_open: bool,
31.         #[allow(dead_code)] // Will be used in Subtask 4
32.         plain_buffer: TextBuffer, // Buffer for accumulating PLAIN content
33.     },
34.     Paragraph {
35.         buffer: ParagraphBuffer, // Interleaved buffer for paragraph content with markers
36.     },
37.     FootnoteDefinition {
38.         content_col: usize,
39.     },
40. }
41. 
42. pub(crate) struct ContainerStack {
43.     pub(crate) stack: Vec<Container>,
44. }
45. 
46. impl ContainerStack {
47.     pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
48.         Self { stack: Vec::new() }
49.     }
50. 
51.     pub(crate) fn depth(&self) -> usize {
52.         self.stack.len()
53.     }
54. 
55.     pub(crate) fn last(&self) -> Option<&Container> {
56.         self.stack.last()
57.     }
58. 
59.     pub(crate) fn push(&mut self, c: Container) {
60.         self.stack.push(c);
61.     }
62. }
63. 
64. /// Expand tabs to columns (tab stop = 4) and return (cols, byte_offset).
65. pub(crate) fn leading_indent(line: &str) -> (usize, usize) {
66.     let mut cols = 0usize;
67.     let mut bytes = 0usize;
68.     for b in line.bytes() {
69.         match b {
70.             b' ' => {
71.                 cols += 1;
72.                 bytes += 1;
73.             }
74.             b'\t' => {
75.                 cols += 4 - (cols % 4);
76.                 bytes += 1;
77.             }
78.             _ => break,
79.         }
80.     }
81.     (cols, bytes)
82. }
83. 
84. /// Return byte index at a given column (tabs = 4).
85. pub(crate) fn byte_index_at_column(line: &str, target_col: usize) -> usize {
86.     let mut col = 0usize;
87.     let mut idx = 0usize;
88.     for (i, b) in line.bytes().enumerate() {
89.         if col >= target_col {
90.             return idx;
91.         }
92.         match b {
93.             b' ' => {
94.                 col += 1;
95.                 idx = i + 1;
96.             }
97.             b'\t' => {
98.                 col += 4 - (col % 4);
99.                 idx = i + 1;
100.             }
101.             _ => break,
102.         }
103.     }
104.     idx
105. }
```

1. //! Continuation/blank-line handling policy.
2. //!
3. //! This module centralizes the parser's "should this line continue an existing container?"
4. //! logic (especially across blank lines). Keeping this logic in one place reduces the
5. //! risk of scattered ad-hoc heuristics diverging as blocks move into the dispatcher.
6. 
7. use crate::config::Config;
8. 
9. use crate::parser::block_dispatcher::{BlockContext, BlockParserRegistry};
10. use crate::parser::blocks::blockquotes::{count_blockquote_markers, strip_n_blockquote_markers};
11. use crate::parser::blocks::{definition_lists, html_blocks, latex_envs, lists};
12. use crate::parser::utils::container_stack::{ContainerStack, byte_index_at_column, leading_indent};
13. 
14. pub(crate) struct ContinuationPolicy<'a, 'cfg> {
15.     config: &'cfg Config,
16.     block_registry: &'a BlockParserRegistry,
17. }
18. 
19. impl<'a, 'cfg> ContinuationPolicy<'a, 'cfg> {
20.     pub(crate) fn new(config: &'cfg Config, block_registry: &'a BlockParserRegistry) -> Self {
21.         Self {
22.             config,
23.             block_registry,
24.         }
25.     }
26. 
27.     /// Registry-based "does this look like the start of some nested block structure" probe.
28.     ///
29.     /// Important: this is intended for *blank-line keep-open decisions*, so it uses
30.     /// `has_blank_before_strict = false` to avoid treating indented code blocks as nested.
31.     pub(crate) fn has_nested_block_structure(&self, content: &str) -> bool {
32.         let block_ctx = BlockContext {
33.             content,
34.             has_blank_before: true,
35.             // For blank-line container-keep decisions we do NOT want indented code blocks
36.             // to count as “nested structure” (that would keep definitions open incorrectly).
37.             has_blank_before_strict: false,
38.             at_document_start: false,
39.             in_fenced_div: false,
40.             blockquote_depth: 0,
41.             config: self.config,
42.             content_indent: 0,
43.             list_indent_info: None,
44.             next_line: None,
45.         };
46. 
47.         // We intentionally pass empty `lines` here so lookahead-sensitive blocks (e.g. setext)
48.         // won't count as nested structure for blank-line keep-open decisions.
49.         self.block_registry
50.             .detect_prepared(&block_ctx, &[], 0)
51.             .is_some()
52.     }
53. 
54.     pub(crate) fn compute_levels_to_keep(
55.         &self,
56.         current_bq_depth: usize,
57.         containers: &ContainerStack,
58.         next_line: &str,
59.     ) -> usize {
60.         let (next_bq_depth, next_inner) = count_blockquote_markers(next_line);
61.         let (raw_indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(next_inner);
62.         let next_marker = lists::try_parse_list_marker(next_inner, self.config);
63. 
64.         // `current_bq_depth` is used for proper indent calculation when the next line
65.         // increases blockquote nesting.
66. 
67.         let mut keep_level = 0;
68.         let mut content_indent_so_far = 0usize;
69. 
70.         // First, account for blockquotes
71.         for (i, c) in containers.stack.iter().enumerate() {
72.             match c {
73.                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::BlockQuote { .. } => {
74.                     let bq_count = containers.stack[..=i]
75.                         .iter()
76.                         .filter(|x| {
77.                             matches!(
78.                                 x,
79.                                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::BlockQuote { .. }
80.                             )
81.                         })
82.                         .count();
83.                     if bq_count <= next_bq_depth {
84.                         keep_level = i + 1;
85.                     }
86.                 }
87.                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::FootnoteDefinition {
88.                     content_col,
89.                     ..
90.                 } => {
91.                     content_indent_so_far += *content_col;
92.                     let min_indent = (*content_col).max(4);
93.                     if raw_indent_cols >= min_indent {
94.                         keep_level = i + 1;
95.                     }
96.                 }
97.                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::Definition {
98.                     content_col,
99.                     ..
100.                 } => {
101.                     let min_indent = (*content_col).max(4);
102.                     if raw_indent_cols >= min_indent {
103.                         let after_content_indent = if raw_indent_cols >= content_indent_so_far {
104.                             let idx = byte_index_at_column(next_line, content_indent_so_far);
105.                             &next_line[idx..]
106.                         } else {
107.                             next_line
108.                         };
109. 
110.                         let has_definition_marker =
111.                             definition_lists::try_parse_definition_marker(after_content_indent)
112.                                 .is_some();
113.                         let has_list_marker =
114.                             lists::try_parse_list_marker(after_content_indent, self.config)
115.                                 .is_some();
116.                         let has_block_structure = has_list_marker
117.                             || count_blockquote_markers(after_content_indent).0 > 0
118.                             || self.has_nested_block_structure(after_content_indent);
119. 
120.                         if !has_definition_marker && has_block_structure {
121.                             keep_level = i + 1;
122.                         }
123.                     }
124.                 }
125.                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::List {
126.                     marker,
127.                     base_indent_cols,
128.                     ..
129.                 } => {
130.                     let effective_indent = raw_indent_cols.saturating_sub(content_indent_so_far);
131.                     let continues_list = if let Some((ref nm, _, _)) = next_marker {
132.                         lists::markers_match(marker, nm) && effective_indent <= base_indent_cols + 3
133.                     } else {
134.                         let item_content_col = containers
135.                             .stack
136.                             .get(i + 1)
137.                             .and_then(|c| match c {
138.                                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::ListItem {
139.                                     content_col,
140.                                     ..
141.                                 } => Some(*content_col),
142.                                 _ => None,
143.                             })
144.                             .unwrap_or(1);
145.                         effective_indent >= item_content_col
146.                     };
147.                     if continues_list {
148.                         keep_level = i + 1;
149.                     }
150.                 }
151.                 crate::parser::utils::container_stack::Container::ListItem {
152.                     content_col, ..
153.                 } => {
154.                     let effective_indent = if next_bq_depth > current_bq_depth {
155.                         let after_current_bq =
156.                             strip_n_blockquote_markers(next_line, current_bq_depth);
157.                         let (spaces_before_next_marker, _) = leading_indent(after_current_bq);
158.                         spaces_before_next_marker.saturating_sub(content_indent_so_far)
159.                     } else {
160.                         raw_indent_cols.saturating_sub(content_indent_so_far)
161.                     };
162. 
163.                     let is_new_item_at_outer_level = if next_marker.is_some() {
164.                         effective_indent < *content_col
165.                     } else {
166.                         false
167.                     };
168. 
169.                     if !is_new_item_at_outer_level && effective_indent >= *content_col {
170.                         keep_level = i + 1;
171.                     }
172.                 }
173.                 _ => {}
174.             }
175.         }
176. 
177.         keep_level
178.     }
179. 
180.     /// Checks whether a line inside a definition should be treated as a plain continuation
181.     /// (and buffered into the definition PLAIN), rather than parsed as a new block.
182.     pub(crate) fn definition_plain_can_continue(
183.         &self,
184.         stripped_content: &str,
185.         raw_content: &str,
186.         content_indent: usize,
187.         block_ctx: &BlockContext,
188.         lines: &[&str],
189.         pos: usize,
190.     ) -> bool {
191.         // A blank line that isn't indented to the definition content column ends the definition.
192.         let (indent_cols, _) = leading_indent(raw_content);
193.         if raw_content.trim().is_empty() && indent_cols < content_indent {
194.             return false;
195.         }
196. 
197.         // If it's a block element marker, don't continue as plain.
198.         if definition_lists::try_parse_definition_marker(stripped_content).is_some() {
199.             return false;
200.         }
201.         if lists::try_parse_list_marker(stripped_content, self.config).is_some() {
202.             return false;
203.         }
204.         if count_blockquote_markers(stripped_content).0 > 0 {
205.             return false;
206.         }
207.         if self.config.extensions.raw_html
208.             && html_blocks::try_parse_html_block_start(stripped_content).is_some()
209.         {
210.             return false;
211.         }
212.         if self.config.extensions.raw_tex
213.             && latex_envs::try_parse_latex_env_begin(stripped_content).is_some()
214.         {
215.             return false;
216.         }
217. 
218.         !self
219.             .block_registry
220.             .detect_prepared(block_ctx, lines, pos)
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 760m 46s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Addressing footnote migration**

I need to tackle the issue of footnote migration. Right now, the FootnoteDefinitionParser in the dispatcher emits a footnote marker token and maybe a paragraph with text, but it doesn't push the container. Previously, the core managed footnote definitions by starting a node and pushing a Container::FootnoteDefinition. Since that logic was removed, tests are failing. I need to reintroduce container semantics, possibly using BlockEffect::OpenFootnoteDefinition, although the effect enum currently only includes None, OpenFencedDiv, and CloseFencedDiv. I should check the block_dispatcher.rs effect enum next.*

1. //! Block parser dispatcher for organizing block-level parsing.
2. //!
3. //! This module provides a trait-based abstraction for block parsers,
4. //! making it easier to add new block types and reducing duplication in parse_inner_content.
5. //!
6. //! Design principles:
7. //! - Single-pass parsing preserved (no backtracking)
8. //! - Each block parser operates independently
9. //! - Inline parsing still integrated (called from within block parsing)
10. //! - Maintains exact CST structure and losslessness
11. 
12. use crate::config::Config;
13. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
14. use std::any::Any;
15. 
16. use super::blocks::blockquotes::strip_n_blockquote_markers;
17. use super::blocks::code_blocks::{
18.     CodeBlockType, FenceInfo, InfoString, parse_fenced_code_block, try_parse_fence_open,
19. };
20. use super::blocks::fenced_divs::{DivFenceInfo, is_div_closing_fence, try_parse_div_fence_open};
21. use super::blocks::figures::parse_figure;
22. use super::blocks::headings::{
23.     emit_atx_heading, emit_setext_heading, try_parse_atx_heading, try_parse_setext_heading,
24. };
25. use super::blocks::horizontal_rules::{emit_horizontal_rule, try_parse_horizontal_rule};
26. use super::blocks::html_blocks::{HtmlBlockType, parse_html_block, try_parse_html_block_start};
27. use super::blocks::indented_code::{is_indented_code_line, parse_indented_code_block};
28. use super::blocks::latex_envs::{LatexEnvInfo, parse_latex_environment, try_parse_latex_env_begin};
29. use super::blocks::line_blocks::{parse_line_block, try_parse_line_block_start};
30. use super::blocks::lists::try_parse_list_marker;
31. use super::blocks::metadata::{try_parse_pandoc_title_block, try_parse_yaml_block};
32. use super::blocks::reference_links::{try_parse_footnote_marker, try_parse_reference_definition};
33. use super::blocks::tables::{
34.     is_caption_followed_by_table, try_parse_grid_table, try_parse_multiline_table,
35.     try_parse_pipe_table, try_parse_simple_table,
36. };
37. use super::inlines::links::try_parse_inline_image;
38. use super::utils::container_stack::byte_index_at_column;
39. use super::utils::helpers::strip_newline;
40. 
41. /// Information about list indentation context.
42. ///
43. /// Used by block parsers that need to handle indentation stripping
44. /// when parsing inside list items (e.g., fenced code blocks).
45. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
46. pub(crate) struct ListIndentInfo {
47.     /// Number of columns to strip for list content
48.     pub content_col: usize,
49. }
50. 
51. /// Context passed to block parsers for decision-making.
52. ///
53. /// Contains immutable references to parser state that block parsers need
54. /// to check conditions (e.g., blank line before, blockquote depth, etc.).
55. pub(crate) struct BlockContext<'a> {
56.     /// Current line content (after blockquote markers stripped if any)
57.     pub content: &'a str,
58. 
59.     /// Whether there was a blank line before this line (relaxed, container-aware)
60.     pub has_blank_before: bool,
61. 
62.     /// Whether there was a strict blank line before this line (no container exceptions)
63.     pub has_blank_before_strict: bool,
64. 
65.     /// Whether we're currently inside a fenced div (container-owned state)
66.     pub in_fenced_div: bool,
67. 
68.     /// Whether we're at document start (pos == 0)
69.     pub at_document_start: bool,
70. 
71.     /// Current blockquote depth
72.     pub blockquote_depth: usize,
73. 
74.     /// Parser configuration
75.     pub config: &'a Config,
76. 
77.     // NOTE: we intentionally do not store `&ContainerStack` here to avoid
78.     // long-lived borrows of `self` in the main parser loop.
79.     /// Base indentation from container context (footnotes, definitions)
80.     pub content_indent: usize,
81. 
82.     /// List indentation info if inside a list
83.     pub list_indent_info: Option<ListIndentInfo>,
84. 
85.     /// Next line content for lookahead (used by setext headings)
86.     pub next_line: Option<&'a str>,
87. }
88. 
89. /// Result of detecting whether a block can be parsed.
90. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
91. pub(crate) enum BlockDetectionResult {
92.     /// Can parse this block, requires blank line before
93.     Yes,
94. 
95.     /// Can parse this block and can interrupt paragraphs (no blank line needed)
96.     #[allow(dead_code)]
97.     YesCanInterrupt,
98. 
99.     /// Cannot parse this content
100.     No,
101. }
102. 
103. /// A prepared (cached) detection result.
104. ///
105. /// This allows expensive detection logic (e.g., fence parsing) to be performed once,
106. /// while emission happens only after the caller prepares (flushes buffers/closes paragraphs).
107. pub(crate) struct PreparedBlockMatch {
108.     pub parser_index: usize,
109.     pub detection: BlockDetectionResult,
110.     pub effect: BlockEffect,
111.     pub payload: Option<Box<dyn Any>>,
112. }
113. 
114. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
115. pub(crate) enum BlockEffect {
116.     None,
117.     OpenFencedDiv,
118.     CloseFencedDiv,
119. }
120. 
121. /// Trait for block-level parsers.
122. ///
123. /// Each block type implements this trait with a two-phase approach:
124. /// 1. Detection: Can this block type parse this content? (lightweight, no emission)
125. /// 2. Parsing: Actually parse and emit the block to the builder (called after preparation)
126. ///
127. /// This separation allows the caller to:
128. /// - Prepare for block elements (close paragraphs, flush buffers) BEFORE emission
129. /// - Handle blocks that can interrupt paragraphs vs those that need blank lines
130. /// - Maintain correct CST node ordering
131. ///
132. /// Note: This is purely organizational - the trait doesn't introduce
133. /// backtracking or multiple passes. Each parser operates during the
134. /// single forward pass through the document.
135. pub(crate) trait BlockParser {
136.     /// Detect if this parser can handle the content (no emission).
137.     fn can_parse(
138.         &self,
139.         ctx: &BlockContext,
140.         lines: &[&str],
141.         line_pos: usize,
142.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult;
143. 
144.     fn effect(&self) -> BlockEffect {
145.         BlockEffect::None
146.     }
147. 
148.     /// Prepared detection hook.
149.     ///
150.     /// Default implementation just calls `can_parse()` and returns no payload.
151.     fn detect_prepared(
152.         &self,
153.         ctx: &BlockContext,
154.         lines: &[&str],
155.         line_pos: usize,
156.     ) -> Option<(BlockDetectionResult, Option<Box<dyn Any>>)> {
157.         let detection = self.can_parse(ctx, lines, line_pos);
158.         match detection {
159.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes | BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
160.                 Some((detection, None))
161.             }
162.             BlockDetectionResult::No => None,
163.         }
164.     }
165. 
166.     /// Parse and emit this block type to the builder.
167.     fn parse(
168.         &self,
169.         ctx: &BlockContext,
170.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
171.         lines: &[&str],
172.         line_pos: usize,
173.     ) -> usize;
174. 
175.     /// Prepared parse hook.
176.     ///
177.     /// Default implementation ignores payload and calls `parse()`.
178.     fn parse_prepared(
179.         &self,
180.         ctx: &BlockContext,
181.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
182.         lines: &[&str],
183.         line_pos: usize,
184.         _payload: Option<&dyn Any>,
185.     ) -> usize {
186.         self.parse(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos)
187.     }
188. 
189.     /// Name of this block parser (for debugging/logging)
190.     fn name(&self) -> &'static str;
191. }
192. 
193. // ============================================================================
194. // Concrete Block Parser Implementations
195. // ============================================================================
196. 
197. /// Horizontal rule parser
198. pub(crate) struct HorizontalRuleParser;
199. 
200. impl BlockParser for HorizontalRuleParser {
201.     fn can_parse(
202.         &self,
203.         ctx: &BlockContext,
204.         _lines: &[&str],
205.         _line_pos: usize,
206.     ) -> BlockDetectionResult {
207.         // Must have blank line before
208.         if !ctx.has_blank_before {
209.             return BlockDetectionResult::No;
210.         }
211. 
212.         // Check if this looks like a horizontal rule
213.         if try_parse_horizontal_rule(ctx.content).is_some() {
214.             BlockDetectionResult::Yes
215.         } else {
216.             BlockDetectionResult::No
217.         }
218.     }
219. 
220.     fn parse(
```

56.         #[cfg(debug_assertions)]

58.             init_logger();

60. 
61.         self.parse_document_stack();
62. 
63.         SyntaxNode::new_root(self.builder.finish())

65. 
66.     /// Emit buffered PLAIN content if Definition container has open PLAIN.
67.     /// Close containers down to `keep`, emitting buffered content first.
68.     fn close_containers_to(&mut self, keep: usize) {
69.         // Emit buffered PARAGRAPH/PLAIN content before closing
70.         while self.containers.depth() > keep {
71.             match self.containers.stack.last() {
72.                 // Handle ListItem with buffering
73.                 Some(Container::ListItem { buffer, .. }) if !buffer.is_empty() => {
74.                     // Clone buffer to avoid borrow issues
75.                     let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
76. 
77.                     log::debug!(
78.                         "Closing ListItem with buffer (is_empty={}, segment_count={})",
79.                         buffer_clone.is_empty(),
80.                         buffer_clone.segment_count()
81.                     );
82. 
83.                     // Determine if this should be Plain or PARAGRAPH:
84.                     // 1. Check if parent LIST has blank lines between items (list-level loose)
85.                     // 2. OR check if this item has blank lines within its content (item-level loose)
86.                     let parent_list_is_loose = self
87.                         .containers
88.                         .stack
89.                         .iter()
90.                         .rev()
91.                         .find_map(|c| match c {
92.                             Container::List {
93.                                 has_blank_between_items,
94.                                 ..
95.                             } => Some(*has_blank_between_items),
96.                             _ => None,
97.                         })
98.                         .unwrap_or(false);
99. 
100.                     let use_paragraph =
101.                         parent_list_is_loose || buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content();
102. 
103.                     log::debug!(
104.                         "Emitting ListItem buffer: use_paragraph={} (parent_list_is_loose={}, item_has_blanks={})",
105.                         use_paragraph,
106.                         parent_list_is_loose,
107.                         buffer_clone.has_blank_lines_between_content()
108.                     );
109. 
110.                     // Pop container first
111.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
112.                     // Emit buffered content as Plain or PARAGRAPH
113.                     buffer_clone.emit_as_block(&mut self.builder, use_paragraph, self.config);
114.                     self.builder.finish_node(); // Close LIST_ITEM
115.                 }
116.                 // Handle ListItem without content
117.                 Some(Container::ListItem { .. }) => {
118.                     log::debug!("Closing empty ListItem (no buffer content)");
119.                     // Just close normally (empty list item)
120.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
121.                     self.builder.finish_node();
122.                 }
123.                 // Handle Paragraph with buffering
124.                 Some(Container::Paragraph { buffer }) if !buffer.is_empty() => {
125.                     // Clone buffer to avoid borrow issues
126.                     let buffer_clone = buffer.clone();
127.                     // Pop container first
128.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
129.                     // Emit buffered content with inline parsing (handles markers)
130.                     buffer_clone.emit_with_inlines(&mut self.builder, self.config);
131.                     self.builder.finish_node();
132.                 }
133.                 // Handle Paragraph without content
134.                 Some(Container::Paragraph { .. }) => {
135.                     // Just close normally
136.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
137.                     self.builder.finish_node();
138.                 }
139.                 // Handle Definition with buffered PLAIN
140.                 Some(Container::Definition {
141.                     plain_open: true,
142.                     plain_buffer,
143.                     ..
144.                 }) if !plain_buffer.is_empty() => {
145.                     // Emit PLAIN node with buffered inline-parsed content
146.                     self.builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::PLAIN.into());
147.                     let text = plain_buffer.get_accumulated_text();
148.                     inline_emission::emit_inlines(&mut self.builder, &text, self.config);
149.                     self.builder.finish_node();
150. 
151.                     // Mark PLAIN as closed and clear buffer
152.                     if let Some(Container::Definition {
153.                         plain_open,
154.                         plain_buffer,
155.                         ..
156.                     }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
157.                     {
158.                         plain_buffer.clear();
159.                         *plain_open = false;
160.                     }
161. 
162.                     // Pop container and finish node
163.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
164.                     self.builder.finish_node();
165.                 }
166.                 // Handle Definition with PLAIN open but empty buffer
167.                 Some(Container::Definition {
168.                     plain_open: true, ..
169.                 }) => {
170.                     // Mark PLAIN as closed
171.                     if let Some(Container::Definition {
172.                         plain_open,
173.                         plain_buffer,
174.                         ..
175.                     }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
176.                     {
177.                         plain_buffer.clear();
178.                         *plain_open = false;
179.                     }
180. 
181.                     // Pop container and finish node
182.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
183.                     self.builder.finish_node();
184.                 }
185.                 // All other containers
186.                 _ => {
187.                     self.containers.stack.pop();
188.                     self.builder.finish_node();
189.                 }
190.             }
191.         }
192.     }
193. 
194.     /// Emit buffered PLAIN content if there's an open PLAIN in a Definition.
195.     /// This is used when we need to close PLAIN but keep the Definition container open.
196.     fn emit_buffered_plain_if_needed(&mut self) {
197.         // Check if we have an open PLAIN with buffered content

214.             plain_open,
215.             plain_buffer,
216.             ..
217.         }) = self.containers.stack.last_mut()
218.             && *plain_open
219.         {
220.             plain_buffer.clear();

<sub>⏱️ 761m 52s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Evaluating footnote parsing**

I think it's acceptable to have minimal tests passing. However, the user's goal is to migrate blocks, and footnotes act as container-openers like fenced divs. We've used BlockEffect for fenced divs with separate open/close lines, but footnotes require opener plus body starting on the same line, making it more complex. It seems like I should implement this similarly to how definition list parsing works, perhaps by checking for footnote markers at the beginning of parse_inner_content.**Analyzing footnote parser integration**

The user wanted to use a registry, so I'm considering creating a FootnoteDefinitionParser that detects prepared footnotes. The parse_prepared function might need to return an effect that opens the footnote with the marker_end without emitting a paragraph. Since parse_prepared is called earlier, I could avoid the standard call for footnotes and create a custom path. This change might need some modifications in the core dispatcher, which seems a bit ad hoc. Maybe the simplest approach is to remove FootnoteDefinitionParser from the registry and restore core footnote parsing. I'll check the old core logic in the git history to see if it's still accessible.**Searching git history**

I think it's time to search the git history to find the core code I'm looking for. I'll run a command in bash like `git log -n 20 -- src/parser/core.rs` to locate the relevant commits. Using tools like this can help me quickly pinpoint changes made to the file. This might reveal the footnote logic or any alterations that would inform my next steps in parsing footnotes properly. Let's see what I can find!*

### 💭 Reasoning

***Revising footnote logic**

I’m figuring out a simple fix for the footnote change: either remove the FootnoteDefinitionParser or keep it without using it, while bringing back the core's explicit definition logic. I’m also thinking about how footnote markers should work and whether they can interrupt paragraphs based on current logic; seems I need to clarify detection conditions.**Clarifying block handling**

I’m looking at how reference definitions are treated regarding blank lines and paragraph interruption. They don't require a blank line before but can interrupt paragraphs, which feels like they should behave as YesCanInterrupt by closing the paragraph if it's open. However, the core's logic doesn’t close paragraphs for Yes, only for YesCanInterrupt. The reference definition parser should ideally return YesCanInterrupt, not just Yes. It’s interesting that the comment says no preparation is needed, implying they should not close a paragraph. This might complicate things!**Examining reference definition handling**

I'm inspecting how the reference definition parser processes indentation and whether it uses builder tokens within a paragraph. 1. //! Paragraph handling utilities.
2. //!
3. //! Note: Most paragraph logic is in the main Parser since paragraphs
4. //! are tightly integrated with container handling.
5. 
6. use crate::config::Config;
7. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
8. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
9. 
10. use crate::parser::utils::container_stack::{Container, ContainerStack};
11. use crate::parser::utils::text_buffer::ParagraphBuffer;
12. 
13. /// Start a paragraph if not already in one.
14. pub(in crate::parser) fn start_paragraph_if_needed(
15.     containers: &mut ContainerStack,
16.     builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
17. ) {
18.     if !matches!(containers.last(), Some(Container::Paragraph { .. })) {
19.         builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::PARAGRAPH.into());
20.         containers.push(Container::Paragraph {
21.             buffer: ParagraphBuffer::new(),
22.         });
23.     }
24. }
25. 
26. /// Append a line to the current paragraph (preserving losslessness).
27. pub(in crate::parser) fn append_paragraph_line(
28.     containers: &mut ContainerStack,
29.     _builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
30.     line: &str,
31.     _config: &Config,
32. ) {
33.     // Buffer the line (with newline for losslessness)
34.     // Works for ALL paragraphs including those in blockquotes
35.     if let Some(Container::Paragraph { buffer }) = containers.stack.last_mut() {
36.         buffer.push_text(line);
37.     }
38. }
39. 
40. /// Buffer a blockquote marker in the current paragraph.
41. ///
42. /// Called when processing blockquote continuation lines while a paragraph is open
43. /// and using integrated inline parsing. The marker will be emitted at the correct
44. /// position when the paragraph is closed.
45. pub(in crate::parser) fn append_paragraph_marker(
46.     containers: &mut ContainerStack,
47.     leading_spaces: usize,
48.     has_trailing_space: bool,
49. ) {
50.     if let Some(Container::Paragraph { buffer }) = containers.stack.last_mut() {
51.         buffer.push_marker(leading_spaces, has_trailing_space);
52.     }
53. }
54. 
55. /// Get the current content column from the container stack.
56. pub(in crate::parser) fn current_content_col(containers: &ContainerStack) -> usize {
57.     containers
58.         .stack
59.         .iter()
60.         .rev()
61.         .find_map(|c| match c {
62.             Container::ListItem { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
63.             Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
64.             _ => None,
65.         })
66.         .unwrap_or(0)
67. }
```

</details>

To parse subsequent lines, I need to open the footnote container. The architecture utilizes a ContainerStack and requires proper indent stripping, but right now, FootnoteDefinition is not pushing, so the content_indent won’t include the base. I need to pinpoint where block_ctx.content_indent is computed and see how footnote definitions should align regarding indentation.**Revisiting footnote parser logic**

I'm working on the ContinuationPolicy in compute_levels_to_keep regarding footnotes. It adds content_col to content_indent_so_far and keeps the level if raw_indent_cols meets the min_indent, which equals the max of content_col or 4. This suggests that content_col serves as additional indent from the footnote prefix. In the current footnote parser, marker_end is measured in bytes instead of columns, and it's not integrated correctly. I think we should return to the container-based parsing method, similar to how we handled other blocks, while also considering how to modify BlockEffect to include the necessary payload.**Analyzing footnote structure**

Once parse_prepared returns lines_consumed, I realize I access block_match.payload, which is passed to parse_prepared with the PreparedBlockMatch reference. 1820.     ///
1821.     /// This method implements the two-phase parsing:
1822.     /// 1. Detection: Check if any parser can handle this content
1823.     /// 2. Caller prepares (closes paragraphs, flushes buffers)
1824.     /// 3. Parser emits the block
1825.     ///
1826.     /// Returns (parser_index, detection_result) if a parser can handle this,
1827.     /// or None if no parser matched.
1828.     #[allow(dead_code)]
1829.     pub fn detect(
1830.         &self,
1831.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1832.         lines: &[&str],
1833.         line_pos: usize,
1834.     ) -> Option<(usize, BlockDetectionResult)> {
1835.         for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
1836.             let result = parser.can_parse(ctx, lines, line_pos);
1837.             match result {
1838.                 BlockDetectionResult::Yes | BlockDetectionResult::YesCanInterrupt => {
1839.                     log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
1840.                     return Some((i, result));
1841.                 }
1842.                 BlockDetectionResult::No => continue,
1843.             }
1844.         }
1845.         None
1846.     }
1847. 
1848.     /// Like `detect()`, but allows parsers to return cached payload for emission.
1849.     pub fn detect_prepared(
1850.         &self,
1851.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1852.         lines: &[&str],
1853.         line_pos: usize,
1854.     ) -> Option<PreparedBlockMatch> {
1855.         for (i, parser) in self.parsers.iter().enumerate() {
1856.             if let Some((detection, payload)) = parser.detect_prepared(ctx, lines, line_pos) {
1857.                 log::debug!("Block detected by: {}", parser.name());
1858.                 return Some(PreparedBlockMatch {
1859.                     parser_index: i,
1860.                     detection,
1861.                     effect: parser.effect(),
1862.                     payload,
1863.                 });
1864.             }
1865.         }
1866.         None
1867.     }
1868. 
1869.     /// Parse a block using the specified parser (by index from detect()).
1870.     ///
1871.     /// Should only be called after detect() returns Some and after
1872.     /// caller has prepared for the block element.
1873.     #[allow(dead_code)]
1874.     pub fn parse(
1875.         &self,
1876.         parser_index: usize,
1877.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1878.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1879.         lines: &[&str],
1880.         line_pos: usize,
1881.     ) -> usize {
1882.         let parser = &self.parsers[parser_index];
1883.         log::debug!("Block parsed by: {}", parser.name());
1884.         parser.parse(ctx, builder, lines, line_pos)
1885.     }
1886. 
1887.     pub fn parse_prepared(
1888.         &self,
1889.         block_match: &PreparedBlockMatch,
1890.         ctx: &BlockContext,
1891.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
1892.         lines: &[&str],
1893.         line_pos: usize,
1894.     ) -> usize {
1895.         let parser = &self.parsers[block_match.parser_index];
1896.         log::debug!("Block parsed by: {}", parser.name());
1897.         parser.parse_prepared(
1898.             ctx,
1899.             builder,
1900.             lines,

<sub>⏱️ 763m 14s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Fixing footnote migration**

I need to address the issue with footnote migration. Currently, the FootnoteDefinitionParser creates a FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION node but doesn't include container semantics, which is making multi-line footnotes fail. One option is to revert recent changes and disable the dispatcher parser, but since the user wants to continue migration, this is tricky. Restoring previous behavior would be a quick solution, but it’s unclear how to implement minimal core-side footnote parsing. Alternatively, I could look into implementing a new BlockEffect for footnote definitions, although that adds complexity with node management across lines.**Revising footnote handling**

To handle footnotes, I want to start the FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION node and push the Container::FootnoteDefinition, parsing the remainder of the line as a paragraph. It makes sense to bring back footnote parsing in the core where the old code was removed and adjust the dispatcher to avoid parsing footnotes. As we’re mid-refactor, keeping the FootnoteDefinitionParser but tweaking it to manage the open container could work. This would allow the core to push the container while maintaining a consistent structure. For the footnote, parse_prepared should handle starting the node and emitting the necessary tokens.**Clarifying footnote parsing structure**

I need to ensure that the rest of the line after the footnote marker is treated as paragraph content, relying on the core's regular parsing for any following lines. The core should know to keep the footnote definition node open after the parse_prepared function returns the consumed lines, which is likely just one for the marker line. After starting the node and emitting the marker tokens, it shouldn’t finish the node until the footnote ends, utilizing the core’s close_containers_to function. I’ll need a container to represent the open FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION and adjust the builder to start the node when the effect opens the footnote.**Restructuring footnote handling**

I’m considering a restructuring in the dispatcher to detect footnote definition lines and return an effect called OpenFootnoteDefinition, which would include the marker_end and content_col. Currently, BlockEffect only supports FencedDiv, but I could extend it to include OpenFootnoteDefinition. However, effects cannot store data directly; maybe I can create variants without data and use payloads. The core's effect handling also needs to be updated to manage opening and closing footnotes. The footnote container should stay open for subsequent blocks until it encounters a line that isn't properly indented.**Searching for footnote logic**

I want to look through older commits to find footnote logic, but my initial grep search shows no matches. I should check the git show command for the commit just before footnote removal, possibly in c0672ae, since it appears that core.rs no longer includes footnote handling. It seems the removal happened around that commit. I'll also consider earlier commits, like dc2a110, to find any remnants. Using `git show` should help me track down the removed footnote logic in core.rs, especially looking for the "FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION" string in the history.*

1352:                .start_node(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION.into());
1357:                .token(SyntaxKind::FOOTNOTE_REFERENCE.into(), marker_text);
1364:                .push(Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col });

1861:            // Emit container indent (e.g., footnote indent) before the marker
        for (i, c) in self.containers.stack.iter().enumerate() {
            match c {
                Container::BlockQuote { .. } => {
                    // Count blockquotes up to this point
                    let bq_count = self.containers.stack[..=i]
                        .iter()
                        .filter(|x| matches!(x, Container::BlockQuote { .. }))
                        .count();
                    if bq_count <= next_bq_depth {
                        keep_level = i + 1;
                    }
                }
                Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col, .. } => {
                    // Track footnote indent for nested containers
                    content_indent_so_far += *content_col;
                    // Footnote continuation: line must be indented at least 4 spaces
                    // (or at the content column if content started after marker)
                    let min_indent = (*content_col).max(4);
                    if raw_indent_cols >= min_indent {
                        keep_level = i + 1;
                    }
                }
                Container::Definition { content_col, .. } => {
                    // Definition continuation: line must be indented at least 4 spaces
                    // After a blank line, only keep if there's nested block content (lists, code, etc)
                    // Plain text after blank line should close the definition
                    let min_indent = (*content_col).max(4);
                    if raw_indent_cols >= min_indent {
                        // Check what kind of content this is
                        let after_content_indent = if raw_indent_cols >= content_indent_so_far {
                            let idx = byte_index_at_column(next_line, content_indent_so_far);
                            &next_line[idx..]
                        } else {
                            next_line
                        };

                        // Keep Definition if there's a definition marker or nested block structure
                        let has_definition_marker =
                            try_parse_definition_marker(after_content_indent).is_some();
                        let has_list_marker =
                            try_parse_list_marker(after_content_indent, self.config).is_some();
                        let has_block_structure = has_list_marker
                            || count_blockquote_markers(after_content_indent).0 > 0
                            || try_parse_fence_open(after_content_indent).is_some()
                            || try_parse_div_fence_open(after_content_indent).is_some()
                            || try_parse_horizontal_rule(after_content_indent).is_some();

                        if !has_definition_marker && has_block_structure {
                            // Keep Definition for nested block content
                            keep_level = i + 1;
                        }
                        // Otherwise let Definition close (either new definition or plain text)
                    }
                }
                Container::List {
                    marker,
                    base_indent_cols,
                } => {
                    // Adjust indent for footnote context
                    let effective_indent = raw_indent_cols.saturating_sub(content_indent_so_far);
                    let continues_list = if let Some((ref nm, _, _)) = next_marker {
                        markers_match(marker, nm) && effective_indent <= base_indent_cols + 3
                    } else {
                        // For non-list-marker lines, must be indented past list content
                        let item_content_col = self
                            .containers
                            .stack
                            .get(i + 1)
                            .and_then(|c| match c {
                                Container::ListItem { content_col } => Some(*content_col),
                                _ => None,
                            })

                    };
                    if continues_list {

                    }
                }
                Container::ListItem { content_col } => {
                    // Keep list item if next line is indented to content column
                    // BUT NOT if it's a new list item marker at an outer level

                    // Special case: if next line has MORE blockquote markers than current depth,
                    // those extra markers count as "content" that should be indented for list continuation.
                    // Example: "> - item" followed by ">   > nested" - the 2 spaces between the markers
                    // indicate list continuation, and the second > is content.
                    let effective_indent = if next_bq_depth > current_bq_depth {
                        // The line has extra blockquote markers. After stripping current depth's markers,
                        // check the indent before any remaining markers.
                        let after_current_bq =
                            blockquotes::strip_n_blockquote_markers(next_line, current_bq_depth);
                        let (spaces_before_next_marker, _) = leading_indent(after_current_bq);
                        spaces_before_next_marker.saturating_sub(content_indent_so_far)
                    } else {
                        raw_indent_cols.saturating_sub(content_indent_so_far)
                    };

                    log::debug!(
                        "ListItem continuation check: content_col={}, effective_indent={}, next_bq_depth={}, current_bq_depth={}",
                        content_col,
                        effective_indent,
                        next_bq_depth,
                        current_bq_depth
                    );

                        // Check if this marker would start a sibling item (at parent list level)

                        effective_indent < *content_col
                    } else {
                        false
                    };

                    if !is_new_item_at_outer_level && effective_indent >= *content_col {
                        keep_level = i + 1;
                        log::debug!(

                            effective_indent,

    /// Get the total indentation to strip from content containers (footnotes + definitions).
    fn content_container_indent_to_strip(&self) -> usize {
        self.containers
            .stack
            .iter()
            .filter_map(|c| match c {
                Container::FootnoteDefinition { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
                Container::Definition { content_col, .. } => Some(*content_col),
                _ => None,
            })
            .sum()
    }

<sub>⏱️ 763m 45s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Addressing footnote issues**

I need to address the current breakage with footnote definitions in the dispatcher not opening a container. I have two paths: I can either add a new BlockEffect for OpenFootnoteDefinition, similar to the fenced div, or adjust the FootnoteDefinitionParser to only start the node and emit the marker before pushing the container. It’s crucial to ensure the closing node occurs properly when the container closes, as previously done in the old core for footnotes.**Clarifying paragraph line handling**

I need to note that the `append_paragraph_line` function expects a raw line string with a newline to preserve line endings. In the old code, it seems like `first_line_content` didn't include the newline because it was derived from stripped content. However, content should include the newline as it's from the `split_lines_inclusive`, meaning each line retains its newline unless it’s the last line. In the dispatcher, the context content passed to the parser looks promising, but my current approach in the FootnoteDefinitionParser might be inadvertently using the wrong newline token.**Mimicking old behavior for footnotes**

I need to ensure we replicate the old behavior by creating a FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION node and emitting the marker token while pushing the container via an effect. However, the block dispatcher in `parse_prepared` doesn't have access to the containers to push them at the right time. I realize that we need both effect and core adjustments to accomplish this. I'll keep the `content_col` constant at 4 for now, ensuring indentation and continuation rules are consistent with tests. Closing logic will also check the minimum indentation correctly.**Understanding footnote and paragraph behavior**

Footnote definitions can indeed interrupt paragraphs without needing a blank line, which matches the old logic. In that code, footnotes would be recognized regardless of whether there was a blank line. The detection mechanism in our dispatcher seems inverted; it should set detection to `YesCanInterrupt` always, rather than only when a blank line is present. In our current setup, if we're in the middle of a paragraph, we correctly set it to `YesCanInterrupt`, so that part is on track!**Clarifying footnote detection and semantics**

For the document start or when there's a blank line before, we set detection to `Yes`, allowing the system to call `prepare_for_block_element`, which closes paragraphs. However, this might affect footnotes' ability to interrupt paragraphs when necessary. It appears that the core uses `Yes` to indicate that interruptions are not allowed without a blank line, meaning that for footnotes, I should set detection to `YesCanInterrupt` to match the old behavior. I also need to ensure that the marker token includes the correct format, including spaces after it.*

209. ///
210. /// Syntax:
211. /// ```markdown

214. pub fn try_parse_footnote_marker(line: &str) -> Option<(String, usize)> {
215.     let bytes = line.as_bytes();
216. 
217.     // Must start with [^
218.     if bytes.len() < 4 || bytes[0] != b'[' || bytes[1] != b'^' {
219.         return None;
220.     }
221.

223.     let mut pos = 2;

225.         pos += 1;
226.     }
227. 
228.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b']' {
229.         return None;
230.     }
231. 
232.     let id = &line[2..pos];
233.     if id.is_empty() {
234.         return None;
235.     }
236. 
237.     pos += 1; // Skip ]
238. 
239.     // Must be followed by :
240.     if pos >= bytes.len() || bytes[pos] != b':' {
241.         return None;
242.     }
243.     pos += 1;
244. 
245.     // Skip spaces/tabs until content (or end of line)
246.     while pos < bytes.len() && matches!(bytes[pos], b' ' | b'\t') {
247.         pos += 1;
248.     }
249. 
250.     Some((id.to_string(), pos))

Now, I need to determine the next steps based on the current state of the repo.**Planning footnote migration fix**

Given the complexity, I propose reverting the footnote migration attempt. This means deleting the FootnoteDefinitionParser registration in the dispatcher and restoring the core footnote parsing code from e55bcc3, updating it for the current environment. I’ll also fix any unused import comments. It’s important to ensure this adjustment doesn’t conflict with dispatcher detection of footnotes, especially since some comments might now be outdated. We need to clarify how footnotes are currently parsed, ensuring they’re handled properly.**Analyzing core module dependencies**

I’m considering whether the core really needs the reference_links module since there’s an unused import warning for `use super::blocks::reference_links;`. Since we’re reintroducing footnote parsing, this import might actually be useful again. In the old code, it used `try_parse_footnote_marker` and `try_parse_reference_definition`, but now reference definitions are managed by the dispatcher. We’ll only need `try_parse_footnote_marker`, so I’ll update core.rs accordingly. Also, we need to manage container closing rules, including for footnotes. I'll re-add the footnote parsing block just before the reference definitions.**Determining footnote parsing placement**

Since reference definitions are now in the dispatcher, I’m considering where to insert the footnote parsing. In the old code, footnotes were placed after fenced code and before reference definitions, as well as indented code blocks. In the current core.rs, dispatcher detection starts earlier, so I need to position the footnote parsing carefully. It should happen before parsing lists, definition lists, or line blocks to ensure the dispatcher doesn’t incorrectly match footnotes after removing the FootnoteDefinitionParser. I think placing footnote definitions before lists is likely the way to go.**Revising footnote handling in parsing**

In Pandoc, footnote definitions can interrupt paragraphs, starting with `[^`, so I should check for footnotes before parsing list markers. This means inserting my footnote check right before line 1067 where lists begin. I need to consider the handling of content indentation after stripping. The old code managed footnote markers and included whitespace for lossless emission, so I'll need to ensure that any indentation from parent containers is emitted correctly. Ultimately, I’ll ensure footnote definitions are properly closed and that we maintain necessary whitespace for lossless parsing. It’s quite the puzzle to make everything fit!**Adjusting footnote parsing logic**

If we emit `indent_to_emit`, the `marker_text` should come from the original unstripped content to exclude any indent. 1. //! Buffer for accumulating list item content before emission.
2. //!
3. //! This module provides infrastructure for buffering list item content during parsing,
4. //! allowing us to determine tight vs loose lists and parse inline elements correctly.
5. 
6. use crate::config::Config;
7. use crate::parser::utils::inline_emission;
8. use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
9. use rowan::GreenNodeBuilder;
10. 
11. /// A segment in the list item buffer - either text content or a blank line.
12. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
13. pub(crate) enum ListItemContent {
14.     /// Text content (includes newlines for losslessness)
15.     Text(String),
16.     /// A blank line (affects tight/loose determination)
17.     #[allow(dead_code)]
18.     BlankLine,
19.     /// A task list checkbox (must be first content after marker)
20.     #[allow(dead_code)]
21.     TaskCheckbox { checked: bool },
22. }
23. 
24. /// Buffer for accumulating list item content before emission.
25. ///
26. /// Collects text, blank lines, and structural elements as we parse list item
27. /// continuation lines. When the list item closes, we can:
28. /// 1. Determine if it's tight (Plain) or loose (PARAGRAPH)
29. /// 2. Parse inline elements correctly across continuation lines
30. /// 3. Emit the complete structure
31. #[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]
32. pub(crate) struct ListItemBuffer {
33.     /// Segments of content in order
34.     segments: Vec<ListItemContent>,
35. }
36. 
37. impl ListItemBuffer {
38.     /// Create a new empty list item buffer.
39.     pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
40.         Self {
41.             segments: Vec::new(),
42.         }
43.     }
44. 
45.     /// Push text content to the buffer.
46.     pub(crate) fn push_text(&mut self, text: impl Into<String>) {
47.         let text = text.into();
48.         if text.is_empty() {
49.             return;
50.         }
51.         self.segments.push(ListItemContent::Text(text));
52.     }
53. 
54.     /// Push a blank line to the buffer.
55.     #[allow(dead_code)]
56.     pub(crate) fn push_blank_line(&mut self) {
57.         self.segments.push(ListItemContent::BlankLine);
58.     }
59. 
60.     /// Push a task checkbox to the buffer.
61.     #[allow(dead_code)]
62.     pub(crate) fn push_task_checkbox(&mut self, checked: bool) {
63.         self.segments
64.             .push(ListItemContent::TaskCheckbox { checked });
65.     }
66. 
67.     /// Check if buffer is empty.
68.     pub(crate) fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
69.         self.segments.is_empty()
70.     }
71. 
72.     /// Get the number of segments in the buffer (for debugging).
73.     pub(crate) fn segment_count(&self) -> usize {
74.         self.segments.len()
75.     }
76. 
77.     /// Determine if this list item has blank lines between content.
78.     ///
79.     /// Used to decide between Plain (tight) and PARAGRAPH (loose).
80.     /// Returns true if there's a blank line followed by more content.
81.     pub(crate) fn has_blank_lines_between_content(&self) -> bool {
82.         let mut seen_blank = false;
83.         let mut seen_content_after_blank = false;
84. 
85.         for segment in &self.segments {
86.             match segment {
87.                 ListItemContent::BlankLine => {
88.                     seen_blank = true;
89.                 }
90.                 ListItemContent::Text(_) | ListItemContent::TaskCheckbox { .. } => {
91.                     if seen_blank {
92.                         seen_content_after_blank = true;
93.                         break;
94.                     }
95.                 }
96.             }
97.         }
98. 
99.         log::trace!(
100.             "has_blank_lines_between_content: segments={} seen_blank={} seen_content_after={} result={}",
101.             self.segments.len(),
102.             seen_blank,
103.             seen_content_after_blank,
104.             seen_content_after_blank
105.         );
106. 
107.         seen_content_after_blank
108.     }
109. 
110.     /// Get concatenated text for inline parsing (excludes blank lines).
111.     fn get_text_for_parsing(&self) -> String {
112.         let mut result = String::new();
113.         for segment in &self.segments {
114.             match segment {
115.                 ListItemContent::Text(text) => {
116.                     result.push_str(text);
117.                 }
118.                 ListItemContent::TaskCheckbox { checked } => {
119.                     // Emit checkbox syntax so it can be parsed
120.                     if *checked {
121.                         result.push_str("[x]");
122.                     } else {
123.                         result.push_str("[ ]");
124.                     }
125.                 }
126.                 ListItemContent::BlankLine => {
127.                     // Skip blank lines in text extraction
128.                 }
129.             }
130.         }
131.         result
132.     }
133. 
134.     /// Emit the buffered content as a Plain or PARAGRAPH block.
135.     ///
136.     /// If `use_paragraph` is true, wraps in PARAGRAPH (loose list).
137.     /// If false, wraps in PLAIN (tight list).
138.     pub(crate) fn emit_as_block(
139.         &self,
140.         builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>,
141.         use_paragraph: bool,
142.         config: &Config,
143.     ) {
144.         if self.is_empty() {
145.             return;
146.         }
147. 
148.         let block_kind = if use_paragraph {
149.             SyntaxKind::PARAGRAPH
150.         } else {
151.             SyntaxKind::PLAIN
152.         };
153. 
154.         builder.start_node(block_kind.into());
155. 
156.         // Get text and parse inline elements
157.         let text = self.get_text_for_parsing();
158. 
159.         // Handle task checkbox specially
160.         if let Some(ListItemContent::TaskCheckbox { checked }) = self.segments.first() {
161.             // Emit checkbox as a token
162.             let checkbox_text = if *checked { "[x]" } else { "[ ]" };
163.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TASK_CHECKBOX.into(), checkbox_text);
164. 
165.             // Parse remaining text
166.             let remaining_text = text.strip_prefix(checkbox_text).unwrap_or(&text);
167.             if !remaining_text.is_empty() {
168.                 inline_emission::emit_inlines(builder, remaining_text, config);
169.             }
170.         } else {
171.             // No checkbox, just parse all text
172.             if !text.is_empty() {
173.                 inline_emission::emit_inlines(builder, &text, config);
174.             }
175.         }
176. 
177.         builder.finish_node(); // Close PLAIN or PARAGRAPH
178.     }
179. 
180.     /// Clear the buffer for reuse.
181.     #[allow(dead_code)]
182.     pub(crate) fn clear(&mut self) {
183.         self.segments.clear();
184.     }
185. }
186.

190. 
191.     #[test]
192.     fn test_new_buffer_is_empty() {

194.         assert!(buffer.is_empty());
195.         assert!(!buffer.has_blank_lines_between_content());
196.     }
197. 
198.     #[test]
199.     fn test_push_single_text() {
200.         let mut buffer = ListItemBuffer::new();
201.         buffer.push_text("Hello, world!");
202.         assert!(!buffer.is_empty());
203.         assert!(!buffer.has_blank_lines_between_content());
204.         assert_eq!(buffer.get_text_for_parsing(), "Hello, world!");
205.     }
206. 
207.     #[test]
208.     fn test_push_multiple_text_segments() {
209.         let mut buffer = ListItemBuffer::new();
210.         buffer.push_text("Line 1\n");
211.         buffer.push_text("Line 2\n");
212.         buffer.push_text("Line 3");
213.         assert_eq!(buffer.get_text_for_parsing(), "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3");
214.     }
215. 
216.     #[test]
217.     fn test_blank_line_not_between_content() {
218.         let mut buffer = ListItemBuffer::new();
219.         buffer.push_text("Content\n");
220.         buffer.push_blank_line();

I need to weigh both options to find the smallest change that accomplishes our goal.**Managing footnote definitions**

In the dispatcher parse, starting the FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION node triggers a marker emission, and the core pushes the container while keeping the node open. The tricky part is figuring out how to close it. Currently, close_containers_to finishes generic containers, which would close the FootnoteDefinition when blank lines or indentation stop. I need to ensure that parse_prepared doesn’t finish the FOOTNOTE_DEFINITION node, but rather leaves it open. I’ll adjust parse_prepared to start the node and emit the necessary indentation to preserve formatting while handling stripped content in the indent emission.**Examining indentation handling**

When I pass ctx.content to the dispatcher, it’s stripped of content, but the builder still needs the original indentation tokens, especially if it’s within a container. For paragraphs and lists, the core emits indent_to_emit tokens before the markers. Now, if I’m parsing stripped content but need to give back the leading whitespace that got stripped, I’ll need to work on that. 979. fn emit_reference_definition_content(builder: &mut GreenNodeBuilder<'static>, text: &str) {
980.     use crate::syntax::SyntaxKind;
981. 
982.     if !text.starts_with('[') {
983.         builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
984.         return;
985.     }
986. 
987.     let rest = &text[1..];
988.     if let Some(close_pos) = rest.find(']') {
989.         let label = &rest[..close_pos];
990.         let after_bracket = &rest[close_pos + 1..];
991. 
992.         if after_bracket.starts_with(':') {
993.             // Emit LINK node with the label
994.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK.into());
995. 
996.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into());
997.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::LINK_START.into(), "[");
998.             builder.finish_node();
999. 
1000.             builder.start_node(SyntaxKind::LINK_TEXT.into());
1001.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), label);
1002.             builder.finish_node();
1003. 
1004.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), "]");
1005.             builder.finish_node(); // LINK
1006. 
1007.             builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), after_bracket);
1008.             return;
1009.         }
1010.     }
1011. 
1012.     builder.token(SyntaxKind::TEXT.into(), text);
1013. }

However, we need to ensure it triggers correctly in both contexts, whether there's a blank line or not. This should respect the configuration for footnotes, so I’ll set it up similarly, considering indentation and losslessness when emitting the footnote marker.**Inserting footnote logic**

I’m thinking about where to insert the footnote logic. It seems logical to add it after the dispatcher block checks, but that wouldn't allow footnotes to interrupt paragraphs. The `parse_inner_content` handles blocks that require a blank line, but for footnotes, we need to recognize them regardless of that. 1737. /// Registry of block parsers, ordered by priority.
1738. ///
1739. /// This dispatcher tries each parser in order until one succeeds.
1740. /// The ordering follows Pandoc's approach - explicit list order rather
1741. /// than numeric priorities.
1742. pub(crate) struct BlockParserRegistry {
1743.     parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>>,
1744. }
1745. 
1746. impl BlockParserRegistry {
1747.     /// Create a new registry with all block parsers.
1748.     ///
1749.     /// Order matters! Parsers are tried in the order listed here.
1750.     /// This follows Pandoc's design where ordering is explicit and documented.
1751.     ///
1752.     /// **Pandoc reference order** (from pandoc/src/Text/Pandoc/Readers/Markdown.hs:487-515):
1753.     /// 1. blanklines (handled separately in our parser)
1754.     /// 2. codeBlockFenced
1755.     /// 3. yamlMetaBlock' ← YAML metadata comes early!
1756.     /// 4. bulletList
1757.     /// 5. divHtml
1758.     /// 6. divFenced
1759.     /// 7. header ← ATX headings
1760.     /// 8. lhsCodeBlock
1761.     /// 9. htmlBlock
1762.     /// 10. table
1763.     /// 11. codeBlockIndented
1764.     /// 12. rawTeXBlock (LaTeX)
1765.     /// 13. lineBlock
1766.     /// 14. blockQuote
1767.     /// 15. hrule ← Horizontal rules come AFTER headers!
1768.     /// 16. orderedList
1769.     /// 17. definitionList
1770.     /// 18. noteBlock (footnotes)
1771.     /// 19. referenceKey ← Reference definitions
1772.     /// 20. abbrevKey
1773.     /// 21. para
1774.     /// 22. plain
1775.     pub fn new() -> Self {
1776.         let parsers: Vec<Box<dyn BlockParser>> = vec![
1777.             // Match Pandoc's ordering to ensure correct precedence:
1778.             // (0) Pandoc title block (must be at document start).
1779.             Box::new(PandocTitleBlockParser),
1780.             // (2) Fenced code blocks - can interrupt paragraphs!
1781.             Box::new(FencedCodeBlockParser),
1782.             // (3) YAML metadata - before headers and hrules!
1783.             Box::new(YamlMetadataParser),
1784.             // (6) Fenced divs ::: (open/close)
1785.             Box::new(FencedDivCloseParser),
1786.             Box::new(FencedDivOpenParser),
1787.             // (7) Setext headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
1788.             // Must come before ATX to properly handle `---` disambiguation
1789.             Box::new(SetextHeadingParser),
1790.             // (7) ATX headings (part of Pandoc's "header" parser)
1791.             Box::new(AtxHeadingParser),
1792.             // (9) HTML blocks
1793.             Box::new(HtmlBlockParser),
1794.             // (12) LaTeX environment blocks
1795.             Box::new(LatexEnvironmentParser),
1796.             // (10) Tables
1797.             Box::new(TableParser),
1798.             // (13) Line blocks
1799.             Box::new(LineBlockParser),
1800.             // (11) Indented code blocks (AFTER fenced!)
1801.             Box::new(IndentedCodeBlockParser),
1802.             // (15) Horizontal rules - AFTER headings per Pandoc
1803.             Box::new(HorizontalRuleParser),
1804.             // Figures (standalone images) - Pandoc doesn't have these
1805.             Box::new(FigureParser),
1806.             // (18) Footnote definitions
1807.             Box::new(FootnoteDefinitionParser),
1808.             // (19) Reference definitions
1809.             Box::new(ReferenceDefinitionParser),
1810.             // TODO: Migrate remaining blocks in Pandoc order:
1811.             // - (4-6) Lists and divs (bulletList, divHtml)
1812.             // - (16) Ordered lists
1813.             // - (17) Definition lists
1814.         ];
1815. 
1816.         Self { parsers }
1817.     }
1818. 
1819.     /// Try to parse a block using the registered parsers.
1820.     ///

I’m contemplating how footnotes should close when encountering less indent. If the next line has less indent, the current logic trims content but keeps the container open, which might cause issues with footnote continuation. The parser likely closes footnotes on a blank line, but maybe it should close on any non-indented line. 

I need to check the earlier part of the core logic, particularly around how containers are closed when indentation changes. It looks like there’s only explicit closure on blank lines. I could revert the removal and implement footnote definition parsing in the core or integrate footnotes using BlockEffect, allowing for better container management. The user emphasized footnote migration as a top priority, so I want to ensure I handle it efficiently!**Evaluating footnote changes**
